Name: European Space Agency - ESA
Description:
The European Space Agency (ESA) is Europe’s gateway to space. Its mission is to shape the development of Europe’s space capability and ensure that investment in space continues to deliver benefits to the citizens of Europe and the world. 

ESA is an international organisation with 23 Member States. By coordinating the financial and intellectual resources of its members, it can undertake programmes and activities far beyond the scope of any single European country. 

ESA's 23 Member States are Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland and the United Kingdom. 

Slovakia, Latvia and Lithuania are Associate Members. Canada takes part in some projects under a cooperation agreement. 

Four other EU states have Cooperation Agreements with ESA: Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus and Malta.

Description:
A new space age is emerging and the extraordinary opportunities in space depend on the transportation systems from Earth to Space. At Skyroot, we are building technologies for responsive, reliable and economic access to space. We envision a future where spaceflight is as regular, reliable and affordable as airflight.

Name: وكالة الفضاء السعودية | Saudi Space Agency
Description:
بقرار من مجلس الوزراء تم اعتماد تنظيم وكالة الفضاء السعودية لأهمية التركيز على الصناعة والابتكار في قطاع الفضاء
صدر قرار مجلس الوزراء باعتماد تنظيم وكالة الفضاء السعودية لتكون خطوة نحو مستقبل أكثر ابتكارًا وتطلعًا لأحدث التقنيات والفرص في قطاع الفضاء السعودي.
تتوافق أهداف وكالة الفضاء السعودية مع تطلعات المملكة نحو حياة أكثر جودة وتقدم، حيث تتوافق مع رؤيتها لخلق بيئات أفضل وأكثر أمانًا لمواطنيها، مع خلق فرص جديدة لمزيد من الابتكارات المربحة الداعمة للاقتصاد السعودي.
تقتضي استراتيجية وكالة الفضاء السعودية وضع مجموعة من الأهداف الأولية التي تركز على علوم الفضاء والبعثات الاستكشافية وخلق فرص جديدة في القطاع وتمكين الكوادر الوطنية لتحقيق النمو والتقدم بما يخدم الوطن والإنسانية.

The Council of Ministers Resolution approved the Saudi Space Agency statute for the importance of focusing on industry and innovation in the space sector

The Council of Ministers Resolution has been issued for the approval of The Saudi Space Agency statute, which is considered as a step towards a more innovative and forward-looking future for the latest technologies and opportunities in the Saudi space sector.

The Saudi Space Agency’s objectives align with the Kingdom’s aspirations for a more advanced quality of life, as they align with its vision to create better and safer environments for its citizens while creating new opportunities for more profitable innovations that support the Saudi economy.

The Saudi Space Agency’s strategy requires determining a set of primary goals that focus on space science and exploratory missions, creating new opportunities in the sector, and empowering national cadres to achieve growth and progress in a way that serves the country and humanity.

Name: Plan-S Satellite and Space Technologies
Description:
From Orbit, for Earth: Meet Plan-S

We’re not just building satellites, we’re building the future.

Founded in Ankara in 2021, Plan-S is Türkiye’s largest private initiative in satellite and space technologies. With a mission to shape a smarter, more connected world, we develop next-generation solutions in IoT communication, Earth Observation, and Space as a Solution, powered by a growing constellation of small satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).

Our Connecta IoT Network delivers low-power, cost-effective, and highly reliable satellite connectivity, especially in regions where terrestrial infrastructure falls short. Actively in use today, it connects millions of devices across critical sectors such as energy, agriculture, logistics, and infrastructure.

With our Observa satellite constellation, we bring Earth Observation data to life, enabling smarter decisions in environmental monitoring, disaster response, and precision agriculture. In a world driven by data, we’re making insights from space accessible, timely, and actionable.

At Plan-S, we design and develop our satellite platforms, payloads, and software entirely in-house. Our global communications network is supported by ground stations in Ankara, Erzurum, and Sweden, along with four additional partner-operated sites. We’ve successfully launched 17 satellites so far, and we’re just getting started. Our goal: a large-scale constellation of hundreds of satellites by 2030.

At Plan-S, we believe space is not the final frontier, it’s the infrastructure of the future. Let’s plan it together. And if you’re ready to redefine what’s possible, join us!

Name: EUSPA - EU Agency for the Space Programme
Description:
The EU Agency for the Space Programme (EUSPA) provides safe and performant space services, enabling synergies, EU innovation, sustainability, and security.

To achieve this, EUSPA manages the Galileo and EGNOS programmes, develops markets for Copernicus services and data, is involved in secure satcom (GOVSATCOM & IRIS2), and carries out work in various areas targeted at Security, Research and Development and increasing space’s contribution to the EU Market. The Agency is also responsible for the security accreditation of all the components of the EU Space Programme. EUSPA also operates the #EUSST Front Desk, protecting more than 400 satellites from the risk of collision with space debris or other operational satellites.

EUSPA, staffed with skilled professionals bringing relevant experience from the public and private sectors, is unique in contributing to one of the most important and ambitious projects ever undertaken by the EU. We are committed to implementing the EU Space Programme and turning the EU space ambitions into a reality.

Name: Deimos
Description:
Deimos is a European technology group founded in 2001, headquartered in Spain, with subsidiaries in five countries—Spain, Portugal, Italy, Romania and the United Kingdom.
Effective November 1, 2024, Deimos was acquired by Indra and merged with the Indra Espacio team, further strengthening its role in the European technology and space sectors.

Name: Astranis Space Technologies
Description:
Astranis builds low-cost satellites for high orbits.

Our first product, MicroGEO, offers the world’s lowest-cost satellite broadband. Operating in geostationary orbit, each MicroGEO satellite can provide dedicated satellites to small and medium-sized countries, Fortune 1000 companies, and other commercial customers. Astranis has built a $1 billion backlog for 12+ MicroGEO satellites launching over the next two years.

Astranis also offers services across orbits and use cases. With contracts in place with Space Force, NASA, and other government partners, Astranis is working across MEO and GEO to support government science, PNT, and communications missions.

Astranis has raised $750 million from top global investors and employs a team of over 400 engineers and builders, headquartered at Historic Pier 70 in San Francisco, California.

Name: Exolaunch
Description:
Exolaunch is a global leader in launch mission management, satellite integration and satellite deployment technologies. With a decade of flight heritage and 650+ satellites launched across 41 missions to date, Exolaunch leverages industry insight to tailor turnkey solutions that meet customer needs and respond to market trends. Exolaunch fulfills launch contracts for industry leaders, the world's most innovative startups, research institutions, government organizations, and international space agencies. The company develops and manufactures its own flight-proven and industry-leading satellite separation systems and payload launch stacks, with the fastest-growing heritage on the market.

Exolaunch, headquartered in Germany, operates globally with offices in the US, France, and Japan. Exolaunch promotes safe, sustainable, and responsible use of space and is committed to making space accessible for all.

We're quietly advancing AI-powered autonomy to redefine how we tackle complex challenges in aerospace. Stay tuned as we prepare to emerge from stealth mode.

Description:
Modern Technology Solutions, Inc. (MTSI) is a 100% employee-owned engineering services and technology solutions company delivering first-choice capabilities to solve problems of global importance in the critical mission areas of missile defense, cyber security, intelligence, unmanned/autonomous systems, aviation, space, and homeland security.  

The MTSI mission is to make important and lasting contributions to the nation's defense and security by providing leadership and best- value solutions to solve America's most technically challenging strategic problems. Founded in 1993, MTSI today has employees at over 20 offices and field sites worldwide. 

For more information about MTSI, please visit www.mtsi-va.com.

Name: Muon Space
Description:
Founded in 2021, Muon Space is an end-to-end Space Systems Provider that designs, builds, and operates satellite constellations delivering mission-critical data.

Our revolutionary, integrated technology stack enables customers to optimize every dimension of their missions for faster time-to-orbit and superior constellation remote sensing performance. From climate monitoring to national security, Muon Space is dedicated to delivering Earth intelligence for a safer and more resilient world.

Name: IN-SPACe
Description:
Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe) is a single-window autonomous government agency with its own cadre that will promote, permit and oversee the space-based activities of Non-Government Private Entities (NGPEs). 
IN-SPACe will enable the private space industry in the fields of Launch vehicles, satellites, ground stations, space technology, space consultancy, and space-based services.  

contact-us@inspace.gov.in

Name: S[&]T
Description:
At S[&]T, we are committed to creating a safer life on earth by transforming space data into valuable and actionable insights. We manage data throughout the entire process, from instrument to application. We apply our expertise within our software frameworks, information products and consultancy. 

Our society relies on space and scientific instruments for crucial services we all use in our daily lives like climate change monitoring, navigation, defence and national security. These instruments generate a vast amount of complex data, and our mission is to unlock its value. 

With over two decades of expertise in instrument calibration, data quality monitoring tools, real-time data processing frameworks and the development of application-specific algorithms, we empower organisations, governments and industries to strengthen their information position and enhance decision-making. We specialize in the following domains: Space & Science, Defence & Security, Environment & Sustainability, and High-Tech Consulting.

Name: ESA Technology
Description: When the majority of new missions at the European Space Agency (ESA) are envisioned, the technology that will make them possible does not even exist – yet. It is the job of ESA’s Directorate of Technology, Engineering and Quality (TEC) to identify the required enabling innovations, make them real and bring them up to the needed level of performance and reliability so that the right technology is ready at just the right time.

Name: Agencia Espacial Española
Description: The Spanish Space Agency is a state agency with legal personality, its own assets, and functional and management autonomy, as established by Law 40/2015. It complies with the provisions of current legislation, including the Science, Technology and Innovation Law, as well as general and specific administrative regulations applicable. Attached to the Ministry of Science and Innovation and the Ministry of Defense, it operates in coordination with other relevant departments and organizations. Based in Seville, the entity is identified as the State Agency "Spanish Space Agency" and uses the acronym AEE. The Spanish Space Agency aims to primarily use space for the benefit, knowledge, and security of Spanish society. It is responsible for establishing, promoting, and coordinating activities and policies that foster research, technological and industrial development, and innovation in the space sector. Its general objectives include promoting, implementing, and developing space research, technology, and innovation, as well as national security, extraterrestrial operations, and satellite applications to develop skills in various areas. It also focuses on the use of satellite data, the technological and economic impact of the space industry, strengthening the national space industry, coordinating Spanish space policy at national and international levels, particularly with the European Space Agency and the European Union space programs. Additionally, the Agency is responsible for competitively and efficiently allocating public resources, monitoring funded initiatives and their impact, and providing advice in planning actions related to research, development, and innovation within the jurisdiction of the General State Administration.

Name: European Space Policy Institute (ESPI)
Description: The European Space Policy Institute (ESPI) is the European think-tank for space. Founded in 2003, ESPI is a not-for-profit association based in Vienna, the world capital of space diplomacy.  The mission of the Institute is to provide decision-makers with an informed view on mid- to long-term issues relevant to Europe’s space activities. To this end, ESPI relies on a multinational team with interdisciplinary expertise to monitor major developments and trends in the global space sector and to conduct independent research and analysis on outstanding issues for European space policy. The Institute is governed by a General Assembly of member organizations and supported by an Advisory Council of independent high-level experts.

Name: e-GEOS
Description:
e-GEOS, a Telespazio (80%) / ASI (20%) company, is a leading international player in the geo-spatial business. e-GEOS offers a complete range of products and services in the Earth Observation and in the geo-spatial application domains, based on both optical and radar satellites as well as on aerial surveys. The Rome-based company employs more than 300 people and operates through the Earth Observation centers in Matera (Italy) and Neustrelitz (Germany). As the European hub for VHR (Very High Resolution) data, e-GEOS grants unique access to the COSMO-SkyMed data, and to the major optical missions.

In particular, COSMO-SkyMed represents a tremendous competitive advantage as it opens up a new era of operational applications. The COSMO-SkyMed earth observation programme of the Italian Space Agency and the Italian Ministry of Defence, based on a constellation of four VHR SAR satellites, provides all-weather, day and night, world-wide radar data acquisitions. e-GEOS exploits COSMO-SkyMed data to offer near-real-time monitoring services and large-area mapping capabilities for customers such as NGA (National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency of the US Government), in combination with its existing product and services portfolio, which comprises solutions for key vertical markets including emergency management, maritime surveillance, agriculture & forestry, cadastre, environmental protection, oil & gas, utilities and industries.

Name: HyPrSpace
Description:
HyPrSpace is making the first scalable hybrid rocket engine, combining simplicity, robustness and reactivity. We embody the audacity and boldness of innovating in a world driven by caution and conformism.

We believe the best way of winning the space race is by building our own tracks. That is why we created HyPrSpace, redefining propulsion with a one-of-a-kind rocket.

Thanks to a robust and reliable patented hybrid rocket technology, HyPrSpace is giving Europe a cost-effective and sovereign path to space. 

We call it ‘Baguettes’.

Name: Azista Space
Description:
Azista Space is a fully integrated satellite technology company, dedicated to advancing secure, reliable, and mission-proven solutions for the global space ecosystem. Built on a strong heritage of successful satellite missions, including contributions to several flagship ISRO programs, Azista Space integrates next generation design, precision manufacturing, and advanced payload engineering to deliver end-to-end capabilities with uncompromising quality.

With a world-class 100,000 sq. ft. facility and an installed annual production capacity of up to 50 microsatellites, Azista Space brings together industrial-scale manufacturing and highly dependable engineering practices. This foundation enables the development of robust satellite platforms, high-performance imaging payloads, and mission-critical subsystems that empowers governments, commercial operators, and strategic partners to protect national interests, enhance Earth observation capabilities, and enable resilient space-based infrastructure.

Name: AstroForge
Description: AstroForge is a deep-space mining company on a mission to extract valuable metals from asteroids. By bringing space resources into Earth's supply chain, we're aiming to reduce the need for traditional terrestrial mining methods and explore a more sustainable future - one mission at a time.

Name: EarthDaily
Description: EarthDaily is the end-to-end provider of Earth Observation solutions, combining deep domain expertise with powerful technology to deliver daily, global insights at unmatched scale and precision. Through our Constellation, a fleet of cutting edge satellites, we detect broad-area change, monitor the planet in near real-time, and deliver actionable intelligence. Our vertically integrated approach spans data collection, processing, analytics, and application, enabling customers to unlock value, improve efficiency, and manage risk across agriculture, forestry, insurance, mining and governmental sectors. We deliver the fuel to power your decisions.

Name: Azista BST Aerospace

This is the first private scale satellite production house in India and one of the first production lines for satellites anywhere in the world. In the words of one of the founders of BST it’s the Henry Ford moment for satellites!

SEAKR, a wholly owned subsidiary of RTX, is a Leading-Edge Provider of Advanced Space Electronics Solutions. 

We develop, build, test and deliver space electronics solutions and products, including, but not limited to:  On-Board Processing and Networking, Memory and Data Management, Avionics, including expertise in FPGA and ASIC based solutions.

We have expertise in utilizing commercial electronics, customized for space applications and have a history of over 40 years of 100% Mission Success.  Our solutions work...period.

SEAKR solutions support any mission across all orbits and environments, for any class, and duration.   Your mission is safe with SEAKR.  

In all areas: Commercial, government, domestic and abroad SEAKR is positioned to support any mission in a proven and reliable manner, every time.  We have experience in both traditional exquisite space, and today's proliferated space.  We have leveraged the exquisite expertise to today's requirements for shorter schedules, and lower-cost solutions.  We have both products and customer solutions and can deliver what the program and mission needs in the time and budget available. 

Contact us at connect@seakr.com

Name: Starlab

The two main priorities of Starlab Space are the design, development, and operation of the commercial Starlab Space Station and the establishment of a sustainable Global Space Enterprise. Starlab’s collaborative approach allows us to leverage the strengths and expertise of individual partners, maximize investments, and accelerate development through our global, commercial space ecosystem.

Description: Expect the unexpected as James Webb Space Telescope unravels the mysteries of this universe. Get a front-row seat to the discoveries made by this engineering marvel by following this page.

Name: GIGA
Description:
Welcome to GIGA Space Exploration, a visionary company founded by Roozy Penrose with the ambitious goal of extending human life across multiple planets and potentially into other galaxies. Building our space startup on a foundational AI model presents us with two significant advantages:

Cost Efficiency: Leveraging AI for our internal operations enhances efficiency and accuracy, dramatically reducing long-term costs.
Accelerated Development: By utilizing AI, we can expedite the design process of rockets by up to 80%, positioning us to establish a company as successful as SpaceX by the 2030s.

Our Immediate Goals Include:
1-Developing Europe's Most Advanced Self-Landing Rockets: Aiming to set new standards in rocket technology.
2-Deploying Defense Satellites: Launching state-of-the-art satellites into near-Earth orbits for the UK government and European space agencies.
3-Establishing a Dedicated Geostationary Orbital Space Station: Creating a pivotal link between Earth and the Moon, serving as a critical hub for extended space missions.
4-Constructing the UK's Dedicated Moon Base: Positioning the UK among the elite group of countries with the capability and ambition to land on the Moon in the 2030s and beyond.

We are always looking for passionate engineers, Consider joining GIGA Space.

Giga space Exploration Company Ltd - a UK registered company

Our professional members include civil space agency officials, government representatives, engineers, scientists, aviation/aerospace/space professionals, business leaders, academics, graduate students, educators, science writers, and concerned private citizens of influence from over 50 countries around the world.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) is the U.S. government agency responsible for the civilian space program and for aeronautics & aerospace research.

Friends of NASA was founded in 2008 by Executive Director, Dwayne Lawrence, as a volunteer-operated organization in honor of the 50th anniversary of NASA’s establishment on July 29th, 1958 by US President Dwight Eisenhower. 

Friends of NASA is headquartered in Montreal, Canada near the Canadian Space Agency (CSA). We work in cooperation with government agencies and non-profit groups, including the Canadian Space Society (CSS), and The Planetary Society.

Name: 𝗚𝗘𝗢𝗙𝗜𝗧
Description:
French company created in 1968 in Nantes, GEOFIT is today a key player in regional and city development, bringing together more than 1,400 employees with its subsidiaries.

Our business consists of measuring, quantifying, and analysing geospatial data to transform them into real decision-making tools.

By relying on its committed teams, GEOFIT contributes every day to supporting and securing development and land projects, in France and around the world.

Name: Starfish Space
Description:
Starfish Space is building the infrastructure for humanity's expansion into Space.

Our Otter servicing vehicle maximizes satellite capabilities through life extension and end of life disposal missions.

Name: ISpA- Indian Space Association
Description: Indian Space Association (ISpA) is the premier industry association of space and satellite companies. ISpA aspires to be the collective voice of the Indian Space industry and undertakes Policy Advocacy and engage with all stakeholders in the Indian Space domain, including the Government and its Agencies, to make India self-reliant, technologically advanced and a leading player in the space arena.

Name: SKA Observatory
Description:
The SKA Observatory is a next-generation radio astronomy driven Big Data facility that will revolutionise our understanding of the Universe and the laws of fundamental physics. Enabled by cutting-edge technology, and bringing together some of the best scientists, engineers and policymakers from more than 20 countries worldwide, it promises to have a major impact on society, in science and beyond. 

The SKAO is a global collaboration of Member States. Headquartered in the UK, at the historic Jodrell Bank Observatory - a UNESCO World Heritage Site - the SKAO's two telescope arrays will be constructed in Australia and South Africa, and be the two most advanced radio telescope networks on Earth.

Together with other state-of-the-art research facilities, the SKAO’s telescopes will explore the unknown frontiers of science and deepen our understanding of key processes, including the formation and evolution of galaxies, fundamental physics in extreme environments and the origins of life. Through the development of innovative technologies and its contribution to addressing societal challenges, the SKAO will play its part to address the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals and deliver significant benefits across its membership and beyond.

Name: SkyFi
Description:
SkyFi is an Earth intelligence company delivering satellite imagery, SAR, AIS, and AI-driven geospatial analytics through scalable data platforms. Headquartered in Austin, Texas, we serve the U.S. Government, international governments, and enterprise customers across defense, civil, and commercial sectors.

Our platform streamlines on-demand access to geospatial data by combining tasking, archive search, and analytics into a single platform. With transparent pricing and rapid delivery, SkyFi helps organizations gain timely intelligence for missions and initiatives.

Name: ARQUIMEA
Description:
ARQUIMEA was founded with the purpose of developing technologies, products, and services that help address the challenges and needs of our society. Since 2005, we have been contributing our passion for innovation and technology  to highly demanding sectors:

 Aerospace

We firmly believe that technology is the key to progress . That is why we reinvest most of our profits in the development of innovative solutions for the sectors in which we operate.

 We operate in 40 countries, allowing us to stay close to our customers worldwide.

At ARQUIMEA, we all share a strong passion for technology. We are constantly seeking new and exciting challenges that enable us to keep moving forward .
Location: Torrejón de Ardoz, Community of Madrid

Name: SPACIM
Description:
Transforming space travel into a profitable frontier with revolutionary launch solutions. Experience faster, smarter, and more profitable access to space with our innovative approach to orbital delivery.

SPACIM is redefining space access with a two-stage rocket designed for precision, efficiency, and affordability. Capable of launching payloads from 100 kg to 1500 kg into LEO altitudes between 200 km and 1200 km, it offers tailored solutions to meet diverse customer mission needs, ensuring smarter and faster orbital access.

Our mission is to revolutionize space access by providing agile, cost-effective, and reliable launch solutions. We aim to empower satellite operators, researchers, and commercial enterprises by simplifying orbital deployment with tailored services, ensuring precise mission execution, and driving innovation in the global space industry.

Why Spacim?
End-to-End Solutions: Comprehensive support for satellite builders, from testing to certification.
Custom Propulsion Systems: Tailored solutions to meet specific satellite requirements
Seamless Integration: Expertise in aligning satellite designs with our launch services.
Precision and Reliability: Ensuring mission success with cutting-edge technology.
Customer-Centric Approach: Dedicated support to meet unique project needs.
Technology & Innovation
Our two-stage rocket system is meticulously designed to deliver payloads ranging from 100 kg to 1500 kg into Low Earth Orbit (LEO) at altitudes between 200 km and 1200 km. We leverage advanced manufacturing techniques, including 3D printing and lightweight composite materials, ensuring optimal performance and reduced costs.

Name: XSam
Description:
XSAM is a space technology company developing advanced Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) technology designed for high-resolution Earth observation. XSAM is building the FOCUS constellation, optimized for monitoring critical infrastructure, providing precise, interferometric-ready data with high-resolution and high-revisit.

Our mission is to empower industries and governments by delivering actionable insights through advanced Interferometric SAR imaging, enabling the detection, monitoring, and predictive analysis of infrastructure health and stability on a global scale with unprecedented precision and reliability, enabling a safer and more sustainable world.

Name: UIU Mars Rover Team (UMRT)
Description: This is the official Linkedln page of UIU Mars Rover Team. The UIU Mars Rover team is formed with more than 25 members of the UIU Robotics Club from different educational backgrounds, including computer science, electrical science and business. This team contains excellent enthusiastic people working in several fields of robotics and automation, such as embedded systems, reinforcement learning, autonomous navigation, drones, 3D design and simulation, detection of life in soil and rock samples, etc. The research team is directly supervised by Lecturer Akib Zaman, Department of Computer Science & Engineering, United International University. The members of the team are divided into 6 subteams named Electrical, Software, Mechanical, Communication, Science and Management.

Name: Amatör Uydu Teknolojileri Derneği (TAMSAT)
Description:
TAMSAT yurdumuzda amatör, bilimsel ve benzer amaçlı uyduların projelendirme, üretim ve işletme çalışmaları ile ilgili araştırma, geliştirme, uygulama ve eğitim hizmetleri sunmaktadır. Gerçekleştirdiğimiz projeleri amatör ve bilimsel kullanım için telsiz ve radyo amatörlerinin ve ilgili kullanıcıların hizmetine sunmanın yanı sıra, bu alanda bilgi ve beceriye sahip gençlerin sayılarını artırmak için eğitimler ve seminerler düzenliyoruz. TAMSAT, bağımsız bir kurumsal çalışma grubu ve sivil toplum örgütü olarak uzun bir süre önce beliren bir ihtiyaç sonucunda oluşturulmuştur. Üniversitelerde ilgili alanlarda çalışmalarını sürdüren öğretim üyeleri ile yurdumuzun muhtelif yerlerinde ve yurt dışında çalışmalara destek veren 17 telsiz ve radyo amatörünün katılımıyla kurulan derneğimiz, amaçladığı hedeflere ulaşmak için sizlerin katkı ve destekleri ile hız kazanacaktır. TAMSAT Derneği, amatör uydu teknolojilerinde geleceğin mimarı olmaya adaydır. 

ENGLISH:Welcome to TAMSAT Association!

TAMSAT is a non-profit organization dedicated to research, development, implementation, and educational services related to the design, production, and operation of amateur, scientific, and similar-purpose satellites in our country. Our mission extends beyond project execution; we actively contribute to the amateur and scientific community by making our projects available to radio amateurs and relevant users. Furthermore, we are committed to organizing training sessions and seminars to cultivate the knowledge and skills of young individuals in this field. Established in 2010 with the participation of university faculty members engaged in relevant fields and radio amateurs supporting projects in various parts of our country and abroad, TAMSAT Association emerged as an independent corporate working group and a non-governmental organization in response to a long-identified need. With your contributions and support, our association is set to accelerate its progress towards the envisioned goals.

Name: MESEO project
Description:
MESEO aims to design, prototype and demonstrate an open, flexible and scalable multi-mission End-to-End Earth Observation system for massive data processing. It aims to overcome performance bottlenecks, both on board and on ground, while ensuring Space data sovereignty.

MESEO is co-financed by the European Union through the grant agreement N° 101135067 concluded with the European Health and Digital Executive Agency (HADEA), under the powers delegated by the European Commission.

Name: LASER - Liverpool Association for Space Engineering Research
Description:
Electronics and Electrical Engineering department supported, student run group focused on research in space based engineering. Currently focusing on competing in the UKSEDS Rocketry competition and working on a payload to be launched on a CubeSat.

The end aim of our society is to develop, build and manage the construction of a CubeSat (or any variations of such a small scale satellite). This is to be done through graded university project work relating to LASER, that are already essential to the student’s EEE related degree.

The integral objective of this group is to promote and maintain interest in the EEE field of study for graduating college students. We want to create a friendly and social atmosphere where students can not only develop their engineering and management skills, but also gain important industrial skills that students 
don’t tend to learn during their time in University.

Name: Trinity Space Technologies
Description:
Trinity Space Technologies is a UK-based space startup developing software to reduce the time and resources required to find, win and manage institutional contracts.

Founded by an ambitious, multidisciplinary team our mission is to reduce the barriers to development for space organisations throughout the world, for the benefit of humanity.

Name: Espaço^3
Description:
Espaço³ is a Portuguese youth association enabling the development of space projects for university students from diverse backgrounds and institutions. Our mission is to provide hands-on learning experiences, empowering students to expand their knowledge and skills through collaborative work on innovative space technologies. Currently, we are focused on TejoOne, a CubeSat project competing in the CubeSat Portugal initiative, with the goal of advancing space debris detection.

Join us on our journey as we push the boundaries of space exploration and contribute to a safer future in orbit!

Name: Taurus Space
Description:
Taurus Space specializes in developing autonomous systems that provide real-time space situational awareness and multi-domain Command and Control (C2) capabilities. Our mission is to safeguard the space domain and empower operators with decision advantage by leveraging predictive modeling and data fusion. We support satellite operators, defense agencies, and commercial space enterprises with solutions designed to enhance mission safety and drastically reduce decision time.

Our core capability is the Network Management Dashboard, a C2 platform that solves the critical problem of data silos. It fuses real-time space telemetry with multi-domain tactical data, including air, land (Link 16), and sea tracks, into a single Common Operating Picture (COP). Our Intelligent Relevancy Engine automatically declutters the view to show only mission-relevant assets, providing commanders with the immediate, context-aware situational awareness needed for proactive asset management.

Additionally, we provide network monitoring and orchestration from space to ground, ensuring mission planners and commanders proactively ensure the most resilient comms path. 

Taurus Space's key differentiator is our data-driven, AI-first approach to both space safety and tactical C2. Our systems are secure, scalable, and designed for seamless integration, providing a holistic solution to ensure space superiority and mission success.

 #ChaosInCalm

As an innovative leader in the field, we are committed to reshaping the future of space travel with our groundbreaking technology and sustainable practices.

At Spica Launch Co, we recognize the immense potential of space exploration and its impact on scientific advancements, communication, and global connectivity. However, we also understand the challenges faced by traditional space missions, such as exorbitant costs and environmental concerns. Name: Space MegaCorp
Description:
Space MegaCorp es una empresa que está redefiniendo los límites de la propulsión espacial con su enfoque en tecnología no convencional. A diferencia de los métodos tradicionales, Space MegaCorp desarrolla y perfecciona sistemas de propulsión avanzados, como los ion thrusters, propulsores de plasma y otras alternativas innovadoras. Estas tecnologías prometen revolucionar la manera en que exploramos el espacio, al ofrecer una mayor eficiencia y potencia.

Lo más notable es que la visión de Space MegaCorp va más allá del espacio profundo. Su objetivo es hacer que estos avanzados sistemas de propulsión sean lo suficientemente potentes y eficientes para lanzamientos terrestres, lo que permitiría un acceso más asequible y ecológico al espacio. Además, buscan que estas tecnologías sean reutilizables, contribuyendo al desarrollo de un modelo sostenible en la exploración espacial.

Pero su misión no termina ahí. Space MegaCorp también se compromete a compartir este conocimiento con la comunidad global a través de su iniciativa educativa, la Space Academy. Aquí, no solo capacitan a las próximas generaciones de ingenieros y científicos espaciales, sino que también ofrecen cursos y publicaciones para el público en general, haciendo accesible la comprensión de estos avances y fomentando la curiosidad científica en todos los niveles.

En resumen, Space MegaCorp no solo está impulsando la innovación en la propulsión espacial, sino que también está liderando una nueva era de accesibilidad y educación en el ámbito espacial.

Muchas gracias.

Name: CENSSS: Centre for Space Sensors and Systems
Description:
The New-Space concept, with large numbers of dedicated small and low-cost satellites, is expected to provide opportunities for small nations to acquire their own space capabilities and services, tailored to own needs. And there is a renewed emphasize in the exploration of space, with a planned establishing of a manned base on the moon by 2028 and the further exploration of other planets. CENSSS will address science and technology challenges and business opportunities within New-Space satellites for Earth Observation and within Space Exploration “to the Moon and Mars”.   The research areas underpinning these business innovation areas will be sensor and sensor systems, and New-Space satellites system integration, operation and exploitation. CENSSS shall advance the knowledge frontiers in terms of novel sensors for small Earth Observation satellites, as well as novel sensors for planetary resource mapping and exploitation. CENSSS will take an active part in NASA`s Mars 2020 rover mission.   

The core activity of the Centre shall support at least 10 PhD candidates (30 PhD years) and 15 researcher-years, providing innovative research, development and engineering results as well as providing high-quality candidates for Norwegian space companies and the government sector. CENSSS shall be a national research team and a hub for collaboration and synergies between SMEs, academia and prominent research organizations, stimulating competitiveness and innovation capabilities in Norway within the space sector. 

The centre will build upon more than 60 years of upper atmosphere space science and technology activities originating from Kjeller, Norway, including upper atmosphere research from Andøya, the recent development and operation of the AISSat / NorSat satellites and the Norwegian georadar RIMFAX that will be on board the NASA Rover Mars 2020.

Name: Canary Wharf Space Innovation Hub

Name: IRIS - Interventional Radiology In Space
Description:
About Us

IRIS is a pioneering initiative (by the SFR and CNES) bringing together leading experts in interventional radiology and aerospace to develop innovative medical solutions for long-duration space missions. As humanity sets its sights on the Moon and Mars, ensuring astronauts' health is vital. Our team is creating the first-ever toolset for performing interventional radiology in space—specifically designed to handle the challenges of microgravity and the isolation of astronauts from Earth.

Name: UoBSat
Description:
Within our work, which is guided by world class space experts and volcanologists from the University of Bristol, we have a ground station, a clean room and the PROVE CubeSat mission. With a mature Cubesat payload, we are actively looking for launch opportunities locally in the UK and globally around the world. Please get it touch to find out more.

Satellites are used everyday in everything we do. Our maps that guide us all come from space and the satellites that orbit our beautiful blue planet. CubeSats are the next generation of small satellites that even some high school students are getting opportunities to build, gain experience with and to send to space. Space might be a hostile environment, but we are learning more and more how we can use satellites in space to help our planet. One of the areas that our research is focused on, is monitoring and predicting volcanic eruptions around the world. Approximately 1.3 billion people live side by side with volcanoes, and eruptions can be catastrophic on a massive scale. With satellites, we have more information at our fingertips that can be used to not only save lives, but to also understand and model how the volcanic eruptions affect our environment as volcanic ash can even blacken out the sun across whole continents.

Description:

At Stellar Freaks, we're on a mission to inspire curiosity, ignite passion, and foster a deep appreciation for the wonders of the cosmos. Whether you're an amateur astronomer, a seasoned stargazer, or simply a curious explorer, you'll find a home among our diverse and enthusiastic community of space lovers.
Founded on the belief that the universe is our ultimate playground, Stellar Freaks brings together like-minded individuals from around the globe to share knowledge, exchange ideas, and embark on cosmic adventures together.At Stellar Freaks we aim to build a space community for everyone where they are free to exchange their knowledge of the universe with each other.

Description: Somos una entidad sin ánimo de lucro, conformada por una red de entidades gubernamentales, universidades y empresas. Name: Neumann Nexus
Description:
At Neumann Nexus, we believe the future of artificial intelligence lies in its ability to seamlessly integrate with human potential. Founded on the principles of psychophysics and cognitive engineering, our mission is to design and implement AI solutions that amplify human innovation, decision-making, and performance.

Drawing on over a decade of experience in aerospace, human factors, psychophysics, AI and advanced system designs, Neumann Nexus bridges the gap between cutting-edge technology and human needs. Our work spans diverse industries, including human space exploration, aerospace, and human performance, where we deliver tailored AI systems that are intuitive, ethical, and aligned with user potential.

What sets us apart is our commitment to collaboration and innovation. At Neumann Nexus, we prioritize interdisciplinary partnerships, bringing together experts from AI, neuroscience, engineering, and beyond to solve complex challenges and redefine what's possible. From building adaptive AI systems for extreme environments to developing user-centered frameworks for business intelligence, our solutions are grounded in science and driven by purpose.

We’re more than a consulting firm—we’re a movement to ensure AI technology works in harmony with humanity. Whether you’re navigating uncharted territory or optimizing existing processes, Neumann Nexus is here to guide you toward an AI-powered future that’s as intelligent as it is human.

Name: SQAT project
Description:
SQAT is a 3.5-year project, funded under the Horizon Europe Programme, that focuses on Implementing Smart Farming Applications using EO Data, Soil Sensors & Robotics.

SQAT's smart soil mapping service is a multi-level, multi-technology and multi-purpose solution. With a keen eye on markets and users, SQAT’s approach is designed to overcome today’s practical, technical and financial challenges to generate high resolution soil properties maps and demand-driven products like application maps for soil improvement or crop management, or compliancy proof for eco schemes. Our vision is that the integration of different technologies provides a flexible and agile service beating the unbundled alternatives that are now in the market: our in-situ sampling and/or sensoring is wrapped in a Copernicus-based artificial intelligence soil mapping product.

Name: FLuorescence EXplorer (FLEX) project office | Projektbüro
Description:
is a satellite that will provide new insights into the photosynthesis of vegetation from 2025 onwards. The German FLEX project office is an interface between the European Space Agency (ESA) and the German users of FLEX data. Our goal is to prepare the German user community for FLEX. Our client is the Federal Ministry of Economics and Climate Protection (funding ID 50EE2106).
__________________________________________

FLEX ist ein Satellit der ab 2025 neue Erkenntnisse zur Photosynthese von Vegetation liefern wird. Das deutsche FLEX-Projektbüro ist eine Schnittstelle zwischen der European Space Agency (ESA) und den deutschen Nutzern der FLEX-Daten. Unser Ziel ist es die deutsche Nutzergemeinschaft optimal auf FLEX vorzubereiten. Unser Auftraggeber ist das Bundesministerium für Wirtschaft und Klimaschutz (Förderkennzeichen 50EE2106).
