Description: Developing and designing a rover, capable of performing crash-site forensics: Find and reach the location(s) of a crashed lander, record the GPS coordinates locations of the lander, equipment servicing, surface sampling, search and rescue, traversal and transport.

Name: PPRD Mediterranean
Description:
The PPRD Med project (Prevention, Preparedness, Response to natural & man-made Disasters in Southern and Eastern Mediterranean countries) has the following objectives:

1. Anchoring the UfM as a regional cooperation platform 
The project aims to strengthen the Union for the Mediterranean (UfM) as a platform for regional collaboration in addressing disasters, fostering cooperation among EU Southern countries.

2. Strengthening operational assistance & response capacity
The project focuses on enhancing the operational capabilities and response capacity of the participating countries in dealing with natural and man-made disasters effectively.

3. Fostering interregional cooperation initiative
The project aims to encourage cooperation and collaboration between EU Southern countries, facilitating the exchange of knowledge, expertise, and best practices in disaster prevention, preparedness, and response.

4. Using advanced technologies for prevention, preparedness, and response
The project aims to leverage advanced technologies to improve disaster prevention, preparedness, and response efforts in the EU Southern countries.

5. Strengthening the interlinkages with all relevant actors
The project seeks to enhance collaboration and coordination with all relevant stakeholders, including governmental organizations, NGOs, international institutions, and local communities involved in disaster management.

By pursuing these objectives, the PPRD Med project aims to enhance the region's overall resilience and capacity to effectively prevent, prepare for, and respond to natural and man-made disasters in the EU Southern countries.

Name: Danish Student Cubesat Program
Description:
In the DISCO project, we work with all the elements involved in satellite transmission. This includes planning, design, development, testing, and assembling all the components of the satellite. The satellites are CubeSats, which enable students to gain insight into the space industry on a larger scale while developing valuable competencies in the field. The satellites are sent into space, where they will assist climate organizations such as the Arctic Research Centre and IClimate. The satellites will collect data to help scientists study various aspects of climate change in Greenland. Follow us on a fun and educational journey that could help grow the space industry in Denmark and increase interest in the field. 

For contact or collaboration, please contact: Cecilie@phys.au.dk.

Name: VOTE TRA
Description:
Digital Voting Hub for Sustainable Urban Transport System

The Challenge
Citizens are the focal point of improving urban transport systems. They are important because they can depict the actual demands and expectations of the passengers. When we involve the citizens in the decision-making process, we guarantee adopting more efficient outcomes.

The Solution
We propose creating a digital voting hub to make the participation process for evaluating and developing the transport system in urban areas more efficient, flexible and demand-based. Allowing citizens, the option to vote through the digital voting hub increases voter turnout and considers the actual expectation of passengers. 

#NationalChallengeFund
#NextGenerationEU

Name: PlayableDataLab
Description:
PLAYABLE DATA LAB
Transformamos la relación entre ciudadanía, territorio y datos a través de mapas interactivos, geodatos y experiencias gamificadas.

Somos una línea de investigación y acción que mezcla tecnología, visualización y juego para hacer accesible y significativa la información territorial. Creamos formas sencillas y eficaces de gamificar datos urbanos para facilitar la participación ciudadana y repensar colectivamente cómo funciona nuestro entorno.

 Foco en:
• Datos abiertos geolocalizados
• Mapas y visualización interactiva
• Experiencias lúdicas para el análisis territorial
• Accesibilidad, sencillez y sentido cívico

 Nuestro objetivo:
Involucrar a las personas en la gestión y comprensión de su barrio, ciudad o territorio desde nuevas perspectivas más cercanas y comprensibles.

Name: New York Consortium for Space Technology
Description:
The New York Consortium for Space Technology Innovation and Development (NYCST), led by Cornell University, is an agile, mission-driven collaboration that establishes a centrally located nexus for space-related education, infrastructure, expertise and capabilities aimed at catalyzing transformative growth in education, space technology, advanced manufacturing, and supply chain capabilities.  

Designated as a Defense Manufacturing Community under the Defense Manufacturing Community Support Program (DMCSP), NYCST bolsters innovation in space technology, supports small businesses, and addresses critical skills gaps in the sector. Development programs span four core domains — education & workforce development, facilities access, R&D and small business assistance.

Distinctives of NYCST are its collocation with the Air Force Research Laboratory Regional Research Hub, the Cornell Center for Materials Research (CCMR) and Cornell University’s research capabilities that include one-of-a-kind facilities for materials science, nanofabrication, spacecraft development, testing and on-orbit operations, and the Cornell High Energy Synchrotron Source (CHESS), a strategic national security asset and 1 of 5 facilities of its type in the world.

Name: Keppler
Description: Keppler Enterprises es una startup creada en el año 2024 para un unico proposito, acelerar y despejar las rutas orbitales en el espacio medisnte tecnologia disruptiva como Inteligencia artificial, realidad virtual, y blockchain.

Name: Farah Law School
Description: Farah Law School (FLS) was established on July 2, 2023, by Farah Diya Yasmine. We aim to strengthen international law communities, especially in space law, cyber law, and constitutional law (comparative), through lectures, development events, posts, and global participation.

Name: Space Research Centre (SRC)
Description:
The Space Research Centre at NAST is Nepal’s leading institution for space science, satellite technology, and aerospace research. We focus on advancing innovation through satellite development, hybrid propulsion testing, and ground station operations. Our mission is to build national capacity, foster global collaboration, and inspire the next generation of space scientists.

From the Himalayas to the stars — Nepal’s journey into space begins here.

Name: Space4ReLaunch
Description:
Space4Relaunch is a collaborative program revolving around two of the sectors combining high accessibility of the Walloon industrial landscape, and high attractiveness to world space markets. 

The research promoted by this program will be carried out through a total of 30 PhD theses answering cutting-edge technological challenges towards adequate Technology Level Readiness for industrial needs in the spatial sector.

Name: ACES Worldwide
Description:
Our Mission:
ACES Worldwide endeavors to strengthen and promote international space cooperation and establish new alliances between diverse educational, technological, civil, and entrepreneurial organizations. ACES Worldwide’s mission is to help advance and support equitable space policies, laws, and regulations on a global basis by which all nations and private and public entities can engage in optimizing humanity’s potential for space exploration and utilization--both on and beyond the Earth.

ACES Worldwide aspires to help harmonize commercial space endeavors, national space agency goals, and the interests of both non-profit and humanitarian organizations seeking to engage with space exploration, utilization, and development. This alliance primarily addresses issues related to space safety, improved sustainability, space traffic management, cosmic hazards, and planetary defense, space education and training, and capacity-building in space systems.

Name: gamma.earth
Description: Uniquely powerful AI solutions for Earth Observation and remote sensing. 10x super-resolution for multispectral and hyperspectral satellite imaging data. Cloud detection and removal, atmospheric correction, as well as land use and land cover classification.

Name: ISPRS 2026 Toronto
Description:
ISPRS 2026 Congress
Toronto, Canada | July 5–12, 2026

The ISPRS 2026 Congress brings together experts, researchers, and professionals from around the world to advance the science and technology of photogrammetry, remote sensing, and spatial information. Organized by the Canadian Remote Sensing Society (CRSS-SCT), this Congress is the premier global event in the geospatial field.

Highlights include:
- Cutting-edge presentations and research 
- Networking opportunities with international colleagues
- Focus on innovation in geospatial sciences
- Special sessions, forums, and panel discussions

Save the date and join us in Toronto for a week of knowledge-sharing, innovation, and collaboration!

#ISPRS2026 #GeospatialScience #RemoteSensing #Photogrammetry #SpatialInformation #Toronto2026

Name: Weeroc
Description:
Weeroc is a spin-off company from Omega laboratory (IN2P3/CNRS, french governmental agency for fundamental research in astrophysics, particle physics and nuclear physics). Weeroc designs and provides analogue and mixed ASICs for industry. Our main customers are major actors in the following fields:

Medical imaging
Homeland security
Nuclear protection
Scientific instrumentation
Space (launchers and satellites)
Weeroc helps its customers with the following products and services:

We provide on-the-shelf programmable analogue and mixed front-end ASICs for photodetector and particle detector read-out.
We design dedicated ASICs for a customer and provide designed ASICs on request with full exclusivity
We can act as a design house and design specific product to a customer with IP transfer
We can act as auditor and provide expertise on external projects

Name: Kvarken Space Center
Description: KvarkenSpaceCenter - Kvarken Region (Finnish-Swedish) Innovation Ecosystem for New-Space. The ecosystem offers support and networking opportunities for industry, public sector and research actors across the Kvarken region. Established by KvarkenSpaceEco project.

Name: Center for Space Governance
Description: The Center for Space Governance is a research institute dedicated to exploring the future of the space economy, currently based in Brussels and San Francisco. Our mission is to address the greatest challenges and opportunities for humanity’s future in space.

Name: GIS/Transport Ltd.
Description:
GIS/Transport, Ltd. was established in 1994 in the Middle East and Africa Region to develop innovative tools and implements integrated information solutions to address spatial information challenges. We provide consulting services to assist clients in project development during design, planning, analysis, implementation, and evaluation phases.
GIS/Transport solutions are designed to meet multi-sectorial needs including Geographic Information Systems, Land Administration, Satellite Data, Remote Sensing and Digital Image Processing, Mapping and Cartography, Environmental and Transportation Planning.

GIS/Transport uses a team of professionals and experts in the engineering, social, and economic fields to design sustainable technical solutions. We have a track record of successful development of sophisticated spatial information databases, applications, and procedures for many agencies worldwide.
We set up teams across many disciplines to serve our clients. We work collaboratively with clients to deliver high-quality applications and state-of-the-art solutions. Our teams have solid skills and experience in translating planning, analytical, data management, and mapping objectives into cost- effective and attractive solutions.
We offer custom software systems and solutions to meet specialized and specific GIS needs. We design information systems for land administration, transportation and several other sectors. We have successfully integrated complex systems and have gained wide experience in linking GIS with specialized planning and data management tools.

Name: STORIES of Space
Description:
Do you have a story? A story about space that you’ve been thinking about for some time?  
Your story has the potential to change the way we explore space, but opportunities to share your story have been limited, challenging, and undiscovered. Until today. 
Today, a simple story will fly to space, changing how we explore it. 
Your story. Right here, right now. www.storiesofspace.com

Name: spacER
Description:
We are a young, interdisciplinary team focused on developing innovative devices and technologies in the field of space medicine. Our mission is to create solutions that support human health and safety in space, both during orbital missions and future interplanetary expeditions.

By combining engineering, medical, and scientific expertise, we develop technologies with dual-use applications for extreme environments beyond Earth and for advanced healthcare on our planet. Our goal is to push the boundaries of modern medicine and enable the long-term human presence in space.

Name: Tehiru Space
Description:
We are building next-generation rocket infrastructure
that supports the uber growth of smallsat utilization worldwide.
Our launch vehicles harness the latest state of the art
manufacturing along with electrical landing, allowing for reduced turnaround time and cost.

Name: EXCELSIOR H2020 Teaming
Description:
A new, autonomous, and self-sustained Centre of Excellence, namely ERATOSTHENES Centre of Excellence of the Cyprus University of Technology (CUT) has been established through the ‘EXCELSIOR’, H2020 Widespread Teaming Phase 2 project. The newly established centre has been created as a result of upgrading the existing Remote Sensing and Geo-Environment Lab, which has been operating within the Department of Civil Engineering and Geomatics of the Cyprus University of Technology since 2007.

The vision of the ERATOSTHENES Centre of Excellence is to become a world-class Digital Innovation Hub for Earth Observation, space technology and Geospatial Information and to be the reference Centre in the Eastern Mediterranean, Middle East, and North Africa.

The Digital Innovation Hub will create an ecosystem which combines state-of-the-art remote sensing, data management and processing technologies, cutting – edge research opportunities, targeted education services and promotion of entrepreneurship.  In order to be dynamic and innovative, the Digital Innovation Hub will be based on two major infrastructures, which are a Satellite Ground Receiving Station and a Ground-based atmospheric remote sensing station. ERATOSTHENES Centre of excellence is paving the way for Cyprus to enter the space arena.

Name: STRATOSYST
Description:
STRATOSYST is a Czech startup formed in 2017 with the aim of revolutionising the use of satellite services in the stratosphere. Our mission is to make satellite technology more affordable and environmentally friendly, while ensuring that everyone has reliable communication and security. We produce solar-powered High Altitude Pseudo-Satellites (HAPS) that provide a range of services including Earth observation, telecommunications, defence and other related operations - our services are tailored to the needs of our customers.  
 
For its creative stratospheric solutions, STRATOSYST was awarded with the Galileo Masters award in 2018. Today, we work on projects with a wide range of partners, including European agencies, governments and armed forces. Additionally, we work collaboratively with several companies to push further the boundaries of technology. 
 
Our team of experts is dedicated to providing the best possible service and support to our clients, pushing the limits of what is possible with HAPS technology. Contact us today to learn more about how we can help your organization achieve its goals with our innovative HAPS solutions.

Name: Panopterra
Description:
Panopterra specializes in advanced Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning for Earth Observation and geospatial applications. We combine expertise in computer vision, evolutionary learning, and large-scale data processing with deep domain knowledge. By integrating scientific rigor with technical innovation, we transform complex datasets into actionable insights that power monitoring, prediction, and analysis – enabling data-driven Earth Intelligence.

Founded in 2020, Panopterra collaborates with partners and clients worldwide on research, development, and operational projects. We are dedicated to delivering scientifically grounded, innovative, and dependable solutions that create tangible, lasting value. Through close collaboration and a strong scientific foundation, we support clients in harnessing advanced technologies effectively, enabling informed decisions and addressing complex real-world challenges across sectors.

Name: National Space Society of Australia
Description: The National Space Society of Australia is an nonprofit educational membership organization dedicated to the creation of a spacefaring civilization.

Name: Hybrogines
Description: Hybrogines is a French innovative startup dedicated to making space accessible to all by providing more affordable, safe, reliable, and sustainable solutions — including suborbital flights, small satellite launches, and orbital services.

Name: SpaceTaxi
Description: We are an Indian Aerospace Startup aiming to provide affordable space tourism and rocket launch services.

Name: EROSS SC
Description: The EROSS SC (European Robotic Orbital Support Services – Servicing Component) objective is to demonstrate European solutions for Servicers and Serviced LEO/GEO satellites, enabling a wide range of efficient and safe orbital support services

Name: The Filmed in Space Company
Description: We are an industry leader in sustainable near-space balloon flights, having completed over countless successful flights. Our services include sending a variety of objects, including products, family photos, models, and personal messages, into space for our clients. We capture high-quality 4k footage of your item in space, making it an excellent choice for a unique gift, thoughtful gesture, or powerful branding tool.

Name: Pramatra Space
Description: Taking a Quantum leap towards security - Pramatra Space is building the next generation of encryption systems to make digital ecosystems and enterprises "Quantum Safe".

Name: Arribes Enlightenment
Description: We help Space Tech Companies to Develop Products and Strategy and we Help Incumbents of all industries to create a Space Strategy

Name: Congreso Espacial Centroamericano
Description:
A ser celebrado en la Universidad del Valle de Guatemala 2024, del 10 al 12 de septiembre.

El CEC será un foro para que personas centroamericanas (dentro y fuera de Centroamérica) puedan informar a la comunidad regional y global sobre los proyectos en los que trabajan, y tiene el objetivo de servir como plataforma para encontrar oportunidades de colaboración entre instituciones y personas en Centroamérica y a nivel global. 

Adicionalmente, el CEC intenta servir como punto de reunión para que space start ups de la región puedan discutir potenciales sinergías.

Name: The Future Space Leaders Foundation
Description:
The Future Space Leaders Foundation (“FSLF”) is a tax exempt 501 c 3 non-profit organization dedicated to the career development of young space and satellite industry professionals. The Foundation organizes events and raises funds for grants to deserving graduate students and young professionals. FSLF works in cooperation with other non-profits, companies and government agencies on space-related educational events. FSLF is also actively promoting the professional development of young Americans targeting careers in the academic fields of Science, Technology, Engineering, Art and Math (“STEAM”).

Through its annual Future Space event, FSLF advances learning and fosters interaction among current space and satellite industry leaders with graduate students and young professionals. The Foundation made eight grant awards in 2013-2014 to outstanding American graduate students and young professionals enabling them to attend and present papers at major space-related symposia and conferences including the IAF International Astronautical Congress, SGAC Space Congress, AAS Goddard Memorial Symposium, NSC Goddard Dinner, Satellite 2014, SSPI Gala, AIAA Spotlight Awards and ISPCS.

Name: Ghana Space Science and Technology Institute
Description: The Ghana Space Science and Technology Institute (GSSTI), operating under the Ghana Atomic Energy Commission (GAEC) within the Ministry of Environment, Science, Technology and Innovation (MESTI), is tasked with harnessing, coordinating, researching, commercializing, and exploring space science and technology for Ghana's socio-economic development. With a diverse workforce and expertise, GSSTI collaborates with local and international partners, including academia, research institutions, and industry, to fulfill its mission.

Name: Lunar Policy Platform
Description:
The Lunar Policy Platform (LPP) is the first and only expert advisory dedicated to lunar policy. We facilitate global and multi-stakeholder dialogues on priorities, policies and standards, and provide expert advice to current and emerging space stakeholders across the globe on the legal and policy aspects of lunar activities. Through our work, we enable the creation of a conducive policy environment enabling the peaceful, safe, and sustainable development of the Moon and its resources. 

For more information on our activities, visit www.lunarpolicyplatform.org or send us an email at lpp@lunarpolicyplatform.org 

Our Portfolio includes:
Open Resources: Reports, Snapshots, Commentaries, Op-Eds, and a Lunar Policy Decalogue

Tailored products: Briefs, Workshops, Tabletop exercises and Crisis Simulations, Reputational Due Diligence, Tailored Research and Analysis

Name: Snowdonia Space Centre - Canolfan Gofod Eryri
ESA Space Mechanisms
Description:
This is a page for space mechanisms enthusiasts, researchers, professionals, and suppliers. It is moderated by ESA's Space Mechanisms team and is meant for keeping our community connected and informed about opportunities in the field of space mechanisms and tribology.

 About ESA's Space Mechanisms team:
We are a dedicated team working in the Directorate of Technology, Engineering and Quality in the European Space Agency (ESA) to ensure the successful development, verification, and deployment of space mechanisms. Our mission is to foster innovation, collaboration, and knowledge sharing within the field.

Follow us if you are interested in:
 Project insights and lessons learned: Stay updated on the latest developments, successes, and challenges within our ongoing projects in ESA. 
 Events & trainings: Get updates about the European Space Mechanisms and Tribology Symposium and other events, workshops, and training sessions aimed at expanding your knowledge of space mechanisms and technology.
 Supplier opportunities: Learn about new opportunities for space mechanisms suppliers and how to get involved in the ever-evolving space industry and ESA projects.
 Community engagement: Connect with fellow members and participate in discussions on all things ‘space mechanisms’.

Name: PolyOrbite
Description:
PolyOrbite est une société étudiante affiliée à Polytechnique Montréal qui se spécialise dans  le  domaine  des  technologies  spatiales. Notre impact auprès des étudiants et des aspirants professionnels se fait au travers nos projets CubeSat et Rover ainsi que nos activités éducatives. PolyOrbite est une société de renom. Notre équipe CubeSat tient toujours le record de hauteur canadien pour un lancement de ballon sttratosphérique. Notre équipe Rover démontre sa persévérance au Canadian Rover Challenge, se rapprochant de plus en plus du podium. Cette année, notre équipe a remporté la 4ième place, mais s'est classée 1ière parmi les équipes canadiennes.

------------

PolyOrbite is a student organization affiliated with Polytechnique Montreal that specializes in the field of space technology. Our impact on students and aspiring professionals is made through our CubeSat and Rover projects as well as through our educational activities. PolyOrbite is a renowned society. Our CubeSat team still holds the candian record for stratospheric balloon altitude. Our Rover team demonstrates its perseverance at the Canadian Rover Challenge, inching closer and closer to the podium. This year, our team landed in 4th place, but ranked first amongst the canadian . Cette année, notre équipe a remporté la 4ième place, mais s'est classée 1ière parmi les équipes canadiennes.

Name: GEO4US
Description:
GEO4US is a non-profit space program and grassroots initiative dedicated to bridging the gap between communities and the vast potential of space and geospatial technology.

We focus on providing free hands-on capacity building and project-based support to underserved communities.

Check out our page: https://geo4us.com/

Name: Star Forge
Description: Star Forge is a deep-tech company delivering products and services in positioning and navigation for both space and terrestrial applications. Our expertise include space optical navigation and attitude estimation, supporting clients developing spacecraft systems that rely on vision-based sensing. We work closely with mission teams to design, simulate, and validate navigation and estimation architectures using multi-sensor optical systems. Star Forge covers algorithm development for relative navigation, feature tracking, and pose estimation. We assist at every phase, from early concept design and technology trades to hardware-in-the-loop testing and flight qualification.

Name: RESSPRENEURS TECHNOLOGIES
Description:
Welcome to Resspreneurs Technologies, a dynamic and innovative company dedicated to Research and Experimental Development in the exciting domains of Satellite and Aerospace Engineering. As a newly established firm, we are driven by a passion for advancing technology and exploring the frontiers of aerospace.

At Resspreneurs Technologies, our primary focus is on Research and Development in the field of Satellite and Aerospace Engineering, along with allied services that cater to our clients' specific needs. We offer specialized engineering services, including customized solutions for designing and developing CubeSats and other small satellites, providing cutting-edge solutions for the space industry.

Our vision is to drive innovation in the Aerospace industry by developing state-of-the-art technologies and processes that redefine the possibilities of space exploration. We believe in nurturing the next generation of aerospace engineers and enthusiasts, and to this end, we conduct training classes and provide hands-on training to students, preparing them to analyze, design, and implement aerospace systems.

Collaboration is at the heart of our philosophy, and we actively engage with educational institutions to promote STEM education, fostering a culture of innovation and curiosity in the field of Aerospace Engineering and its allied disciplines.

As a newly started company, we may have a young team, but our dedication, passion, and drive to make a difference in the Aerospace industry are unwavering. Join us on this journey of exploration and technological advancements, and let's shape the future of aerospace together.

#AerospaceEngineering #SatelliteTechnology #Innovation #STEMEducation #SpaceExploration #CubeSats #ResearchAndDevelopment #YoungAndDynamic #FutureOfAerospace

Name: IRPI
Description: IRPI is and engineering firm specializing in fluid-thermal engineering across a variety of industries including aerospace, biomedical, and energy.

Name: Imagine Engine
Description:
Imagine Engine Studios is a multinational private Space agency that is a world leader in space research and cutting-edge technology innovation. Our mission is to revolutionize humanity's future presence in space by developing groundbreaking solutions in quantum technology, advanced spaceports, next-generation telecommunications, aerospace engineering, and robotics.

With a commitment to excellence and a vision to become the largest and most influential technology provider in the space industry, we aim to redefine what’s possible. Imagine Engine Studios is dedicated to empowering global exploration, enhancing connectivity, and unlocking new frontiers in science and technology for a better tomorrow.

Name: IEEE GRSS Sri Lanka Chapter
Description: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society Sri Lanka (GRSS-SL) chapter unites geoscience and remote sensing enthusiasts for collaboration, education, and networking in Sri Lanka.

Name: ZIULLÍN GEOMATICS
Description:
Soluciones innovadoras basadas en imágenes satelitales e inteligencia artificial, desarrolladas por profesionales especializados en geomática y percepción remota. Ofrecemos una amplia gama de servicios, entre los cuales destacan:

+ Análisis espacial.
+ Cartografía digital.
+ Dashboards y mapas web.
+ Estadística espacial y geoestadística aplicada.
+ Gestión de SIG y BBDD espaciales.
+ Modelos digitales de elevación (DEM) con InSAR.
+ Modelos de geoprocesamiento.
+ Modelos predictivos con inteligencia artificial y machine learning.
+ Procesamiento y análisis de imágenes espectrales de sensores electroópticos, térmicos y SAR.
+ Series de tiempo y análisis multitemporal de imágenes satelitales. 

Combinamos tecnología avanzada de sensores remotos espaciales, para ofrecer soluciones precisas y eficientes.

Name: Space Technology Students' Society
Description:
A student organization to promote space related actvities in College Campuses around and in IIT Kharagpur.

Space Technology Students' Society , known as spAts, is a student group for space research, astronomy and astrophysics under the Kalpana Chawla Space Technology Cell, IIT Kharagpur. Our activities encompass conducting lectures, events, competitions and workshops on space related technologies, astronomy, astrophysics and various related fields at the national level thereby channelizing ideas, innovation and energy of students in this direction.

Name: METU Vertical Landing Rocket Technologies Society
Description:
METU Vertical Landing Rocket Technologies Club is a student team that started its activities in January 2022 by engineering students who are all seniors at METU. Initially working as the METU-X Rocket Team, the team later became a club that includes different projects related to vertical landing rocket technologies.

Our first goal is to develop innovative approaches by conducting research on pneumatic systems, mechanical design, structural analysis, guidance control and autopilot system design, flight simulation, and avionics design in the field of vertical landing rockets and to raise awareness about these technologies. Our second goal is to provide a professional engineering simulation for our engineering candidates in technical and social terms. We are a club where our teammates can determine their areas of interest before graduating, take an active role in studies conducted in different fields, and gain experience as if they were working in a professional company in various different fields, including project management and organization. Our third and perhaps biggest goal is to bring successful and technically capable engineers to our country.

Name: StdAux
Description:
StdAux, Space Exploration Company

We research, design and develop technologies to be used in space exploration.

Name: EirSpace
Description:
As a student-run non-profit organization based in Ireland, our mission is to design, build, and launch student rockets for international competitions such as EUROC 2024 and the Collegiate Propulsive Lander Challenge. With a focus on innovation, collaboration, and excellence, we strive to push the boundaries of space exploration and inspire the next generation of rocket scientists in Ireland.

Founded by a group of passionate and driven students, our organization brings together individuals from diverse backgrounds and skillsets, including engineering, physics, computer science, and business. Through hands-on experience and mentorship from industry professionals, we aim to provide our members with the tools and knowledge they need to become leaders in the aerospace industry.

At the heart of our organization is a commitment to sustainability and responsible engineering practices. From the materials we use to the processes we follow, we prioritize environmental stewardship and ethical decision-making in all aspects of our work.

Join us on our journey to make history in the world of rocketry and contribute to a more sustainable future for space exploration.

Contact us at management@eirspace.org

Name: Indian Society of Remote Sensing
Description:
Indian Society of Remote Sensing (ISRS) was established in 1969 with the main objective of advancement and dissemination of remote sensing technology in the fields of mapping, planning and management of natural resources and environment by organising seminars/symposia and by publishing a monthly journal (JISRS), bulletins, proceedings, etc. Starting with 56 members, the Society has now grown into a premier professional body with membership of over 6094 members. Currently, ISRS is a member of the International Society of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ISPRS) and Asian Association on Remote Sensing (AARS).

The Journal of the Indian Society of Remote Sensing(JISRS) publishes original research contributions in all the related fields of remote sensing and its applications. Official publication of the prestigious Indian Society of Remote Sensing, founded in 1969. 100% of authors who answered a survey reported that they would definitely publish or probably publish in the journal again.

Name: Imagine Engine Studios
Description:
Imagine Engine Studios is a multinational private Space agency that is a world leader in space research and cutting-edge technology innovation. Our mission is to revolutionize humanity's future presence in space by developing groundbreaking solutions in quantum technology, advanced spaceports, next-generation telecommunications, aerospace engineering, and robotics.

With a commitment to excellence and a vision to become the largest and most influential technology provider in the space industry, we aim to redefine what’s possible. Imagine Engine Studios is dedicated to empowering global exploration, enhancing connectivity, and unlocking new frontiers in science and technology for a better tomorrow.

Name: Abu Dhabi Space Debate
Description: Abu Dhabi Space Debate, hosted by UAE Space Agency, is a unique two day event platform for global leaders to discuss the future of space industry.

Name: مجتمع الفضاء السعودي Saudi Space Community
Description:
مجتمع الشباب السعودي للفضاء هي اول منصة وطنية تجمع شبابًا وشابات شغوفين بعلوم الفضاء وتقنياته. نهدف إلى تمكين المهتمين بالبحث العلمي والابتكار وريادة الأعمال عبر خلق مساحة للتواصل، وتبادل المعرفة، وتطوير مبادرات تُسهم في بناء قطاع فضائي سعودي منافس عالميًا 

رسالتنا:
نلتزم في مجتمع الفضاء السعودي بتقديم ما يلي:

* نشر المعرفة العلمية في مجالات الفلك وعلوم الفضاء
* توفير جلسات وورش عمل تعليمية متخصصة بشكل مستمر
* دعم المواهب الشابة وتمكينها من إبراز قدراتها
* تعزيز التواصل بين المجتمع والجهات الأكاديمية والمهنية
* خلق منصة موحّدة تجمع الهواة، الطلاب، المهنيين، والباحثين في قطاع الفضاء تحت مظلة واحدة

رؤيتنا : 
أن نكون منصة ملهمة وموثوقة تسهم في خلق مجتمع معرفي فعّال ومبادر، وتربط شباب المملكة بالفرص التعليمية والمهنية في قطاع الفضاء، وتدعم دورهم في بناء مستقبل هذا المجال داخل المملكة وخارجها.

Name: RED | Rocket Experiment Division
Description:
RED (Rocket Experiment Division) is a project within AeroTéc (the aerospace engineering students association of Instituto Superior Técnico), in Lisbon, Portugal, whose main objective is to design, build and launch rockets. We participate yearly at EuRoC (European Rocketry Challenge), the largest European university rocketry competition. In the last 2 years, we've achieved the title of vice champions!

Follow our page and share our passion for rockets!

 Our History: 
RED was founded in 2017 and we have had striking technical evolution in the last 6 years.

- Our very first rocket was called Pilot. 
- We then built 3 editions of Aurora. 
- In 2021, these were followed by Blimunda: the 1st ever Portuguese rocket to be in an international competition, marking our debut in EuRoC. 
- In 2022, the Baltasar rocket was endowed with the Flight Award, following a brilliant 3006m apogee.
- This year, Camões was rewarded with the Payload Award!

Stay tuned for the years to come! Ad Astra

Name: Uzinova Space Systems
Description:
Uzinova is a space technology startup focused on research and development, providing advanced engineering solutions for sounding rockets, vertical takeoff and landing systems, avionics and ground station technologies.

The startup specializes in the design, manufacturing and testing of solid and hybrid propulsion systems, flight control electronics and telemetry architectures.

With a strong commitment to STEM education, Uzinova develops educational materials and tools that introduce rocketry and space science to younger generations, provide an early understanding of engineering principles and inspire future innovators.

 Learn more: www.uzinovas.com
 Corporate inquiries: info@uzinova.com

Name: ESA Business Accelerator Germany
Description:
The ESA Business Accelerator Germany is a programme for space scale-ups in their growth stage backed by a powerful alliance of UnternehmerTUM, TUM Venture Labs, acitoflux, ESA and DLR. 

During the six-month acceleration phase, we provide the teams with tailored support that precisely meets their individual needs—from strategic coaching and investor readiness to capital access and corporate networks. An additional six month of extended support helps to ensure a successful investment journey and deeply connected alumni community. 

With locations in Munich, Stuttgart and Hamburg we are covering teams from all over Germany that have reached TRL 7+ and aim to raise at least their Series A round.

The first batch in 2025 features six pioneering companies advancing core technologies for the space economy—from next-gen propulsion and composite materials to AI-driven Earth observation and autonomous space traffic management. Together, they’re redefining how we operate in orbit—and how space innovation drives impact on Earth.

Name: Atlantis Industries, Inc.
Description: Atlantis (AI) provides space radiation expertise for humans and electronics in the harsh environment of space. AI was founded to deliver industry leading full body medical and vehicle analysis to identify risks and provide threat assessments + test and design capabilities for optimized monitoring, protection & on-orbit countermeasures.

Name: L10 Innovations
Description: At L10 Innovations, we specialize in strategic advisory, mission optimization, and technical expertise across the space, drone, and AI domains. Our mission is to help organizations accelerate innovation and achieve success in the most demanding operational environments.

Name: Space Cluster Lithuania
Description:
[LT]

Lietuvos kosmoso technologijų klasteris (LKTK) jungia Lietuvos įmones veikiančias ir vystančias kosmoso technologijų sritį. LKTK įkurtas 2021 m. Visorių informacinių technologijų parke susibūrus 4 įmonėms – NanoAvionics, Geomatrix, Blackswan Space ir Elsis PRO, a Novian Group. 2023 m. prie klasterio prisijungė Sensmetry. 2024 m. klasterio nariu tapo Astrolight.

Bendruomenės kompetencijos apima mažųjų palydovų projektavimą ir gamybą, misijų simuliaciją ir projektavimą, palydovinių duomenų apdirbimą ir analizę bei įvairius programinius sprendimus.

[EN]

The Lithuanian Space Technology Cluster (LSTC) brings together Lithuanian companies operating in and developing the field of space technology. The LSTC was established in 2021 at the Visoriai Information Technology Park by 4 companies – NanoAvionics, Geomatrix, Blackswan Space and Elsis Pro, a Novian Group. In 2023, Sensmetry became a part of the cluster. In 2024, Astrolight became a member of the cluster.

The community's competences include the design and manufacture of small satellites, mission simulation and design, satellite data processing and analysis, and various software solutions.

Name: Deneb Space
Description: Deneb Space specialises in autonomous satellite technologies that enhance the manoeuvrability, reactivity and collision avoidance capability.

Name: RHP group
Description:
RHP group is a material and solution provider of powder metallurgy with 15 years of experience in the design, development and commercialisation of customised products within advanced powder processing techniques.
In our attophotonics facility we focus on sensors, engineered surfaces and nano materials.

Our in-house design, development, test and powder technological processing techniques has allowed us to process and work into more than 500 different material target compositions. The variety cover pure metals to ceramics including precious metals, oxides, nitrides, carbides, borides, sulphides, … 

RHP offers customized sputtering targets used in thin film coating, metal diamond composites which are used in high performance electronics and laser applications, self lubricating composites for space applications, high specific stiffness and light weight materials. 

Recent development is focusing on additive manufacturing using a blown powder process. Large scale manufacturing of near net shape parts made from multi-materials or composite materials.

Main Departments: Machining, Hot Pressing, Powder Injection Moulding, Additive Manufacturing, nano materials, surface engineering, sensors.

Name: EuroSpaceport
Description: We are developing Europes sustainable offshore spaceport.

Name: AR Tech.
Description:
Core Technology:
Intelligent Perception System: Utilizing advanced sensors and artificial intelligence technology to achieve accurate environment perception and data processing.
Autonomous navigation and control: Combining deep learning and computer vision technologies to realize autonomous robot navigation, path planning and environment adaptation.
Human-robot collaboration: develop safe and efficient human-robot collaboration systems to improve the robot's ability to operate in complex environments.
Cloud data analysis: using big data analysis and cloud computing to optimize robot performance and provide intelligent decision support.
Main Products:
Industrial robots: for automated production lines, welding, assembly and handling operations.
Medical robots: including surgical robots, rehabilitation robots and nursing robots to improve the quality of medical services.
Service robots: such as intelligent customer service robots, household cleaning robots and elderly care robots.
Logistics robots: used in warehousing, sorting and distribution to improve logistics efficiency.
R&D team: The company has a R&D team consisting of top engineers, scientists and technologists, focusing on innovation and breakthroughs in cutting-edge technologies.
Partners: AR Tech. has established strategic partnerships with a number of internationally renowned companies and research institutions to promote technical exchanges and project cooperation.
Social Responsibility: We actively participate in public welfare activities and are committed to technology popularization and educational support to promote social progress and scientific and technological development.

Name: Flowdron
Description:
Offriamo alle aziende strumenti e soluzioni per innovare o migliorare i processi aziendali; ci occupiamo di formazione specifica per piloti.

 SERVIZI
Proponiamo servizi legati alla drone economy
prevalentemente nei settori:
 Agricoltura
 Edilizia
 Controllo infrastrutture
 Entertainment/eventi

 FORMAZIONE
Organizziamo percorsi formativi specifici su: fotogrammetria, termografia, search & rescue, gestione dei rischi.

Name: Qwaltec
Description:
Qwaltec was founded in May of 2001 on the belief that success begins with quality. We have an extensive history with both government and commercial programs in technical training, systems engineering, and program management. Based on this history, we combine the best practices from our experience with the DoD, NASA and other government agencies along with commercial aerospace and network operations systems. These best practices provide a foundation for helping customers solve their unique operations, systems engineering, and program management challenges. As a privately held company, Qwaltec is dedicated to doing what is right for our customers. 

Qwaltec consists of a highly motivated staff of systems engineers, technical training consultants, program managers, and technical experts. Our experience with satellite and network operations has given us the insight and adaptability necessary to respond to highly dynamic programs. Our dedication to program success goes beyond our contractual agreement. We know that organizations that are trained, operated, and managed well reduce the long-term costs of your project. We continue to adapt our services, as needed, to achieve success for your personnel and ultimately your business. By quickly assimilating ourselves into your organization, we join in the allegiance to your success.

Qwaltec is certified as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB).

Name: Talisha Aerospace
Description:
TALISHA Aerospace is committed to building the world’s best reusable space technology while pioneering advancements in CubeSat deployments into orbit. Our mission extends beyond technological innovation; it aims to inspire and empower every Indian youth with opportunities in space science and exploration. By fostering education, research, and hands-on experiences in aerospace engineering, satellite technology, and reusable systems, TALISHA SPACE envisions India as a global leader in sustainable and accessible space solutions. Together, we will pave the way for a brighter future in space exploration, driven by the talent and dreams of our youth.


THE MISSION OF TALISHA
TALISHA SPACE

At TALISHA SPACE, our mission is to revolutionize space exploration through affordable and reusable space technology. We focus on developing low-cost satellite payloads while pushing the boundaries of innovation in space science. By exploring and discovering the wonders of space, we aim to make space technology accessible to all. Our programs are designed to inspire the next generation of scientists and engineers. We provide students with the opportunity to engage in hands-on experimentation, fostering creativity and practical skills in real-world space projects. Through initiatives like CubeSat development and reusable satellite systems, we aim to build a sustainable future in space exploration. TALISHA SPACE is committed to advancing our nation's capabilities in space technology, creating a platform for Indian youth to lead global innovation. Together, we dream big, innovate endlessly, and work tirelessly to shape the future of space exploration.

Name: STAR Dresden e.V.
Description: We are a university group at TU Dresden and want to test our excellent scientific education in exciting and challenging hands-on space projects. For this purpose, we participate in various programs and challenges, such as the ERC Space and Robotics Event or the German-Swedish REXUS/BEXUS program. We develop various hardware and software for these projects ourselves to test and develop our skills as engineers and scientists and to improve our teamwork.

Name: SpaceRake
Description: Many industries would benefit from satellite data, but lack the means to obtain and use it. SpaceRake is working to make data from space available to users on Earth, simply.

Name: Responsive Space Cluster Competence Center (RSC³)
Description: The Responsive Space Cluster Competence Center (RSC³) is the center of expertise within the German Aerospace Center (DLR) for all topics related to Responsive Space, i.e., the timely deployment and provision of space-borne capabilities. The RSC³ was founded in September 2020 and is funded by the German Federal Ministry of Defence. It is tasked with establishing a technology base for Responsive Space and conducting technology demonstrations in this field. To this end, the RSC³ follows a holistic approach. With the entire space and security ecosystem in view, RSC³ aims to identify more responsive ways to provide actionable data and information from orbit. This is reflected by the organization of the RSC³ into four departments: Launch Segment, Ground Segment, Space Segment and Technology Demonstration. The RSC³ is focused on connecting existing expertise within industry and the research community and to develop in close exchange with the user (the Bundeswehr, German Federal Armed Force and the Federal Ministry of Defence) a roadmap for a responsive and resilient space infrastructure. In this context, RSC³ provides to the user independent analysis and evaluation of Responsive Space concepts. The RSC³ is growing and always looking for skilled and motivated future colleagues. If you take an interest in “thinking outside the box” to develop the space into a responsive infrastructure, do not hesitate to contact us. You can reach us at: info-rs@dlr.de Impressum: https://www.dlr.de/DE/Service/Impressum/impressum_node.html Privacy Policy: https://www.dlr.de/EN/Service/PrivacyPolicy/privacypolicy2.html

Name: Entangled Space
Description:
At Entangled Space, we are aware that data security is of utmost importance and understand the menace that Quantum Computing poses to the encryption algorithms employed nowadays.

That's why we are developing a Quantum Keys Distribution (QKD) system for long-range communication that provides the highest level of security by providing ultra-secure keys to encrypt your information and ensure that it is protected. 

Our team of experts has an unparalleled level of experience in the field of research and development, and they are leveraging their expertise to develop a system that will be the cornerstone for the future of long-range data security. We are working hard to offer, in the near future, QKD products/solutions and later evolve towards Quantum Keys as a Service (QkaS).

Quantum keys: as unbreakable as the Laws of Physics.

Name: SPACE CLUB ENSAF
Description: S.P.A.C.E (Spacial Programs & Astronomy Club of Engineers) est un club astronomique, fondé le 7 décembre 2016, au sein de l'école nationale des sciences appliquées, géré par un groupe d'astronomes amateurs.

Name: Space V
Description: The multilayer Adaptive Greenhouses System is a turning point for growing plants in space.

Name: SPACE International
Description: SPACE International, le magazine de la conquête spatiale.

Name: Vyolarix
Description:
Vyolarix Private Limited is India’s first startup dedicated to the development and deployment of Space-Based Solar Power (SBSP) technology, marking a transformative step in the nation's renewable energy landscape. Established with the vision of addressing critical energy and environmental challenges, Vyolarix aims to revolutionize the way solar energy is harnessed, transmitted, and utilized globally.
Vision and Mission
Vision: To become a global leader in providing sustainable, scalable, and clean energy solutions through space-based solar power technology.
Mission: To develop cutting-edge SBSP systems that deliver uninterrupted, renewable energy to Earth, driving energy security, reducing carbon footprints, and supporting global sustainability goals.

Name: Myriad - Autonomous Gardening
Description: We believe everyone should have the opportunity to grow their own vegetables – no matter if they live in an urban metropolis, the desert or in space. To achieve this, we use the most innovative technologies to develop efficient and user-friendly products. We build Autonomous Gardens that fit into every home and lifestyle.

Name: International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST)
Description: ICAST brings together leading experts, academics, and industry professionals to collaborate and share advancements in space science and technology. It serves as a platform for exploring research breakthroughs, technological innovations, and collaborative efforts that shape the future of space exploration and applications.

Name: FIRE ARROW
Description: Fire Arrow is redefining space access by designing and building licensed spaceports that empower governments and private companies to launch safely, affordably, and sustainably. From concept to orbital insertion, we deliver end-to-end spaceflight solutions for a responsive and reliable path to orbit.

Name: Australian Centre for Space Engineering Research (ACSER), UNSW Sydney
Description:
For more than a decade at UNSW Sydney, the Australian Centre for Space Engineering Researc (ACSER) has provided leadership in Australian space engineering research. Striving to foster collaboration between research, industry and government we seek to nurture links between our national and international partners to achieve Australia's space ambitions. Our goal is to develop space capabilities relevant to Australia's needs through research, innovation and education.

ACSER has research strengths in an ever-broadening range of fields, including in GNSS receiver design, Cubesat/Satellite technologies, UAV Reflectometry, and the exciting new forefront of Space Resource Utilisation. We offer a wide variety of PhD opportunities for students looking to expand their knowledge and qualifications in these areas.

ACSER engages and connects through regular forums, workshops and events. We bring together the niche, and the not so niche, to encourage and facilitate future partnerships and collaborations so that Australia can secure and grow our place in space.

Name: Aalto Satellites
Description:
𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐒𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐒𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐬 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐩, led by 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐟𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐨𝐫 𝐉𝐚𝐚𝐧 𝐏𝐫𝐚𝐤𝐬 at the Department of Electronics and Nanoengineering, School of Electrical Engineering, Aalto University, focuses on the design, development, and utilization of 𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐨𝐬𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐬 for various scientific and technological applications. The group has been a pioneer in nanosatellite development in Finland, having launched several satellites, including Finland's first scientific satellite, 𝐀𝐚𝐥𝐭𝐨-1.

The research group's projects include the 𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐚𝐢𝐥 satellite missions of the 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐢𝐬𝐡 𝐂𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐄𝐱𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐒𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞, hosting two payloads: the Particle Telescope (PATE) from the University of Turku and the deorbiting plasma brake device from the Finnish Meteorological Institute. The spacecraft is designed and built by the group.

Additionally, the group participates in the 𝐃𝐈𝐀𝐌𝐀𝐍𝐓 project, which aims to develop large deployable thin membrane antennas for small satellites, enhancing their communication capabilities to meet the demands of new space missions.

The group collaborates closely with international research institutions and industry partners, driving innovation and training future experts in space technology. Their work spans a broad spectrum of space technology areas, including satellite design, manufacturing, operations, and the development of new technologies for functioning in the space environment.

The work of the research group has also been instrumental in advancing Finland's space technology sector. Companies such as 𝐈𝐂𝐄𝐘𝐄 and 𝐊𝐮𝐯𝐚 𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞, both leaders in Finnish space innovation, originated from this group—showcasing its impact on the industry and its role in nurturing cutting-edge space technology ventures.

Name: SpaceGold Technologies
Description: SpaceGold Technologies builds modular AI-powered mining systems that extract metals, oxygen, and helium-3 from the Moon. Our compact lunar refinery plugs into existing rovers and uses mass spectrometry to analyze and extract resources in real time. The system is built from off-the-shelf components, tailored for lunar conditions like low gravity and vacuum, and protected under a filed patent. A built-in mass driver launches the refined materials to lunar bases, orbit, or Earth without using fuel.

Name: Spacebackend
Description:
Spacebackend is redefining how aerospace teams handle mission-critical integration. Our platform, lynapse.com, is an AI-powered system integration environment designed for engineers-in-the-loop. We empower software integration and AIT/AIV teams to bridge the gap from hardware interface to flight-ready software - starting in the design phase and continuing through end-to-end integration.

By decoupling software readiness from physical hardware and leveraging AI to automate the implementation of complex interfaces, we reduce integration effort from years to days. Lynapse uses AI to convert complex ICD documentation into digital models, automatically producing, testing, and validating platform-agnostic source code.

Our workflow ensures every line of code is deterministic, engineer-approved, and fully traceable from documentation to deployment. Whether you are generating platform-agnostic code from scratch or validating existing flight software against a digital ICD model, Lynapse exposes failures and readiness gaps early - when it matters most. Build integration readiness today.

Name: FAST-EO
Description: The Fostering Advancements in Foundation Models via Unsupervised and Self-supervised Learning for Downstream Tasks in Earth Observation (FAST-EO) project is funded by the European Space Agency (ESA) Φ-lab under contract No. 4000143501/23/I-DT.

Name: i5 Space
Description: i5 Space is a national student organization that operates in conjunction with the U.S. Space Force’s Space Training and Readiness and Command (STARCOM). We aim to deliver educational, training, and professional development resources to students around the country who are interested in pursuing a career in the newest branch of the US military—the Space Force. We are growing every day and currently boast more than 1000 members from 67 different Squadrons! We are also proud to have research partnerships with the Space Force and NATO.

Name: Vrisva Space
Description: Vrisva isa space company dedicated to building SSTO and required components at scale. Follow for all posts related to SSTO and the making of it.

Name: NASA International Space Apps Challenge Dublin
Description:
A Community In & Out Of This World!

Returning to Dublin on October 4 and 5, 2025, NASA International Space Apps Challenge is the largest annual global hackathon on the planet. Growing since 2012, the 2022 edition built a collaboration of 93,520 registered participants, 15,444 teams across 485 local events in 163 countries and territories.

The event offers space and time for coders, scientists, artists, designers, storytellers, makers, builders, entrepreneurs, technologists, and other innovators around the world to come together and use NASA and partner space agencies' open data to create solutions to real challenges we face on Earth and in space.

There’s Always Space For One More!

NASA International Space Apps Challenge is open to everyone, including those who have never “hacked" before. 
In fact, teams with the most diverse sets of skills are often the most successful
All ages, skill levels, social, cultural and professional backgrounds are welcome to join the community.

Stay tuned for mission updates!

Name: Paramium Technologies
Description:
Paramium Technologies has developed technology that is meeting a need in the rapidly changing world of radio communication.  We have the ability to manufacture fast, flexible, and accurate freeform curved metal panels.  
Paramium is focusing on Ground Station as a Service (GSaaS) technologies. GSaaS is a high growth/high demand industry, and the Paramium solution will allow affordable, seamless satellite communications across the globe for small to mid-size companies.

Name: 5th Symposium on Space Educational Activities
Description: The Symposium on Space Educational Activities (SSEA), organised by the European Space Agency’s Education Office, provides a forum for university students, professionals working in education, and other young professionals to present and discuss their experiences organising, running and/or participating in Educational Activities related to Space.

Name: Canadian Space Health Research Network
Description:
CSHRNet's mission is to develop and support research and training initiatives in space health by fostering collaboration between researchers and providing trainees with opportunities to advance their careers in the field. To expand and strengthen the collaborative effort of representatives from academia, private industry and government agencies to work together to facilitate scientific discovery and knowledge translation for the benefit of all Canadians.

Our vision is to be a collaborative research network for the space health community in Canada, and to generate academic knowledge for the benefit of space exploration and for the health and well-being of all Canadians.

Name: GAIA SPACE
Description:
GAIA SPACE is a pioneering Indian startup at the forefront of sustainable space technologies. Our journey began with groundbreaking research in efficient propulsion systems, laying the foundation for next-generation in-space mobility. Today, we are revolutionizing space innovation with cutting-edge propulsion solutions and a mission to deliver real-time air traffic insights from space.  

Committed to pushing the boundaries of space sustainability, we integrate advanced propulsion, and data driven air traffic insights to create a safer, more efficient, and environmentally responsible future. Our vision is to drive progress through innovation, ensuring a greener and more secure pathway for space exploration.  

At GAIA SPACE, we don’t just explore the cosmos, we redefine how humanity reaches for the stars. Join us as we propel beyond limits and shape a more sustainable future in space.
Location: Port Blair, Andaman and Nicobar Islands

Name: Oxford Space Initiative
Description: The Oxford Space Initiative (OxSI), based at the University of Oxford, Saïd Business School, has as its primary mission: fostering space related social sciences research. The aim is to rigorously examine which forms of governance both business and political are needed to meet the moment and which voices beyond the traditional engineering/aerospace and defense verticals can broaden perspectives to highlight our shared responsibilities as well as our opportunities.

Name: SpaceComputer | Orbital Root of Trust
Description:
SpaceComputer is building a satellite-based distributed network and open settlement layer for confidential smart contracts: A space-native compute layer where trust is rooted beyond Earth.

Our mission is to create physically-verifiable, tamper-proof computation in orbit. By leveraging the isolation and resilience of the space environment, SpaceComputer establishes a new cryptographic trust model that is independent from Earth-based infrastructure and censorship.

Name: The ESA Stakeholder Engagement Facility
Description:
Within the Applications element of its Science for Society programme, ESA has supported the development of a wide range of Earth-observation based solutions that address key stakeholder needs driven by International and European policies.
 
There is however still a need to engage with new users and stakeholders to maximise the uptake of these solutions, while also ensuring that the engagement with existing stakeholders continues following the end of each project.
 
The role of the SEF is to implement a systematic knowledge and capacity sharing service across different ESA-funded Applications projects, continuing and expanding engagement with diverse stakeholder communities through a range of tailored approaches.

Name: NASA Space Apps Challenge - Lucerne (Switzerland)
Description:
We are the organizing team behind the NASA Space Apps Challenge – Lucerne 2025, one of the Swiss edition of the world’s largest space and science hackathon. Our mission is to position Switzerland at the forefront of global innovation by connecting students, professionals, startups, and institutions to co-create impactful solutions using NASA's open data.

Led and coordinated by Swiss Engineering, the event brings together multidisciplinary teams in a dynamic environment to tackle challenges in climate, AI, health, and space technologies. Activities include pre-event workshops, a 48-hour hackathon, expert talks, and strong initiatives for inclusion and sustainability.

By driving collaboration between academia, industry, and civil society, we empower the next generation of innovators and position Switzerland as a key player in space and deep-tech innovation. Together, we transform ideas into real-world impact from the heart of Switzerland.

Join us in shaping the future of space and technology.

Name: JAOPS
Description: JAOPS provides mission operations design, tools, training and support to achieve mission success in Earth orbit, cislunar space and beyond.

Name: Prospero Fellowship
Description: We are a fellowship encouraging university students to become leaders in the UK space industry

Name: International Space Alliance
Description: International Space Alliance (ISA) is a non-governmental, non-profit organization that fosters inclusivity and representation within the STEM and Space Ecosystem, focusing on providing easier access and enabling diversity involvement. International Space Alliance (ISA) focuses on entrepreneurship and innovation and caters to solving environmental, economic, and technological challenges related to Earth and Space Exploration.

Name: BEAMIDE
Description: BEAMIDE was founded in 2021 in Perugia as a spin-off of the Italian National Institute of Nuclear Physics (INFN), obtaining national approval from the INFN’s Tech Transfer National Committee (CNTT). We develop advanced technologies to analyze the impact of radiation on various systems and components, used in a wide range of sectors: from medicine to space, from nuclear to IT.

Name: Zəhra Kosmosda
Description: Kosmos, elm, texnologiya macəraları • Space, science, tech adventures

Name: KuupKulgur
Description:
KuupKulgur: Estonia's Pioneering Lunar Rover Mission 

Introducing KuupKulgur, Estonia's first lunar exploration mission. As the first Estonian Lunar Rover mission, KuupKulgur is designed to be compact and versatile, prioritizing technology demonstrations and scientific experiments on the lunar surface.

 Developed at the Tartu Observatory in collaboration with local and international industry partners, KuupKulgur is yet another project from the Tartu Observatory with diverse collaborations. Our rover is not only a vehicle; it's a sophisticated vehicle developed to execute cutting-edge experiments in the challenging lunar environment.

 Spearheading this initiative are students from the Tartu Observatory, leading the project with a focus on imparting invaluable hands-on experience. KuupKulgur is not just a lunar rover; it's a platform for fostering the skills and knowledge needed for space exploration.

Join us on this scientific endeavor where innovation meets education, and together, we push the boundaries of space exploration. KuupKulgur — bridging the gap between theory and practice, and inspiring the next generation of space pioneers.

Name: Rymdforum
Description:
Rymdforum Sverige är en ideell förening vars syfte är att sprida kunskap om svensk rymdverksamhet och öka informationsflödet mellan olika aktörer i rymdbranschen. Bland annat ordnas återkommande arrangemang för industri, forskare, beslutsfattare, användare av rymdteknik, media och den svenska allmänheten. 

Vår vision
- Vara rymdens röst i Sverige genom att generera och sprida information.
- Genom fler medlemmar bredda kompetens och trovärdighet.
- Öka den operativa kraften.
- Medvetandegöra beslutsfattare så att de har redskap att kunna agera.
- Bidra med kunskap till riks-, regionala och kommunala politiker.
- Vara en plattform för informationsutbyte mellan våra medlemmar.

Name: Space for Island Nations Conference
Description: Island Nations are particularly threatened by climate change. We need solutions from space to survive. We're back, join us on the Maldives from 23-25 April 2024!

Name: Team Anant
Description: Team Anant is a group of 40 undergraduate students from BITS Pilani, currently developing a 3U Nanosatellite for multispectral imaging. Our technical team takes a first-principles approach to design the nanosatellite under the Cal Poly CubeSat standards. The project thrives under the guidance of the Institute and the Indian Space Research Organisation.

Name: FVMat
Description:
Deep Tech Startup optimizing R&D workflows through AI-driven design of Meta-Materials, lattice structures & CAE (Computer Aided Engineering).

At FVMat, we revolutionize engineering with advanced AI/ML tools for the design, simulation, and optimization of Meta-Materials, encompassing lattice structures and general-purpose physics modeling. Our software seamlessly integrates into existing CAE platforms like Ansys and Abaqus, boosting productivity and simplifying user experience with intuitive plugins. We specialize in both Conventional and Dynamic & Multi-Functional Meta-Materials, enhancing R&D efficiency across key sectors such as aerospace, defense, and automotive.

Our Conventional Meta-Materials leverage unique microstructures to enable functionalities beyond the reach of traditional materials, suitable for optics, electromagnetics, and more. In contrast, our Dynamic Meta-Materials dynamically adapt to environmental stimuli, providing customizable properties on demand.

Supported by robust multiphysics simulations and a vast database of pre-designed Meta-Materials, our tools streamline design processes, markedly reduce R&D durations and costs, optimize material usage, and lower energy consumption - advancing sustainability and technological progress.

Committed to advancing material science, FVMat's scalable SaaS model delivers Meta-Material design tools that elevate product performance and market competitiveness. Looking forward, we plan to expand support for lattice structures and evolve our offerings into a comprehensive AI-driven CAD/CAE solution, catering to the diverse needs of high-tech industries.

FVMat is redefining industry approaches to complex engineering challenges through transformative Meta-Materials. Join us in shaping the future of engineering.

Deep Tech Startup optimizing R&D workflows through AI-driven design of Meta-Materials, lattice structures & CAE.

Name: Spacewink
Description:
Spacewink is a pioneering company dedicated to transforming financial analysis, space exploration, and digital consciousness. Founded by Anand Damdiyal, a visionary data scientist and advanced AI programmer, we blend cutting-edge technology with ancient wisdom to empower individuals and shape a sustainable future.

In financial analysis, we leverage AI, machine learning, and neural networks to provide market predictions with over 90% accuracy. Our approach integrates technical and fundamental analysis alongside insights from trading astrology using WD Gann cycle methods. This comprehensive methodology helps us identify critical entry and exit points, stop-loss targets, and peak market conditions, enabling clients to make informed, data-driven decisions.

We also offer complete financial automation solutions powered by custom AI agents that enhance your trading experience. Our personalized services include in-depth financial planning, goal-setting sessions, and ongoing support, ensuring tailored strategies and resources for success in a dynamic financial landscape.

Beyond finance, Spacewink is committed to space exploration, focusing on dark matter and dark energy with the ambitious goal of harnessing these forces by 2036. We envision a future where sustainable interstellar travel is possible, allowing humanity to expand its reach beyond Earth and discover new worlds.

Additionally, we explore digital consciousness, aiming to preserve human identity through innovative methods that capture our experiences. By integrating insights from ancient philosophies and modern science, we seek pathways for digital immortality, enabling our knowledge to transcend physical boundaries.

At Spacewink, we are dedicated to empowering our clients with innovative solutions and unwavering support. Join us as we push the boundaries of finance, space exploration, and digital consciousness, unlocking limitless potential for a brighter future.

Name: IMPRO SATELLITE IMAGE PROCESSING AND TELECOMMUNICATION INC.
Description: IMPRO Inc. is founded as a 100% SSTEK Defence Industry Technologies Inc. subsidiary with the mission of becoming an Image Processing Excellence Center and providing the national and international aerial platform imagery market with the information obtained from raw or processed aerial platform imagers by using our national reconnaissance and observation satellites both core and surplus image capacities.

Name: Euclid Consortium
Description:
The Euclid Consortium is an organisation that brings together more than 2000 researchers across theoretical physics, particle physics, astrophysics and space astronomy, alongside engineers, technicians, and management and administrative staff. Consortium members work in public research laboratories or universities and together contribute to the future success of the European Space Agency (ESA) Euclid mission.

Euclid is a medium-class ESA mission designed to map the universe and to better understand its unknown components, dark energy and dark matter, and its eventual fate. Scheduled for launch on July 1st, 2023 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket (SpaceX), within a month Euclid will join ESAs GAIA satellite and NASA's JWST at Lagrange point L2. From there it will survey 15,000 deg², around one third of the entire sky, over the course of its 6-year mission.

Name: Gamma Team @PoliTO
Description:
Gamma Team @PoliTO is a research group of the Department of Applied Science and Technology (DISAT)of-Politecnico di Torino and takes part to the research activities of the Integrated Additive Manufacturing (IAM) laboratory. 
http://iam.polito.it/ https://www.disat.polito.it/it/ 
The group deals with the materials related aspects of the Additive Manufacturing (AM) processes for metals and polymers. The main investigated topics are the powder production and characterisation, the AM processability of different materials and the evaluation of the effects of the post processing steps. 
The materials studied are processed trough most of the AM technologies such as E-PBF, LB-PBF, DED, DLP, SLA, SLS, FF. 
Metallic powders are produced in house trough a VIGA gas atomiser. HIP is also used as a production and post AM process. 
The main materials processed and studied are Al-alloys, Ni-Alloys, Ti-alloys, steels, intermetallics, metal matrix composites, polymer matrix composites thermoplastic and thermosetting polymers

Name: Space DAO
Description:
Space DAO is a a professional community driven initiative to enhance the reliabilty of space data for SSA and STM. Our mission is to foster collaboration and consensus on space data to ensure its quality. Therefore, we use Blockchain technology as consensus mechanism to reduce risks associated with a single dominant entity controlling vital information for decision-making.

Key Features:

1. Consensus Mechanism and Governance: Space DAO effectively establishs consensus on measurements and data from multiple sources.

2. Data Trust Augmentation: By combining data from various sources, Space DAO enhances quality assurance through network consensus.

3. Network Consensus Distribution: Users can evaluate data conformity using the platform's consensus distribution.

4. Delta to Network Consensus: Providers can benchmark their data against network consensus, ensuring their data quality assurance and improvement.

5. Space Data Marketplace: Space DAO provides a thriving marketplace for high quality space data insights and analytics



Space DAO empowers a diverse range of stakeholders to contribute and benefit:


- Satellite operators can use Space DAO to enhance their Space Situational Awareness (SSA)

- Data providers who sell and share information, measurements and analyses

- Space Data users who leverage Space DAO knowledge for their projects

- Regulators and Space Policy makers who develop rules for space traffic, disaster response, or other space applications


Space DAO is open for collaboration with stakeholder in space. Reach out to us for any request on information, partnership or contributions.

Name: TOLIMAN Space Telescope
Description:
The TOLIMAN space telescope takes another step forward in planning for humanity’s interstellar journey, by identifying if there an Earth Analog in Alpha Centauri, our nearest neighbour star system. TOLIMAN is a low-cost, agile, and ambitious mission led by scientists at the University of Sydney School of Physics. By employing methods of astrometric detection, TOLIMAN is able to record the ‘wobble’ signal in the separation of binary stars caused by gravitational forces of a planet. 

The TOLIMAN payload is a 12.5cm space-based telescope which is now under construction. It will be carried by a 16U CubeSat bus and is scheduled to launch in 2024 into 550km sun-synchronous orbit around Earth.

The mission is primarily funded by the Breakthrough Initiatives, which investigate the fundamental questions of life in the Universe: 

Are we alone? 
Are there habitable worlds in our galactic neighbourhood? 
Can we make the great leap to the stars? 
And can we think and act together – as one world in the cosmos?

Name: Risk, Hazard & Climate Lab
Description:
The RISK, HAZARD & CLIMATE LAB at the Department of Geoinformatics - Z_GIS, University of Salzburg, aims to identify spatial risk, hazard, and vulnerability patterns through geo-spatial analysis and remote sensing, integrating inter- and transdisciplinary approaches.

To measure, monitor, and represent the complexity of natural hazards and their impact on the environment and society in the face of climate change, we develop and integrate spatial methods and Earth observation (EO) data to support science- and knowledge-based hazard and risk management.

A particular focus is on EO-based mapping and monitoring of natural hazards, particularly landslides, and geomorphological features using knowledge-based and data-driven methods.

With our research, we aim to contribute to the required transformation of human society towards manageable risks, sustainability, and evenly shared well-being in the face of climate change.
