Name: Polimak Space
Description:
Advancing the frontier of space exploration through ground-breaking ISRU and bulk solids handling solutions, Polimak is redefining the realm of possibilities.

In-situ resource utilization (ISRU) — the use of local resources found on extraterrestrial bodies like the Moon or Mars — is a key pivot in space exploration. It offers the promise of sustained human life and operations, reducing the need for costly, challenging transportation of materials from Earth. From extracting water to manufacturing rocket propellants, ISRU stands at the brink of ushering in a new era of extraterrestrial civilization.

Leveraging our extensive know-how in designing and producing top-tier space mining technologies, we bring the concept of ISRU to life. Our comprehensive range of solutions encompasses processing, conveying, storage, dust mitigation, and feeding systems, essential for efficient resource extraction from celestial bodies such as the Moon, Mars, and asteroids.

As an engineering firm with a deep-rooted specialty in bulk solids handling technologies, Polimak brings 40 years of industry expertise to the forefront of space exploration. We've evolved from providing solutions for industrial processes to offering advanced solutions for the excavation, processing, and handling of lunar, asteroid, and Martian regolith.

Our ISRU solutions are ingeniously designed to minimize dependence on Earth-based resources, thereby reducing the environmental footprint of space mining operations. With our extensive experience in bulk solids handling, we customize systems to meet specific requirements, optimizing resource recovery in the process.

Polimak's mission echoes the spirit of pioneering: to enable efficient and sustainable space exploration. Together, we're venturing into the uncharted, making the infinite accessible.

Name: Lunaria One
Description:
Pioneering sustainable space exploration and pushing the boundaries of human potential.

At Lunaria One, we blend Australia's unique expertise with global talent to make sustainable activities in space a reality. Our team’s diverse strengths in biology, engineering, psychology, and education drive a shared vision for the future—one where space exploration benefits all.

 Our First Mission: The ALEPH Project
With our first payload launching to the Moon in 2025, the ALEPH project aims to demonstrate that life can germinate and thrive in harsh lunar conditions. By placing a carefully selected payload of seeds and plants on the lunar surface and learning how they adapt, we will take early steps towards the goal of a sustainable human presence on the Moon. This isn't just a lunar mission - what we learn about adaptability in conditions on the Moon will benefit agriculture in extreme environments here on Earth.

 Community and Outreach
We believe that space belongs to everyone. That’s why Lunaria One integrates citizen science and educational outreach into our projects. Our aim is to engage people of all ages and backgrounds, inviting them to be part of this groundbreaking journey. Explore how you can participate and be involved at https://plantsonthemoon.au.

Join us in advancing sustainable space research and inspiring the world through shared exploration.

Name: SpaceProjects.uk

For students, taking part allows them to apply their knowledge to a real-life space problem, develop space specific skills, and get job insights.

The space sector is one of the fastest growing sectors in the UK. Employers, however, often highlight a lack of skilled graduates, and students, a lack of opportunities to get experience. SpaceProjects.uk was created to give students space experience before they graduate.

We advertise projects to students across the UK, and are open to project submissions in any discipline on a rolling basis.

SpaceProjects.uk was created by UKSEDS and SUN. Find out more at our website, SpaceProjects.uk.

Notre objectif est d’aider l’Humain à explorer l’espace et à développer la résilience des voyageurs spatiaux pour les voyages à long terme. Notre mission au sein de ce laboratoire est la mise en place de protocoles de gestion du stress des astronautes dans l’espace. Une partie de ces protocoles sera aussi utilisée pour le grand public, grâce aux outils mis en place par Coreod Space : l’application Coreod Land.

Coreod Space is the first specialized e-Mental Health facility, offering digital therapies, based on studies conducted at the Space Neuropsychology Lab (LIRNAS). Our goal is to help humans explore space and to develop the resilience of space travelers for long term travel. Our mission in this lab is to develop protocols for managing astronaut stress in space. Part of these protocols will also be used for the general public, thanks to the tools set up by Coreod Space: the Coreod Land application.

Nabla Zero Labs is an Aerospace and Defense research, development, and engineering services company; we make Rocket Science the Easy Part.

Space Mission Design and Operations
Rely on our expertise designing and optimizing the most complex spacecraft trajectories ever flown. We are aware of Planetary Protection requirements, and can develop powerful infrastructure for space mission design and operations.

Operations Research
Solve your most challenging optimization problems to drive business value. Whether it is related to transportation, planning and scheduling, resource allocation, or vehicle routing, we can help you develop and solve optimization models using state-of-the art tools and techniques for large-scale systems.

Cybersecurity
Be proactive in the protection of your most critical assets. We can audit your Cloud Computing resources looking for vulnerabilities, provide pre- and post-mortem forensic services, and help you maintain regulatory compliance, such as the requirements imposed by the Department of Defense in the DFAR 252.204-7012 and NIST SP 800-171.

High-Performance Computing
Save time and money by letting us implement your most sophisticated scientific and numerical computing algorithms (we speak C++ and Python better than English). No datacenter? No problem — we can deploy solutions securely in the Cloud.

Analytics and Machine Learning
Go beyond the hype to create explanatory and/or predictive models based on messy and incomplete datasets. Though we love the tools, our analytics are more than just using Pandas, R, or Spark. Similarly, our machine learning solutions don't stop at `from sklearn import *`.

Training and Education
Cultivate in-house expertise by letting us help your team get rapidly up-to-speed in the most relevant tools and techniques available for your industry. From introductory, short Python courses, to custom week-long seminars in highly-specialized topics, such as astrodynamics and large-scale numerical optimization.

Deep Space Dynamics (DSD)

Deep Space Dynamics is an aerospace data-intelligence company developing AI-driven spacecraft and orbital sensor systems that deliver continuous monitoring, navigation, and defense capabilities across the Earth–Moon system and inner solar orbits. The company’s modular Neuman-class CubeSats operate autonomously from Lagrange vantage points to form a scalable space-based sensor network.

Core Offerings

Tactical Space Weather Intelligence (TSWI) – Provides real-time solar and plasma event monitoring from Lagrange-point constellations, enabling defense, telecommunications, and satellite operators to mitigate radiation and geomagnetic risks before impact.

Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) – Offers secure, resilient cislunar and interplanetary communication relays that enhance precision navigation and timekeeping for spacecraft, lunar assets, and critical infrastructure.

Lagrange-based Asteroid Detection Grid (LAD-G) – Establishes an early-warning network for near-Earth object tracking and orbital debris detection, protecting national assets and future industrial missions.

Commercial Value:
DSD’s technology supports national security, aerospace, and energy sectors by providing space situational awareness, radiation intelligence, and navigation redundancy. These services have immediate contract value with U.S. and allied agencies.

Strategic Vision:
To create a Lagrange-point infrastructure layer that underpins the next decade of space operations, serving defense, industry, and research customers while laying the groundwork for interplanetary expansion.

Name: ORION Space Generation Foundation / ORION Űrnemzedék Alapítvány
Description:
The Orion Space Generation Foundation promotes the exciting world of space exploration and spreads scientific knowledge through innovative projects in Hungary. 

 Our mission is to make the significance of space exploration accessible to all ages, support research, and ensure that space is accessible to everyone. We are committed to environmental consciousness and seek global partnerships to prepare future generations to face scientific challenges. 

The Orion Space Generation Foundation not only popularizes space – we open the gateway to the infinite possibilities of space! 

Join us and experience how we integrate space into everyday life through innovative projects. 

 Don't just dream about space, live it, be a part of it! Support us in making space accessible to everyone!

 Step into the future of space – because the future is now! Don’t forget, space is for all! 




Az Orion Űrnemzedék Alapítvány az űrkutatás izgalmas világát népszerűsíti és tudományos ismereteket terjeszt innovatív projektekkel Magyarországon. 
 Küldetésünk, hogy minden korosztály számára érthetővé tegyük az űrkutatás jelentőségét, támogassuk azt, és biztosítsuk, hogy az űr valóban mindenki számára elérhető legyen.

Elkötelezettek vagyunk a környezettudatosság mellett, és globális együttműködéseket keresünk, hogy a jövő generációi felkészülten nézzenek szembe a tudományos kihívásokkal. 

Az Orion Űrnemzedék Alapítvány nem csak népszerűsíti az űrt, de kaput is nyit annak végtelen lehetőségei felé!

Csatlakozz hozzánk, és tapasztald meg, hogyan integráljuk ezt a mindennapokba innovatív projektekkel!

 Ne csak álmodozz az űrről, éld meg, légy részese!
Támogass minket, hogy ez a csodálatos terület mindenki számára elérhetővé váljon! 

Lépj be az űr jövőjébe – mert a jövő most van!

Name: METU ROVER
Description:
Founded in 2018 under the Middle East Technical University (METU) Machinery and Innovation Society, METU Rover is a student team specializing in the development of space exploration vehicles. Our team unites students from engineering, fundamental sciences, and design disciplines, fostering a multidisciplinary environment dedicated to innovation. 

We actively prepare for renowned competitions such as the European Rover Challenge (ERC) and the Anatolian Rover Challenge (ARC), striving not only to deliver advanced projects but also to build an innovative ecosystem that motivates young individuals to pursue their interests in space exploration.

At METU Rover, we are committed to pushing boundaries—not only by designing advanced exploration robots but also by fostering technologies that support national industrial growth and pioneering advancements in space science. For us, success is not limited to our robots’ capabilities but is measured by the systems that facilitate their evolution. We welcome all curious minds to become part of this transformative journey.

Name: Space Kids Foundation
Description:
Somos una fundación de carácter internacional, con raíces en América Latina y una creciente presencia en Dubai, Emiratos Árabes y Qatar. Nuestra dedicación radica en acercar a niños y jóvenes al apasionante mundo de la industria espacial, la ciencia y la tecnología.

Nuestra misión es preparar a más de un millón de jóvenes para el año 2035, marcando el inicio de la era interplanetaria (2032 - 2035), dotándolos de sólidos conocimientos en tecnología e industria espacial para que puedan adaptarse a esta nueva era de descubrimiento.

Para alcanzar estos nobles objetivos, implementamos proyectos educativos que abarcan cursos especializados, charlas y eventos. Nuestra labor depende del apoyo de partners y del respaldo de diversas empresas, lo que nos permite cubrir costos, invertir en la educación y ampliar nuestra presencia. Gracias a las donaciones, hemos otorgado más de 10,000 becas en América Latina, asegurando que ningún niño quede excluido y que todos tengan la oportunidad de capacitarse en áreas esenciales para el futuro.

Orbotic Systems was formed to address the growing threat of space debris. After investigating solutions from both a business and technical perspective, Orbotic Systems was born. The goal of Orbotic Systems is to safely advance human progress in space with innovative business methods and new technology.

Orbotic Systems was designated a top ten satellite solution provider in 2021 by Aerospace & Defense Review. The D3 was one of the five winners in the aerospace category of the 2021, "Create the Future Contest."

Name: TexSpace
Description:
TexSpace assists and activates Texas' space initiatives, bolstering its competitive edge and leadership role. It supports the state's space economy across civil, commercial, and military sectors while enhancing contributions to national and global space efforts. 

When we land together in Texas, The State of Space is Ours.

Name: Center for Space and Habitability (CSH), University of Bern
Description: The mission of the Center for Space and Habitability (CSH) is to foster dialogue and interactions between the various scientific disciplines interested in the formation, detection and characterisation of other worlds within and beyond the Solar System, our search for life elsewhere in the Universe, and its implications for disciplines outside of the sciences. Our members, affiliates and collaborators include astronomers, astrophysicists and astrochemists, atmospheric, climate and planetary scientists, geologists and geophysicists, biochemists and philosophers. We are home to the CSH and Bernoulli Fellowships, which host young, dynamic and talented researchers from all over the world to conduct independent research. We actively run a series of programs to stimulate interdisciplinary research within the University of Bern, Switzerland, including collaborations and/or open dialogue with Medicine and Philosophy. We are active in scientific mediation and outreach activities with the general public and in promoting equality, diversity and inclusion in science.

Name: OPINCHARGE
Description:
Reinventing the way we invent batteries!

Welcome to the official LinkedIn account of OPINCHARGE consortium! We are a  of 10 organizations from 7 countries, dedicated to advancing battery innovation. Our mission is to unravel atomic-level processes in batteries using state-of-the-art operando nanoanalytical techniques. By combining expertise in chemistry, physics, and isotopes, we aim to understand battery interfaces like never before. Through our commitment to Open Science and collaboration, we strive to achieve the objectives of the BIG-MAP Batteries partnership and 2030+ programs. With a 36-month project duration and led by LIST, we maximize impact by disseminating our findings and promoting scientific communication. Join us on this exciting journey of revolutionizing the world of clean energy! #BatteryInnovation #CleanEnergy #Collaboration

The project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under Grant Agreement n°101104032.

OPINCHARGE is part of the large-scale BATTERY 2030+ research initiative aims to invent the batteries of the future by providing breakthrough technologies to the European battery industry

We are the creators of the world's first pure electric propulsion system for spacecraft, the IVO Quantum Drive.

We are the creators of the world's first true form of wireless power, Capacitive Based Aerial Transmission (CBAT).

Name: North American Space Institute (NASI)
Description:
The North American Space Institute (NASI) is a forward-thinking organization dedicated to shaping the future of the space industry through comprehensive training, standardization, and strategic collaboration. Recognizing the rapid growth and evolving demands of the space sector, NASI offers industry-aligned programs designed to equip individuals—from technicians to entrepreneurs—with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in this dynamic field.

Key Features of NASI:

Flexible, Career-Focused Training: NASI provides adaptable learning paths tailored to various career stages, ensuring accessibility for students, early professionals, and seasoned experts alike.

Global Reach: With online, hybrid, and in-person programming, NASI ensures high-quality education is accessible worldwide, fostering a diverse and inclusive space community.

Industry Integration: By collaborating with space sector leaders, NASI ensures its curriculum meets current industry needs, facilitating seamless transitions from education to employment.

Standardization & Insights: NASI plays a pivotal role in developing occupational guidelines and providing investment insights, contributing to a more structured and efficient space industry ecosystem.

Cutting-Edge Technology: Leveraging advancements in AI and data analytics, NASI enhances learning experiences through real-world simulations and hands-on training.

NASI's mission extends beyond education; it aims to be a catalyst for innovation and growth in the space economy. By setting benchmarks for training and fostering strategic alliances, NASI empowers professionals to lead and innovate in a sector poised for exponential growth.

Using a distributed satellite network, a wide variety of signals can be detected, identified, and precisely geolocated in near real time. The Centre includes a dedicated and modern Professional Learning space, with technology provision to allow in person and remote access to learning opportunities regardless of location.

Name: iSaisei Corporation
Description:
At iSaisei, we imagine a future where humanity doesn’t visit space — we live in it.
Our work begins with a simple idea: create the first habitats designed for human life beyond Earth.

We are developing inflatable lunar structures that expand with purpose:
more space to live, to work, to dream.
Modular, lightweight, and built for the realities of the Moon.

Every design starts from a question:
What does it take to make another world feel human?

Our flagship project, Oxygen Lunar Habitat, is the foundation of this vision —
a hybrid-inflatable module engineered to become the first home for the next generation of explorers.

As members of the International Astronautical Federation (IAF), we contribute to shaping the architecture of tomorrow’s space missions with solutions that blend engineering, design and imagination.

The Moon is only the beginning.
Life multiplanetary is the future we are building.

Development of sustainable space community in the Asia Pacific Oceania region by providing simple continuous platforms to connect and discuss diverse topics and to forge a community of problem solvers.

Our media platform: Space Smurfs, https://linktr.ee/spacesmurfs

Name: Space Villages
Description:
WE SUPPORT SPACE ASSET OWNERS TO RECOVER THEIR LOST REVENUES FROM FAILED SPACE MISSIONS.

We do it with our multiservice-robotic spacecraft equipped with robotic arm manipulated in real time, able to repair, service and refuel other spacecreaft.


Space Villages is a Dutch start-up, a part of Space Infrastructure Ventures (a fund intended to promote companies developing deep-tech products).

We have a bold and ambitious goal, developing and commercialising LEO as well as servicing customers in MEO and GEO orbits. 

Our innovative robotic spacecraft (RoWo – Robotic Worker) is designed to supplement the work of the astronauts in orbit. 

RoWo is designed to revolutionise the space ecosystem and in-orbit operations by enabling complex and precise manipulations, ranging from orbital cargo transportation to the installation and assembly of large-scale structures.

Our solutions include advanced testing coordination for industrial products and satellite missions. We are committed to empowering the Egyptian and global space industry with innovative and cost-effective testing solutions.

Name: EU PROTECT
Description:
Launched in June 2022, PROTECT aims at levering innovation procurement and foster the funding of R&I services that will help unlock the climate services’ market potential to support urgent climate change adaptation and mitigation  

 During its two-year duration, PROTECT will prepare the operational ground for one or more joint, cross border or coordinated pre-commercial procurement (PCP) processes to be implemented after the project end (May 2024). PROTECT will engage with an extensive and varied community of public buyers to foster and support a ‘buying with impact’ approach. Clearer, less fragmented demand shall guide and support R&D for future climate services.  

Specifically, PROTECT will provide: 
o	Assistance in defining and aggregating clear and realistic needs for climate services 
o	Experience sharing and good practice transfer with procurement peers from all over the EU
o	Insights into climate services’ marker providers’ existing and future solutions and technologies. 
o	Targeted training, continuous knowledge and fresh information on Earth observation, climate services and innovation procurement
o	Strategic positioning for participation in the upcoming Pre-Commercial Procurement procedure(s)

 In this frame, PROTECT will support the shaping of climate change adaptation and mitigation solutions and services using Earth Observation data that best address the needs of the public demand, both specific and systemic

Name: SPACE 4 TALENTS
Description:
Projekt SPACE 4 TALENTS to unikalna na skalę europejską inicjatywa, która łączy rozwój nowoczesnych kompetencji, przestrzeń dla innowacji oraz współpracę międzysektorową. Jego misją jest przyciąganie i zatrzymywanie talentów w Stalowej Woli – mieście, które zmierza do stworzenia nowej, multidyscyplinarnej specjalizacji związanej z przemysłem kosmicznym i obronnym. Projekt jest realizowany w ramach programu Europejskiej Inicjatywy Miejskiej i opiera się na eksperymentalnym podejściu do rozwiązania problemu kurczenia się miasta poprzez rozwój sektora kosmicznego i obronnego. Branża obronna, dzięki swojej zbieżności na wielu polach z przemysłem kosmicznym, będzie miała szczególne miejsce w hubie technologicznym. Jest to zgodne z charakterem i historią Stalowej Woli jako miasta o strategicznym znaczeniu dla polskiego przemysłu.

„SPACE 4 TALENTS”, współfinansowany jest z Unii Europejskiej, ze środków Europejskiego Funduszu Rozwoju Regionalnego (ERDF), w ramach Europejskiej Inicjatywy Miejskiej (European Urban Initiative - EUI). Jego celem jest stworzenie modelu przyciągania i zatrzymywania talentów w Stalowej Woli poprzez rozwój sektora kosmicznego.”

Name: DestinE Platform
Description:
The DestinE Platform is the entry point to Destination Earth, providing an open ecosystem of services that enable users to discover, access and exploit the DestinE data and resources. 

Destination Earth (DestinE) is a European Union funded initiative, with the aim to build a digital replica of the Earth system to model, monitor and simulate natural phenomena, hazards and the related human activities. 

The initiative is being jointly implemented by three entrusted entities: the European Space Agency (ESA), responsible for building the ‘Core Service Platform’, the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF), responsible for the creation of the first two ‘digital twins’ and the ‘Digital Twin Engine’, and the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT), responsible for the creation of the ‘Data Lake’. 

The DestinE Platform, implemented by ESA and developed by a consortium of industrial partners, integrates and operates an open ecosystem of services in an open and secure cloud environment to support DestinE data exploitation, with the aim to meet the needs of a large and diverse community, that includes citizens, scientists and academics, commercial entities, and policy makers. 

The DestinE Platform additionally provides onboarding support for integrating external services and resources, making the ecosystem flexible, scalable, and easily adapted to the community needs.

Name: Advanced SpaceLife Research Institute
Description: The Advanced SpaceLife Research Institute (ASRI) is a nonprofit research organization dedicated to studying human reproduction and sexual health factors as they apply to outer space environments.  ASRI is intended as a focal point for research organizations, space agencies, commercial space companies, space societies, universities, and individual researchers and scientists in the planning, execution, and data archival of all research related to those factors, enabling permanent, multigenerational human settlement beyond Earth.

Name: Alya Space
Description:
Alya Space is focused on earth observation through a nanosatellite constellation in low orbit.

Alya's central vision is to create a global network of partnerships for mutual and sustainable development, utilizing shared space systems to generate positive impacts on the quality of life in communities. This includes fostering economic development and job creation as a way to combat and eradicate poverty.

The project's primary goal is to produce reliable and accessible data to monitor various aspects such as the environment (forests, oceans, rivers), agriculture, mineral exploration, and oil and gas. Additionally, it aims to monitor clean energy generation systems.

Alya stands out as a pioneering private company in the space sector, headquartered in Salvador-Bahia. It possesses an experimental farm in the semi-arid region of Tucano, where calibration activities for sensors in orbit will be conducted. This will generate reliable satellite data and simulate areas for the production of vegetation, soil, and water parameters. Through this process, algorithms will be developed to assist the artificial intelligence systems of orbiting sensors in recognizing identical parameters, creating alerts, and enabling efficient monitoring.

Strategically located in Brazil, we will construct one of the country's first commercial ground stations. This facility will serve as the hub for controlling and managing the activities of our satellite constellation, as well as tracking, monitoring, or facilitating data transfer for any other satellite entering our territory.

Alya is also in the process of incorporation in Europe, a strategic move to globalize our activities and leverage competencies from previous experiences. This involves forming more than a dozen commercial partnerships, adding significant value and reliability to our space activities with earth observation nanosatellites and developing shared mission systems.

Name: UNSW Rocketry Team
Description:
Based at UNSW Sydney, we are a vibrant, student-led team on a mission to design, build, and launch high-power rockets. With a culture driven by innovation and curiosity, we push the boundaries of space exploration while fostering growth and success across engineering and business disciplines. 

What makes us unique? We welcome students from all backgrounds and skill levels, providing comprehensive training and mentorship to help you grow. Whether you're taking your first steps in rocketry or have technical experience, we offer unmatched opportunities for hands-on learning, technical mastery, and leadership development.

Our team competes on both national and global stages, achieving remarkable success. Recent highlights include:
- Successful development and test flight of Zenith, our 2024 competition rocket, yielding valuable insights for future projects
-  3rd place in the 10,000 ft COTS category at the 2024 Australian University Rocketry Competition (AURC)
-  Best Media Award and  3rd place in the 30,000 ft COTS category at Spaceport America Cup (SAC) 2023

In 2024, we launched groundbreaking research rockets, including:
- Genesis: Our first-ever two-stage rocket
- Tom and Gerry: Showcasing in-house SRAD Flight Computer, Tuffy
- She’ll Be Right: A single-separation dual-deploy system with advanced avionics
Looking ahead, our multi-stage rocket Spectre represents a culmination of a year of engineering innovation as we prepare for the International Rocketry Engineering Competition (IREC) 2025.

As part of a thriving ecosystem of student-led projects at UNSW, we are passionate about inspiring the next generation of engineers, scientists, and business leaders. Through education, outreach, and collaboration, we create a community where students thrive and learn together.

Ready to reach new heights? Join us as we continue pushing boundaries and building Australia’s rocketry future. For partnership opportunities, contact us at unswrocketry@gmail.com.

Name: Planetary Systems AI
Description:
Planetary Systems AI accelerates data at the speed of space. We are space data explorers building Earth’s orbital digital infrastructure leveraging one of the world’s fastest growing markets, the space and satellite industry, working with some of the toughest customers solving some of the toughest problems on and off the planet.
 
PSAI accelerates data flow and insight generation for decision-making in the space sector, optimizing space operations. We address the challenges of building software applications with space and satellite data, artificial intelligence and machine learning tools that are foundations for data fusion and neural networks in the space industry, digital twin technology, data models, digital reliability engineering to empower interoperability and computationally accessible data in a booming $1.8 trillion space economy. 

PSAI’s IDMP and AI software products support operational efficiency, space domain awareness, and logistical planning to serve companies, government agencies, and small businesses that will increasingly rely on clarity and speed in the multiple data sources they must consult to make decisions around space and satellite operations. 

PSAI is an approved analytic provider for the SDA TAP Lab at USSF Space Systems Command with several service contracts and has received a Phase 2 SBIR Award from SPACEWERX Ventures.

Name: International Electric Propulsion Conference 2025
Description:
For over four decades, IEPC has been the premium gathering occasion for industrial and academic institutions in the field of Electric Propulsion for spacecraft. Guided by the Electric Rocket Propulsion Society (ERPS), this biennial conference has played a foundational role in shaping the advancements of crucial space propulsion technologies since the 1970s. These technologies have transformed space applications for the greater good of the society and are extending mankind’s access to the Moon, Mars and beyond.
Coming to the United Kingdom and London for the very first time, the 39th edition of the IEPC serves as an exciting opportunity to showcase the long heritage of the UK in EP as well as the endeavours and the initiatives of the academic and industrial communities across the nation to help address the challenges and the novel frontiers of our field toward the future.
Featuring the theme of “Electric Propulsion, a Gateway to the Cosmos”, we hope the conference provides all of you with ample opportunities for thought-provoking and engaging technical exchanges and discussions, enabling the global EP community to coordinate and consolidate its roadmaps and directions of progress for the greater good of the society and to realise the humanity’s aspirations to explore outer space.
It is our sincere hope that you will fully enjoy the conference, experience unforgettable moments, and truly feel belonged and at home.

Name: The Florida Space Institute
Description:
The Florida Space Institute had been supporting space research, development, and education activities since 1996. FSI is a cornerstone of the University of Central Florida.

From observations of small bodies in the solar system to extraterrestrial soil mechanics, our research spans a wide spectrum. 

Administratively housing the Florida Space Grant Consortium (FSGC) for NASA, The Center for Lunar and Asteroid Surface Science (CLASS), Solar System Exploration Virtual Research Institute (SSERVI), and The Radiation Effects on Volatiles and Exploration of Asteroids and Lunar Surfaces (REVEALS), we're at the forefront of space innovation and collaboration.

Name: Columbia Space Initiative

We come from diverse academic fields, and we are involved in competition-based engineering challenges, as well as independent technological pursuits. We take part in space-oriented outreach, and we organize speaker events and educational workshops to showcase many different aspects of the global space community.

Our members are some of the most motivated individuals that our university has to offer, and have worked at places like Boeing, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and SpaceX.

Name: Oklahoma Air and Space Port
Description: One of only 14 FAA-licensed spaceports in the entire United States. With its 13,503’x300’ runway and unique 150-mile space flight corridor operated and controlled by the Oklahoma Space Industry Development Authority (OSIDA), the Oklahoma Air and Spaceport and 1,100-acre Industrial Park at Clinton-Sherman Airport in Burns Flat is an incredible resource ready for service. Flight research and development and testing capabilities abound where the limitations on restricted airspace and operations in or near Military Operation Areas (MOAs) do not exist. The flight corridor, with its northwestern Polar trajectory, is nearly 150 miles long and averages about 45 miles in width traversing the Texas and Oklahoma panhandle from the ground all the way to space providing an airspace capable of testing multiple types of aerospace vehicles. The Oklahoma Air and Spaceport has a state-of-the-art capable launch facility complete with a telemetry & monitoring room, air traffic control tower, fire and rescue team, and even fueling (FBO) capabilities.

Name: Ethereal Space, Inc.
Description:
Ethereal Space is the first commercial company to build a dedicated space weather satellite constellation as a sentry to monitor potentially hazardous solar activity and conditions on the Sun, in the solar wind, magnetosphere and ionosphere which can degrade, damage, or devastate all spacecraft in orbit and the Earth-based industries which rely upon them.

Our team is comprised of aerospace and defense industry professionals who have spent their careers in space. From the design and development of science missions for both GEO and LEO, to the spacecraft and instruments such as: multispectral and hyperspectral spectrometers, passive microwave and infrared atmospheric sounders, ozone profilers, GNSS Radio Occultation, GNSS Reflectometry, Electro-Optical/Infrared Imagers, and radar altimeters. The Ethereal Space team has successfully designed and led multiple space missions including satellite assembly, integration, test, launch and operations. Our work included the US Government, Foreign Governments, and Civil/Commercial missions globally.

Name: Australian Regolith Geoscientists Alliance (ARGA)
Description:
ARGA promotes the important role that regolith science plays in Australia. Regolith geoscience is critical in the Australian context, as more than 70% of the continent is covered by transported material and deep weathering profiles. The alliance has existed since the ending of the Cooperative Research Centre for Landscape Environments and Mineral Exploration (CRC LEME) in 2008. It builds upon the legacy of decades of Australian regolith research, including work at universities, state and Australian government agencies that culminated in CRC LEME.

We welcome all people who have an interest in regolith geoscience and its many facets, including geology, geochemistry, geophysics, geochronology, mineral exploration, pedology, biology, hydrology, meteorology, agronomy, forestry, etc.

The objectives of ARGA are to further the study of regolith geoscience and its allied disciplines (including Critical Zone studies) by:
- facilitating the exchange of information among members of the association, and in general for all those interested in regolith geoscience
- stimulating interest in regolith geoscience
- encouraging the practical applications of regolith geoscience research
- providing high-quality education and training
- promoting regolith geoscience to the broader community.

ARGA’s goal is to promote research and education in regolith geoscience by fostering interaction and knowledge exchange between academia, government agencies, the minerals industry and the general public. To achieve these goals as a Specialist Group, ARGA facilitates:
-an ARGA Symposium and/or field workshop at the next Australian Geoscience Council Convention (AGCC) and/or the Australian Earth Sciences Convention (AESC), together with organisational and editorial support
-a biennial conference and field trips in opposite years to the AGCC and/or AESC, with extended abstracts volumes
-the Keith Scott Memorial Award for best presentation at the biennial ARGA conference
and many other initiatives!

Name: SpaceValley Foundation
Description:
Space Valley Region
Located along the Rio Grande River corridor from southern New Mexico to southern Colorado, Space Valley stands as a pioneering center for scientific inquiry, space exploration, and deep technology innovation. With a storied past as the birthplace of modern rocketry and a history steeped in groundbreaking discoveries, Space Valley merges scientific excellence with human experience to ignite visionary breakthroughs and inspire transformative solutions.

Here, bold vision, cutting-edge science, rich cultural heritage, and diverse voices converge in a dynamic environment that fosters creativity, discovery, and ingenuity. The region’s powerful landscapes and collaborative spirit spark the big ideas needed to tackle the world’s most complex challenges—and shape the future of space.

As a catalyst for global progress, Space Valley brings together the military, business, and public sectors in an incubative environment that supports the earliest stages of innovation. This fusion of advanced technology, technical expertise, cultural wisdom, and entrepreneurial energy powers a thriving community where history and innovation intertwine to change the world.

SpaceValley Foundation
Founded in 2024, SpaceValley Foundation, based in Albuquerque, New Mexico, connects diverse people, ideas, and interests in a singular mission to unlock the transformative power of space to benefit the Space Valley community and humanity as a whole.

As the space industry becomes increasingly relevant to a wider range of people and industries, the foundation optimizes the Space Valley region’s ability to harness this growth by systematically:

Connecting community to build transparency and partnership
Promoting Space Valley stakeholders and their work
Uniting non-space organizations with space opportunities
Cultivating awareness of space and its value to humanity.

Name: Initium Space
Description:
Positioned uniquely at the fringe of technology and business, Initium Space has extensive knowledge in areas like space technology development (thermal hardware, mechanical hardware, structures, materials, additive manufacturing, etc.), project management and implementation, documentation and space product assurance. We provide tailored solutions, seamlessly blending technical expertise with strategic insights aiming to go beyond the technology development process.

Our team's expertise comes from our participation in actual space projects and missions. We have been involved in multiple ESA technology development studies (new materials and processes, antennas, mechanical and thermal hardware) and we have been active in leading teams in projects related to missions such as JUICE, BIOMASS, HERA, ALTIUS, Sentinel 4 UVN, Proba-3, etc.

We have also experience with use and implementation of quality processes related to ISO EN9100 for Space activities (Certification, procedures, documentation), Cleanroom documentation (Internal procedures, Operational procedures).


Contact us at office@initium-space-consulting.eu
#WeSpeakSpace

Name: Amsterdam Space Symposium
Description:
Welcome to the Amsterdam Space Symposium. We take you on a journey through Space for Climate and Space for Security & Defence. 

We are not your run-of-the-mill space gathering. Nope, we’re a convergence of diverse minds who grasp that space holds the key to tackling Earth’s biggest challenges.

Space infrastructure and space services are increasingly important to our society. Imagine a day without navigation, global communication, weather forecasts, or daily observations across the globe. In times when society faces global challenges in areas like security and climate, it is imperative to continue sharing our ideas, developments, and achievements in the fields of space technology, space infrastructure, and space applications.

At this symposium, we champion collaboration between the global space ecosystem and adjacent sectors by linking political and strategic discussions with solutions across the entire supply chain.

Expect the unexpected. Brace yourself for thought-provoking sessions and enlightening discussions that challenge your perspectives.

Immerse yourself in a dynamic programme filled with knowledge-sharing, inspiration, and collaborative opportunities.

Name: Space Science and Geospatial Institute (SSGI)
Description:
The Space Science and Geospatial Institute (SSGI) is Ethiopia’s leading institution dedicated to advancing space science, satellite technology, aerospace research, and geospatial technologies. Through innovation, research excellence, and technical expertise, SSGI drives the nation’s capacity to design, develop, and operate cutting-edge space- and geospatial-based solutions.

Our mandate spans from satellite design, assembly, and operations to the development of advanced aerospace systems and geospatial technologies, supporting national priorities in communication, earth observation, navigation, mapping, and scientific exploration. We lead the generation, processing, and provision of geospatial and space data, offering reliable services that inform decision-making across sectors. We also develop robust infrastructure, including ground stations, data processing centers, and research laboratories, enabling Ethiopia to independently manage and utilize space and geospatial technology for development and security.

SSGI’s work extends to capacity building and knowledge transfer, ensuring the growth of skilled professionals in aerospace engineering, satellite applications, and geospatial sciences. By fostering partnerships with local and international organizations, we are positioning Ethiopia as a hub for space and geospatial innovation in Africa.

With a vision to enhance national resilience and global competitiveness, SSGI is committed to using space and geospatial technologies to address critical challenges in agriculture, climate monitoring, disaster management, urban planning, and connectivity—contributing to sustainable development and scientific progress for the nation.

Name: DeepTech Explorers
Description:
DeepTech Explorers: Your gateway to the top Stealth mode companies.

Join DeepTech Explorers to gain access to a community of like-minded individuals and resources that can help you achieve your career goals in deep tech and space research.

As a member, you will have the opportunity to:

1. Join a research group: Look for research groups or labs that focus on deep tech and areas such as space research. This will give you hands-on experience and provide you with opportunities to work on real-world projects.

2. Conduct your own research: Once you have gained enough knowledge and experience, you can start conducting your own research. This could involve designing and testing, developing, or optimizing existing work.

3. Publish your work: Publish your research in scientific journals and conferences to share your findings with the broader scientific community. This will help you establish yourself as an expert in the field and open up opportunities for collaboration and further research.

4. Join a real-world project: Seek opportunities to work on real-world projects outside of academia, such as internships, research fellowships, or industry collaborations. This will allow you to apply your knowledge and gain valuable work experience.

5. Connect with relevant employers: Network with potential employers in your field of interest, attend job fairs, or connect with alumni from your institution to learn about job opportunities and make valuable connections.

Our community offers a unique opportunity to connect with peers, mentors, and employers in the deep tech and space research fields. Whether you are just starting your journey or are already an expert in the field, DeepTech Explorers can help you take your career to the next level.

Name: AstroFlux
Description:
Welcome to Astroflux, the ultimate space club for enthusiasts and explorers alike! 

At Astroflux, we're dedicated to fostering a vibrant community of space science enthusiasts who share a passion for all things cosmic. Whether you're an experienced astronomer or just launching into the wonders of space, our LinkedIn page is your portal to a universe of fascinating content and engaging discussions.

Join our group of space aficionados as we embark on a journey through the cosmos, sharing awe-inspiring images, mind-blowing facts, and the latest discoveries in space science. From breathtaking nebulae to distant galaxies, there's always something new to discover and discuss.

 We're also a hub for learning and exploration, offering resources and activities to help members deepen their understanding of space science. Whether it's through virtual stargazing sessions, educational workshops, or lively debates, there's no shortage of ways to expand your cosmic knowledge and engage with fellow enthusiasts.

So whether you're a seasoned astronomer or a curious newcomer, join us at Astroflux and embark on a journey to the stars. Together, we'll explore the wonders of the cosmos and ignite a passion for space science that's truly out of this world!

Name: Starbound Space Solutions
Description:
We accelerate great engineering by transforming tacit knowledge into deployable wisdom.

Starbound’s platform lets you access world class human rocket science experience via our helpful AI, Virgil. 

Virgil helps companies to access the space market by streamlining the technical compliance processes. It liberates Systems Engineers from MS Word and Excel. 

Our mission is to empower everyone to access the space sector, including space industry start-ups and terrestrial or earthbound companies excited to enter the space domain.
 
Virgil is constantly learning from the world’s foremost space engineers. It draws upon centuries of real human spaceflight experience. This significantly reduces the effort lost to paperwork, so businesses can focus on their core mission. 

Starbound is building a future where anyone can be a rocket scientist, irrespective of background.

Curated Human Space Engineering Expertise. Delivered by locally run Al, without hallucinations.

Name: Cloud Underground
Description:
Cloud Underground builds sovereign AI infrastructure — systems that you own, not rent.

We help founders, educators, engineers, and teams create real infrastructure with AI coworkers and intelligent automation, instead of relying on external platforms or large engineering departments.

Our technology ecosystem merges learning, automation, and sovereignty to make complex systems intuitive and portable.

Our evolving product lineup includes:
- Cloud Jam → hands-on DevSecOps and sovereign cloud training guided by AI.
- Twin Technologies → personal and enterprise AI coworkers that learn your systems and help you build faster.
- Underground Nexus → open-source, hyperconverged cloud that runs anywhere.
- Jelly Apps → reusable automation capsules that simplify operations.
- RAG Packs → portable knowledge modules that can be used in any AI or workflow.

We serve:
- Builders and founders turning ideas into working MVPs.

Why it matters:

From laptops to data centers, Cloud Underground delivers AI-managed, compliance-ready environments that empower the next generation of builders.

Name: NASA International Space Apps Challenge Chiclayo
Description: Space Apps is a incubator innovation program of National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

Name: Durham University Spaceflight
Description: We are a society of students at Durham University, who are passionate about all things space. We aim to provide an environment in which our cohort can develop their interest in space through hands-on projects, concurrent R&D, and networking with industry professionals. Engaging in the local community is an important aspect of shortening the gap between education and careers in the space-sector. As such we assist a richer STEM education system by bringing the UK Space Design Competition to local schools.

Name: Needronix
Description:
Needronix is a designer, manufacturer and integrator of reliable CubeSat and SmallSat components. We are a collective of highly motivated enthusiasts helping advance the way CubeSats will be developed in the near future. We produce miniature and easy to integrate components like sun sensors, earth sensors, transceivers and more. We offer all support and services a customer needs to use our products for example coaxial cable assemblies and development kits.

Our mission is to significantly reduce cost and time of CubeSat integration so delivering customers payload into space will be faster and cheaper than ever.

Name: Future Space Architecture
Description: Future Space Architecture (FSA) is an innovative startup dedicated to reshaping the future of space logistics through the development of cutting-edge in-orbit infrastructures. Founded by space architect Omar Alayli, FSA operates at the intersection of aerospace, technology, and space engineering design to provide reliable, cost-effective, and efficient solutions that drive the space industry’s growth.

Name: Indian Planetary Science Association
Description: The Association aims to promote Planetary Science and related branches of science in India. To achieve this, it encourages the study of all aspects of Planetary Science and facilitates the advancement, dissemination, and application of knowledge in this field. The Association also promotes active interaction among individuals, educational and research institutions, and industries interested in Planetary Science, and issues publications such as bulletins, journals, and newsletters to disseminate information. It holds periodic scientific meetings, encourages coordinated research programs among Indian scientists, and exchanges research personnel between institutions and universities in India. The Association also keeps liaisons with other planetary science societies worldwide, institutes lectures, prizes, and fellowships, and expresses the views of the Planetary Science community in India regarding the current status and future developments in the field. Additionally, the Association performs any other activities and functions that further advance Planetary Science and related sciences in India.

Description:
We are working on developing fault-tolerant and high-performance data handling systems in order to increase the computational performances of your systems.

Contact : contact@coros-space.com
Job offers : https://www.coros-space.com/jobs/

Name: NeurAstra
Description:
NeurAstra is an independent science organisation based in the French and Swiss Alps, gathering international professionals in domains as diverse as biology, medicine, engineering, history, and geography. Together, we aim to develop inspiring projects to investigate, understand, and highlight the human potential for adaptation in remote and extreme environments on Earth—and beyond. We aspire to bridge the gap between terrestrial life and the cosmic realm through exploration, innovation, and collaboration. In addition to its main activities, NeurAstra also proposes educational initiatives and artistic projects to encourage knowledge share, spark conversations, and inspire science enthusiasts of all ages.

Sic Itur Ad Astra,

Name: Stratolia
Description:
Stratolia is pioneering a new standard in Earth observation by deploying a network of autonomous stratospheric balloons, operating at 20 km altitude, to capture ultra high-resolution imagery at scale.

We believe the next revolution in Earth intelligence will be powered by domain-specific AI agents. These systems will require continuous, rich visual context of our planet to operate.

Stratolia is the foundational layer — the sensing infrastructure that enables them.

Name: The Spanish Remote Sensing Association (AET)
Description: The Spanish Association of Remote Sensing is the result of the interest in creating a scientific and professional network among people interested in the principles and applications of this technology. It was created in March 1986 as the Remote Sensing Working Group (GTT). Subsequently, in September 1988, this group became the Spanish Remote Sensing Association, after being registered in the Associations Registry of the Ministry of the Interior. Since then, it has held scientific congresses biennially and multiple technical meetings, becoming the leading professional association in this field. Additionally, it has uninterruptedly published the Spanish Journal of Remote Sensing since 1993, on a biannual basis.

Name: SpaceHopper
Description:
A hopping robot for asteroid exploration.

In the SpaceHopper project, students at ETH Zurich developed a robot capable of moving in low gravity environments through hopping motions. It is intended to be used in future space missions to explore small celestial bodies. The exploration of asteroids and moons could provide insights into the formation of the universe and they may contain valuable minerals that humanity could use in the future.

The project began in 2021 as an ETH focusproject for bachelor's students. Now, it is being continued as a regular research project. A particular challenge in developing exploration robots for asteroids is that, unlike larger celestial bodies like Earth, there is low gravity on asteroids and moons. The students have therefore tested their robot's functionality in zero gravity during a parabolic flight. The parabolic flight was conducted in collaboration with the European Space Agency as part of the ESA Academy Experiments Programme.

Name: The Orbital Locker
Description:
The Orbital Locker Company Ltd. is pioneering the world’s first industrial park in orbit - a permanent, modular facility designed for manufacturing, research, and recycling operations in space.

Built from our proprietary ORBlock system, the Orbital Locker is engineered for autonomous assembly, scalable growth, and sustainable operation. Each ORBlock forms part of a larger architecture that expands humanity’s industrial footprint beyond Earth.

Our portfolio unites physical, digital, and financial infrastructure to create a self-sustaining orbital economy:

• ORBlock – modular construction units enabling rapid assembly of orbital facilities.
• ORLaunch – a digital platform connecting innovators, investors, and institutions through AI-powered venture tools.
• ORBucks – the currency of orbital civilization, facilitating secure transactions between module owners and partners.
• OREcycle – an integrated recycling module converting orbital debris into reusable stainless-steel ingots.

By merging engineering precision with visionary design, The Orbital Locker Company is establishing the backbone of the future space economy - one built on collaboration, sustainability, and long-term presence in orbit.

With the Orbital Locker Demonstrator scheduled for launch in 2026 and the first operational industrial facility targeted for 2027, we are transforming how humanity builds, operates, and thrives beyond Earth.

“The infrastructure of civilization no longer ends on Earth.”

Name: ASTROSPACIOUS
Description:
Welcome to ASTROSPACIOUS
 ASTROSPACIOUS is a student-led space research and education platform built out of pure passion for the stars. We’re a small team of curious minds working on independent projects, breaking down complex aerospace concepts, and creating content that makes space feel a little more reachable. We prov

Our goal is simple: to inspire, educate, and build a community where young people can explore the wonders of aerospace no matter where they’re from. We believe big dreams start small, and we’re here to turn curiosity into action.
 Built with curiosity. Driven by purpose. Aiming for the stars.

Mail us at: astrospacious.club@gmail.com

Name: West of Scotland Space Cluster
Description:
The West of Scotland Space Cluster is the UK's newest regional space cluster. As part of the UK Space Ecosystem developed as a result of the collaboration between the Satellite Applications Catapult and the UK Space Agency, the Cluster will focus on supporting the West of Scotland's space sector at a critical time in its evolution. 

Formed by the region's key sector players across industry, academia and economic development, the cluster's strategy will reflect the technological and industrial strengths of its members, with a mission to work collaboratively to ensure that the region helps shape and deliver on the UK's strategic priorities.

Name: Helogen Corporation
Description:
Helogen is a space infrastructure company that enhances life on earth and enables life in space. Our technological advancements help companies bring breakthroughs in medicine and materials to market faster than anyone else. Using biology in space, we focus on accelerating early stage drug discovery and development that would otherwise be impossible on Earth.

Helogen builds instrumentation that can grow, observe, and manufacture biological systems in alien conditions, leading to advancements in our understanding of novel mechanisms, geometries, and evolutions. This allows for more relevant physiological tissue models, accelerated biological processes, and the discovery of novel biologics. These instruments can be deployed on our own satellites, on space stations, and even celestial bodies. 

Our autonomous labs offer direct control from Earth, allowing for observation, construction, and engineering of biological systems in a reliable and predictable fashion. Product applications include Drug Discovery, Therapeutics, Oncology, Tissue Engineering, Engineered Biology, Agriculture, and more. 

Helogen stands at the cutting edge, harnessing the unique conditions of space to improve human health, planetary well-being, and commercial capabilities. 

You provide the science, we handle the rest.

Visit our website to see how you can fly with us: https://www.helogen.com.

Name: GeotecLab - CPMTC/IGC/UFMG
Description:
O Laboratório de Geotecnologias para Levantamento e Processamento de Dados em Geociências é associado ao Centro de Pesquisa Manoel Teixeira da Costa - CPMTC, órgão complementar ao Instituto de Geociências da UFMG. 
O GeotecLab desenvolve pesquisa, produtos e serviços na área das Geociências, com foco em levantamentos com a utilização de Aeronaves Remotamente Pilotadas (RPAS, VANTs, Drones). 
O GeotecLab desenvolve projetos de Ensino, Pesquisa e Extensão nas seguintes áreas: Cadastro Territorial Multifinalitário, Geodesign, Qualidade de Produtos Cartográficos, Projeto de Cidades Inteligêntes (Smart Cities) e Geomorfometria.

Name: EO4EU
Description:
EO4EU is a European Commission funded innovation project unlocking the potential of Earth Observation (EO) data through innovative tools, services, methodologies and approaches. 

The project promotes pre-operational European services like DestinE and will utilise existing platforms and services in a consolidated manner through the extensive use of disruptive technologies. It is assisting a wide spectrum of users from domain experts to simple citizens unaware of the plethora of data and capabilities offered by EO services, to access and process data and utilise the existing and future offered services.

Our deployable mesh reflector antenna design, using our unique electrostatic actuation technique, renders a heritage design far more agile & flexible for use cases in satellite communications and RF situational awareness from high orbits.

Name: Space Futures
Description:
We are a global center established by the Saudi Space Agency (SSA) and the World Economic Forum (WEF).  As part of the WEF's Centers for the Fourth Industrial Revolution (C4IR) network, the CSF is dedicated to advancing the global space economy through policy development, technological innovation, and strategic partnerships.


Vision
A global hub for a thriving, sustainable and inclusive space economy

Mission
To raise awareness about safeguarding and growing the space economy by empowering innovation and collaboration. This involves integrating best practices, developing advanced frameworks, fostering strategic alliances, and promoting sustainable practices to maximize the benefits of space for all stakeholders.

Name: Space Sweden North
Description: Space Sweden North is an innovation partnership between Luleå University of Technology, LTU Business, the Swedish Institute of Space Physics, SSC Swedish Space Corporation, Arctic Business Incubator. Space Sweden North is sponsored by European Regional Development Fund, the Swedish National Space Agency, Utveckla Norrbotten, Kiruna Municipality and Sparbanken Nord.

Name: Orbital Universe | شركة الكون المستدير
Description:
We believe in the power of earth observation to solve humanity's biggest problems, and our mission is simple: how can we leverage our experience to set new standards in accuracy and ROI in Saudi Arabia to drive growth and cut costs for customers.

Powered by QL Space, we utilise high-resolution satellite imagery, smart sensors, machine learning, AI, and edge computing to provide descriptive, diagnostic, and predictive analytics solutions tailored to the Saudi Arabian market. This allows our clients, across multiple industries, to make smarter and more timely decisions by leveraging data from space.

We work across a variety of applications in areas such as tree counting, blue carbon, urban heat monitoring, precision mining, sustainability, agriculture monitoring, and supply chain mapping — helping to equip our customers with the information they need to optimise, plan, monitor, model, design, and execute better business decisions and processes.

Name: Team Anveshak | IIT Madras
Description: We, Team Anveshak, are a team of 50+ passionate students from IIT Madras who live & breathe robotics. Since our beginnings around early 2016, we have spent countless days & nights building our remote-operated all-terrain rovers, complete with a robotic manipulator & digger. We recently celebrated our eighth anniversary & thus far, it’s been a pretty incredible journey.

Name: Space Gurion
Description:
Our Space & Rocketry community at Ben Gurion University in the Negev focuses on expanding knowledge about space and rocketry among university students as its first and foremost goal.
With the help of the best lecturers we have at our university, we intend to launch a brand new rocket every year for the purpose of research, as a massive engineering project for our community, compete in international competitions, organize hackathons and lectures with the biggest companies, and minds in the industry.
prepare yourselves for a smooth sailing and exciting ride!

Name: Cenital Space
Description:
At Cenital, we envision a world where resources are managed sustainably and efficiently, powered by cutting-edge space technology and IoT. our mission is to revolutionize the way businesses and industries operate, optimizing resource management and reducing operational costs, while promoting environmental stewardship and social responsibility.
 
Our core values are rooted in innovation, collaboration, and sustainability. We believe that by working together, we can unlock the full potencial of space technology to drive  to drive positive change and create a better future for all.
 
Join us in our vision to revolutionize resource management, and together, let's build a more sustainable, efficient, and connect the world.

Name: Orbital Lasers Co., Ltd.
Description:
Orbital Lasers is at the forefront of high-power laser solutions, driving a secure and sustainable space environment. 

Our advanced laser technology revolutionizes space debris removal with a non-contact method for detumbling and laser-based control, significantly lowering the complexity and cost of Active Debris Removal (ADR) while enhancing safety. We have developed the world’s most powerful and efficient compact space laser, delivering 10x the peak power of conventional space lasers with unmatched efficiency. Additionally, our cutting-edge Satellite LiDAR Services provide high-resolution global measurements for disaster prevention, infrastructure optimization, and environmental monitoring. Unlike traditional bulky and heat-intensive space lasers, our proprietary technology reduces satellite weight by 70% and costs by over 90%, making high-precision LiDAR mapping more accessible for small satellite constellations. By transforming space-based LiDAR with compact, efficient, and cost-effective solutions, we enable next-generation space applications, from Earth observation to deep-space exploration.

Description:

Name: Nord Space
Description: Nord Space provides end-to-end support for space projects, built on more than 30 years of experience in systems engineering & Software development, mission design, project management, and international collaboration. We help customers navigate every phase of a mission - from early concept development to successful operations in orbit.

Name: Astrostructures Ltd
Description:
As thermal and mechanical hardware-focused experts, we empower our clients to develop reliable, high-quality space instruments and subsystems. From requirements generation and concept development to qualification, acceptance, and hardware testing, we ensure every step is seamlessly executed.

Our approach is straightforward and driven by expertise. With a diverse skill set and a commitment to excellence, we offer end-to-end support throughout the entire lifecycle of your project.  From initial analysis and design to manufacturing, assembly, and full thermal and mechanical qualification, we are your trusted partner at every stage. By collaborating with specialist UK manufacturers, we deliver tailored solutions that meet the highest standards.

Our services include:
Analysis: Thermal, structural
Design: Thermal, mechanical, MGSE/TGSE
Manufacture & AIT Support
Assembly & Integration
Testing: Thermal, mechanical
Process Qualification for custom parts and processes
Partnership & Support in securing project funding

While our primary expertise lies in thermal and mechanical design for the space sector, our experience with extremely high and extremely low temperatures extends to other industries. We have a proven track record in the renewables/energy storage and medical sectors, as well as in scientific exploration and space applications.

Alongside hardware design and testing capabilities Astrostructures has excellent expertise in thermal and mechanical material behaviour, especially in extreme temperatures and hostile environments including thermal resistances across boundaries.

Name: Cluster Latinoamericano de Industria Espacial CLIE
Description:
El CLIE (Cluster Latinoamericano de Industria Espacial) surge de la iniciativa del CNIEM (Cluster Nacional de Industria Espacial de México). 

El CLIE actualmente realiza la integración, desarrollo y puesta en marcha de los ecosistemas nacionales de industria espacial mediante puntos nacionales de contacto. presentes en 10 paises de todo latinoamerica.
