Name: Space Aura
Description:
Space Aura is a space tourism and human spaceflight company from India for the world. 

Space Tourists will travel to the edge of space sitting inside a luxurious Spaceship SKAP1. Space tourists will witness the magnificent Earth & vastness of space traveling by a unique Space Balloon system. 

Space Aura Vision: Empower humanity through space missions, enable India to become multi-planetary, and build a prosperous and bright future.

An epic adventure is about to begin.

Name: Committee on Space Research - COSPAR
Description:
After the USSR opened he space age by launching its first Earth Satellite in 1957, the International Council of Scientific Unions (ICSU), now the International Science Council (ISC), established its Committee on Space Research (COSPAR) during an international meeting in London in 1958.

COSPAR promotes scientific research in space at an international level, with emphasis on the exchange of results, information, and opinions, and provides a forum, open to all scientists.  We endeavor to ensure that a vibrant international space research effort can be conducted without impediment from geopolitical tensions or differences.

In its first years of existence COSPAR, as an entity that ignores political considerations and views all questions solely from the scientific standpoint, played an important role as an open bridge between East and West for cooperation in space. When this role became less prominent with the decline in rivalry between the two blocs, COSPAR, as an interdisciplinary scientific organization, focused its objectives on the progress of all kinds of research carried out with the use of space.

Name: Erets Space
Description:
ERETS, headquartered in London with a growing presence in Australia and India, is the world’s first registered company to pioneer laser-based space debris mitigation. We are building the first cohesive system for detection, tracking, and mitigation of orbital debris, while advancing space situational awareness to ensure safer, sustainable use of space.

Through strategic academic and corporate collaborations across leading universities, research labs, and industry partners, ERETS combines expertise in Artificial Intelligence, Optics, Autonomous Robotics, and Advanced Propulsion. Our vision goes beyond technology—we are spearheading a movement to preserve the cosmos, shaping a responsible space future, and inspiring the next generation to dream beyond Earth.

Name: Involve Space
Description: We offer intelligent space balloons for Earth Observation and defence, flight validation and science. Discover all the use cases on our website!

Name: Omnidea
Description:
"Our mission is to contribute to the expansion of human society's frontier. Our society is a global system nowadays, through the universal laws of physics it tends to become homogeneous. Therefore, the only way to ensure a general growth is to keep constantly expanding the boundaries of our activity."

We have a multidisciplinary team of highly qualified professionals with comprehensive engineering backgrounds, who provide solutions to the challenges inherent to the aerospace industry, namely in the development of propulsion systems, airborne platforms and advanced manufacturing technologies.

Omnidea is part of Omnidea Group composed by Omnidea (PT), Omnidea-RTG (DE), Armilar (PT) and Pleione (GR), all dedicated to the aerospace industry.

Name: Planetek Hellas
Description:
We simplify the adoption of space data in order to understand the world better.
***
Planetek Hellas, a member of the D-Orbit Group, is a leading Greek company active in Earth Observation, geospatial intelligence, and software development for both satellites in orbit and their ground systems. 

Founded in Athens in 2006, the company combines deep expertise in satellite data exploitation, geoinformation systems, and participation in major ESA and European Commission programmes. 

Today, Planetek Hellas stands as a strategic pillar of the Greek space industry, contributing to Europe’s and Greece’s efforts to build the next generation of integrated space infrastructures and achieve operational autonomy in space. 

The company operates its own Satellite Command and Control Centre (C2) in Kifisia, Athens – a modern infrastructure serving as a national hub for satellite mission operations that supports both civilian and defence applications,

Name: The National Space Science and Technology Center
Description:
The National Space Science and Technology Center (NSSTC) was established in 2016 through the strategic support of UAE University and the UAE Space Agency, with a clear mandate to advance the UAE’s capabilities in Earth Observation and Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) technologies. NSSTC leads the design, development, and operation of advanced satellite missions that align with national priorities.

At the heart of NSSTC’s mission is a leadership-driven strategic mindset that focuses on bridging academia and industry accelerating innovation while ensuring the development of local expertise in high-technology sectors. The Center provides hands-on access to real space missions, empowering students, researchers, and engineers to contribute directly to mission success.

NSSTC also operates the UAE’s only comprehensive Assembly, Integration, and Testing (AIT) facility, enabling the country to build, test, and qualify space systems in-house. Flagship missions such as Satellite 813 and the LEONAV LEO-PNT demonstrator reflect NSSTC’s  growing role in building national capability and contributing to global space innovation.

Name: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)
Description: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is the national space agency of India. It operates as the primary research and development arm of the Department of Space (DoS), which is directly overseen by the Prime Minister of India while the Chairman of ISRO also acts as the executive of DoS. ISRO is primarily responsible for performing tasks related to space-based operations, space exploration, international space cooperation and the development of related technologies.

Name: Translational Research Institute for Space Health
Description: The Translational Research Institute for Space Health (TRISH) is a lean, virtual institute empowered by the NASA Human Research Program to solve the challenges of human deep space exploration. TRISH relentlessly pursues and funds novel research to deliver high-impact scientific and technological solutions that advance space health and help humans thrive wherever they explore, in space or on Earth. Led by Baylor College of Medicine's Center for Space Medicine, our consortium leverages partnerships with Caltech and MIT.

Name: Space Centre Australia
Description: Space Centre Australia is an integrated global space sector enterprise, with operations in Australia, the United Kingdom and the United States, soon to provide secure, permanently licensed facilities, technology and services to support the manufacture, maintenance, launch, flight and retrieval of space craft and their payloads into space for Private and Government clients.

Name: BioOrbit
Description:
BioOrbit is space manufacturing company developing hardware for the large-scale crystallisation of protein drugs. Our grand aim is to develop a pharmaceuticals factory in space within the next 10 years. We are dreaming big and making it happen. In-orbit demo early 2025.

Accelerators (most recent first)
- Airbus Accelerator Cohort 1 
- Seraphim Space Accelerator Mission 12
- ESA BSGN Life Sciences
- ESA BIC UK
- UK Space Agency LEO Accelerator 




If you are interested in learning more, please reach out to business@bioorbit.space

Name: Starpath
Description: Starpath designs and manufactures robotic propellant production factories for the Moon and Mars, reducing the cost of deep space transport, and enabling humans to be a multi planet species.

Name: Esper Satellite Imagery
Description: Esper Satellite Imagery is a Melbourne based startup focusing on collecting hyperspectral imagery from micro satellite constellations to supercharge industries with exploration data.

Name: IAC 2023
Description:
The premier event of the global space industry, the International
Astronautical Congress brings together the leading space players from
around the world and offers a perfect opportunity to join a vast network
of like-minded people.

Held annually, the IAC is the epicentre of the latest developments
and achievements in the sphere of space, the forge of the vision for
the future of the industry, and the hub where the most prominent
players across the industry get together to conjointly reflect on the
accomplishments and pave the way forward.

In 2023, the IAC is back to Baku exactly half a century after the capital
of Azerbaijan hosted it for the first time in 1973, becoming the first and
the only city in the region to do so. The 74th edition of IAC is coupled with
unique exposure to an emerging market, first-hand experience of the
unmatched Azerbaijani hospitality, genuine cultural immersion, and the
diverse charms of this beautiful country.

Name: AgriFi.AI
Description: AgriFi.AI is a cutting-edge AI-powered spacetech analytics platform, is dedicated to assisting businesses by providing them with impact-driven insights.

Name: Spacemanic
Description:
Spacemanic is a turnkey nanosatellite solutions provider and CubeSat components manufacturer.

The company focuses on the design, development, and testing of fundamental small satellite components based on the plug-and-play methodology.

Spacemanic's primary objective is to shorten the time needed for nanosatellite missions to move from the drawing board to space.

With a proprietary line of sub-systems, Spacemanic also aims to lower the costs of launching customer payloads; making space more accessible to scientific institutions, universities, and private customers with various budget sizes.

We make access to space as easy as ordering a cab!

Name: Space4Climate
Description: Space4Climate is a public-private-academic partnership working collaboratively to ensure a seamless supply chain for climate data from space. We support the UK’s world-leading climate community to deliver, sustain and make use of climate information from space, enabling it to be integrated “as standard” in a variety of climate services for global economic and societal benefit.

Name: ERATOSTHENES Centre of Excellence
Description:
A new, autonomous, and self-sustained Centre of Excellence, namely ERATOSTHENES Centre of Excellence of the Cyprus University of Technology (CUT) has been established through the ‘EXCELSIOR’, H2020 Widespread Teaming Phase 2 project. The newly established centre has been created as a result of upgrading the existing Remote Sensing and Geo-Environment Lab, which has been operating within the Department of Civil Engineering and Geomatics of the Cyprus University of Technology since 2007.

The vision of the ERATOSTHENES Centre of Excellence is to become a world-class Digital Innovation Hub for Earth Observation, space technology and Geospatial Information and to be the reference Centre in the Eastern Mediterranean, Middle East, and North Africa.
The Digital Innovation Hub will create an ecosystem which combines state-of-the-art remote sensing, data management and processing technologies, cutting – edge research opportunities, targeted education services and promotion of entrepreneurship.  In order to be dynamic and innovative, the Digital Innovation Hub will be based on two major infrastructures, which are a Satellite Ground Receiving Station and a Ground-based atmospheric remote sensing station. ERATOSTHENES Centre of excellence is paving the way for Cyprus to enter the space arena.

The Roo-ver Mission will see the ELO2 Consortium design, build, and operate a lunar rover on behalf of Australia, and with the Australian Space Agency. 

Our Australian-made rover, ‘Roo-ver’ – made possible with a $42 million Australian Space Agency grant – will roll across the Moon’s surface around the end of this decade. The Roo-ver Mission will foster new horizons in the Australian space sector, focusing on the development of essential services for lunar exploration missions. The mission will showcase and advance Australian extreme environment and robotics capabilities through the design of a cutting-edge, remotely operated, autonomous robotic asset on the lunar surface. By actively contributing to NASA's Artemis program, we are paving the way for an exciting future in space exploration.

We want to mine space resources from near-Earth asteroids to provide abundant, sustainable energy and resources in space and for Earth.

Asteroid mining has lived at the intersection of scientific research and popular culture for decades, with as many academic papers published as there are books, TV shows and movies about it. Access to resources, with its finite nature and disparate distribution of resources, plays an essential part in the geopolitical web of power and wealth. It has defined economic growth to such an extent that we have been using them as primary demarcation lines and identifiers in our history, from the stone to bronze and iron age to our current silicon age.

We believe the Regolith Age, powered by abundant space resources, is an inevitability that we can accelerate.

We support space hardware development and research by providing scientifically validated simulants that improve testing accuracy and give engineers and researchers confidence that their designs will perform in the harsh environments of the Moon, Mars, and beyond. Our materials help bridge the gap between laboratory testing and real extraterrestrial conditions, ensuring reliable results that advance human and robotic exploration.

For defense and national security applications, our simulants provide realistic environments to test and improve equipment and operational readiness for extreme conditions beyond Earth. As we expand into the solar system, accurate ground-based simulation is essential to ensure safety, reliability, and technological superiority.

We value our collaboration with educators and research institutions around the world to inspire and equip the next generation of scientists and engineers. Description:
Titan4 is committed to leveraging innovative earth observation downstream solutions to advance environmental stewardship and safety on a global scale. Building on our successful incubation period in the European Space Agency BIC program, Titan4 harnesses innovative technologies to process and analyze optical and radar satellite images with unparalleled speed, cost-effectiveness, and accuracy.

Our mission is to empower stakeholders to monitor and safeguard the health of our planet's ecosystems and infrastructure, providing actionable insights and timely information to inform strategic planning, risk management, and policy development. With the Titan4 platform, users can assess and monitor public and private infrastructures, land stability, structural deformations, seismic areas, and more. Our automatic InSAR data processing tool delivers near real-time results at a millimeter scale, complemented by data fusion features, visual and historical reports, trend analysis, and critical deformation alerts.

Key features of Titan4 platform include:
- Rapid and highly accurate results
- Flexible pay-per-use pricing model
- Scalable high-performance computing for large geographical areas
- Streamlined automation of all processing phases

At Titan4, we pride ourselves on our interdisciplinary team of telecom engineers, data scientists, and software architects. This collaborative approach ensures the robustness, reliability and innovation of our solutions, making Titan4 the preferred choice for both public and private sector clients seeking to leverage satellite technology for environmental monitoring and sustainability initiatives.

Join us in our vision to preserve our planet for future generations. Let's work together to unlock the full potential of satellite data integration and drive positive environmental impact.

Studying medical, life and health science in the environment of space offers a unique opportunity to uncover those critical components that are deemed essential for life, and sheds light on new pathways and discoveries of important and key genes. The Spaceomix team is positioned to make the next generation breakthroughs leading to a high quality of life for the next decades and at the same time pushing forward in making life become truly multi-planetary and ensure not just survival but also thriving of future generations of life beyond Earth.

Name: Archangel Lightworks
Description:
Archangel Lightworks is a laser communications company building high-volume, secure, wireless technology to create the space-enabled networks of the future.  

Today, space and terrestrial networks are not well-connected leading to insecure, insufficient, and vulnerable infrastructure. Nearly 3 billion people are unconnected and many more are under-connected. Subsea cables, which form the backbone of global network infrastructure, are at risk from climate disaster and bad actors. Links between satellites, which offer an opportunity for resilient connectivity, and the ground are reliant on radio, which is bandwidth constrained, finite and not secure.  

Archangel Lightworks was set up to solve the space-ground connectivity problem. Wireless laser communications (lasercom) technology offers new solutions; however, adoption of the technology has been slow due to availability concerns, lack of interoperability standards, difficulties associated with lasercom on the move, and high production costs. 

 Our tech solves these problems. 

We are driving down the size, weight, power & cost (SWaP-C) of optical ground equipment to make space-ground lasercom available and affordable. Our core product, TERRA-M is a miniature optical ground station which is approximately the size of a mini fridge. Compared to traditional networks such as radio, the TERRA-M allows more licence-free data transferred at low probability of detection (LPD) and low probability of interception (LPI). TERRA-M brings deployable, resilient connectivity to wherever it is needed, solving some of Earth’s most critical problems from economic inclusion to climate change. 

Archangel Lightworks is based in Oxford, UK.

In addition, New Space Portugal will create a digital platform called Digital Planet to merge data from multiple sources and provide value-added services.

Name: impulso.space
Description:
How do I launch a satellite?
Whom do I contact to secure a launch?
How do I manage logistics, integration, and coordination effortlessly?

Impulso.Space exists as the answer to these questions.

We provide satellite launch services that take missions from ground to orbit. Our role is to simplify access to space by handling launch procurement, logistics, integration, and mission execution, allowing satellite teams to focus on building their spacecraft while we manage everything required to get it safely to orbit.

Impulso.Space works closely with NewSpace missions and emerging satellite constellations that require responsive, reliable, and cost-conscious launch solutions. Through our integrated logistics and operational approach, we coordinate the ground and space segments of a mission into one coherent launch process.

Our operations are supported by facilities in Florida, near the Cape Canaveral launch site. From there, we provide cleanroom integration activities, payload offload and upload, fueling operations, safety oversight for hardware and personnel, and security operations. These capabilities enable us to deliver end-to-end launch services that are controlled, repeatable, and aligned with mission timelines.

You build the satellite. We get it to space.

From initial design to flight demonstration, every system is built to the highest standards, providing confidence and scalability for mission-critical applications.

By concentrating on a single high-value technical wedge, Infinity Space ensures that our capabilities are measurable, defensible, and directly aligned with the needs of primes and sovereign operators. We are building the future of secure space communications — today.

Name: Innspace
Description: We are a space collective bringing together people from various industries. We live in interesting times - in front of our eyes, humanity crosses new boundaries. Space is a fascinating topic but also a great challenge, and we want to bring it closer to a wider group!

Description:

16 habitats

Name: SpaceChain
Description:
SpaceChain aims to democratize access to space by removing entry barriers and creating opportunities for enterprises and users to participate in and benefit from the new space economy. Founded in 2017, SpaceChain is dedicated to building a space ecosystem that promotes trust, secures sensitive data and transactions, and safeguards the integrity of space-based activities, through championing inclusivity and embracing openness and transparency. The company currently operates a network of space nodes on satellites and aboard the International Space Station (ISS), empowering stakeholders to leverage the limitless possibilities of space while driving progress and prosperity for humankind for generations to come. 

Discord: https://bit.ly/3hjc9BO
LinkedIN: https://bit.ly/2HbemfD

Name: Frontier Space
Description: At Frontier Space we’re breaking down barriers to space; changing space from being the final frontier to your next horizon. We advance biotechnology developments by enabling biomanufacturing and R&D in space. Our team of space engineers and life scientists are industrialising on-orbit manufacturing through our in-space bioreactor platform and providing microgravity R&D service via our autonomous lab-in-a-box, SpaceLab.

Name: Nova Space Inc.
Description:
The Nova Space Workforce Development Program is the only solution in industry that provides the ability to on-board talent, both within and outside of the space industry, quickly and become competent across a wide range of space-related topics.

Designed for Business, Government, and Individuals, we are dedicated to delivering cutting edge solutions to space literacy to all levels of space professionals.

As the premier workforce development company dedicated to solving the talent challenges in the space industry, we specialize in upskilling and certifying space professionals, empowering them to advance their careers within the dynamic space sector.

Our award winning Nova Space Professional Course is built to decrease cost and increase learning and retention, all while being easily accessible to your entire staff. 

 3x Brandon Hall Award Winner

Name: Space Workforce For Tomorrow
Description: Space Workforce for Tomorrow (SWFT) is the premier initiative dedicated to closing the space industry’s growing STEM talent gap while driving the development of a highly skilled and mission-ready workforce. Powered by leading industry, government, and academic partners, SWFT is structured to INSPIRE, PREPARE, and EMPLOY the next generation of space professionals.

Name: Matter Intelligence
Description:
Matter specializes in advancing sensors and geospatial AI that capture "extreme-resolution" images of natural and artificial materials from space to surface, creating never-seen data of all matter globally. Our sensors simultaneously measure shape, composition, and temperature to understand and predict real-world events like never before.

Matter's data accelerates computer vision and contextual geospatial modeling to unlock the next generation of intelligence.

Name: International Space Federation (ISF)
Description:
The International Space Federation ISF is dedicated to advancing scientific understanding and technological progress in energy and gravity. The organization focuses on quantum vacuum research to develop sustainable energy solutions and contribute to solving global ecological challenges. By integrating diverse scientific disciplines ISF creates a unified framework that bridges the quantum and cosmic scales.

The ISF is committed to fostering global academic collaboration and promoting education in these critical scientific areas. The organization aims to unlock untapped technologies that address humanitys most pressing challenges. This work unites physicists engineers policymakers and students who are invested in a future shaped by scientific understanding and rigorous discovery.

Research with us. Study with us. Build the future with us.

Name: Space at Arizona State University
Description:
Space inspires and holds the promise of bounty for humanity

At Arizona State University, we believe a deeply transdisciplinary approach is needed to create the social and systems-level solutions that will pave our way as an interplanetary species. We bring together top researchers and scholars from across the university to work with our students and commercial partners, across sectors and disciplines to help us better understand and expand our reach into the universe.

Name: Sybilla Technologies
Description: Sybilla Technologies designs, builds, and operates robotic optical observatories for monitoring objects in space. Company specializes in the software for automated data acquisition, analysis and scheduling of networks of sensors, as well as designing and building turnkey observatories. We work in the domains of Space Situational Awareness, Space Surveillance and Tracking, Space Traffic Management, Near Earth Objects, stellar and planetary research, education, and commercial on-demand observations.

Name: SpaceCavez
Description: Empowering Access to Space in the MENA Region through Education, Manufacturing, and Ecosystem Development.

Name: National Space Society (USA) - Mumbai
Description:
This is an authorised chapter of National Space Society - Washington DC, USA. 

NSS Vision: “People living and working in thriving communities beyond the Earth, and the use of the vast resources of space for the dramatic betterment of humanity.”

NSS Mission: “to promote social, economic, technological, and political change in order to expand civilization beyond Earth, to settle space and to use the resulting resources to build a hopeful and prosperous future for humanity.

Name: LEAP
Description:
In today’s fast-moving space economy, orbital access is limited by long wait times and complex integration. LEAP is changing that. We’re transforming space logistics with a new generation of rapid-response, mass-producible solutions built for speed, simplicity, and reliability.

Using proprietary propulsion and modernized vehicle manufacturing, LEAP delivers dedicated, on-demand transport for national security and commercial payloads, as well as point-to-point space logistics that enable global delivery in under 45 minutes. Our mission: eliminate bottlenecks and provide scalable, cost-effective access to space when and where it's needed.

LEAP is built on three pillars: simplicity, responsiveness, and mass production. Our architectures inherently reduce complexity and ensure reliability. We support all time-sensitive missions, with repeatability and affordability.

From national security to commercial innovation, LEAP is redefining how, and how fast, we reach space. The future of responsive space logistics is here, and it moves at the speed of LEAP.

Name: Australasian Centre for Space Governance
Description:
The Australasian Centre for Space Governance (ACSG) is a not-for-profit that advocates for responsible laws, norms and policies on space activities, to foster sustainability, security and shared prosperity across the Indo and Asia-Pacific. It grew out of he Australian Centre for Space Governance. We focus on three areas of effort:

   1) Producing interdisciplinary research to solve national and global space governance challenges; 

   2) Serving the region's space law and governance needs (government, industry, and broader stakeholders); and,

   3) Engaging in outreach, advocacy, and education and training across various sectors to increase space literacy. 

To help achieve the above, we bring together academic experts in fields such as space law, governance, policy, science and technology studies, security, property, history, ethics, political, and social sciences.

We are committed to the values of interdisciplinary collaboration; safety, security and sustainability in space; diversity and inclusivity in the space sector; and ensuring the space sector incorporates Indigenous co-design and governance values to the use of land, sea and skies for space activities.

Name: Orbit NTNU
Description:
Orbit NTNU is a non-profit, volunteer student organization, stationed at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology. We design and build small satellites that we launch into space. Our aim is to create the next generation of space talent.

As a nonprofit student organization supported by sponsorship, Orbit NTNU selects our own missions with the purpose of learning and challenging space knowledge. We are in the unique position to be able to lauch payloads that would not be affordable on commercial options. Satellites are built with our in-house developed subsystems, hands-on experience and devotion. We set a new example by proving how volunteer students can spend their spare time booking a launch with SpaceX while gaining unique knowledge in the development of spacecraft.

Our current mission is the FramSat satellite. This is Orbit NTNU's first partner mission with our sponsor Eidsvoll Electronics AS aimed to test and verify their sun sensor. Orbit NTNU has tailored this mission system in-house and will take part in the historic first launch from European soil.

Our first mission, SelfieSat, is Norway's first operational student satellite. It is designed to take selfies from space equipped with a display screening and deployable selfie stick.

Our next mission will challenge us in ways not thought possible, pushing technology far beyond what has been done before. The BioSat Project will develop and construct the necessary systems needed to keep a plant alive in the harshest environment known to humanity.

Name: Icarus Robotics
Description:
Icarus is developing dexterous mobile robots to augment astronaut crews by handling routine, time-consuming, and hazardous tasks at a fraction of the cost of human labor—unlocking scalable commercial manufacturing and experimentation in space.

With design partnerships already established with leading commercial space destinations and NASA, we raised $6.1M in seed funding in just 12 days from top-tier investors. We're currently preparing for our first deployment to the ISS in partnership with one of our customers for full scale testing.

Our long-term vision is to become the leading space robotics company, enabling automated orbital factories and supporting lunar, Martian, and deep space exploration as the space economy rapidly expands.

Name: Interplanetary Initiative at Arizona State University
Description:
Interplanetary Initiative is a leading space center, creating private-public partnerships and driving our positive human space future for exploration by finding the key needs and filling them with interdisciplinary teams. 

Vision Statement
An interplanetary future built upon cooperative and inclusive new structures, systems and perspectives.

Our Mission
We deploy new ways of building teams and solving problems, at scale, partnered across disciplines, sectors and cultures to shape an inclusive and sustainable interplanetary future.

Name: NAASC Centre Spatial Universitaire de Nouvelle Aquitaine
Description:
NAASC est une association d’établissements d’enseignement supérieur de Nouvelle-Aquitaine, qui forment pour le domaine spatial civil et militaire.

Ses principaux objectifs :

Stimuler l’innovation en impliquant les étudiants dans des projets collaboratifs interétablissements, tout en leur permettant de découvrir l’importance stratégique du transport spatial ainsi que le haut niveau technologique des produits et la qualité de notre outil industriel.
Dynamiser le tissu industriel en développant des modules de formation initiale et continue au service du transport spatial.
Favoriser la dynamique intergénérationnelle et les vocations en créant un lieu de rencontre, de partage et de travail entre industriels, académiques et étudiants, véritable vitrine de la filière aérospatiale.
NAASC permet également de :

Favoriser une connaissance mutuelle des activités menées par les différents membres, et l’animation de la communauté ainsi rassemblée, de façon à développer des collaborations pertinentes plus étroites.
Favoriser le lien entre l’enseignement supérieur et les acteurs institutionnels et industriels du spatial.
Assurer au plan national et international une visibilité aux activités menées dans le cadre du NAASC à travers, en particulier, l’organisation de conférences, de séminaires et d’écoles d’été.

Name: Interplanetary Chamber of Commerce Inc. 501(c)6
Description:
The Interplanetary Chamber of Commerce (ICoC) is the premier global advocate for businesses venturing into space, fostering a sustainable space economy worldwide. We proudly represent businesses of all sizes, offering trusted support, partnerships, and networking opportunities. 

We have a services directory listing area for our clients to network - visit our directory is www.ichamberx.com to add your company. 

We conduct research, write white papers, develop policies ensure ICT standards with global authorities collaborate with leading universities, governments, and private sector. Develop UseCases with national security and civil space implications. Identifying products, sourcing partnerships, drafting ICT procurement tendering specifications for global supply chain and advisory.

As the inaugural and sole Interplanetary Chamber of Commerce, we are pioneering a new frontier, enabling industries to expand into space and enhance life on Earth while unlocking research opportunities for space habitation. Join us at the forefront of exploration as we venture beyond boundaries!

We enable industries to expand into space and enhance life on Earth while unlocking research opportunities for space habitation. Join us at the forefront of exploration as we venture beyond boundaries!

Name: The Spring Institute for Forests on the Moon
Description:
Our mission is to democratise space by enabling anyone, regardless of background or expertise, to contribute to the research, creativity, and collaboration required for humanity to thrive responsibly beyond planet Earth.
We support student-led experiments, public engagement, and interdisciplinary collaborations that explore how sustainable ecosystems could one day exist in space settlements.
Follow us for updates on our missions and projects.
Check out our website: https://thespringinstitute.com
Join our newsletters: https://thespringinstitute.com/join-our-newsletters/

Name: Mars Rover Manipal
Description:
Mars Rover Manipal is an interdisciplinary student project that aims to explore robotics and its application in interplanetary expeditions. 

The project MRM annually participates in international and national competitions, The University Rover Challenge, European Rover Challenge, International Rover Challenge, International Rover Design Challenge and the International Mars Hackathon,  organised by The Mars Society- The United States and South Asia since 2016.

We have six subsystems focused on acquiring competency in their respective sectors for efficient working.

The basic skeleton of the rover is provided by the Mechanical Design & Manufacturing subsystem, which includes the wheels, suspension, chassis, robotic manipulator, and the science module. 

The AI & Automation subsystem processes and interprets data from various sensors such as cameras, GPS, LiDARS, and IMUs. 

The Electronics & Controls subsystem is in charge of powering various segments of the rover, which work together to achieve the rover's desired performance in all tasks for effective control and communication.


The Life science subsystem is in charge of conducting a comprehensive analysis of biological, geological, and chemical features, based on which conclusions about the presence of extinct or extant life can be reached. 

The Research subsystem maintains a portfolio of projects driven by fundamental research and new product innovation. We work not only on great ideas but also implement, deploy, experiment, contribute to and learn from the world around us. We have an outstanding rate of publishing papers and have presented our work at some of the world's top conferences like NAACL and IEEE access.


The management & pr subsystem is responsible for ensuring proper management of the finances, public relations, and logistics of the project along with ensuring efficient communication within its members delegated into its technical subsystems.

Name: WarpWare
Description: WarpWare standardizes space software to reduce development time, cost, and risk and helps you get to space fast.

Name: SatLab AGH
Description: SatLab AGH is a student scientific organization whose goal is the construction of CubeSat nanosatellites at the AGH University of Science and Technology in Krakow. Our members come from many university faculties, jointly creating a technical and scientific background for real-life space missions. The first project implemented since 2022 is the creation of an observation satellite equipped with a hyperspectral camera.

Name: ethicallyHackingspace(eHs)

We develop Space Cybersecurity Operations and Resilience Platform Professional (SCOR-P2) capability solutions today to support the workforce requirements of tomorrow. We are the only SCOR micro-credentialing solution focused on #keepingspacesafe.

Name: Women in GMES and Africa
Description:
An initiative created by the Africa Union Commission (AUC) in the context of the GMES & Africa Programme.
Vision: Connecting the African woman to the opportunities and benefits of Earth Observation.
Mission:
Increase the number of women actively contributing, participating and benefitting from Earth Observation (across the continent) through awareness creation (sensitization) among women and girls, capacity enhancement opportunities and promoting gender-inclusion in the implementation of the GMES & Africa program and beyond.
Objective:
To strengthen the active involvement and participation of women in EO/Space;
To bring women together to share knowledge and best practices so they can become fully involved in implementing GMES & Africa and in Earth Observation (Space) initiatives and projects in general.

Name: iMETALX Inc.
Description: IMETALX's vision is to create a future where space is accessible and sustainable for all. Our mission is to provide in space domain awareness and in space servicing solutions for governments and businesses.

Name: Organization for Space Medicine, Engineering, and Design (OSMED)
Description:
We are OSMED.
The future of space will see more people going more places and doing more things. We are here to make human spaceflight safer, more productive, and inclusive of all.

The Organization for Space Medicine, Engineering, and Design (OSMED) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit founded to support the future of human spaceflight.

We are creating a global community to advance science communication and space literacy, to improve education and training, and to integrate best practices, both on and off planet.

Name: Xavier Space Solutions
Description:
Xavier Space Solutions was founded in 2019 with a single mission: build small & affordable satellites for all. 

We are the first private space startup company in Ghana and aim to lead the Ghanaian space industry. 

Apart from satellite R&D, we also provide services such as space science consulting, lessons (Astronomy, Coding, Electronics & Robotics), and more. 

We have a lot of experience working with people who love Space Science & Technology, hence we are able to approach each of our clients with fresh eyes to develop customized, unique strategies for all their problems.

Give us a call today and see what we can do for you.

Name: ELGRA (European Low Gravity Research Association)
Description:
The European Low Gravity Research Association (ELGRA) is a non-profit international society devoted to the promotion of scientific research under various gravity conditions in Europe. The organization, established in 1979, provides a networking platform for all scientists interested in life and physical sciences and technology in space or on ground. ELGRA aims at representing and strengthening the scientific community of gravity-related research and helping young scientists and engineers get involved in low- and hyper-gravity research through educational programs.

The members and the addressees of ELGRA are:

- Scientists at universities working in µg
- Scientists of non-educational research organizations working in µg
- Space science managers at national and international agencies (e. g. ESA, CNES, DLR)
- Flight opportunities and operations managers and engineers (e. g. ESA, NASA)
- Upcoming payload providers (e. g. Space X)
- Engineers and business developers of Space Industries (big and small ones)

Name: S[&]T Norway
Description:
S[&]T Norway specialises in bringing Earth Observation and Artificial Intelligence-technology to the market. We follow satellite instrument development and operations and provide instrument data simulators, satellite on-board processing and data processor pipelines.

In addition, we analyse satellite images for monitoring the environment by applying advanced machine-learning methods.

S[&]T's machine-learning activities include building computer vision models to monitor lakes on Greenland for the European Space Agency (ESA), classify drivers of deforestation in Indonesia for the World Resources Institute, and detect tree species for the Norwegian forestry industry.

We are a customer-tailored, agile research provider able to deliver a solution with:

-Short, frequent iterations.
-Transparency over the process.
-Control over the expenses.
-A low amount of paperwork/bureaucracy.

Name: NASA International Space Apps Challenge Arequipa
Description: Space Apps Challenge es una Hackethon organizada por NASA y respaldado por 13 Agencias  Espáciales en todo el mundo y está abierto a programadores, científicos, contadores de historias, inventores, diseñadores, artistas y a cualquiera con una pasión por el espacio. El evento se lleva a cabo de manera simultánea en todo el mundo y en él, durante 48 horas, los participantes forman equipos para desarrollar propuestas de solución a desafíos propuestos por la agencia espacial, idealizando recursos de acceso libre proporcionados por la misma.

Name: Space MarketPlace
Description:
Space MarketPlace is a pioneering platform dedicated to the space industry, connecting a global community of space professionals, enthusiasts, and businesses. Our marketplace offers a wide array of services and applications, ranging from satellite imagery and data analytics to space tourism and satellite launch services. 

We aim to democratize access to space by providing a seamless and secure platform for buying and selling space-related services, fostering collaboration, and accelerating innovation in the final frontier. Join us in shaping the future of space exploration and commerce.

Name: olee.space
Description: Olee is a space tech startup that delivers limitless, high speed and secure communications through its laser based satellite constellations and quantum level security architecture.

Name: Puerto Rico Space Foundation
Description:
Puerto Rico Space Foundation is a 501c3 non-profit organization established in April 12, 2023. It is a visionary endeavor aiming to revolutionize Puerto Rico's role in the global space industry. Born from the aspiration to create a vibrant educational and entrepreneurial environment, our foundation is dedicated to elevating Puerto Rico as the leading space hub in the Caribbean.

 Educational Excellence: At our core is the commitment to establish educational space programs. These initiatives are not just about imparting knowledge; they're about igniting a passion for space exploration and technology in the hearts and minds of our students.

 Talent Retention and Growth: Recognizing the wealth of local talent in Puerto Rico, we are steadfast in our efforts to provide opportunities that encourage our bright minds to flourish right here on the island. By nurturing this talent, we aim to strengthen our community and ensure that our local experts lead the way in space innovation.

 Collaboration and Promotion: Understanding the power of collaboration, the Puerto Rico Space Foundation is committed to fostering coordination among various space-related initiatives. Our goal is to create a synergistic ecosystem where ideas, resources, and expertise are shared not just within Puerto Rico but across the wider Caribbean region.

Join us in this exciting journey as we embark on a mission to make Puerto Rico a beacon of space exploration and innovation.

Follow us on:

With a focus on cutting-edge technologies, STEM education, and sustainable development, Stardust inspires the next generation through programs like Stardust Odyssey while driving advancements in space policy, habitats, mining, and more. Name: Réseau Consulting
Description: Our work is connected to the use of geospatial data, tools and services, including Earth observations to support nature and climate action. We do three things. Firstly, advise small companies on communications, market positioning and influencer strategy, as well as market research around geospatial topics. Secondly, we work with International Organisations on communications, stakeholder engagement and operational implementation of strategy. Finally, we facilitate an international group of women geospatial practitioners from the global south who are working on topics, such as climate justice, biodiversity preservation, climate action, food security, land degradation, urban development and water security.

Name: Space Denmark
Description: Space Denmark is a community and a global brand for the Danish space ecosystem. The membership-based organisation is dedicated to uniting and advancing Denmark’s space sector. It serves as a central hub connecting universities, companies, start-ups, government bodies, and enthusiasts across the space ecosystem. Space Denmark fosters collaboration through networking events, international delegations, and industry insights, aiming to position Denmark as a key contributor to the global space value chain. With a focus on emerging technologies, science, and research, the organisation supports its members by amplifying their impact and showcasing Denmark's capabilities on the world stage.

Name: Uzcosmos
Aerospace Team Graz
Description:
We, the Aerospace Team Graz are an Austrian, interdisciplinary team of approximately 90 students with the goal of developing our own rocket. With this goal in mind, we take part in international competitions every year, such as the European Rocketry Challenge (EuRoC) in Portugal. 

We are engaged in the development and construction of rockets and rocket components, participation in international competitions and we accomplish research and development in the aerospace sector (bachelor and master theses). Additionally, we cooperate with technical colleges (Cubesat projects) and hold internal team-building events.

In October 2021, our team took part in the European Rocketry Challenge in the category 3km self-developed solid propellant. We were able to bring the Technical Award for the best technical documentation to Graz with our AVES rocket. This year we hope to be even more successful! As we are already working on our new rocket and are preparing for the upcoming events. 

To be updated about our progress and new advancements, do not hesitate to sign up for our newsletter, follow us on Instagram, or contact us via our website.

Name: Odisha Space Applications Center (ORSAC)
Description:
Odisha Space Applications Centre (ORSAC), the apex body of the State of Odisha for space technology applications, was established in the year 1984. The Centre is located at Bhubaneswar in its own building at Chandrasekharpur. The Centre is equipped with sophisticated GIS & Computer laboratories with latest GIS and Image software. It has a team of well-experienced multidisciplinary application scientists to undertake Remote Sensing, GIS & GPS based projects. In the field of communication technology application, the center runs GRAMSAT & EDUSAT programme. GRAMSAT includes interactive training programme, production & telecast of developmental programmes in fiction based drama format, documentary format as well as news magazine format. EDUSAT includes telecast of live class room programmes and enrichment programmes for the benefit of High School students.
Remotely Sensed data from Earth Observation Satellites, in conjunction with field observations and collateral data, facilitates generation of information for decision making in a time and cost effective manner for scientific management of our natural resources, for monitoring the environment and ecosystems as well as for assessment of damage due to natural disasters. To give substance to the above capability of the technology and to further the goals of Indian Space Programme, ORSAC has defined its objectives and relentlessly attempts to satisfy the same in the context of the needs of the state.

Name: TOLOSAT
Description: TOLOSAT is a student nano-satellite Project. It gathers more than 70 students from Toulouse from 5 different schools: ISAE-SUPAERO via its Cubesat Club Supaero, and INSA, ENAC, ENSEEIHT, and Paul Sabatier University UT3 through the ASTRE association. Organized into various subsystems teams, we developing a 3U Cubesat, which will carry a dual student mission. The first will be to conduct a study of Earth's gravitational field, which represents a significant challenge. The second mission will demonstrate the technology of using a satellite constellation to communicate with a Cubesat in low orbit. This ambitious project is led by students passionate about space. To learn more, meet us, or join the adventure by offering any kind of help, do not hesitate to contact us.

Name: Mission - ShakthiSAT
Description: ShakthiSAT ShakthiSAT is a groundbreaking global mission that unites 12,000 young girls from 108 countries to build a lunar satellite collaboratively. This inspiring initiative is more than just a space mission—it's a movement to empower the next generation of female leaders in science, technology, and space exploration. By breaking down barriers and fostering international collaboration, ShakthiSAT is shaping a brighter future both on Earth and in space.

Description: Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre

Name: baüne
Description:
At Baüne, we are pioneers in space medicine and technology, dedicated to addressing preventable diseases and health disparities from Space to Earth, and from Earth to Space.

Our mission is to develop innovative healthcare solutions that ensure the well-being of astronauts during deep space missions while improving health outcomes for people on Earth. Our flagship technology, a powerful state-of-the-art integrated medical system of systems (SoS), provides flexible and real-time personalized care, ensuring a continuous care continuum that enhances the safety and success of missions. The Baüne Team is unique, comprising passionate, talented, and driven individuals who form a diverse and integrated team. We not only have the resources but also the expertise to achieve our goals. Join us on our journey to revolutionize healthcare from the depths of space to the heart of our communities.

Name: Global Expert Group on Sustainable Lunar Activities (GEGSLA)
Description:
The Moon Village Association (MVA) established the Global Expert Group on Sustainable Lunar Activities (GEGSLA) to increase coordination mechanisms, identify present and future challenges of lunar missions, and de-risk future operations. Its vision is to enable globally inclusive participation in this next stage of human endeavors in space. For this reason, the GEGSLA has engaged widely with lunar stakeholders from industry, government, and academia. 

The GEGSLA first phase operated between February 2021 and December 2022 in order to develop the “Recommended Framework and Key Elements for Peaceful and Sustainable Lunar Activities.” This report was presented to the general public during the 60th Session of the United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (UN COPUOS) Scientific and Technical Subcommittee.

Since January 2023, the GEGSLA is in its Operational Phase. The goal of this phase is to promote dissemination and implementation of the Recommended Framework produced by the Group, with special focus on discussions conducted within the UNCOPUOS, as well as to exchange information on ongoing lunar missions. The support to these discussions is possible since MVA has permanent observer status within COPUOS. It also aims to gather feedback and input from stakeholders, such as industry representatives, academics, and NGOs.

Name: The Mars Society of Canada
Description:
𝗢𝗨𝗥 𝗩𝗜𝗦𝗜𝗢𝗡: The Mars Society of Canada (MSC), is committed to developing a multi-planetary future for humanity through the exploration and inhabitation of Mars. Establishing a permanent human presence on Mars is not only a logical objective, it is pivotal in the future of human life and is arguably one of the most important things that our species will ever do.  It will bring profound benefit to humanity, to our home world, and to the biosphere that raised us.  This will ultimately inspire and guide human societies into a new era of sustainability, environmental responsibility and peaceful coexistence.

𝗢𝗨𝗥 𝗠𝗜𝗦𝗦𝗜𝗢𝗡
As a volunteer and membership-based organization, the MSC will help build the foundations for humanity’s leap into space and support settlement activities on Mars once it arrives. To support Canada’s participation in the future establishment of communities on Mars, we are committed to the development  of educational programs, research & innovation projects and analog studies for the benefit of Canada and everyone who shares our vision.  We are dedicated to the values of diversity, inclusion and sustainability and will continue to conduct outreach programs in Canada through the development of local chapters, the staging of conferences and workshops and by forging active relationships with interdisciplinary organizations in academia, industry and the government.  In cooperation with our local chapters and chapters of the Mars Society throughout the world, we will develop and sustain a knowledge-exchange network that is capable of engaging our general membership, the public and space professionals with the evolving challenges of building sustainable communities on Mars with the lessons learned in the terrestrial ecosphere.

𝗛𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘂𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘇𝗲 𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗯𝘆 𝗯𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮 𝗺𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝘁𝗼𝗱𝗮𝘆: https://www.marssociety.ca/become-a-member/

Name: Mirai Aerospace
Description:
At Mirai Aerospace, we're dedicated to transforming the geospatial industry with our cutting-edge solutions. We offer a full suite of aerial, terrestrial, and mobile LiDAR scanning systems, paired with top-notch software and services. Our focus is on providing hardware for geospatial data acquisition and delivering high-performance, reliable data collection, processing, and application services.

We’re particularly known for our expertise in oblique photography, a specialty that has earned us many satisfied customers. Our product lineup includes half-frame, full-frame, and medium-format orthophotography cameras, along with advanced oblique photography cameras. By partnering with leading innovators and incorporating the latest advancements in remote sensing technology, Mirai Aerospace is committed to delivering precise, high-resolution data that drives success for our clients.

Name: LYRASENSE
Description:
Empowering decision-makers to rapidly build and deploy geospatial intelligence applications at scale.

In a rapidly evolving world, scientists, frontline defense operators and decision-makers face mounting challenges - ranging from natural disasters like floods, oil spills and wildfires to critical security and defense missions. Current geospatial tools often fall short, limiting their ability to act decisively. LYRASENSE combines cutting-edge agentic AI and modern software architecture to eliminate technical barriers, enabling users to build and deploy geospatial intelligence applications with precision and speed.

Name: SeRANIS - Mission
Description: SeRANIS "Seamless Radio Access Networks for Internet of Space" is the world's first and only small satellite mission to provide a publicly accessible multifunctional experimental laboratory in orbit.

Name: Celestial Citizen
Description: Celestial Citizen is a platform for promoting a more equitable and just vision of planetary settlement beyond Earth. This organization seeks to provide a medium for conversation and discourse around how to be a better interplanetary citizen and responsible steward of Earth at the same time. Key initiatives include making space exploration and settlement planning a diverse and inclusive enterprise, while promoting sustainability across the solar system. Celestial Citizen aims to solve many challenges facing Earth today with technologies and solutions derived from the space industry.

Name: APJ AeroASTRO
Description:
A new space age is emerging and the extraordinary opportunities in space sector is emerging rapidly. 
Welcome to APJ AeroASTRO! 
APJ AeroASTRO is a dynamic community of scientists, engineers, and students from around the world who are passionate about advancing space science, aerospace, and related industries. Established in 2022, we are a research and development startup committed to pushing the boundaries of innovation in aerospace and atmospheric sciences.
Our mission is to foster collaboration and knowledge exchange within the global space community. We organize conferences and invite eminent personalities to share their expertise, providing valuable insights and networking opportunities for our members.
In addition to our conference series, we actively participate in hackathons, events, and international projects. Through these initiatives, we leverage our collective skills and expertise to address real-world challenges and drive forward the forefront of space exploration.
At APJ AeroASTRO, we believe in the power of innovation and collaboration to propel humanity's understanding of the cosmos. Join us on our journey as we explore the depths of space and pioneer breakthroughs in aerospace and atmospheric sciences. Together, we are shaping the future of space technology.

Name: SME4SPACE
Space Society of Kenya
Description:
The Space Society of Kenya is a registered membership-based organization committed to promoting space science, education and technology across the country. We unite students, professionals, researchers and enthusiasts who share a vision of strengthening Kenya’s role in the global space landscape.

Leveraging our strategic position near the equator, we’re building an inclusive and vibrant space ecosystem through educational outreach, community engagement and practical projects.

As a member, you’ll gain access to exclusive opportunities such as workshops, webinars, mentorship and hands-on experiences in astronomy, satellite technology, GIS, aerospace, software engineering and more.

You’ll also engage in impactful initiatives like satellite development, space data analysis and stargazing events, while networking with industry leaders and contributing to space policy and international collaboration.

Whether you're just getting started or already experienced, the Space Society of Kenya invites you to join and be part of the country’s growing space movement.

 Apply for membership:  
https://linktr.ee/spacesocietyofkenya

Name: Parametry.ai
Description:
We are self-hosting solutions makers, for your AI, for your Blockchain or your strawberry logistic service, anything that run on a computer. We are also space engineers, and because we did not like the cloud as it is, we made it better and simpler, yes! An independent greener European cloud infrastructure (above the clouds, but not above GDPR). We are also AI researchers and developers, so we use it for you, and for us, from cybersecurity to novelty detection, for your DORA compliance.

Parametry.ai is well known to apply blockchain and AI to shift space traffic management into more transparency, trust and automation. Almost everything depends on our services in orbits, we'd rather not have them collide because someone could not speak Chinese, Russian or proper English. A lot of things are happening in orbit, above our heads. With more than 50 000 tracked objects, from different governments, orbiting the Earth, we need a global collaboration, by nature decentralized. We want to avoid what could become a huge space traffic jam and turn our beautiful Earth orbit into a giant colliding junk yard obstructing our beautiful view of the stars.

"Because we could not reach consensus" !?

Parametry.ai sets up a decentralized autonomous organization of space (a Space DAO) where satellite operators, space authorities, external AI services and autonomous space assets can interact to assess situations and use incentives to fasten decisions. The Space DAO also helps a more proactive management of disaster and emergency response and is foreseen to serve as a market place and as an ecosystem of space faring stakeholders.

All details and participation here: https://spacedao.ai

For our cloud go there: https://celestical.net
Celestial (for the space stuffs) and Analytical (for things we make the machines do)

Name: IAU CPS
Description: The mission of the IAU CPS is to coordinate efforts and unify voices across the global astronomical community with regard to the protection of the dark and quiet sky from satellite constellation interference. The CPS brings together astronomers, satellite operators, policy makers and the wider community and acts as a bridge between all stakeholders to protect the dark and quiet skies. The CPS builds on the vast amount of work carried out by the two host institutions and by the astronomy community in general, recognising the various interests of different observatories according to wavelength, existing regulations, and expected impact.

Name: Da Vinci Satellite
Description: The Da Vinci Satellite is a student team from the faculty of Aerospace Engineering at the TU Delft working on an educational satellite project. The main goal of the project is to enthuse and inspire children (primary/high school) in the Netherlands about space and engineering by giving them a hands-on experience with real satellite data provided by a satellite that is built and tested by ourselves. If you would like to read more about our project, we encourage you to visit our website! For further questions, you can always contact us at davincisatellite@tudelft.nl

Name: Venturi Space
Description:
From 2000 to 2021, the Monegasque company Venturi established itself in the field of high-performance electric mobility: world records, expeditions in extreme environments, Formula E, technological innovations, and iconic two- and four-wheeled vehicles.

Since 2021, Venturi Space (Monaco-Switzerland-France) has expanded this expertise to space exploration by designing mobility solutions for the Moon and Mars. A strategic partner of the North American company Venturi Astrolab, the company is developing critical technologies—hyper-deformable wheels, high-performance batteries, and battery management systems—for Venturi Astrolab's lunar rovers, FLIP and FLEX.

In 2025, Venturi Space unveiled MONA LUNA, a 100% European lunar rover designed to support the ambitions of the European Space Agency (ESA) and the French Space Agency (CNES). Encouraged by these agencies for its innovative and agile approach, Venturi Space is developing the astromobile within its Toulouse-based entity.

Name: Chair of Space Geodesy, ETH Zurich
Description:
The Chair of Space Geodesy will face the challenges and profit from the opportunities that come with “big data” in geodesy. We will design and implement advanced machine learning techniques to automate the processing of GNSS and VLBI data, resulting in a faster availability and higher quality of geodetic products. Also, machine learning will be applied in the large-scale analysis of GNSS time series to detect and investigate geophysical signals, inter alia related to hydrological and seismic events, as well as to severe weather events and ionospheric disturbances. Finally, we aim to improve the prediction of several types of geodetic parameters by machine learning. In particular, we will focus on determining future Earth orientation parameters, station coordinates and atmospheric parameters.

Prof. Dr. Benedikt Soja

Description:

Name: Destination Earth (DestinE)
Description:
Destination Earth (DestinE) is an ambitious initiative of the European Union to create a highly accurate digital model of Earth. It utilises an unprecedented amount of data, innovative earth system models, Artificial Intelligence (AI), cloud computing, high-speed connectivity networks and data from multiple existing and new sources. Europe's cutting-edge computing is utilized to monitor the effects of natural and human activity on our planet, enable users to anticipate extreme events and test and adapt policies addressing climate-related challenges.

The initiative is jointly implemented by three entrusted entities: the European Space Agency (ESA), the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT) over the next 7-10 years.

Name: Sanctuary On The Moon
Description:
Sanctuary is a compact collection of sapphire discs engraved with billions of pixels describing who we are, what we know and what we make as humans.

It will land on the Moon, as part of the NASA Artemis program, within the next few years and stay on the surface for the millions of years to come - making it a unique philosophical & anthropological journey and a never-seen-before opportunity for humankind to protect and transmit its heritage to future generations.

 Sanctuary est une capsule temporelle composée de disques de saphir gravés avec des milliards de pixels. Sa mission : décrire qui nous sommes, ce que nous savons et ce que nous faisons en tant qu'humain.  

Elle sera posée sur la surface lunaire dans le cadre du programme NASA Artemis et y restera pour des millions d'années à venir. Un voyage philosophique et anthropologique unique pour l’éternité. Une opportunité sans précédent pour l'humanité de protéger et transmettre son héritage aux générations futures.

Name: LISA Community
Description:
The Laser Interferometer Space Antenna (LISA) is a large-scale space mission designed to detect one of the most elusive phenomena in astronomy – gravitational waves. This space-borne Gravitational Wave Observatory will have an arm-length of 2.5 million km, compared to the few km’s of the ground-based observatories.

LISA will observe the entire universe and discover parts that are invisible by other means. It will enhance our knowledge about the beginning, evolution and structure of our universe. LISA will complement earth-bound gravitational wave and other astronomical observatories, and significantly enrich multimessenger astronomy.

The LISA Consortium is a large international collaboration that combines the resources and expertise from scientists in many countries all over the world. Together with ESA, its member states, and NASA, the LISA Consortium is working to bring the LISA Mission to fruition.

Name: BEYOND Centre IAASARS/ NOA
Description:
BEYOND Centre of Earth Observation Research & Satellite Remote Sensing

At BEYOND, we are committed to harnessing the power of space-based technologies to address real-world challenges and serve the needs of European citizens. As a leading Centre of Excellence, we translate cutting-edge remote sensing science into actionable, operational services that support informed decision-making across sectors.

Our Mission
To deliver authoritative, user-driven Earth observation services by understanding stakeholder needs, maximizing the value of satellite data, and supporting evidence-based policy and planning.

Our Vision
To be a European hub of excellence in satellite remote sensing, advancing scientific innovation and transforming mature research into impactful, operational applications for society.

Name: ATTX

Name: INAF-Osservatorio Astronomico di Brera
Description:
L’attività scientifica dell’OAB copre diversi ambiti dai pianeti alle stelle e galassie, dagli ammassi di galassie alla cosmologia fino allo sviluppo tecnologico di strumentazione astronomica.
Un aspetto specifico della ricerca all’OAB è l’astronomia delle alte energie.
Dal punto di vista scientifico osservazioni BeppoSAX, XMM-Newton, Chandra, Swift, Rossi-XTE e INTEGRAL, unitamente all’utilizzo di dati d’archivio dalle precedenti missioni X, hanno permesso la realizzazione e la partecipazione ad importanti progetti.
I principali oggetti celesti studiati sono stelle di neutroni e buchi neri nella nostra galassia, buchi neri massicci al centro di galassie (AGN), plasma caldo stellare, galassie ed ammassi di galassie. Dal punto di vista teorico si studia il plasma relativistico attorno ai buchi neri ed in particolare i getti relativistici ed i Gamma-Ray Burst (GRB).
Dal punto di vista tecnologico il gruppo di tecnologia che sviluppa gli specchi è riconosciuto come uno dei gruppi leader in questo settore.
OAB sta inoltre partecipando all’implementazione di nuovi progetti spaziali per astronomia delle alte energie come Simbol-X (la prima missione per raggi X duri basata su ottiche focalizzanti e sull’architettura satellitare di volo in formazione), XEUS (unica missione di astrofisica Large Size selezionata dall’ESA per lo studio di fase A nell’ambito del programma Cosmic Visions), SVOM, HXMT e Polar-X.
Inoltre sta partecipando alla realizzazione dei telescopi Cherenkov da terra MAGIC, per raggi gamma di altissima energia, avendo la responsabilità della produzione di parte degli specchi.
Per quanto riguarda l’astronomia ottica, il gruppo stellare dell’OAB si dedica soprattutto allo studio per la comprensione delle pulsazioni stellari e alla ricerca di pianeti extrasolari.

Name: IAC2026
Description:
Turkish Space Agency (TUA), and SAHA İstanbul Defense & Aerospace Cluster are proud to invite you to the 77th International Astronautical Congress, IAC, which will take place in Antalya in October 2026. As the premier event for the global space industry, the International Astronautical Congress (IAC) brings together leading space stakeholders from around the world and serves as an ideal platform to bring together everyone related space industry. 

IAC 2026 is an essential event for the international space community, and we are thrilled to welcome over 10,000 participants to Antalya, the capital of tourism. Türkiye, having established its National Space Program with the vision of becoming a significant space nation in the international arena and a key regional space industry player, offers a unique opportunity to access an emerging market, showcase your brand, and raise awareness. 

We also believe that this important and high-profile event will serve as a significant milestone for the National Space Program being pursued by our country, as well as for raising awareness of the evolving space ecosystem that is developing in parallel. At IAC 2026, attendees and exhibitors will explore the latest developments in the space industry and experience the renowned Turkish hospitality amidst the breathtaking beauty of Antalya. 

On behalf of the host, the Turkish Space Agency, and co-host, SAHA Istanbul Aerospace Cluster, we are delighted to extend an invitation to you to take place in this exceptional event and have unforgettable memories at IAC 2026. We believe that IAC 2026 will be one of the keystones in the IACs history and one of the most remembered one in the space community's memory. Moreover, this event will create unique opportunities to build strong collaborations and networks. 

We look forward to your valued support to make the next year’s space celebrations in Antalya truly unforgettable!

Name: ASSC - Astrommar Space Systems Co., S.A.E
Description:
ASSC - Astrommar Space Systems is an Egyptian joint-stock company specializing in providing innovative and integrated solutions in the space sector. Its expertise includes the design and development of space systems, as well as space monitoring and surveillance services, such as tracking space debris, space weather, celestial objects, and other space assets. With extensive experience in advanced technology applications, the company delivers cutting-edge technological solutions to help organizations and institutions effectively monitor and analyze the space environment, with a strong focus on enhancing space research sustainability and strengthening space security.

Astrommar is supported by the Academy of Scientific Research and Technology (ASRT) of Egypt, positioning it as a key partner alongside the Egyptian Space Agency (EgSA). This backing enhances the company’s ability to develop advanced space solutions and supports its vision of achieving technological progress. Additionally, Astrommar is one of the companies incubated by the Egyptian Space Agency’s business incubator, providing it with an ideal environment for growth through access to resources, training, and mentorship. This enables the company to expand its operations and contribute to Egypt Vision 2030.

With a deep understanding of satellite operators' needs, Astrommar specializes in tracking satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), accurately mapping their trajectories, and identifying potential collisions with space debris. By providing precise and reliable data, the company empowers satellite operators to adjust flight paths and prevent costly damages, ultimately enhancing global space safety.

 Why Astrommar?

 Exceptional Coverage – Precise space monitoring and risk assessment.
 Unmatched Speed – Instant response and advanced data analysis.
 Limitless Insights – Accurate information shaping the future of space.

 Innovating for a Safer Space.

Name: Colorado Space Grant Consortium
Description:
About Us
The Colorado Space Grant Consortium is a state-wide program that provides Colorado students access to space through innovative courses, real-world hands-on satellite programs, and interactive outreach programs. We are funded by NASA as part of National Space Grant Program. There are 52 consortia in the National Space Grant program, with one consortium in each state, in addition to Puerto Rico and Washington, D.C.

Our vision:
Providing an inclusive and interdisciplinary community of Colorado students with experiences in space to prepare them for our Nation’s future space programs.

Our mission:
COSGC uses the excitement of our Nation’s aeronautics and space program to inspire, educate, and develop America’s future technological workforce by enabling an inclusive and interdisciplinary community of college and university students to participate in space-based hands-on projects, courses, research, and public service activities.

The COSGC involves 21 colleges, universities and institutions around Colorado. Each year COSGC engages over 300 college and university students. Many of our members teach space-related courses.

The COSGC headquarters is located in the Discovery Learning Center on the campus of the University of Colorado at Boulder. Each affiliate institution has a Director who oversees student programs on their home campuses, in addition to faculty, industry, and community mentors..

Name: CREATION-SPACE
Description:
CREATION-SPACE is a global innovation hub that accelerates the development of dual-value space exploration technologies to address humanity's most pressing challenges.

We are deeply committed to empowering startups and entrepreneurs, guiding them from their early development stages, through launch, and into expansion.
Our support encompasses cutting-edge methodologies, technical engineering expertise, access to research facilities and test sites, as well as funding opportunities through grants and connections with our partnered VCs and investors

Name: Space Workforce Incubator for Texas (SWIFT)
Description:
As a 501(c)(3) non-profit, SWIFT quarterbacks Texas’ space workforce training ecosystem—connecting high schools, community colleges, universities, and industry to eliminate duplication, maximize resources, and unleash untapped talent. At SWIFT, we don’t just build rockets; we build connections.


The booming commercial space market is fueling unprecedented demand for a skilled workforce to fill a variety of high-paying jobs throughout Texas. Great progress has been made to inspire young Texans through STEM pathways and engaging rocketry courses, while universities like UT and TAMU are leading the way with advanced engineering programs.


Yet, there remains a critical gap in accessible aerospace workforce training programs at the community college level. SWIFT is bridging that gap, creating multiple entry points for Texans of all backgrounds. By scaffolding skills and upskilling workers from other industries, we’re ensuring more people can take part in the thriving space economy.


SWIFT unites rocket designers and builders, fostering seamless collaboration across educational and technical programs. Through shared testing platforms and hands-on experience in liquid rocket hardware, we give students the opportunity to dream, build, and launch.

SWIFT: Build. Test. Fly.
By emphasizing hands-on design, fabrication, and testing of liquid rocket hardware, we ensure that graduates possess practical skills that meet the immediate needs of the defense and aerospace sectors.

We then streamline the process for organizations to access and onboard these exceptionally qualified individuals.
