Name: ESA Technology Broker United Kingdom
Description:
Becoming the ESA Technology Broker in the UK, as part of the ESA ScaleUp programme, underscores TWI’s commitment to driving innovation and fostering collaboration, both within the space industry and between space and non-space sectors.

The overall programme of work is split into two main parts:


 Technology Brokerage: This part of the work will see TWI support and promote activities between space and non-space entities, including match-making between interested organisations.


 Prepare for Space: This technology transfer programme will allow non-space companies to apply for business and technical support, lowering the barrier of entry to the space industry.

Our aim, as the ESA Technology Broker in the UK is focused on building an ecosystem that fuels technical innovation and mutual growth between terrestrial and space industries to create a collaborative environment that delivers tangible outcomes. 

These collaborative, commercial activities will create a bridge between different industry sectors to share knowledge and information, stimulating non-space companies to enter the space industry as well as leveraging space technologies for terrestrial applications.

‘Prepare for Space’ has been created to support UK companies in finding commercial opportunities in the space industry by providing dedicated business case development training, supply chain understanding, and routes to source funding for projects. 

This aspect of the project will also facilitate on-boarding for non-space companies into ESA programmes, providing information and training on ESA opportunities, technology transfer and calls.

Name: Red Balloon Aerospace
Description:
Red Balloon Aerospace Pvt. Ltd., headquartered in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, is a deep-tech startup in stratospheric mobility with sustainable, AI-powered lighter-than-air platforms. Founded by leaders with experience at ISRO, Skyroot Aerospace, HAL, and academia, the company is developing three flagship platforms - VISTA, Versatile Intelligent Stratospheric Asset (high-altitude super-pressure balloons for persistent intelligence, communication relays, and earth observation), ALTIS, Advanced Long-Endurance Tethered Intelligence System (advanced long-endurance tethered aerostats for border surveillance, maritime domain awareness, and disaster response), and HELIX, Hydrogen-Elevated Logistics Infrastructure eXpress (hydrogen-lift cargo airships enabling sustainable long-range logistics with payloads up to 20 tons). Together, these modular, interoperable platforms fill the critical gap between drones with limited endurance and costly satellites, providing persistent operations in the 1 to 50 km near-space domain. Complementing the platforms, the Data Inference & Visualization Engine (DIVE) transforms stratospheric data into actionable intelligence for defense, disaster relief, agriculture, climate monitoring, and infrastructure planning. 
	•	VISTA - High-altitude super-pressure balloons operating in the 20–50 km stratosphere for persistent earth observation, science payloads, and communication relay.
	•	ALTIS - Long-endurance tethered aerostats for continuous surveillance, communication, and data-relay along fixed or semi-fixed points.
	•	HELIX - Medium-to-heavy-lift hydrogen airships (20-ton class) for sustainable cargo transport, long-range logistics, and strategic payload delivery.

Name: Blue Dot Solutions
Description:
We turn satellite data into business solutions

Here at Blue Dot Solutions we aim to foster the development of space industry in Poland and Europe. We create our own services, systems and applications. We support companies in their first steps in the space industry. Thanks to interdisciplinary team of experts and presence in numerous industrial events in Poland and abroad, we have first-hand insights into all the latest developments in the space sector.

We are a Polish company specializing in the following space technologies:

- Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS)
- Earth Observation (EO)
- Integrated Applications
- Satellite Components

Our many years of experience translate into in-depth knowledge and extensive collection of data on Polish, European and global space sector.

Our company is formed by an interdisciplinary team of experts and space-geeks, who have a broad knowledge of and competences in space technologies.

A number of our projects have been funded or co-funded by the European Space Agency (ESA), European Investment Bank (EIB) or European Commission (Horizon 2020).

We develop our own products and services utilising satellite data. We create our own electronic and software solutions and provide various services – analytical, consulting etc. While we focus mostly on the “downstream” aspects of the sector, we are working hard on developing our “upstream” competences as well.

Name: SwanSAT - Geospatial Intelligence
Description: SwanSAT embeds deep technology and precision intelligence to transform decision making for nations, businesses, and communities.  We integrate multi-sensor satellite data (SAR, Optical, Thermal and LiDAR) with AI/ML analytics to deliver actionable insights across key sectors including Agriculture, Financial Services, Disaster, and Defense. Our platform converts complex earth observation data into precise, decision-ready intelligence, empowering organizations to plan better, respond faster, and grow sustainably. Scientifically validated and extensively certified, our solutions deliver proven accuracy and scalable impact – from space to soil to security.

Name: ASTRO - Advanced Space Tethers for Remote Sensing Operations
Description: ASTRO aims to develop Tethered Satellite Systems optimized for remote sensing missions.

Description: The Australia Space Launch LinkedIn page is under construction.  We launching soon!

Name: NORSTEC – Norwegian Space Technology Collective
Description:
NORSTEC is a non-profit organization focused on bringing together space technology enthusiasts across Norway. Founded in early 2024, our aim is to support collaboration among student organizations and individuals with a passion for space exploration and technology.

We offer several initiatives, including the Northstar Incubator for emerging student organizations, the Pathway Rideshare program to help satellite teams secure launch opportunities, and the Skyline Launchpad, which facilitates access to rocket launch facilities.

In addition, we are organizing the Norstec Summit, which will be one of Norway’s first large-scale student space conferences in 2026. Our community, known as The Collective, serves as a platform for students to connect, share ideas, and work together on common challenges.

Name: ERÜ Space'S
Description: As a club; We aim to increase the necessary theoretical, technical skills and knowledge of our members and make them compatible with the needs and qualifications of the sector in the field of Defense Industry and Space Technologies. We want to contribute to the training of leaders who will be involved in future space exploration and technologies for the defense industry. Our vision; It is built on the foundations of sustainability, innovation, student focus and social responsibility.

Name: Geospatial Research Forum
Description:
The Geospatial Research Forum (GRF) is an organization dedicated to advancing research and innovation in the field of geospatial technology and data analysis. With a focus on exploring the intersection of geography, spatial data science, and technology, GRF brings together experts, academics, and professionals from various disciplines to collaborate, share knowledge, and address challenges in geospatial research.

GRF serves as a platform for networking, collaboration, and knowledge exchange among researchers, practitioners, and policymakers interested in harnessing the power of geospatial data for diverse applications such as urban planning, environmental monitoring, disaster management, agriculture, transportation, and public health.

Through conferences, workshops, publications, and online resources, GRF facilitates discussions on emerging trends, methodologies, and best practices in geospatial research. By fostering interdisciplinary dialogue and promoting cross-sector partnerships, GRF contributes to the development of innovative solutions and approaches to address complex spatial challenges facing society.

Furthermore, GRF actively engages with the broader community to raise awareness about the significance of geospatial research and its implications for decision-making, policy development, and sustainable development initiatives. By promoting education and advocacy, GRF strives to enhance the understanding and appreciation of geospatial technology and its transformative potential in shaping a more resilient, equitable, and sustainable world.

Name: AAC SpaceQuest
Description:
AAC SpaceQuest is a full satellite solutions firm with extensive capabilities including design, development, integration and launch of small (<100kg) satellite systems, as well as the implementation and on-orbit operation of space-based data missions. We have undertaken the design, manufacture and integration of space systems for select blue-chip commercial and government clients and have successfully delivered on-orbit, a variety of satellites for our customers throughout our 25 year history.

We also offer an extensive range of proven spacecraft components, including RF communication subsystems for S-band, X-band, UHF and VHF frequencies, solar panels, power management systems, GPS Receivers, antennas, and AIS receiver payloads. All our satellites systems and components have extensive flight heritage.

The company is now in the process of building its next generation of advanced maritime data satellites, which will complement its existing assets on-orbit, with the next launches for this constellation due to begin in Q4 2023.

Name: Chair of Space Geodesy, ETH Zurich
Description:
The Chair of Space Geodesy will face the challenges and profit from the opportunities that come with “big data” in geodesy. We will design and implement advanced machine learning techniques to automate the processing of GNSS and VLBI data, resulting in a faster availability and higher quality of geodetic products. Also, machine learning will be applied in the large-scale analysis of GNSS time series to detect and investigate geophysical signals, inter alia related to hydrological and seismic events, as well as to severe weather events and ionospheric disturbances. Finally, we aim to improve the prediction of several types of geodetic parameters by machine learning. In particular, we will focus on determining future Earth orientation parameters, station coordinates and atmospheric parameters.

Prof. Dr. Benedikt Soja

Name: OHB Austria
Description:
OHB Austria GmbH, formerly OHB Digital Solutions, is an Austrian company part of the OHB Group, focused on advancing space technology through innovative solutions in GNSS interference detection and localization, GNSS signal simulation, and GNSS technical consultancy.
 
With over two decades of expertise, we have become a trusted partner for aerospace, defense, telecommunications, and R&D institutions, collaborating on critical R&D projects with organizations such as the European Space Agency and the European Commission. Our key products families, GIDAS and XPLORA, help ensure safe, reliable, and precise navigation.
 
As OHB Austria GmbH, we are committed to contributing to space systems projects and expanding our role within Europe’s space sector, reinforcing our leadership in GNSS technology and positioning Austria at the forefront of space exploration and satellite development.

Name: Interstellar Technologies
Description: Stealth Mode

Name: Astrum Drive Aerospace
Description:
Astrum Drive Technologies is a forward-thinking US-based space startup focused on developing advanced propulsion systems. Our team has developed a groundbreaking propulsion system that directly converts electrical energy into continuous thrust, eliminating the need for conventional fuels. 
This breakthrough enables deep space travel, significantly extends satellite lifespans, boosts spacecraft range, speed, and payload capacity, and lowers operational costs - benefiting satellite operators, space agencies, and defense organizations alike. 
With proven prototypes, secured patents, and partnerships with leading universities & space companies, we are poised to redefine the future of space travel.

Our propellantless propulsion technology offers significant advantages for satellite applications. By eliminating the need for onboard fuel storage, satellites equipped with our system can be lighter, more compact, and have extended operational lifetimes. This technology enables the satellites to perform efficient orbital maneuvers, precise station-keeping, and opens up new possibilities for satellite constellations and formations.

Our research is published in prestigious Q1 journals, we have LOIs and collaborations with leading US institutions, including the University of Alabama in Huntsville, NASA, SpaceX and others.

Name: Institute on AI for Space Sustainability
Description:
The International Institute on AI for Space Safety and Sustainability is supported by the UK Space Agency (UKSA) and other public and private investments. 

It brings together stakeholders from academia, industry, and government, with the aim of driving sustainable innovation and promoting responsible practices in the use of AI for space applications.

Our mission is to accelerate the development of AI technologies from early-stage concepts to full-scale adoption in the space sector.

Purpose 

The goal of the Institute is to address the pressing challenges facing both Earth and Space by harnessing the power of AI technologies. With the raising number of satellites and ever-increasing space traffic, there is a critical need for effective space traffic management systems to ensure the safety and sustainability of space activities. 

The Institute seeks to train new talents within the UK space sector and attract international partners to establish presence in the country for the development and exploitation of technologies of interest.

Objectives

•	Develop and commercialise AI-powered solutions tailored to the specific needs of the space industry

•	Forge strategic partnerships with space agencies, satellite operators, and aerospace companies to pilot and deploy AI technologies

•       Become a leading facilitator & provider of AI solutions for space applications, especially within space traffic management

Name: Aerotyne International
Description: We are a cutting-edge, high-tech firm out of the Midwest. Awaiting imminent patent approval on the next-generation of radar detectors that have both huge military and civilian applications.

Name: IMS Space Consultancy GmbH
Description: We are a dynamic company operating in the field of the satellite engineering and operations, currently providing consultancy for European Space Agencies. Our experience spans from Spacecraft Design to Operations and has grown in the years through the development of challenging projects and collaborations with a wide range of industrial partners and international organizations. The services provided by IMS include specialized consulting, project support, software development, and research.

Name: Space Aye
Description:
At Space Aye, we are revolutionising how you interact with the world and understand your surroundings. We are the only way to identify assets and people, in real-time from space.

Unlike traditional maps supported by outdated, historic satellite images, Space Aye enables identification of people, vehicles and objects in a real-time satellite image, not just on a map. This game changing capability is powered by our inter-continental patents and technology, which merges IoT (Internet of Things) data such as imagery, video, location, biometrics and audio from smart devices with cutting-edge real-time satellite imagery in multiple formats. 

Our unique process combines two exponentially growing capabilities: Real-Time Earth Observation, while identifying the billions of IoT devices within those images. Everything. Everywhere. Now. Making real-time satellite imagery accessible, more specific and more actionable, helping to pave the way for mass-market adoption and more effective commercialisation of space. 

With simplified ordering, universal image formats, and seamless IoT data integration, we provide actionable insights that are transforming multiple industry sectors, enabling smarter, faster decisions that save time, money and lives. 

Space Aye - Everything. Everywhere. Now.

Name: Norwegian Space Cluster
Description:
The Norwegian Space Cluster brings together stakeholders in the space economy,
aiming to contribute to economic development, promote knowledge, ensure societal security, and lead a successful transition towards a sustainable future.

Name: EtherWing Aerospace
Description:
EtherWing Aerospace is dedicated to advancing India's aerospace and defense sectors through technological innovation and strategic partnerships. Our mission is to develop cost-effective, reliable solutions that address practical challenges while contributing to national and global advancements in space and defense technologies.

At the core of our efforts is the EC-Series Satellite Platforms, a suite of CubeSats designed for research, education, and governmental missions. Each platform offers reliability, scalability, and high performance to meet diverse mission needs.

EC-12: Precision 1U CubeSat
The EC-12 is a compact 1U CubeSat designed for scientific experiments and educational use. Its precision engineering and customizable options allow institutions to conduct cost-efficient space missions with confidence.

EC-25: Enhanced 2U CubeSat
The EC-25 builds on the EC-12 with increased payload capacity and enhanced power systems in a 2U design, supporting more complex missions such as advanced communication and environmental monitoring.

EC-42: Advanced 3U CubeSat
The EC-42 is our most advanced CubeSat, designed for sophisticated experiments and governmental missions. It offers higher data transmission rates, optimized payload integration, and advanced power management systems.

Beyond CubeSats, EtherWing Aerospace is developing guided missile platforms to strengthen national defense with precision-engineered systems addressing evolving security challenges.

We collaborate with universities, research institutions, and industry partners to foster a knowledge-driven ecosystem promoting technological growth. Our multidisciplinary team is committed to high-quality, reliable solutions that support the long-term growth of the aerospace and defense sectors.

Name: Rwanda Space Agency
Description: Rwanda Space Agency (RSA) is the National Space agency that was established in 2020 with the mission of developing Rwanda's space sector towards social-economic development

Name: Winter Satellite Workshop
Description:
Winter Satellite Workshop is the leading New Space event in Finland, Scandinavia and the Baltic region. It combines multiple space-focused gatherings, including FinCOSPAR and Remote Sensing Days, into one vibrant forum for collaboration and knowledge exchange.

The workshop brings together space technology experts, scientists and students to explore emerging topics in the rapidly evolving space sector. Its scope covers New Space developments, small satellite missions and platforms, space science and instrumentation, Earth observation and the sustainable use of space.

Founded in 2018, the Workshop is a community-driven event technically organized by Aalto University’s Space Technology Research Group, with contributions from governmental agencies, institutes, companies and student organizations.

Name: NI SPACE
Description:
What is NI SPACE? 
NI SPACE is a regional space cluster that has been established to promote and support regional capabilities in both the national and international space sector. 

Who do NI SPACE support?
The role of NI SPACE will be to develop, inspire and support opportunities with Northern Ireland stakeholders that includes:
o	Manufacturing, software and cyber security businesses
o	Public sector as users of technology
o	Capital fund investors
o	Academia and students

What is NI SPACE’s mission?
NI SPACE facilitates meaningful knowledge exchange and develops collaborations to maximise potential, drive economic growth and progress the space sector in Northern Ireland.

How is NI SPACE funded and who do they work with?
Funded by the UK Space Agency, NI SPACE works alongside InvestNI - the regional business development agency, ADS - the national aerospace, defence and security trade association, the UK Space Agency and other established UK space clusters.

Name: WeSpace Technologies
Description:
WeSpace Technologies was established with the aim of developing Autonomously Flying Robotic Systems for deep-space exploration.

The flagship project of WeSpace is an autonomous hopper designed for lunar exploration. This vehicle is specifically engineered to operate under extreme environmental conditions. It serves as a versatile platform capable of carrying a diverse range of sophisticated payloads, specifically tailored for astrobiology, planetary geology, and other exploration purposes.

Name: CoSMOS Corp
Description:
We are embarking on a mission to expand our knowledge and commercialization of the space industry. Our CoSMOS team is delivering excellence to keep Space Flight Participants (SFPs) safe and healthy to perform at the highest level in any environment.  

CoSMOS Corp and its partners support underrepresented researchers that are translating their research to space health applications or developing their careers to work on human health and performance challenges during space exploration. 

CoSMOS Corp is a member of the Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF), ASTM International F47 Committee on Commercial Spaceflight, Aerospace Medical Association (AsMA), Space Force Association (SFA), and the FAA Center of Excellence for Commercial Spaceflight Transportation (COE-CST). Safety is our priority.

Name: THE BR-DGE
Description:
THE BR-DGE is an outreach program for technology transfer and transition (T3). The program is powered by Disruptive Technology Index (DTI), a California nonprofit, in partnership with SpaceWERX, the US Space Force’s innovation arm for technology advancement. We support small businesses as they leverage the Small Business Administration’s SBIR/STTR program and aim to help further commercialize their dual-use technology efforts through programming and ecosystem building.

With the sector projected to grow from $630 billion in 2023 to $1.8 trillion by 2035, THE BR-DGE serves as the center of gravity supporting entrepreneurs building space capabilities for the rapidly growing space economy.

Name: SpaceBase Limited
Description: SpaceBase is a purpose driven consultancy focused on democratizing access to space.  Our vision is to live in a world where space plays a critical role in addressing planetary challenges, and provides the pathway for unlimited resources and economic benefits. There is a technological divide between space faring nations and the rest of the world. Our mission is to address this challenge by catalyzing space industries as a service in emerging and developing countries.  Over the past 8 years, we have developed the expertise and prototyped a playbook for building entrepreneurial space ecosystems in New Zealand and are expanding our services around the world.

Name: ConnectSAT aerospace Lab
Description: ConnectSAT Aerospace Lab  is dedicated to advancing space technologies, including propulsion systems, satellite payloads, ground systems, and deep space logistics. We collaborate with startups, major companies, academic institutions, and research organizations on innovative space projects aligned with our goal of democratizing access to space and its resources. Our focus is on delivering the world's most affordable connectivity, supporting space explorers with logistics, and providing geospatial data to organizations.

AeroSpaceTech is a multidisciplinary research group comprised of specialists in aerospace, telecommunications, industrial, and computer engineering. With over 17 years of experience, we began our journey with the launch of XatCobeo in 2012, Spain’s first nanosatellite. Since then, we have contributed to high-impact missions such as HUMESAT-D, DustCube, SERPENS, and Lume-1.

Currently, we not only develop CubeSat platforms but also specialise in systems engineering, integration, testing, and training. Furthermore, we have expanded our capabilities into the aeronautical sector, with a particular focus on Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), encompassing everything from design to implementation.

Our commitment is to lead innovative projects that drive space exploration and the development of cutting-edge aerospace technologies.

Name: Resolute Lab India
Description: Resolute Lab India is pioneering microgravity-powered biotech innovations to accelerate life sciences, drug discovery, and cancer diagnostics. By leveraging ground-based & space-based platforms, we enable researchers, pharma companies, and healthcare institutions to unlock breakthrough insights, reduce R&D timelines, and transform global healthcare.

Name: Space Mining Technologies
Description:
We provide critical resources and technologies enabling sustainable space exploration and future settlements of the Moon and Mars.

Water is the most valuable resource enabling permanent human presence in outer space. Water is vital for astronauts to drink, extensively used in life-support systems, and an ideal mean for radiation shielding. Additionally, we intend to split water molecules into oxygen and hydrogen to support fuel cell based energy storage systems and for indigenous rocket propellant production (several tons of O2 and H2). This is will be a billion dollars market. This is why Space Mining Technologies will be your water and fuel station on the Moon.

Name: Metakosmos
Description:
Metakosmos is redefining human performance in extreme environments on Earth, in orbit & beyond. As space commercializes and defense operations become more autonomous and data-driven, the world is entering a decade that will demand life-support, mobility & protection systems that are smarter, lighter, safer & radically more adaptive. Metakosmos exists to deliver exactly that.

Our flagship platform, Kosmosuit, is an AI-native, sensor-dense spacesuit & survival systems architecture that brings the intelligence of a digital cockpit directly to the human body. By integrating bioastronautics telemetry, environmental sensing & predictive analytics, we create a real-time adaptive layer between the operator and the harshest conditions imaginable.

From next-generation space suits for commercial spaceflight to modular life-support and protective systems for defense, aerospace & subsea missions, our technology transforms raw data into actionable foresight. Where hardware innovation is traditionally slow, Metakosmos accelerates compressing development cycles from years to months through rapid prototyping and iterative validation with space agencies standards, defense units & advanced manufacturing.

While legacy OEMs focus on components, we are building the operating system that orchestrates them creating a defensible systems and data layer that becomes more intelligent with every use case.

As global demand surges for human-machine integration across orbital servicing, lunar operations, high-risk defense tasks, and industrial robotics, Metakosmos stands at the intersection of three explosive markets: SpaceTech, Bioastronautics & Intelligent Wearables.

We are not just building suits.
We are building the future of augmented human capability.

 Watch our Apple TV documentary:
https://tv.apple.com/us/movie/suited-for-the-stars/umc.cmc.6uk1ff80nkzji0op1l8kcq61n

Name: INTELLCAP SARL, Filiale Africa/Morocco, INTELLCAP Corp.
Description:
Fondée en 2008 par Idriss ILALI, Ph.D. Sciences de l’Ingénieur, Ingénierie Mécanique & Expert Aéronautique, IntellCap existe dans l’ambition d’exceller dans l’innovation technologique et divers domaines particulièrement complexes.

Parmi ces domaines :

L’aéronautique et l’espace :
IntellCap a à sa disposition une équipe d'experts exceptionnels dans les domaines de l'aéronautique et de l'espace afin d'initier des projets innovants : AR Assisted Navigation System, FAP, VRFlight simulator, Design and assembly assisted by AR & VR, Flight monitoring and control systèmes.

L’intelligence artificielle appliquée à l’automobile et systèmes intelligents:
AutomobileUne nouvelle approche de projets R&T qui consiste à développer un système intégré d'intelligence artificielle semblable au cerveau en utilisant une approche modulaire basée sur la sécurité, la relatabilité, la maintenabilité.

Bâtiment Intelligent :
INTELLCAP propose des solutions matérielles et logicielles clés en main pour les bâtiments intelligents via des systèmes de surveillance de déification (indicateurs pertinents pour l'énergie, l'eau, la qualité de l'air et le confort thermique, la gestion des déchets, l'empreinte carbone...), impliquant les technologies les plus avancées, par exemple des capteurs intelligents multiphysiques, base de données et analyse du temps, approche IoT... le cœur de métier détenu et les solutions logicielles s'adaptent aux besoins des clients pour de nombreux cas d'usage : administrations, écoles, universités, hôpitaux...
Et aussi d’autres domaines tels que la Bioingénierie (la simulation, la modélisation et la conception de processus biotechnologiques complexes),  le Divertissement (écrans tactiles haptiques, des systèmes intégrés AR et VR, non seulement au niveau de l'application mais au niveau de la technologie), et la Santé.

-Apprentissage et formation spécifique

Name: JoinThe.Space - Global Hub for Space Industry
Description:
JoinThe.Space is a global hub for the space industry, operating globally in several key areas:

Job Opportunities: We offer over 7,000 job listings from around the world, connecting candidates with roles in the dynamic space sector.

JoinThe.Space Club: This exclusive space is dedicated to our partner universities globally. Here, we focus on the personal development of our members, supporting their recruitment efforts and expanding their knowledge about space.

Events: We organize a range of space-themed events, including webinars, podcasts, and in-person gatherings at universities and beyond, promoting knowledge sharing and collaboration.

Global Networking: Our platform facilitates networking opportunities by collaborating with 120 scientific societies across 25 countries, fostering connections among professionals and organizations in the space industry.

JoinThe.Space is designed for students, young professionals and experienced individuals from around the globe who are either already part of the space industry or aspire to join it. We are committed to building a vibrant community that nurtures growth and collaboration within this exciting field.

Visit us: www.JoinThe.Space

Name: Stellar Technology, LLC
Description:
Stellar Technology, LLC (Formerly Recruit Veterans) is a 100% service-disabled veteran-owned small business (SDVOSB), headquartered in Cedar Park, Texas, providing best-in-class engineering, technical and value added reseller services. We team with prime contractors in the aerospace, defense and other federal government industries to bid on, win and provide outstanding services on federal contracts. 

At Stellar Technology, we do not just meet expectations, we strive to "Go Above and Beyond” them, delivering quality-driven, mission-focused solutions backed by deep industry knowledge, operational excellence, and a steadfast culture of service.

Description: Recruiting Force, LLC, an award-winning SDVOSB established in 2006, proudly serves clients throughout the United States. Located in Cedar Park, Texas, our expertise lies in providing professional, managerial, technical, and administrative support services to federal agencies and federal prime contractors. With a team of experienced veterans at the helm, Recruiting Force, LLC is committed to delivering superior support services. For more information call 1-888-921-HIRE (4473). Recruit Veterans “Accomplish the Mission!”

Name: CroCube
Description:
CroCube is the first Croatian nanosatellite, a mission that marks Croatia's entry into the global space community. Launched to inspire, educate, and innovate, CroCube is a technological demonstrator and a platform for scientific research. 

Our mission is to advance space technology, foster STEM education, and connect enthusiasts, scientists, and industry leaders. With CroCube in orbit, we are laying the foundation for future space initiatives and promoting Croatia as a hub for innovation in the space industry.

Name: Rada 360
Description: Rada 360 is a space-tech company dedicated to delivering space-based solutions that enhance societal well-being. Our technologies advance social livelihoods by specializing in precision agriculture, forest monitoring, utilizing earth observation data for natural resources management, mitigating climate change, and early warning systems for improving disaster response.

Name: Vector Space Biosciences
Description: Vector Space Biosciences, Inc., (SBIO), parent company of Vectorspace AI (VAIX), along with its scientific collaborators, launch biological CubeSats for purpose of generating data related to microgravity and radiation. This data leads to the development of countermeasures against diseases associated to stressors connected to protecting and repairing the human body during spaceflight. This includes using a network of scientific data engineering pipelines for building targeted language models resulting in real-time datasets which power Artificial Intelligence (AI) operations in space biosciences, biotechnology and pharmaceutical development. Working with leading scientific labs in the areas of human aging, cancer, and nutrigenomics, our goal is to accelerate the process of new hypothesis generation and novel discoveries in space biosciences, including materials sciences in the area of nanotechnology and nanomedicines. Developing advanced large and small language modeling technologies, our platform is capable of producing more than 100,000,000,000 different real-time datasets for the purpose of accelerating discoveries. Innovations in space biosciences result in products and services for all industries, including the financial markets, more importantly, new forms of precision medicine for all humankind.

Name: Orbite
Description: Orbite is building a commercial astronaut training and spaceflight gateway complex. Orbite will provide an end-to-end customer experience, from pre-mission training, to preflight accommodations, day of departure preparation, mission monitoring during flight, and post mission accommodations, re-acclimation and mission debriefing.

Name: SEOPS
Description: SEOPS LLC is a leading provider of rideshare integration and launch solutions for smallsats headed to LEO, cislunar and beyond. The team brings years of experience and trusted relationships with launch vehicle providers, helping customers expertly execute missions campaigns for education, scientific advancement, and national security needs, including tactically responsive rideshare launch and dynamic 24/7 on-orbit operations. SEOPS’ comprehensive launch services, from capacity procurement to flexible deployment systems, OTV solutions, mission design and integration services, ensure payloads get on orbit in the most seamless, cost-effective way possible.

Name: Michigan Aeronautical Science Association (MASA)
Description: MASA is the student-run rocketry team at the University of Michigan. We take a multidisciplinary approach to engineering as well as getting students from across the college together to design, build, and launch pioneering liquid-fueled rockets. MASA aims to push collegiate world records while educating students on the nuances of rocketry. Together, MASA works to take Michigan to new heights!

Name: FOSS4G North America
Description:
FOSS4G North America is the premier open geospatial technology and business conference with a vibrant and welcoming atmosphere for technologists, end-users and decision makers.

This year's conference will be held at the Hilton Baltimore Inner Harbor in Baltimore, Maryland. Conference dates are October 23 - 25, 2023.

Name: CalgaryToSpace
Description:
CalgaryToSpace is a student-led organization based out of the Schulich School of Engineering at the University of Calgary. The team is currently designing, assembling, and planning the operation of FrontierSat, a 3U CubeSat set to launch in early 2026 as Calgary's first student-engineered satellite. 

FrontierSat will carry high-tech science payloads developed by researchers at the University of Calgary and demonstrate their long-term capabilities in orbit for the first time.

As the only student team in Calgary currently working on a real spacecraft, our goal is to encourage interest in the space and aerospace industries in our community while inspiring students with a unique and challenging mission.

CalgaryToSpace advocates for diversity and no experience is required to join our team—university students from all backgrounds and years are welcome. Apply on our website today and let us know about your interests!

We love collaborating with other student teams and industry partners. Visit our website to connect and learn more about how we can work together. 

We are thankful to be supported in our mission by the Schulich School of Engineering, the University of Calgary, the European Space Agency through the educational “Fly Your Satellite!” Test Opportunities programme, and by several industry partners that have made significant contributions to the Canadian space and aerospace sectors.

If you’re interested in supporting our project through a monetary contribution or in-kind donation, please contact general@calgarytospace.ca to learn about available partnership packages for sponsors.

Name: Rete Innovativa Regionale Aerospace Innovation and Research - RIR AIR
Description:
The aerospace regional innovation network of Veneto AIR – Aerospace Innovation and Research aims to act as an integrated and collaborative ecosystem of companies, universities, and regional stakeholders.

Established in 2020, its scope is to position itself at the forefront of the New Space Economy by adopting a holistic approach, promoting the internationalization and financial growth of companies represented, and enhancing local skills and expertise.

Today, the network includes more than 100 partners, actively contributing to innovation and competitiveness in aerospace.

PROJECTS FINANCED WITH PR Veneto FESR 2021-2027: https://rir-air.it/progetti-finanziati/

Name: Cascade Space
Description: Cut through the noise with our software stack and ground station network. Iterate faster, test efficiently, launch with confidence.

Name: Bharat Space Education Research Centre (भारत अंतरिक्ष शिक्षा अनुसंधान केंद्र )
Description: Bharat Space Education Research Centre is dedicated to promoting space science and technology across India. Through a variety of hands-on workshops, interactive courses, and engaging educational programs, India Space Lab aims to inspire students and educators alike.

Name: PTI
Description: Hi

Name: inteligenciaGeoespacial SRL
Description: inteligenciaGeoespacial SRL is the official distributor of Esri in Bolivia. Other representations include Maxar, Cyme, NTT Data, Iceye, and Schneider Electric.
Location: Santa Cruz de la Sierra, Santa Cruz

Name: PoliTo Rocket Team
Description:
PoliTo Rocket Team is a student team of the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace engineering of Politecnico di Torino, located in Turin, Italy. The Team is entirely devoted to the design and construction of experimental sounding rockets and rocket engines for scientific research purposes.

PoliTo Rocket Team’s mission is to contribute to Italy's space innovation, with the study of next-generation space exploration technologies in a student-driven context. The Team wants to provide students with the possibility to complete the theoretical knowledge learnt in the classroom with projects that involve considerable challenges and solid results.

Name: EOLAS Insight Ltd
Description: EOLAS Insight is a consultancy-driven SaaS business specializing in geospatial intelligence solutions. By combining our expertise with our modular and customizable software platform, THEIA, we enable clients to reduce their reliance on GIS-intensive workflows while enhancing their geospatial capabilities. Our approach simplifies access to advanced technology, keeps clients at the forefront of innovation, and mitigates risks by standardizing and pre-configuring workflows.

Name: National Students' Space Challenge
Description:
National Students' Space Challenge is the sole technical fest of its genre dedicated to promoting space enthusiasm in India. It was conceived by Space Technology Students' Society, a group of Kgpians from various disciplines working towards a common goal of motivating space interest among the student community of India, which is trying to emerge as one of the major forefronts in the field of space technology. 

Officially, spAts is the student body of Kalpana Chawla Space Technology Cell (KCSTC), the contact point of Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), at IIT Kharagpur. We have had the constant support of ISRO in all our activities and initiatives. Having had an experience of a tremendous participation last year, we plan to make it even bigger this time and rise to a higher level of intellectual and technical expertise.

Name: Bushfire Research Centre of Excellence
Description:
The Bushfire Research Centre of Excellence was established jointly by the Australian National University (ANU) and Optus, in response to the 2019-20 Australian ‘Black Summer’ of bushfires. With increasing changes to climate, the conditions seen during the Black Summer are likely to recur, and possibly worsen, in Australia’s future, highlighting the need for a robust technological system for managing bushfires Australia-wide. 

This sovereign capability to manage catastrophic fire conditions in 21st Century Australia will require a paradigm shift. Combinations of new and existing technologies, often developed for different purposes, such as space, defence, communications, and earth observations, will be key in enabling new approaches and new capabilities. These technologies will need to be integrated into existing firefighting processes within established institutions, without disruption to existing capabilities, and with strong support from and integration with end-users.

The Centre’s role is to provide thought leadership and develop, demonstrate and evaluate novel technological solutions for bushfire management in Australia, with the vision of a sovereign capability to rapidly and reliably detect and suppress fires. 

The Centre has a broad vision of research activities that will contribute to a paradigm change in how bushfires are managed in Australia.  This vision is captured in six interrelated research programs:

1.	Lightning forecasting and detection
2.	Landscape flammability
3.	Ignition detection
4.	Data analytics and communications
5.	Safe and rapid suppression
6.	Legal and economic analysis
Location: AUSTRALIAN NATIONAL UNIVERSITY, Australian Capital Territory

Name: ESA Space2Connect
Description:
The European Space Agency is organizing the second edition of the “Space2Connect” conference, which will take place between 7 and 9 June 2023 in Matera, Italy.
“Space2Connect”s goal is to explore and discuss the emerging space-based solutions that are addressing the opportunities of today’s and tomorrow’s SATCOM market together with the most relevant and active actors of this domain.

The conference will consist of both plenary and parallel sessions, with several networking opportunities.
The opening will be a plenary session where manufacturers, satellite operators, space agencies, small companies, entrepreneurs, technical experts and investors will be encouraged to quiz executives and guests who will deliver keynote speeches. 

In parallel the conference will provide many possibilities to exchange ideas, share plans, develop relationships and seek inspiration to thrive in the new space age. You will be able to choose from a wide range of thematic sessions that will show you the latest advanced technology developments, the most powerful satellite communications products and services. Take the opportunity to understand the role of space to boost and secure connectivity.
It’s time to connect2thefuture, it's time to take part in the SPACE2CONNECT event!
Discover & Sign up Now

Name: Curtin's Space Science and Technology Centre (SSTC)
Description:
Welcome to the largest planetary research group in the Southern Hemisphere.

At Curtin’s Space Science and Technology Centre (SSTC), we conduct planetary research to address some of humanity’s greatest science and technical challenges. We do fundamental and applied space science research to build a strong future in space for Australia with the next generation of planetary explorers.

Our scientists study the formation and geological history of the solar system, through
impact modelling, machine learning and analysis of recovered meteorites and material from the Moon and Mars (and beyond!). 

Our engineers have built remote camera systems for the tracking and recovery of fireballs, flown Western Australia’s very first cube satellites, and are now developing interplanetary spacecraft missions.

Name: AlbertaSat
Description:
AlbertaSat is made up of close to 80 undergraduate and graduate students who work with the assistance of several faculty advisors at the University of Alberta. We represent a large range of backgrounds and capabilities with a common goal of a better future for humanity. We are human beings looking for a brighter tomorrow – a tomorrow that exists with the journey of humanity to the stars.

Our mission is to build a better future through the development of space technology. We believe that this will allow Alberta to augment many aspects of Albertan industry and society, and move towards a province capable of contributing to a worldwide push for global peace, prosperity and expansion into the solar system.

AlbertaSat built the Experimental Albertan #1 (Ex-Alta 1) Satellite as a part of the QB50 consortium mission.  Along with 37 other cube satellites in the QB50 mission, Ex-Alta 1 measures patterns of space weather on a completely new scale of science.  Ex-Alta 1 launched to the International Space Station April 18, 2017 from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station and was deployed May 26,2017.  More importantly, Ex-Alta 1 represents an opportunity for the University of Alberta and the rest of the province to demonstrate a capability for space technology and betterment of humankind on a global stage.

Name: s2a systems
Description: Developing and operating systems for Space Situational Awareness (SSA). ->  you will find more frequent updates on X.

Name: LarioSpace
Description:
May 19–23, 2026
Five days to define the future of space.

LarioSpace returns to Lake Como with a fully renewed edition in format, identity, and ambition. Now in its fourth edition, the exclusive event dedicated to the New Space Economy brings together industry, research, investors, and the wider public for five days of innovation and dialogue among the leading players of the sector.

LarioSpace 2026 takes place across the Lake Como area, with its core activities hosted at the Lario Space Center of Telespazio, within a further strengthened collaboration between Involve Space and Telespazio (Leonardo Group). Together, they transform the Northern Italy space district into a hub for high-level discussion, strategic exchange, and long-term vision, while opening selected moments of the program to scientific outreach and public engagement.

Name: SUAS Aerospace
Description: Irish for UP! SUAS Aerospace is a private upstream space service provider based in the Republic of Ireland. SUAS will facilitate the critical test and launch capabilities of the European space sector with a dedicated rocket engine test site and orbital spaceport on the west coast of Ireland. SUAS’ spaceport will provide access to polar orbits from a location with a temperate climate, within the European Union, and with excellent logistical connections to mainland Europe.

Name: AtAll
Description: AtAll unlock Human-potential. Visionary deep-tech company aimed at stimulating Humanity in the direction of progress and driving its change.

Name: Titans Space
Description:
Titans Space Industries, Inc. and Titans Space Development Corporation (jointly called Titans Space) comprise a vast portfolio of incredible, revolutionary space infrastructure that will allow safe and efficient end-to-end space transportation, including spaceplanes and space stations for space tourism, commercial and industrial purposes, as well as for research, governments, and military usage. 

Titans Space’s single-stage-to-orbit spaceplanes will facilitate orbital space flights for orbital cruises or going to Low-Earth Orbit, sub-orbital flights for zero-g space tourism flights, and ultra-fast point-to-point transportation for humans and cargo.

Our space tourism division is building the future of luxury space exploration with spaceplanes, spaceships, space stations, and lunar transport vehicles. Titan Space’s revolutionary LEO Space Station and Lunar Space Station will redefine humanity’s place amongst the stars, with lunar tourism, scientific research, and commercial mining applications.

Name: GReD
Description:
GReD is a Politecnico di Milano spin-off SME that studies, designs and implements innovative and highly customized solutions based on geomatic technologies.

GReD is characterized as a center of excellence focused on Research and Development, professional advice and algorithmic elaboration for the creation of state-of-the-art solutions based on geomatic technologies in the following application areas:
- advanced surveying and cartographic applications,
- augmented GNSS applications,
- high precision and high resolution gravimetric applications,
- space applications.

R&D activities concern the maintenance of state-of-the-art internal skills and in particular the definition and development of products and services for:
- high precision GNSS navigation and positioning,
- high precision and high resolution gravimetric maps determination.

Name: YNSAT
Description:
YNSAT is a forward-thinking company dedicated to revolutionizing the accessibility and usability of remote sensing data across various industries. Our flagship platforms, Remote Miner and Remote Enviro, empower mining and environmental professionals to seamlessly process and analyze remote sensing data, transforming complex datasets into actionable insights within seconds.

By integrating advanced analytical capabilities into the industry's easiest-to-use suite of tools, we streamline workflows, enhance decision-making, and drive efficiency for our clients. We are on a mission to make remote sensing data effortless, making it an accessible and invaluable resource for professionals worldwide.

Our products are utilized by leading companies in areas such as mineral prospecting, land rehabilitation, and big-data management, underscoring our commitment to delivering innovative solutions that meet the diverse needs of our clients.

With a global footprint spanning 13 countries and processing millions of square kilometers of data monthly, YNSAT is at the forefront of transforming how industries leverage remote sensing data.

Name: SFA Florida
Description: Space Force Association's mission is to achieve superior national 𝐬𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫 by shaping a Space Force that provides credible deterrence in competition, dominant capability in combat, and professional services for all partners.

Name: Radoi Engineering
Description:
La baza proiectelor remarcabile.

Lideri consacrați în domeniul științelor și tehnologiilor geospațiale, suntem o companie specializată în achiziția, analiza, înțelegerea și procesarea datelor geospațiale, livrând produse care maximizează beneficiile acestora. Credem în începuturile bune iar experiența acumulată în cele mai diverse industrii ne-a convins că orice proiect remarcabil, indiferent de anvergura sa, pornește de la o bază construită impecabil.

Cu o experiență de peste 15 ani în industria de proiectare și restaurare, eforturile noastre sunt canalizate constant către a dezvolta o companie ghidată de profesionalism, inovație și încredere. Echipa noastră de experți se află într-un proces continuu de specializare, iar tehnologiile pe care le folosim țin pasul cu inovațiile din industrie. Toate acestea sunt garanția obținerii unor rezultate remarcabile pentru clienții noștri.

Valorile noastre

Încredere

Suntem o echipă foarte bine pregătită, într-un proces continuu de cercetare, pentru a ne asigura că avem expertiza necesară pentru rezolvarea proiectelor noastre. 

Inovație

Ne asigurăm că metodele de analiză și rezolvare a activităților sunt în conformitate cu cele mai performante tehnologii disponibile în domeniul în care activăm.

Profesionalism

Suntem o companie centrată pe performanță, dedicată scopului de a livra rezultate excelente pentru fiecare proiect în care suntem implicați. Avem setate standarde înalte atât pentru modul în care lucrăm ca echipă, cât și pentru rezultatele care ajung la clienții noștri.

Name: Blue Skies Space
Description:
Blue Skies Space is pioneering a new model to deliver high-quality space science data in accelerated timescales to the global scientific community to enable them to answer humanity’s greatest scientific questions. Through a fleet of low-Earth orbit satellites, we aim to serve the global demand for high-quality science data across many research areas including the monitoring of stars, understanding what the atmospheres of faraway exoplanets are made of as well as the composition of asteroids in our solar system.

With offices in the UK and Italy, we have assembled an experienced team who have previously worked at organisations such as NASA, Airbus, Surrey Satellite Technology, Caltech and UCL, bringing a wealth of expertise in space science, satellite engineering, satellite construction and operations.

Name: ULOG3
Description:
ULOG3 is an Indian deep-tech company focused on developing next-generation computing solutions for edge environments. Founded with the vision of advancing neuromorphic technology, ULOG3 is building processors that combine intelligence, efficiency, and adaptability for real-world applications.

Our first product, OLI (On-chip Learning Intelligence), is India’s first digital neuromorphic processor. Designed to deliver high performance with minimal power consumption, OLI enables real-time learning and decision-making in challenging conditions.

The OLI chip can be integrated into autonomous vehicles, drones, defence systems, AMR devices, and space missions, where reliability and energy efficiency are critical.

At ULOG3, we are driven by the belief that the future of computing lies in systems that think, adapt, and evolve — just like the human brain.

Name: European Space Deep-Tech Innovation Centre (ESDI)
Description:
The European Space Deep-tech Innovation Centre (ESDI) is a collaborative initiative between the European Space Agency (ESA) and Switzerland, established to accelerate the commercial adoption and availability of deep-tech for the benefit of ESA, its Member States, and the broader European space sector.

Starting with the ESA Phi-Lab Switzerland, ESDI designs, establishes and runs platforms for deep-tech research in quantum, data, and materials, bringing to use technologies of tomorrow for the world of today.

Name: Baltic Sea & Space Cluster
Description: BALTIC SEA & SPACE CLUSTER (BSSC) is an effective promoter of the Polish Maritime Industry and Space Technology and utilizes Polish coastal location as a national treasure. Cluster acts as a partner and a participant in numerous endeavours regarding Polish maritime and space economy, including by creating it as an equivalent of the national and European intelligent specialization. Cluster also interacts in the International Ocean Governance programme.

Name: Space Sustainability Rating
Description:
The Space Sustainability Rating (SSR) is a voluntary rating system providing space actors with a transparent rating and certification system to assess the level of #sustainability of their missions. 

It also offers practical guidance on how to improve missions’ sustainability performance, with the goal of helping to address the challenges raised by the proliferation of space debris. 

Developed in the last three years by a consortium including the World Economic Forum, the European Space Agency, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, BryceTech and the University of Texas at Austin, the rating provides an innovative solution for space operators to support their debris mitigation efforts and increase the sustainability of their missions. 

All space actors are welcomed to join the SSR. If you wish to learn more about the rating and explore opportunities for engagement please visit our website.

Name: GReD
Description:
GReD is a Politecnico di Milano spin-off SME that studies, designs and implements innovative and highly customized solutions based on geomatic technologies.

GReD is characterized as a center of excellence focused on Research and Development, professional advice and algorithmic elaboration for the creation of state-of-the-art solutions based on geomatic technologies in the following application areas:
- advanced surveying and cartographic applications,
- augmented GNSS applications,
- high precision and high resolution gravimetric applications,
- space applications.

R&D activities concern the maintenance of state-of-the-art internal skills and in particular the definition and development of products and services for:
- high precision GNSS navigation and positioning,
- high precision and high resolution gravimetric maps determination.

Name: Quasar Space
Description:
Quasar Space is a brazilian IoT and Space Services company that provides integrated solutions for optimizing renewable energy generation and smart water consumption for agribusiness.
 
By combining high-resolution imaging technologies provided by modern satellites with multi-process digital interconnection in the cloud, the company develops a complete ecosystem that seeks to facilitate the link between the agents of the production chain and the essential resources needed.

With a mission to innovate to connect the world, the ecosystem largely favors the energy sector through precise techniques of strategic planning, installation and predictive maintenance of solar and wind farms.

For the agricultural sector, Quasar Space offers complete artificial intelligence solutions applied to monitoring and aiding in decision making for small and large farmers, aiming at increasing production and reducing costs, while decreasing environmental impacts through sustainable management of available land and water resources.

The digital integration of the production chain also allows the rural community to benefit from accurate and constantly updated information, in order to generate alerts on drought and pest indices, provide data for production pricing, allow the leasing of idle machinery and make the flow of production cheaper. Our stakeholders also rely on continuous monitoring by geolocation of machinery and movement of employees.

Guided by strong ethics and a focus on process transparency, the company has an arm specialized in georeferencing for land tenure regularization and pricing of rural properties. Finally, Quasar Space advises insurance companies in the Agrarian Insurance sector, offering risk predictability and monitoring solutions, with the purpose of individualizing the product, lowering the cost and facilitating commercialization.

Name: Paladin Space
Description: Paladin Space is a corporation committed to space sustainability. It has launched the first reusable space trash removal system in history.

Name: Asteroid Day
Description:
Asteroid Day is a United Nations proclaimed global educational awareness movement held annually on 30 June, the anniversary of the 1908 Siberian Tunguska asteroid event. Asteroid Day events are hosted worldwide by independent organisations including schools, museums, space agencies and clubs, who seek to educate their communities about the threats and opportunities of asteroids. 

The Asteroid Day website, social media, newsletters and events are produced by Luxembourg based charity Asteroid Foundation. 

Asteroid Day was co-founded in 2014 by Dr. Brian May, astrophysicist and lead guitarist of Queen, with Danica Remy, President of B612 Foundation, Rusty Schweickart, Apollo 9 Astronaut, and filmmaker Grig Richters.

Name: B-Open
Description: B-Open is an Italian SME specialised in algorithm design and software development for Earth Observation, image processing and data-/work-flow management. Our team combines a research-oriented attitude – with a majority of the resources being post-doc or PhD – with a strong industrial background. We have extensive experience working for international organisations and national institutions in Europe and in the Mediterranean Sea, as well as for large Italian companies and multinationals.

Name: BrianSpace
Description:
𝐀𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐁𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐧𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞

BrianSpace is a space education, research, and technology organization dedicated to making knowledge of space science and technology accessible to everyone — especially across Africa and other underserved regions of the world.

We believe that space exploration is not just for a few nations or professionals but for all humanity. Through education, innovative research, and practical technology development, BrianSpace empowers students, educators, and early-career scientists to participate in the global space ecosystem.

Our work focuses on creating inclusive learning opportunities, mentorship programs, and collaborative projects that connect communities to the frontier of space science. By bridging the gap between curiosity and capability, we aim to inspire the next generation of space thinkers, engineers, and explorers who will shape the future of our planet and beyond.

𝒗𝒊𝒔𝒊𝒐𝒏:
To create a world where every community — regardless of geography or resources — can contribute to and benefit from space exploration.

𝒎𝒊𝒔𝒔𝒊𝒐𝒏:
To expand access to space education, foster innovation, and promote scientific collaboration across Africa and other underserved regions of the globe.
Location: Sao Jose dos Campos, Sao Paulo

Name: Aetherspace
Description:
OUR MISSION
The first computer built, filled an entire room. Today they fit in our pocket. The space industry is going through the same revolution and Aether is a response to that phenomena. 

CubeSats are nanosatellites of 10x10x10 cm, the size of a Rubik's cube. They have allowed countless new innovations in the space industry and have numerous applications. We design, build and launch these bad boys into space! 

We distinguish ourselves by focusing on the design of CubeSats that survive atmospheric re-entry. A challenging and ambitious goal, which will allow CubeSat sample return missions to become a reality.

CONTACT
Want to learn more? Check out our website, our other social media, or send us an email! All links can be found in our Linktree:
https://linktr.ee/aetherspace

Name: Lunar and Planetary Institute
Description:
The Lunar and Planetary Institute’s (LPI’s) mission is to advance understanding of the solar system by providing exceptional science, service, and inspiration to the world.

Today, the LPI is an intellectual leader in lunar and planetary science. The Institute serves as a scientific forum attracting world-class visiting scientists, postdoctoral fellows, students, and resident experts; supports and serves the research community through newsletters, meetings, and other activities; collects and disseminates planetary data while facilitating the community's access to NASA astromaterials samples and facilities; engages and excites the public about space science; and invests in the development of future generations of scientists. The research carried out at the LPI supports NASA's efforts to explore the solar system. More information about LPI is available at www.lpi.usra.edu.

Name: Paradox
Description:
Paradox is a youth-led scientific entity with a clear mission: to create structured, professional pathways into space science, astronomy, and aerospace engineering especially for individuals who haven’t had access to formal academic opportunities.

We bring together education, research, and industry offering practical training, technical programs, and meaningful scientific experiences.
Paradox empowers aspiring scientists, engineers, and space enthusiasts with the tools, knowledge, and exposure they need to grow from curiosity to contribution and to play an active role in shaping the future of the regional space sector.

Name: Tharsis UNI
Description:
Tras el éxito de las participaciones de PUMII en 2019 y THARSIS en 2020, en el Human Exploration Rover Challenge (HERC), organizado por la NASA. 

Regresamos con todo para competir y ganar en esta edición 2023 del HERC-NASA, con un equipo renovado, que nos identificará en las distintas actividades que se llevarán a cabo, así como en la competencia central.

Name: Vanguard Technology
Description: High power rockets, rocket motors, stratospheric balloons and circuits

Name: Space Exploration and Research Agency
Description: SERA was founded to build a global community dedicated to space exploration and research. Its mission is to create a space agency for everyone.

Name: Mirai Aerospace
Description:
At Mirai Aerospace, we're dedicated to transforming the geospatial industry with our cutting-edge solutions. We offer a full suite of aerial, terrestrial, and mobile LiDAR scanning systems, paired with top-notch software and services. Our focus is on providing hardware for geospatial data acquisition and delivering high-performance, reliable data collection, processing, and application services.

We’re particularly known for our expertise in oblique photography, a specialty that has earned us many satisfied customers. Our product lineup includes half-frame, full-frame, and medium-format orthophotography cameras, along with advanced oblique photography cameras. By partnering with leading innovators and incorporating the latest advancements in remote sensing technology, Mirai Aerospace is committed to delivering precise, high-resolution data that drives success for our clients.

Name: Australian Space Diversity Alliance
Description:
Our purpose is to transform and expand the participation of diverse and minoritised groups in space, fostering a thriving and innovative space community that reflects our nation's capabilities.

There is an enormous opportunity in Australia with a growing industry to normalise and mandate equality and leadership from the start. Increasing diversity can contribute to meeting the Australian Space Agency’s Civil Space Strategy workplace goals.

ASDA is a not-for-profit organisation that is bipartisan and independent to maintain its neutrality.

Name: B612
Description: B612 and the Asteroid Institute bring together scientists, researchers, and engineers to develop tools and technologies to understand, map, and navigate our solar system, and protect our planet from asteroid impacts. A program of B612 Foundation, the Asteroid Institute leverages advances in computer science, instrumentation, and astronomy to find and track asteroids. Since 2002, the Foundation has supported research and technologies to enable the economic development of space and enhance our understanding of the evolution of our solar system in addition to supporting educational programs, including Asteroid Day. Founding Circle and Asteroid Circle members, and individual donors from 46 countries provide financial support for the work. For more information, visit B612foundation.org or follow on social: Twitter, Facebook, YouTube and Instagram.

Name: Princeton Satellite Systems
Description: Princeton Satellite Systems, Inc. is a small company extending the state-of-the-art in energy and aerospace systems. We are actively developing exciting technology for fusion power and propulsion, spacecraft control and navigation, solar sails, spacecraft formation flying and proximity ops, and home solar/battery systems. Also dba Princeton Fusion Systems.

Name: Space Angel
Description:
Pioneering Sustainable Space Access in the Indo-Pacific

Space Angel is redefining the future of space exploration with cutting-edge, sustainable space infrastructure. As the first company to develop a dual-corridor spaceport, strategically positioned for both polar and equatorial launches, we are unlocking new frontiers in global space access.

With state-of-the-art green spaceports in Eucla, WA, and Christmas Island, Space Angel provides a seamless platform for launch, re-entry, and landing, ensuring safe, efficient, and environmentally responsible space operations.

Our mission is to democratize access to space—empowering research, industry, and international collaboration while setting new benchmarks in safety, innovation, and sustainability. By transforming the Indo-Pacific into a global space hub, Space Angel is leading the charge in the next generation of space exploration.

Name: Astronautical Society Of India
Description: works for promotion of Astronautics in India

Name: Impossible Sensing
Description:
We are a space exploration company driving climate-tech innovation for a better planet.

We create new technologies to find life in our solar system and develop the lunar resources we need to support human presence on the Moon.  And we turn these space innovations into commercial solutions that help mitigate the climate disruption: greener energy, climate-smart agriculture, carbon capture and sequestration, greenhouse gas emissions monitoring, clean-tech minerals, and many more.

Name: Space Education Research Lab
Description: Space Education Research Lab (SERL) a constituent component of NCGSA, is entrusted for the space awareness, education and outreach . The lab, by organizing specialized seminars, workshops & trainings, conferences, destination camps, and winter/summer schools, aims to incorporate the diverse segments of the society towards a common goal of harnessing the potential of space science & technology for the socio-economic development and to achieve UN SDGs.

Name: Beyond Orbit
Description:
Beyond Orbit is a non-profit organization and the largest space community in Greece. We provide hands-on opportunities in engineering projects that bridge academic knowledge with real-world aerospace applications 

Our mission is to inspire, educate, and connect the next generation of space engineers—whether undergraduate, graduate, or PhD students—by fostering collaboration, creativity, and groundbreaking research within the aerospace ecosystem.

Explore our projects:

Beyond Robotics 
The first rover and drone team to represent Greece in the European Rover Challenge (ERC) under the auspices of the National Technical Univeristy or Athens and the University of Patras.

Beyond Rocketry

ODTÜ Fizik Bölümü öğretim üyesi Prof. Dr. M. Bilge Demirköz’ün 07 Mayıs 2019 tarihinde Merkez Müdürü olarak atandığı İVME-R; Türkiye’nin uzay ve hızlandırıcı konusunda ortaya koyduğu ulusal hedeflere, bilimsel teknolojik ve ekonomik dışa bağımlılığı en aza indirgeyerek ulaşabilmesi ve geliştirilecek özgün teknolojilere dünya pazarında pay ve söz sahibi olabilmesi için, ulusal bilimsel ve teknolojik gelişimi destekleyecek bir uzmanlık merkezi olmayı amaçlamaktadır.

Uzay ve hızlandırıcı teknolojilerine yönelik analiz, tasarım, test, devreye alma ve entegrasyon çalışmaları yaparak bu alanlarda Türkiye’nin rekabet gücünü artırmayı hedefleyen Merkezimizin amaçları arasında; Türkiye’de uzay ve hızlandırıcı teknolojileri alanında çalışan genç araştırmacılar için cazibe ortamı yaratarak beyin göçünü azaltmak, böylece yurt dışında bu alanda çalışanların ülkeye dönüşü için olanak yaratarak bu alandaki nitelikli insan gücü ihtiyacını gidermeye katkı sağlamak bulunmaktadır.

Name: ESA Space Solutions Centre Ireland
Description:
ESA Space Solutions Centre Ireland was established in 2016 and is headquartered in Tyndall National Institute, a leading European research centre in integrated ICT (Information and Communications Technology) hardware and systems.
ESA Space Solutions Centre Ireland is part of the largest space innovation network in the world. Across ESA Member States the ESA Space Solutions network is supporting European start-ups and SMEs to develop businesses using space technology and data. It does this by providing funding, business and technical support to help generate successful businesses and create sustainable jobs. As a constituent member of this network ESA Space Solutions Centre Ireland is the go-to place for great businesses that use space to improve everyday life. 
Space is an industry sector of growing importance in Ireland as reflected by the publication of the National Space Strategy for Enterprise 2019-2025 in June 2019. The space sector offers a plethora of commercial opportunities to Irish companies, with significant supports now being targeted at those developing downstream or terrestrial applications.
At ESA Space Solutions Centre Ireland we are supporting Irish companies (start ups and established companies) through a range of different initiatives, namely:
ESA Business Incubation Centre (BIC) Ireland
ESA Spark Funding Programme
ESA Space Solutions Ireland is composed of five Consortium Partners. These are the Tyndall National Institute (Lead Partner), Athlone Institute of Technology, Maynooth University, MaREI and University College Dublin.
Our local partner is Enterprise Ireland, which acts as the contact point for Ireland’s National Delegation to ESA along with the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment.

Name: PERSEI Space
Description:
At PERSEI Space, we specialise in the development of cutting-edge subsystems for space deorbiting and In-Orbit-Servicing. 

Our innovative solutions are rooted in the transformative potential of space tethers, revolutionizing the way we fly and safeguard the celestial environment.

What Are Space Tethers?
Space tethers are a remarkable leap in space technology. They involve the deployment of conductive tapes in space, which may seem deceptively simple, but their applications are truly game-changing. These tethers serve a dual purpose offering drag and propulsion capabilities, making them a versatile choice for a wide range of space missions.

Deorbiting - Ensuring Space Sustainability:
One of the primary applications of space tethers is to facilitate space deorbiting, a critical component of space sustainability. By deploying these tapes, we generate an electromagnetic and atmospheric drag force in low Earth orbit. This process accelerates the satellite's descent towards Earth, ultimately leading to reentry and safe disposal at the end of its mission. In this way, PERSEI Space is committed to mitigating the issue of space debris, a growing concern in the space industry, by offering efficient and sustainable deorbiting solutions.

Propulsion - Active Mode for Spacecraft:
In addition to deorbiting, space tethers offer a dynamic propellant-less propulsion method for spacecraft. Our technology allows us to actuate in an active mode, enabling us to propel spacecraft to their desired orbits with unparalleled efficiency. By leveraging the inherent electrical conductivity of the tether, we harness electromagnetic forces within Earth's magnetic field, creating a green and cost-effective means of orbital maneuvering.

At PERSEI Space, we are passionate about the endless possibilities space tethers present in the realm of space exploration and sustainability. Our dedication to innovation, safety, and environmental responsibility drives us to push the boundaries of technology.

LSAS began as a Japanese partner for AGI and served that market directly for more than 15 years.  This included direct sales and engineering support, but has now evolved to LSAS exclusive product offerings which extend these core partner products to focus on workflow automations and satellite operations solutions. This is our area of expertise, and where we have many subject matter experts focused on continued development of these solutions and helping our customers get the most out of these commercial products.    

LSAS has built a reputation as a trusted solution provider ranging from small commercial space ventures to large systems integrators and government customers. In addition to delivering the COTS software tools from our industry product partners, LSAS also delivers its own software solutions which leverage these technologies to offer complete space operations solutions tailored to the needs of our customers and their organizations. 

While we recognize the growth of the space operations business as a key focus area, LSAS continues to support mission design and operations in all domains, hence our company name's acronym, Land, Sea, Air and Space, and would love an opportunity to help customers achieve mission simulation and systems testing success across any/all domains! Contact LSAS Tec for more information on how we can help achieve your goals.
