Name: Intella
Description:
Intella builds data intelligence software for satellite operations. 

As satellite fleets grow, operations do not scale linearly.
What breaks first is not the spacecraft, but the human effort required to continuously confirm that everything is nominal across fragmented tools, shifts, and missions.

Intella is a space-technology company that helps satellite operators scale operations safely and reliably without increasing headcount, cognitive load, or operational risk.

Intella’s software platform, Mercury, acts as an operational intelligence layer on top of existing ground systems. It continuously reviews housekeeping telemetry in the background, filters out non-actionable noise, and surfaces only what truly requires human attention. This allows operators to spend less time on routine checks and more time on real operational decisions.

By automating nominal monitoring, detecting weak signals before static thresholds are breached, and structuring investigations into reusable operational workflows, Mercury enables calmer operations, faster triage, and consistent practices across fleets and missions.

Mercury is designed to augment—not replace—mission control systems and existing ground-segment tooling. It integrates into heterogeneous environments, supports progressive automation that operators can trust, and keeps humans in control at every step.

Intella works with several satellite operators, including D-Orbit, and with partners across the space ecosystem, like Telespazio, to deploy Mercury in live operational environments.

As fleets, missions, and service commitments grow, operational complexity becomes a silent risk. Intella exists to make that complexity manageable—so teams can scale with confidence, stay in control, and trust their operations as they grow beyond what manual processes and human attention alone can safely sustain.

Name: KosmosOne
Description:
KosmosOne is a space-biotech R&D company focused exclusively on aging and longevity therapeutics. We use microgravity as a practical tool to solve the formulation and manufacturing problems that cause promising molecules to fail on Earth stability, delivery, and CMC constraints.

Our work is built around tight, hypothesis-driven microgravity runs that measure what actually changes: nucleation and crystal growth kinetics, phase behavior, aggregation pathways, and colloidal stability. We then translate those insights into Earth-scalable manufacturing playbooks with clear parameters, analytics, and reproducibility targets.

On top of that, we apply AI to learn from every run optimizing design-of-experiments, narrowing the parameter space faster, and predicting failure modes earlier, so microgravity becomes a repeatable, data-driven manufacturing edge for longevity medicines.

Name: Neuraspace
Description:
Smarter Space Traffic Management.

To contribute to the evolution of spacecraft operations, Neuraspace aims to solve the space debris problem by protecting satellites operators from the losses caused by collisions, liabilities from leaving debris in orbit and allowing insurance companies to better price the risk that each satellite poses.

The continuous increase in space traffic and debris in the last years lead to a significant increase in the amount of information related to conjunction events. This situation poses a very complex challenge to space operators as the conjunction events need a careful “manual'' analysis, which is time, cost and effort consuming. AI (specifically ML) algorithms represent an essential technique in the automation of this process, mainly due to its ability to handle big amounts of data.

By using a data driven approach with state of the art Machine Learning techniques, Neuraspace is able to outperform the current methods, while automating the process of collision risk avoidance. Neuraspace enables the timely detection of up to 33% more high-risk collisions that have been so far undetected, thus securing space operations.

Description:

Through interactive learning, hands-on experiments, and expert-led sessions, we bring the universe closer to students from 6th to 10th grade, sparking their interest in astronomy, astrophysics, model rocketry, and stargazing.

Name: ESA Navigation
Description:

ESA Navigation has pioneered satnav since the 1980s, propelling Europe to the forefront of global navigation.

ESA’s Navigation Directorate leads the design, development and qualification of the EU programmes Galileo and EGNOS and is entrusted with R&D activities for their future through Horizon Europe.

With FutureNAV, ESA Navigation responds to the rapidly growing needs for more ubiquitous, resilient and reliable positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) through demonstrator missions such as Celeste and Genesis.

The Navigation Innovation and Support Program (NAVISP) is a public funding initiative and key enabler for European innovation and industrial competitiveness in PNT systems beyond satellite navigation.

Follow us for the latest news and collaboration opportunities from ESA's Directorate of Navigation.

Description:

Initié par le Ministère de l'Enseignement Supérieur, de la Recherche et de l'Innovation (MESRI) avec pour ambition de propulser le Sénégal sur la scène technologique mondiale, SENSAT vise à développer et lancer des satellites innovants conçus par des Sénégalais pour répondre aux défis environnementaux, économiques et technologiques du pays. ESRIC’s activities are based on four main pillars: space resources research and development; support for economic activities; knowledge management and community management. 

The Centre, launched in Novembre 2020, is an initiative of the Luxembourg Space Agency (LSA) and the Luxembourg Institute of Science and Technology(LIST) in strategic partnership with the European Space Agency (ESA).

Notre objectif est de renforcer les compétences techniques et professionnelles des étudiants à travers des projets concrets, des workshops, des formations, des compétitions technologiques et des collaborations avec des entreprises et des experts du domaine. Le club vise également à créer un environnement d’échange, de créativité et de développement personnel, permettant aux membres de se préparer efficacement au monde professionnel et aux défis de l’industrie. Innovation | Mécatronique | Technologie | Formation | Esprit d’équipe

Description: Founded in 2022, our company has evolved towards innovation in the space technology industry, driven by ambition and the constant pursuit of excellence. With humble beginnings and bold dreams, we set out on a mission to redefine space missions. Today, XDLINX has a dedicated team of bright minds, each bringing unique expertise to our mission. The Star Catcher Network wirelessly transmits concentrated solar energy via optical power beaming directly to satellites' existing solar arrays — no retrofit required. 

By enabling up to 10x more power generation than onboard systems alone, we’re ending power scarcity in orbit and enabling the space industrial revolution.

The South African National Space Agency was created to promote the use of space and cooperation in space-related activities while fostering research in space science, advancing scientific engineering through the development of our human capital and supporting industrial development in space technologies.

UNAUTHORISED USE OF SANSA NAME, ABBREVIATION AND LOGO

1. The South African National Space Agency would like to remind all stakeholders and partners that the name ‘South African National Space Agency’ and its abbreviation ‘SANSA’ and the logo of SANSA are all trademarks of SANSA.
2. Therefore, the use of any of these trademarks may only be made with the express written authorisation of SANSA. Unauthorised use constitutes a trademark infringement in respect of which penalties may be imposed by a court of law.
3. Unauthorised use includes:-
- the use of the words South African National Space Agency and/or the abbreviation SANSA in e-mail addresses, on websites, social media platforms, documentation, marketing material or any other materials; and
- the use of the SANSA logo or aspects of the logo on any websites, social media platforms, documentation, marketing material or any other material.
4. Persons that are using these trademarks are requested to change their e-mail addresses, website domain names, and remove all elements from their websites, social media platforms, documentation or other material with IMMEDIATE EFFECT.
5. Failure to comply will result in SANSA commencing legal proceedings against such person(s), in order to protect its intellectual property.

Name: Axelspace アクセルスペース
Description:
Axelspace is a pioneer in microsatellite technology, with the vision "Space within Your Reach." We have developed and operated 11 satellites so far. We are creating a new future for space utilization as an international team from more than 30 countries.

We provide 1) Earth Observation Platform "AxelGlobe" with 5-satellite constellation and 2) One stop service for microsatellites "AxelLiner". AxelGlobe captures anywhere on Earth once in a few days. 

For the details, please check our website!

Also you can find us
on Twitter @theaxelspace
on Instagram @theaxelspace

Name: Aalyria
Description:
Aalyria enables commercial and government customers to operate resilient, high-throughput networks in motion. Aalyria brings together two technologies originally developed at Alphabet: 
1) Tightbeam - Proven ultra-high-speed Free Space Optical Communication capability
2) Spacetime - A managed PaaS for developers to orchestrate networks in motion

It is backed by leading Silicon Valley investors including the founders of Accel and J2 Ventures.

Name: Gimbal Space
Description:
Gimbal Space provides low-cost, reliable, and readily-available components for LEO 

We also provide integrated & custom solutions suited to your requirements.

Name: Kayhan Space
Description: For operators of small or large constellations of satellites who need to have a complete situational awareness of their fleet with utmost reliability, Kayhan provides a fully scalable cloud-based collision avoidance service. Unlike other services, Kayhan’s notification system easily integrates with any form of notification channel and ensures that the operators are aware of the upcoming high-interest events. Kayhan uses complex mathematical algorithms and advanced machine learning techniques to aid operators to make the safest avoidance decisions, because at Kayhan we understand that a safe space domain is the best space to operate in.

Name: AGNIRATH AEROSPACE AND DEFENCE RESEARCH PRIVATE LIMITED
Description:
Welcome to Agnirath Aerospace and Defence Research Private Limited, where innovation, education, and science come together to shape the future of space. Our focus lies in education through STEM projects, specifically within the realm of space exploration.
Through a variety of mediums, including webinars, seminars, and workshops, we offer engaging platforms for enthusiasts and learners alike to delve into the wonders of space. Our webinars delve into topics such as space technology, aerospace advancements, and astronomy, providing accessible and informative sessions for our audience.

Moreover, our seminars serve as opportunities for in-depth discussions and knowledge-sharing on subjects spanning space technology, aerospace innovations, and astronomy. These events are meticulously organized and conducted to ensure a high-quality learning experience for all participants.

Our workshops on aerospace technology, astronomy, and sky observation offer hands-on learning experiences, allowing participants to explore the intricacies of space firsthand. Equipped with three telescopes, we provide individuals with the opportunity to gaze at the stars and celestial bodies, accompanied by insightful guidance on identifying various stars and constellations.

Having pioneered these educational initiatives for the years, we formalized our operations with registration in 2023, ensuring professionalism and commitment to our mission. With participation from thousands of students in our webinars, seminars, and workshops, we are proud to cultivate a growing interest in space exploration among young minds.

Name: SDA TAP Lab
Description: The Space Domain Awareness TAP Lab ensures space superiority missions succeed by rapidly onboarding apps to close gaps. We decompose kill chains, prioritize needs with operators, map needs to technologies, and onboard technology to existing platforms -- getting solutions into operations quickly. We need to partner with industry, academia, and across the government to succeed.

Name: Little Place Labs
Description:
Little Place Labs brings space-based insights to the end users in 7 minutes, thereby reducing latency by several hours/days and reducing the data downlink by up to 98%. 

LPL is making satellites and spacecraft smarter, more intelligent and autonomous - with the power of Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence - and leveraging Edge Computing.

We are a global team of space and technology professionals with several years of industry experience under our belt.

Name: AstroAgency
Description:
AstroAgency is the world’s first strategic communications firm dedicated exclusively to the commercial space sector. 

By combining marketing expertise with our team’s technical understanding and space industry experience, we’ve successfully delivered projects with more than 80 international space companies, as well as several governments and academic institutions around the world (including national Space Agencies and regional economic development bodies leveraging space to bring prosperity through job creation and satellite data applications). 

We support existing space companies within the launch supply chain through to downstream data analytics, and non-space organisations looking to take advantage of the growing space economy, to shape their space sector messaging and target their outreach. We provide an integrated, full-service solution that combines strategic communications, digital marketing, PR, networking introductions, events strategies, market guidance and investment support, all underpinned by market intelligence.

AstroAgency - Changing the Perception of Space.

Name: FarEarth Labs
Description:
FarEarth is an image pre-processing and data management software for Earth observation satellites. 

FarEarth can handle anything from a small mission with uncomplicated processing pipelines to multiple constellations with a variety of imagers. We support thermal, multispectral, and hyperspectral missions.


Monitoring change in FarEarth
Conveniently monitor ground displacement, mine tailings, and ESG impact for all your sites remotely in a single interface.

Name: Pinkmatter Europe
Description:
FarEarth is an image pre-processing and data management software for Earth observation satellites. 

FarEarth can handle anything from a small mission with uncomplicated processing pipelines to multiple constellations with a variety of sensors. We support thermal, multispectral, and hyperspectral missions.

Name: IRAP - Institut de Recherche en Astrophysique et Planétologie
Description:
IRAP’s scientific objectives concern the study and understanding of the Universe and its contents: the Earth as a planet, its ionized space environment, the sun and its planets, the stars and their planetary systems, the interstellar environment , galaxies, the very first stars and the primordial Big Bang.
With activities of design, construction, integration and operation of instruments on the ground and in space as well as laboratory experiments allowing the characterization of physical processes, IRAP is one of the major poles of ground-space astrophysics in France.
IRAP has numerous collaborations both regionally and internationally with organizations such as CNES, ESA, ESO, NASA, JAXA …
IRAP benefits from numerous fundings through calls for projects, notably within the framework of the National Research Agency (ANR) and the Horizon 2020 program of the European Union.

Name: Cognitive Space
Description: Cognitive Space brings an AI-SaaS solution for intelligent satellite automation to empower and enable the use of space. We work with satellite operators looking for an effective and automated mission planning solution to improve yield and reduce latencies in scheduling, and with Government entities looking to make better use of space capacity.

Name: Cosmicport
Description: At Cosmicport, our journey is an opus of innovation, ambition, and vision. We’re reshaping the cosmos with cutting-edge technology, sustainability, and excellence.

Name: Surrey Space
Description:
This is the home of the Surrey Space Institute – our new and ambitious, pan-university interdisciplinary hub. We’re channelling the expertise of the Surrey Space Centre – our world leading Centre of Excellence in Space Engineering – and its astonishingly successful spin-out, Surrey Satellite Technology Limited. 

Launched in 2025, the Surrey Space Institute harnesses forty-five years of Surrey satellite leadership to power bold, cross-disciplinary breakthroughs for today’s pressing challenges.

Working across engineering, law, bioscience, AI and beyond, we build missions that safeguard Earth’s resources, shore up critical orbital infrastructure, and unlock new frontiers for explorers en route to the Moon, Mars and beyond. 

We work hand in hand with government, companies and universities, locally through Space South Central and through strong national and global partnerships, to push research from concept to orbit. 

By expanding Surrey’s renowned degrees, short courses and live mission opportunities, we cultivate the skilled minds that tomorrow’s space sector needs. 

Through our SpaceCraft programme, our doors are open to organisations including businesses and colleges who can use our facilities and expertise to create space-flight equipment and conduct space experiments. SpaceCraft also enables students and companies to develop practical engineering skills through training.

Follow us to keep space resilient, hear of the latest space innovation and watch our launchpad for human discovery take shape.

See the Surrey Space Centre website at https://www.surrey.ac.uk/surrey-space-centre.

Name: Vyoma
Description: Vyoma plans to build and operate an innovative, satellite-based observation system for space debris monitoring. Being space-borne allows us to operate our sensors continuously, sweeping a large volume of space, and re-observing objects with unprecedented frequency. Together with state-of-the-art data processing algorithms, we will provide services such as debris cataloguing, collision warnings, up to the full automation of planning and performing evasive manoeuvres to ensure safe and efficient satellite operations.

Name: NorthStar Earth & Space
Description:
NorthStar seeks to empower humanity to preserve our planet through a unique Space and Earth information & intelligence platform using space-based sensors. NorthStar strives to help transform the way governments, industry and institutions assess risk, enforce regulations and make decisions to foster the sustainable development of our planet and deliver a safe and secure near-Earth environment.

NorthStar’s space-based commercial Space Situational Awareness services address many of the critical and immediate challenges facing all satellite operators.  Striving to see every object in every orbit, NorthStar will deliver more frequent and precise observations of resident space objects than any other current system. Through a suite of high-speed decision quality information services derived from its unparalleled coverage, object custody, and enhanced predictive analytics, NorthStar generates its Space Information & Intelligence services.

Name: Space Zone India
Description: Space Zone India is a space company located in Chennai with the goal of providing low-cost, long-term solutions in the space industry. Space Zone India (SZI) provides hands on training experience on Aerodynamic principles, Satellite Technology, Drone Technology and Rocket Technology. It also creates awareness on the career options pertaining to this industry. SZI works with Private institutions, Engineering and Art and Science colleges, Private and Government schools. The organization is supported by a team of professionals in Satellite Technology, Rocket Technology, Drone Technology, and other fields.

Name: Scandron
Description:
Welcome to Scandron Pvt. Ltd., your trusted partner in the world of advanced drone technology and services. As a leading drone manufacturing and service provider, we cater to a diverse range of industries, including industrial, commercial, agricultural, and defense sectors.

 Our Expertise:
At Scandron, we specialize in a wide array of services that drive innovation and efficiency. Our offerings encompass drone-based confined space inspections, precision thickness measurements at height, GIS services, comprehensive building inspection solutions, and the development of specialized agri-drones, customized drones, cargo drones, and logistics drones.

 Driving 'Make in India':
Our commitment runs deep in supporting the "Make in India" initiative. We are dedicated to developing indigenous drone and robot technology, reducing our dependence on imported solutions. This, in turn, empowers various sectors, such as aerospace, aviation, logistics, defense, and agriculture, with enhanced drone and robotics performance.

 Setting Industry Standards:
Scandron takes pride in designing and manufacturing drones that adhere to the highest industry standards of safety and workmanship. Our cutting-edge drone systems leverage the latest technology to deliver top-notch solutions, ensuring optimal performance and safety.

 DAAS (Drone as a Service) Excellence:
We are trailblazers in providing Drone as a Service (DAAS) solutions. With Scandron, industries can now conduct inspections at a fraction of the cost and time compared to traditional methods, all while ensuring the safety of on-ground inspectors.

Join us in shaping the future of drone technology and explore the endless possibilities it offers. Together, we can elevate your business to new heights with Scandron Pvt. Ltd.  #ScandronInnovates #DroneTechnology #MakeInIndia

Name: Cornwall Space Cluster
Description: Welcome to the Cornwall Space Cluster.

Name: Skyline Space
Description: Dedicated to explore the universe and expand our knowledge of the cosmos

Name: SARsatX
Description:
We provide Earth Observation data and analytics using ML models to serve the Agriculture, Environment, Financial, Energy, and Mining sectors. We provide reliable insights from SPACE for better decisions on EARTH.

Join us here: https://sarsatx.notion.site/Careers-at-SARsatX

Name: World Space Week
Description: World Space Week (WSW), October 4-10 annually, is the biggest space event on Earth. Declared by the UN General Assembly in 1999, WSW has grown to over 16,000 events held in 83 countries.  To join the celebration, or see what's planned near you, please visit www.worldspaceweek.org.  WSW is open to all, with events held by schools, space agencies, aerospace companies, museums, astronomy clubs, and individuals.  It is coordinated globally by World Space Week Association, a non-profit organization managed by an international volunteer Board of Directors and supported by voluntary donations.

Name: SpaceFounders
Description:
SpaceFounders is a unique European community driving momentum in the space industry. 

A partnership between the French space Agency, the Italian space agency and the Bundeswehr University Munich, we’re uniquely placed to bring private and public sector pioneers together to create the space leaders of tomorrow.

Name: Space Concordia
Description:
Space Concordia is an award-winning, multidisciplinary student space program dedicated to the inspiration and education of the next generation of space engineers.

Space Concordia designs and builds novel space technologies, notably launching the first student-built satellite in Quebec to orbit, and firing the most powerful student-built rocket engine in the world. SC also works on advanced Mars rover analogues and performs space related health research.

Name: Space Network
Description:
Founded in 2023, Space Network has become the world’s fastest-growing space sector ecosystem. 𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲 𝘆𝗲𝘁 𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗳𝘂𝗹: To build the world’s most connected and commercially impactful space sector ecosystem - an international network of locally led hubs under the Space Network global brand and leadership.

At 𝗦𝗽𝗮𝗰𝗲 𝗡𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸, we are more than just a network - we are ecosystem builders. Our support is grounded in three core pillars:

𝗦𝘂𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁 | 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁 | 𝗙𝘂𝗻𝗱

We provide tailored business guidance, meaningful connections between talent, suppliers and intelligent funding solutions to empower space companies to innovate, scale and thrive.

Our values are the foundation of everything we do.

𝗦𝘂𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 – We empower businesses and individuals to achieve their full potential through guidance, collaboration, and opportunity.
𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗿𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 – We drive economic growth by fostering commercially viable opportunities, ensuring sustainability and scalability for the space sector.
𝗔𝗺𝗯𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝘂𝘀 – We champion bold thinking, innovation, and the drive to push boundaries in the global space economy.

𝙅𝙤𝙞𝙣 𝙪𝙨 𝙖𝙨 𝙬𝙚 𝙝𝙚𝙡𝙥 𝙨𝙝𝙖𝙥𝙚 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙛𝙪𝙩𝙪𝙧𝙚 𝙤𝙛 𝙨𝙥𝙖𝙘𝙚 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙙𝙧𝙞𝙫𝙚 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙣𝙚𝙭𝙩 𝙬𝙖𝙫𝙚 𝙤𝙛 𝙞𝙣𝙣𝙤𝙫𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣, 𝙜𝙧𝙤𝙬𝙩𝙝, 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙤𝙥𝙥𝙤𝙧𝙩𝙪𝙣𝙞𝙩𝙮.

Space Network is delighted to have Sustainable Alpha, Burness Paull, Eureka!Europe, Johnston Carmichael, Marks & Clerk, ID Cyber Solutions, PortfolioXD and The Events Hub as our founding partners.

Name: SkyServe
Description: SkyServe is an AI & edge computing technology company for satellites platforms, enabling analytics  providers and core industries to deploy applications for earth observation and other space based solutions.

Name: AIRMO
Description:
AIRMO is an international climate and space tech company with German roots. We monitor methane emissions using proprietary instruments: spectrometer and atmospheric LiDAR. Our machine learning learning algorithm runs processing enabling leakage detection and quantification. 

We use in-house built instruments to measure methane from the ground, from the air, and - starting next year - from space. 

We are a VCs and European Space Agency (ESA) backed startup with the goal to become the global leader in methane emissions monitoring.

Name: NATO Space Centre of Excellence
Description:
NATO Space COE is not a part of the NATO Command Structure or other NATO entities nor an operational entity. Its Steering Committee (SC) is the body responsible for guidance and supervision of all activities and administration of the COE, within the framework of the Operational Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) that was signed by 15 sponsoring nations (Kingdom of Belgium, the Czech Republic, the French Republic, the Federal Republick of Germany, the Hellenic Republic, the Italian Republic, the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, the Kingdom of the Netherlands, the Republic of North Macedonia, the Republic of Poland, the Republic of Portugal, Romania, the Kingdom of Spain, the Republic of Türkiye, and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland) in January 2023.
The role of the NATO Space COE is to act as a catalyst for the comprehension, improvement, and transformation of Space Power by delivering effective and efficient solutions to the benefit of NATO and its Allies.
This role includes: Concept development and experimentation; Development of space military doctrine and standardization; Education, training and exercise; and Analysis and lessons learned process.
The NATO Space COE will provide knowledge, analysis, and products covering the three operational Space functions (Space Domain Awareness, Operational Space Support, and Space Domain Coordination), stimulate Space expertise sharing and interoperability, and constitute a bridge between NATO and all relevant national and international Space organizations from various sectors (Defense, Civil, Industry, Academia and Research).

Name: General Galactic
Description: General Galactic is a deep-tech startup manufacturing platforms for space mobility and propellant generation.

Name: Neurom Innovations
Description:
Neurom Innovations is a cutting-edge technology company driving innovation in Space Technology, Agentic AI, Digital Twins, IoT, and Product Development. We deliver advanced solutions that help businesses accelerate growth, improve operational efficiency, and make data-driven decisions.

At Neurom, we blend deep technical expertise with real-world problem solving to:
• Revolutionize aerospace and space exploration technologies
• Transform industries with next-gen AI systems and autonomous agents
• Enable real-time monitoring and predictive analytics with Digital Twins
• Build smart connected ecosystems using IoT solutions
• Craft impactful products from concept to completion

Our solutions empower organizations across sectors such as aerospace, manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, construction, oil & gas, and automotive

Name: Colombian Space Agency - CSA
Description:
The Colombian Space Agency (CSA; Spanish: Agencia Espacial de Colombia, AEC), was established on October 4, 2017 in Bogotá D.C. as a private, nonprofit corporation, created with the aim of articulate the academy, the government and the private sector and development the aerospace services and technologies industry in Colombia.
The board of directors is formed by interdisciplinary organizations from different economic sectors such as the National Planning Department, the Planetarium of Bogota, the Mercantile Exchange of Colombia, the Embassy of France and the Colombian Areonautic Industry Corporation CIAC, among others.
In October of 2018, AEC was named as an official member of the International Astronautical Federation - IAF. Its founder is the PHd. Pilar Zamora Acevedo.

Name: Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Comission (SUPARCO)
Description: Realizing the importance of Space Science and Technology applications for sustainable national development, the Government of Pakistan established Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission. Being the National Space Agency of Pakistan, SUPARCO is mandated to conduct research and development work in the field of space science, technology and its applications for peaceful purposes and socio economic uplift of country. Its headquarter is located at Islamabad and technical facilities are spread over Karachi, Lahore, Multan, Quetta, Peshawar and Gilgit. It has state-of-the-art research, development and manufacturing facilities of satellite manufacturing, space sciences, technology and applications. It undertakes a diverse range of research & development activities in the field of space science, technology and its applications as well as satellite design and development programmes. SUPARCO has a strong element of international cooperation and strongly believes in peaceful uses of space science & technology for the well-being of humanity.

Name: Ubotica Technologies
Description:
Founded in 2016, Ubotica Technologies is the leader in SPACE AI, providing the intelligence that makes satellites truly smart. Our onboard processing solutions enable satellites to think, see, and act autonomously, delivering real-time, actionable insights while reducing data bottlenecks and latency.

We collaborate with leading space organizations, including NASA, and ESA to advance the capabilities of autonomous satellite operations. From intelligent space cameras to autonomous satellite collaboration, we are shaping the future of space intelligence.

Discover how Ubotica is revolutionizing satellite operations—visit our website to explore our cutting-edge SPACE AI solutions.

Name: Orbital Paradigm
Description: Reusable in-space transportation: launch, operate, return, repeat.

Name: TerraWatch Space
Description: Strategic Advisory, Market Intelligence and Communications firm focused on the Earth Observation sector.

Name: AVS Added Value Solutions
Description:
AVS Added Value Solutions (AVS) is an international SME which provides complex instrumentation that boost the value of our customer throughout a wide range of applications. AVS is strongly focused on the development of outstanding devices, instruments, mechatronics and vacuum systems. Our expertise covers design, manufacturing, assembly, tests and supply, covering the whole product lifecycle for our customers. 

AVS skills on engineering design, mechatronics, instrumentation, thermal-vacuum systems, micro-mechanisms, opto-mechatronics, and high-precision positioning systems in hazards environments (vacuum, radiation, high magnetic fields, cryogenics...). We "boost scientific knowledge" in the fields of Accelerators, Astronomy, Nuclear Fusion, and Space.

And all is done under the ISO 9001 and EN 9100 certifications, with the advantage of the on-site manufacturing workshops, assembly, integration and cleaning labs, clean rooms, thermal and fluidic testing facilities, bake-out chambers, dust vacuum chambers, propulsion testing labs, etc. across the main 6 sites of the group (3 in Spain, 2 in the UK and 1 in the US).

Name: catalyx space
Description:
We're building AWS for space, providing space experiments and technology demonstrations across LEO, deep space, and lunar orbit. 

Our offerings include unmanned labs, testbeds for both dedicated and rideshare missions, and re-entry capsules for payload retrieval. 

We simplify access to space with affordable pricing, end-to-end solutions, no minimum mass limits, zero integration fees, and quick deployment (6 months or less). 

We serve biopharma, semiconductors, materials science, defense, and more.

Name: IAC2024
Description: The IAC - International Astronautical Congress, the one place and time of the year where all global space actors come together, is delighted to welcome the international space community in Milan, Italy for its 75th Congress whose theme is “Responsible space for sustainability”. #IAC2024

Name: SpaceFactory
Description:
SpaceFactory is at the frontier of developing groundbreaking technologies for building in Space. But our innovations don't stop there. We 'spin out' these space-age solutions to engineer world-class 3D printing machines, high-performance materials, and intuitive software for a full spectrum of industries right here on Earth.

Our mission is to be the first builders on the Moon and beyond, laying the foundations of humanity’s future in Space and harnessing those discoveries into an era of sustainable construction on Earth.

Name: GEOSAT
Description:
We bring the potential of Earth Observation to People, Businesses and Institutions around the world. Our capabilities and experience, combined with our flexibility to adapt to customer needs, allow us to maximize value, helping to address global challenges from Space.

GEOSAT, Your Eyes on Site.

Name: Space Happy Hour
Description:
Space Happy Hours are informal gatherings of companies in the space industry as well those in the broader space ecosystem. We offer lively events at breweries as well as exclusive events at space companies. We also have invite-only marquee events such as astronaut appearances or new space book signings. There are also “Spaceball,” gatherings at select Major League Baseball games. 

Now offered in nine major cities, with 75 events in 2025, Space Happy Hours are a great way to connect with space industry peers, learn about new innovations, recruit employees, find jobs in the industry, make new friends, and simply have a fun evening.

Name: Spaceflux
Description: Spaceflux is a UK-based seed-stage company founded in 2022 with the aim of tackling the problem of space congestion in view of rising satellite deployment. Leveraging on our proprietary optical sensor network and a unique, AI-driven analytics platform, we provide unparalleled tracking and mapping services for space objects. Our dynamic analytics can offer actionable insights about objects’ characteristics and behaviors derived from static image data. Catering primarily to satellite operators, governments, and the insurance industry, our goal is to reduce congestion and create a safer space environment. We are driven by an exceptional team and thrive on innovation.

Name: World Space Week Association
Description: World Space Week (WSW), October 4-10 annually, is the biggest space event on Earth. Declared by the UN General Assembly in 1999, WSW has grown to over 16,000 events held in 83 countries.  To join the celebration, or see what's planned near you, please visit www.worldspaceweek.org.  WSW is open to all, with events held by schools, space agencies, aerospace companies, museums, astronomy clubs, and individuals.  It is coordinated globally by World Space Week Association, a non-profit organization managed by an international volunteer Board of Directors and supported by voluntary donations.

Name: EMROD
Description:
Emrod shapes a world where energy transmission is not constrained by wires. 

Our power beaming technology can take on some of the world's biggest energy and environmental challenges.

Find out more on www.emrod.energy

Name: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem
Description:
The Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE) is the next step in the evolution of Earth observation data. The ecosystem offers immediate access to large amounts of open and free Earth observation data from the Copernicus Sentinel satellites, including both new and historical Sentinel images, as well as Copernicus Contributing Missions.

CDSE offers comprehensive browsing, interactive exploration, and in-depth processing capabilities to unlock the full potential of Earth observation data. CDSE aims to support users in building various applications needed to provide accurate, timely and objective information which are crucial to create a more sustainable future.

As the primary source of Copernicus Sentinel data, CDSE integrates open APIs and standards such as STAC, OGC, and openEO. Designed for seamless integration and federation, CDSE allows users to design, prototype, and operate applications while maintaining control over data access, visualization, user identity, processing integration, and data privacy. Explore how CDSE combines publicly funded services with commercial offerings to support a wide range of use cases.

Discover more at https://dataspace.copernicus.eu/ and follow us to stay informed!

Name: Rogue Space Systems Corporation
Description: In this New Space Age access to space is expanding exponentially, opening up new opportunities for innovators and explorers never before imagined.  Rogue designs satellite vehicles and subsystems to provide on-orbit services to satellite operators both now and well into the future.  Building from there, we look to provide a vital role in setting the standard for these services in support of the New Space Economy.

Name: Open Lunar Foundation
Description:
Open Lunar is an independent nonprofit organisation operating globally to create a peaceful, self-sustaining presence on the Moon.

We focus on contributing to progress and innovation in technical standards, policy and regulation, and shared infrastructure and hardware.

Our projects are sourced in partnership with a global inter-disciplinary professional network and developed through projects that benefit from independent evaluation, coaching and financial support.

Name: PAVE Space SA
Description:
PAVE Space is a Swiss company built on the success of the Gruyère Space Program, which developed Europe’s first reusable rocket demonstrator.

Its expertise spans space propulsion, control algorithms, and rapid integration of complex systems.

PAVE Space now targets last-mile satellite delivery, using its kickstage to move multi-ton payloads from low Earth orbit to any orbit within 24 hours.

Name: Navigate Commodities
Description:
Navigate Commodities monitors the movement & consumption of dry bulk commodities over land & sea using the most powerful technologies available.

We help commodity professionals identify real-world opportunities by revealing company-specific real-time trade and smelting activity.
Location: Downtown Core, Central Singapore Community Development Council

Name: Acceleron Aerospace
Description: Acceleron Aerospace is a dynamic startup focused on revolutionizing the field of space exploration and education. Our mission is to inspire and educate students of all ages, foster groundbreaking research, and develop cutting-edge space propulsion technologies for ambitious interplanetary missions. With our comprehensive range of services and partnerships with esteemed institutions, we aim to propel the future of space exploration.

Name: Mission Control
Description:
Mission Control empowers explorers by innovating to make advanced software viable for use in space. Using our software, customers can simplify mission development and operations while unlocking the potential of new scientific and commercial opportunities on the Earth, Moon, Mars, and beyond. 
Our software is used by mission controllers, scientists, and software developers who seek faster deployments, lower-cost mission development, and valuable data returns.

Name: African Space Leadership Institute (ASLI)
Description: The African Space Leadership Institute (ASLI) is an independent non-profit organisation, committed to developing Africa's capabilities in space policy, strategy, law, governance and leadership.

Name: Etlaq Spaceport
Description: Etlaq Spaceport, launched by Oman's NASCOM in 2022, capitalizes on the unique geography of Wilayat Al Duqm, strategically positioning Oman in the global space sector. NASCOM, a key player in Oman's space industry, prioritizes national capacity building and international partnerships. Etlaq aims to be a comprehensive space launch complex with three launch pads, catering to global companies for diverse orbits. This project aligns with Oman's commitment to advancing its space capabilities. NASCOM unveiled Etlaq's master plan at the January 2024 Middle East Space Conference in Muscat, showcasing Oman's advancements in space technology and collaboration on a global scale.

Name: Tekrevolution
Description: Space & Defence systems design, validation, production and qualification.

Name: Agence sénégalaise d'études spatiales-ASES
Description:
Une agence transversale au service du développement.

Name: Zero-G
Description:
Zero-G is the world leader in zero gravity flights. As the first and only FAA-approved provider of zero gravity flights, Zero-G does it all: Astronaut training; scientific research; corporate incentives; entertainment, TV, & film; and even public flights.

With a spotless safety record over the past 19 years, Zero-G has made the excitement and adventure of space accessible to the general public. Whether you’re a training astronaut, a scientist conducting groundbreaking research, or a civilian pursuing a lifelong dream, it all starts in the same place–a Zero-G flight.

Name: Space Ocean Corp
Description:
Space Ocean is pioneering the indispensable logistics layer for space operations, solving critical challenges that enable a thriving space economy. We specialize in sustainable resource management solutions for the next frontier of human activity.

Our mission centers on three core pillars: enabling a thriving orbital economy through innovative logistics infrastructure, making transformative investments in space infrastructure development, and building a seasoned team with growing talent across the space industry.

As space commercialization accelerates, reliable logistics become essential for sustainable operations beyond Earth. Space Ocean positions itself at the forefront of this transformation, providing the foundational infrastructure that space-based businesses need to succeed.

We're actively seeking strategic investors, forward-thinking customers, and innovative partners to join us in reshaping the future of space logistics and sustainability. Whether you're looking to invest in the space economy, require logistics solutions for your space operations, or want to collaborate on groundbreaking projects, Space Ocean offers the expertise and vision to make it happen.

Join us as we build the essential infrastructure for humanity's expansion into space. We are hiring! https://spaceoceancorp.com/careers

Name: Alpha Impulsion
Description:
Alpha Impulsion is pioneering the autophage rocket engine to propel the next generation of light launchers. By using the fuel as the rocket’s structure, we eliminate the need for fuel tanks, multiple stages, and other heavy components that don’t contribute to propulsion. Based in France and Italy, we aim at building the most cost-efficient European launcher for small satellites by 2028  

We are proud to be trusted by numerous European partners, including CNES, ESA BIC Sud France & Turin, the Takeoff accelerator & Bpifrance.

Name: ANU Institute for Space | InSpace
Description:
InSpace connects space research at Australian National University with society's biggest challenges to deliver positive impact. We do this by shaping and growing the Australian space ecosystem in partnership with Academia, Industry, Government, and Communities. 

The Australian National University Institute for Space operates under the Research & Innovation Portfolio as a team of individuals dedicated to the common goal of crossing disciplinary borders and developing new opportunities for collaborations in space research across ANU, the Australian space sector, and globally.

Space is more than the traditional image of astronauts exploring our solar system or astronomers looking through telescopes. Space is about innovation, technological advancement, and making life better for people on Earth. It’s about satellites and the valuable data they can collect from space, space health and medicine advances that translate to your local hospital, and rovers and robotics that work off-world and in places on Earth that humans cannot reach.

ANU InSpace works with Academia, Industry, Government, and Community partners to catalyse space research. We engage with the global space industry, bringing cross-disciplinary capability together to solve national challenges and enable ANU and Australia to contribute to global initiatives such as climate and water resilience, remote healthcare access, and regional security.

InSpace team members are advocates for space innovation. They represent ANU, its space capabilities, and world-leading research expertise as members and leaders of influential groups that are shaping the future of Australia’s space industry.

Name: Katalyst Space Technologies
Description:
At Katalyst Space Technologies, our goal is to fundamentally shake up how the satellite industry works. We’re a R&D team focused on next-gen satellite and mission designs that break through the long cycle times, rigid architectures, and inflexibility in mission planning that dominates the space industry. We incorporate robotic servicing and autonomous space infrastructure into our satellite technology to service MEO, GEO, and XGEO / Cislunar markets. 

Founded in 2019 by a small team and headquartered in Flagstaff, AZ, the Katalyst team is made up of dynamic innovators, looking to make an impact.

Astralytical takes space consulting beyond the conventional, uniting diverse sectors within aerospace to pave unique profitable paths. We’re your dedicated space industry navigator, operating with a depth of expertise that underpins your journey toward success. 

We develop sophisticated market entry and expansion strategies that equip our clients with the intelligence they need to thrive. Azista hosts India's largest satellite mass manufacturing facility, with all the testing facilities under one roof.

Azista's strategic business unit, "APRIL – Advanced Pixel Research & Intelligence Lab," is built to cater to customer requirements for data processing and analytics using state-of-the-art AI/ML algorithms. Name: IEEE AESS YU
Description:
Welcome to the IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Society (AESS) at Yarmouk University (YU), a community of professionals dedicated to advancing aerospace and electronic systems engineering. Established in 2022, our chapter is committed to fostering innovation, collaboration, and knowledge sharing among its members.

Our Services:

1. Technical Publications: AESS YU members have access to technical publications, including the renowned IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems, conference proceedings, and other resources covering the latest advancements in the field.

2. Conferences and Events: AESS YU organizes and sponsors conferences, workshops, and symposia, providing members with opportunities to present research, network with fellow professionals, and stay up-to-date with industry trends.

3. Educational Resources: AESS YU is committed to the professional development of its members, offering educational resources such as webinars, tutorials, and online courses to help engineers expand their knowledge and skills.

4. Technical Committees: Our technical committees focus on specific areas of interest within aerospace and electronic systems, providing members with opportunities to collaborate on research projects, share knowledge, and contribute to the development of industry standards.

5. Networking and Collaboration: AESS YU fosters a strong sense of community among its members, providing numerous opportunities for networking, mentorship, and collaboration through local chapters, online forums, and social media platforms.

Our Commitment:

At IEEE AESS YU, we are dedicated to promoting the growth and development of the aerospace and electronic systems engineering community. Our commitment to excellence, innovation, and collaboration ensures that our members have access to the resources and support they need to excel in their careers and contribute to the advancement of the field.

Thank you for your interest in IEEE AESS YU.

Description: Lunar Helium-3 Mining, LLC (LH3M) is solely focused on advancing practical technologies for detecting and extracting Lunar Helium-3, a critical resource for achieving global energy independence through nuclear fusion. We are strongly aligned with the President’s clean energy initiative with respect to fusion power commercialization.  LH3M’s primary objective is to establish sustainable and practical processes for extracting and transporting He-3 from the Moon to Earth. We are focused on continuing to advance our Technology Readiness Levels strategically and methodically by working “within” the constraints of the environment of the moon.   Leveraging Helium-3 as a primary fuel source for nuclear fusion has the potential to replace reliance on nuclear fission and fossil fuels, paving the way for a low-cost, truly global green energy solution worldwide.

Name: QUASAR ASTRONOMY
Description:
QUASAR ASTRONOMY is an educational initiative and astronomy community based in Morocco, founded by astronomy enthusiast Mohammed Haimoud. Our mission is to make astronomy accessible, engaging, and inspiring for people of all ages and backgrounds.

We offer hands-on astronomical experiences through public observation nights, astrophotography workshops, school outreach, and online educational content. We also proudly run the COSMAN Astronomical Observatory — a homemade, DIY observatory built from recycled materials and driven by a passion for space.

Through collaboration, education, and science communication, we aim to cultivate curiosity and deepen understanding of the universe, especially among youth and underserved communities in rural and urban areas.

We believe astronomy is not only a science but also a powerful tool for unity, inspiration, and ambition. We are actively involved in events across Morocco and maintain a growing network of amateur and professional astronomers, educators, and science lovers globally.

Whether you're a curious beginner or a seasoned stargazer, QUASAR ASTRONOMY invites you to look up, get involved, and discover the universe with us.

 Based in Casablanca, Morocco  
 Motto: “Enjoy Looking up, it is free.”

Name: Astro Tech Hub
Description:
We are focusing on multiple facts regarding the Earth and the Space mechanism, mainly on the solar systems including the Sun and the planets belonging to it, aims to understand the workings of our solar system, galaxy, and beyond, promote interdisciplinary collaboration and cooperation, ensuring that the most significant and complex questions in space science can be addressed. The facility also provides valuable teaching and training opportunities for students and scholars through academic programs and internships.

The research teams come from diverse backgrounds, including astrophysics, cosmology, geology, and engineering. These teams work together to undertake comprehensive studies which explore the far reaches of space and time. Some of the research conducted includes the investigation of black holes, the exploration of exoplanets, the search for evidence of extraterrestrial life, the study of gravitational waves, and the analysis of cosmic background radiation. Also encompasses theoretical astrophysics, seeking to develop coherent explanations and models for the workings of the cosmos.

We are a highly advanced research facility designed to advance our understanding of the cosmos and promote meaningful scientific collaboration. It strives to push the boundaries of human knowledge and to inspire scientific and intellectual curiosity in the broader community.

Name: SwanSEDS
Description:
SwanSEDS is Swansea University's UKSEDS (UK Students for the Exploration and Development of Space) branch launched in 2019. We are comprised of a team of resolute and passionate Students, who are enthusiastic about innovating the future of spaceflight.

Last year in 2022-2023, we took part in the National Rocketry Challenge, hosted by UKSEDS, coming seventh on our first attempt. Our payload featured a prototype custom flight computer that logged the vehicle performance for processing on the ground. This allowed us to further progress on our quest to develop our own COTS Flight computer for use in further projects.

This year, we are taking part in two competitions, The national Rocketry Challenge, and the MACH-24 competition. Where we will be launching a 1kg can sat to 3km on our in house developed high performance rocket at the Machrihanish spaceport. Our team is also exploring the potential of manufacturing Composite components in house, as well as continuing the development of our own custom flight computer. 

These competitions are a stepping stone towards our current end goal, which is the development of a well characterised, flightworthy Liquid rocket engine. We hope to test this engine at the Race to space competition and fly the liquid engine at the European rocketry challenge (EUROC) in two years’ time.

Partaking in these opportunities allows members to increase their knowledge, expertise and develop new skills. By being a part of our society members can explore a potential career in the space sector, from the opportunities we and the UKSEDS provide. For example, last year we had talks from both Skyrora and Oxford space systems, which allowed students to learn about the potential careers in UK space sector.

Interested in what we are getting up to? Then reach out to our email below if you are interest in becoming a member, advisor or even a potential partner or sponsor!

swanseds@swansea-societies.co.uk

Where do we come from?

Name: PCP WISE
Description:
What is PCP WISE?

The PCP WISE project is all about pushing the boundaries of water management innovations to build climate resilience across Europe. Funded through the PCP instrument, this initiative enables public sector buyers to access cutting-edge R&D solutions reaching up to Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 8.

Key activities and expected outcomes:

•	Development of interoperable water intelligence tools to address pressing challenges such as flooding, droughts, and soil erosion, subsidence both in rural and urban settings;
•	Collaboration between public stakeholders and R&D providers to design, prototype, and field-test solutions in real-world environments; and
•	A focus on supporting regional water management authorities, cities, and crisis response teams to better anticipate and mitigate climate-related water crises across the EU.

Description:
At ClearSKY Vision, we transform earth observation by delivering daily, cloud-free, multi-spectral satellite imagery. Our advanced AI seamlessly integrates optical and radar data to eliminate clouds and shadows, providing analysis-ready images that empower industries such as agriculture, environmental monitoring, and forestry. Experience uninterrupted insights and make informed decisions with our up-to-date, reliable satellite data. Name: ESA BIC Lithuania (Business Incubation Centre)

Through collaboration with the European Space Agency and key local stakeholders, ESA BIC Lithuania provides startups with access to expertise, financial resources, and industry networks - essential elements for turning ideas into successful ventures.

WHAT WE OFFER:
 Up to 2 years of business incubation at Visoriai IT Park or Tech-Park Kaunas
 €50k funding for product and IP development
 An additional €25k funding for top startups based in Vilnius
 Technical support from Lithuanian partners and ESA specialists
 Business coaching and hands-on development support
 Legal and intellectual property rights (IPR) advice
 Access to business partners and industry networks
 Workshops, training sessions, and events
 Guidance and opportunities for fundraising

WHO CAN APPLY:
ESA BIC Lithuania welcomes entrepreneurs working on:
 Applications using space-based systems (e.g., satellite navigation, earth observation, satellite communication);
 Space technologies applied in non-space domains
 Innovative products and services for the space sector

CONTACTS:
For more information please visit our website www.esabic.lt or contact us via info@esabic.lt

PROJECT
Project No. 02-010-P-0001 is implemented by Visoriai Information Technology Park.
Duration: 2023-10-04 – 2026-01-01 | Funding: EUR 1,000,000 Co-funded by Lithuania and European Union (NextGenerationEU) “New Generation Lithuania”.

Name: MUSE. Master in Space Systems Eng. at Universidad Politécnica de Madrid (UPM)
Description:
The Master's Degree in Space Systems Engineering (MUSE) of the Universidad Politécnica de Madrid is an initiative of the IDR/UPM Research Institute.

Since its origins, the IDR/UPM Institute has developed a type of unofficial teaching based on what is known as Project Based Learning (PBL), consisting in the incorporation of students from the last courses of the second and third cycle in space projects in which the IDR/UPM was or is involved. These projects include: the development, construction, testing, launch and operation of the UPMSat-1 satellite, the development of a payload for the MINISAT satellite, or the development of thermal and structural control subsystems of instruments for scientific missions of the European Space Agency (ESA) and the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) of the USA.

Within the framework offered by the European Higher Education Area, the IDR/UPM Institute organizes the MUSE with the aim of continuing to develop these same activities, but with the added value of offering students an Official Master's Degree.

The aim of this Lwebsite is to make visible the work of the students and professors of this Master's Degree in the area of Space Systems Engineering.

More information on this Master's program: http://muse.idr.upm.es/

Name: Earth∞Space Sustainability Initiative
Description:
The Earth∞Space Sustainability Initiative brings together industry, academia, governments, international organisations, with the finance and insurance communities to ensure that space continues to support the environmental, economic and scientific interests of current and future generations.

To achieve these goals, the Earth∞Space Sustainability Initiative will develop sets of Space Sustainability Principles that will facilitate the establishment of the Space Sustainability Standards. These will be the global, transparent Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) space sustainability standards, recognised by the finance and insurance communities and internationally by policy makers.

The award of a Space Sustainability Standard will be a meaningful, world-leading acknowledgement that an individual, company or organisation has met ESSI’s requirements of space sustainability in key strands of the Space Sustainability Principles.

In developing these principles through a wide ranging dialogue, ESSI will listen to a diverse set of opinions from across the space sector. It will access commercial, industrial, academic and cultural considerations. In this way, it will endeavour to make sure that its sets of Space Sustainability Principles are based upon common human values.

Name: Trottier Institute for Research on Exoplanets
Description:
We are living at a pivotal time in human history, as technology is advancing to the point that we will be able to answer one of the most fundamental questions: Are we alone in the universe? This quest alone justifies the investment of billions of dollars in robotic exploration of our solar system and the construction of powerful astronomical observatories on Earth and in space.

Since the discovery in 1995 of the 1st planet orbiting a star other than our Sun, astronomers have confirmed the existence of several thousand exoplanets. Thousands of additional candidates have been identified, including rocky Earth-like planets. Over the coming decade, the new generation of telescopes and instruments will allow us to probe the atmosphere of extrasolar planets similar to ours for the first time, looking for water vapour and perhaps even signatures of biological activity like oxygen, ozone and methane.

The Institute for Research on Exoplanets (iREx) brings together top researchers and their students so as to benefit as much as possible from major current and upcoming observation projects, with the ultimate goal of finding life elsewhere. The Institute is devoted to exploring new worlds and seeking life on other planets.

We offer a space asset protection service for large space assets such as private space stations by mitigating potential conjunctions with defunct assets and orbital debris. 

Aadi’s Space Logistics Vehicle (SLV) clustering platform can carry out logistics missions for targets of any form factor.  which means we will be able to relocate/transport space assets as small as a cube-sat and as large as biggest rocket bodies.

Our target market is ISAM (In-Space Servicing, Assembly and Manufacturing) with intent to offer Space Domain Understanding (SDU) solutions.

Name: MERLIN BURROWS
Description:
Merlin Burrows is best described as a Treasure “Finder” as opposed to a Treasure Hunter and due to the track record to date, and based on the right levels of research, artefacts that are lost, hidden or forgotten can be located and pin-pointed if it is there. The scope of capability is global and encompasses both land and sea based opportunities. Merlin Burrows does not engage in “browsing” on a speculative basis for possible targets of interest – there is simply too high a volume of matter to deal with. Historical records whether considered to be fact or fiction relating to myth or legend form the basis of any starting point for the exploitation or engagement of Merlin Burrows capability. 

The opportunity for Merlin Burrows is so vast because with virtually an endless supply of work across the world there is a need for clear channels of communication and dialogue that forms a uniform and consistent method of engagement. Merlin Burrows is accumulating a sizable inventory of finds that offer either internally or externally funded project opportunities and the progress being made is dependent on a variety of factors, land or sea based, jurisdiction, legislation and resources. At this stage in the development of the organisation, it is envisaged that a “volunteer research consultant” role is required to support and assist in the progress of any opportunities listed under the current inventory or that come to light, during the course of day-to-day operations or through other third-party engagements or enquiries.

What are we dealing with? 
The “project” can be any existing find or proposed target that would require a scope of works involving Merlin Burrows regarding research, scanning, reconnaissance, recovery, media, exploitation, commercialisation and overall project fulfillment through to completion for either the company (in house) or a third party stakeholder. The types of project usually fall into clear categories of:

a). Treasure
b). Archaeology
c). Historical

Name: Max Planck Institute for Solar System Research (MPS)
Description:
The research focus of the Max Planck Institute for Solar System Research is our cosmic neighborhood: the solar system with its planets and moons, comets and asteroids as well as the sun. The aim of the scientists is to describe the processes in the solar system in models and to simulate them on the computer. In addition, instruments are being developed and built to study these bodies from space. The Institute is involved in numerous space missions.

The institute is organized in three scientific departments and further research groups.

- Department Sun and Heliosphere
- Planetary Science Department
- Department Solar and Stellar Interiors

Name: Space East
Description:
Space East is the UK’s newest space cluster and brings together leaders from across industry, research and Government in the East of England to develop and champion exciting new opportunities in space technology. Space East is hosted by the New Anglia LEP and supported by the UK Space Agency.

The applications of space and satellite technologies stretch across the region’s leading industries including food and agriculture, offshore wind, coastal and marine science, health and social care, and transport and logistics. Combined with existing technology strengths and innovation assets including Adastral Park, Norwich Research Park, Hethel Innovation, OrbisEnergy, CEFAS, University of East Anglia and University of Suffolk, there are a range of opportunities for organisations to join our growing space cluster. 

We are already leaders in areas such as artificial intelligence, 5G communications, microgravity R&D, agri-tech, marine and life sciences and health tech, all offering exciting potential to unlock space-related commercial opportunities.

Name: Virtus Solis Technologies
Description:
The impetus for Virtus Solis is to provide clean, low-cost renewable energy that directly supplants primary energy production for all terrestrial uses and eventually even extra-terrestrial.  Fossil fuels power 85% of the world in a non-sustainable fashion. This must change. There are limited options for disruption because terrestrial renewable power is intermittent - it is seldom available when and where needed.  Ubiquitous, inexpensive and clean energy are desperately needed. Cleaning up electric power requires replacing the equivalent of more than a million tons of coal use every day for the next 30 years. But even that is not enough. Globally, most of the planet’s population suffers from energy poverty, and access to low cost energy is the greatest lever for universal prosperity. Limitless low-cost energy solves these problems and promotes sustainable growth and harmony.

Virtus Solis is the first low-cost space solar power system able to address the entire $8T energy market.

Name: Scuola di Ingegneria Aerospaziale

Starting from the 1960’s the School’s activities began focusing mainly in astronautics, designing and manufacturing the satellites of the San Marco series, leading Italy to become the third nation in the world to have a satellite in orbit. 

Nowadays, thanks to the UniSat educational series and later satellites such as Lares and TigriSat, and the multidisciplinary approach between technological research and teaching, the School has become a worldwide flagship institution in astronautics.

More info at: https://web.uniroma1.it/scuolaingegneriaaerospaziale/en/about-us/about-us

Name: Lunasonde
Description:
SEEING the UNSEEN

Lunasonde is creating novel sensors across the electro-magnetic spectrum, deployed from space, to see orders of magnitude deeper under the surface of the Earth, asteroids, and other planets.

Our technology is geared to:
•	Develop the first-ever map of the Earth’s mineral and hydrological underground
•	Map the ionosphere in real time for national security and satellite safety
•	Locate underground potable lifesaving and life-sustaining water

Our unique blend of geophysicist and aerospace engineers creates a synergy that propels our innovation to new heights. 

Established in 2018, Lunasonde is the world’s only commercial provider enabling global mapping of the Earth’s subsurface and Ionosphere for a wide range of lifesaving and life-sustaining applications. Our satellites are equipped with state-of-the-art Syntheic Aperture Radar technologies, robotic systems, and additional proprietary sensors and software. They are capable of imaging underground orders of magnitude deeper than traditional methods, allowing us to identify, locate, and map valuable resources with unparalleled accuracy.
 
Combining geoscience, proprietary radar technology, and satellite constellations, our cartographic modeling will help us discover large underground water supplies for communities in dire need of potable water, to locate and characterize critical metals on Earth and in space, and to develop space weather modeling, all  from space.

Name: Maldives Space Research Organisation (MSRO)
Description:
Maldives Space Research Organisation (MSRO) is the first space organisation dedicated to the research and development of the space sector in the Maldives. We are a non-governmental organisation whose main purpose is to use space to help fight climate change through climate resilience and adaptation, to diversify and develop the economy of Maldives, and to inspire and educate the youth and island communities through science and technology.

Some of the challenges MSRO aims to tackle and solve through Earth observation and space applications are; 

- Coastal erosion and land subsidence
- Land use policy
- Ecosystem health and biodiversity
- Maritime safety
- Illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing
- Drug trafficking
- Marine pollution
- Groundwater contamination 
- Seabed mapping
- Disaster response 

MSRO also seeks to take advantage of the opportunities space can offer;

- Climate and marine research
- Astronomy 
- Space science
- Spaceport for unique equatorial orbit applications 
- Data services
- Ground station platform for LEO, lunar, and cis-lunar orbits 
- Space tourism
- Carbon sequestration services

These focus areas are not exhaustive. In consultation with the government, non-governmental organisations, educational and commercial entities, civil society, and other emerging space states, MSRO is working to forge equitable partnerships in the Maldives and among the wider international community to deliver an integrated strategy for targeted and collaborative capacity development.

The benefits of space should be shared and accessed by all. In this era of climate crisis, work with us in hope to drive inclusive, inspiring, and sustainable development through space, together.

Name: Aerospace Medical Association
Description:
PURPOSE: The Aerospace Medical Association is organized exclusively for charitable, educational, and scientific purposes.

MISSION: Apply and advance scientific knowledge to promote and enhance the health, safety, and performance of those involved in aerospace and related activities.

PUBLICATIONS: Aerospace Medicine and Human Performance (formerly Aviation, Space, and Environmental Medicine), a monthly journal of the aerospace medical sciences, including abstracts from the annual scientific meeting. Other regular features include letters to the editor, aerospace medicine reviews, editorials, book reviews, news items, meetings calendar, and news of members.

AsMA is an umbrella group providing a forum for many different disciplines to come together and share their expertise for the benefit of all persons involved in air and space travel. The Association has provided its expertise to a multitude of Federal and international agencies on a broad range of issues, including aviation and space medical standards, the aging pilot, and physiological stresses of flight. AsMA's membership includes aerospace medicine specialists, flight nurses, physiologists, psychologists, human factors specialists, physician assistants, and researchers in this field. Most are with industry leaders, civil aviation regulatory agencies, departments of defense and military services, airlines, space programs, and universities. Approximately 30% of the membership originates from outside the United States.  Through the efforts of the AsMA members, safety in flight and man's overall adaptation to adverse environments have been more nearly achieved.

CONTACT US at inquiries@asma.org.

Name: GRASP Earth
Description:
We are a research-driven company specializing in Earth Observation, remote sensing, and environmental sciences. Our goal is to develop and implement technologies that improve the monitoring and understanding of the Earth's atmosphere and surface.

Our main area of expertise is the monitoring of aerosols and particulate matter. We use polarimetric observations, which are widely recognized in the scientific community as the most effective method for observing these particles in the atmosphere. Our work combines advanced algorithms, computing tools, and data from both ground-based and satellite instruments to extract accurate and consistent information on air quality and atmospheric composition.

One of our key innovations is the GRASP algorithm (Generalized Retrieval of Atmosphere and Surface Properties), which improves the interpretation of satellite and ground data through synergetic retrieval, providing detailed information on atmospheric and surface properties. GRASP is used operationally in public Earth Observation missions such as Sentinel-4, 3MI, and CO2M. It is also central to our second major innovation: a private constellation of MAP (Multi-Angle Polarimetric) nano-satellites. These satellites collect aerosol data that is integrated with public datasets using GRASP, providing a more complete and accurate understanding of atmospheric processes.

Our team combines expertise across a broad spectrum of scientific and engineering fields including physics, atmospheric and environmental sciences, mathematics, ML/AI, data science, and remote sensing, working collaboratively to develop solutions that address urgent global environmental issues.

With the first private polarimetric mission operational, we strive to be leaders in air quality monitoring.

Name: Weather Stream
Description:
Weather Stream is reshaping how the world captures and applies atmospheric intelligence. 

Through our Global Environmental Monitoring System (GEMS)—a constellation of disaggregated cube satellite payloads—we deliver proprietary microwave sounder data that’s frequent, dependable, and ready to use across any platform. With the successful pathfinder GEMS1 mission (2018–2021) and the upcoming launch of GEMS2A and GEMS2B, we’re building on a proven track record to deliver next-generation capabilities. 

Our advanced sensors—developed through federally funded R&D—are now near flight-ready, offering standalone mission potential for agencies and organizations seeking custom payload solutions. For commercial clients, our suite of monitoring and forecast products supports real-time decision-making through scalable data access, from legacy latency tiers to cutting-edge predictive insights. 

Whether you need hardware expertise, high-resolution weather data, or a strategic partner in environmental intelligence, Weather Stream offers a new standard in atmospheric monitoring.

Name: MELiSSA Space Research Program
Description:
The MELiSSA Project (Micro-Ecological Life Support System Alternative) is a long-running European research programme developing regenerative, circular life-support systems.
Initiated by the European Space Agency, MELiSSA integrates biology, engineering, and systems science to produce oxygen, water, and food from waste streams under extreme constraints of mass, energy, safety, and crew time.
Inspired by Earth’s ecosystems and grounded in functional ecology, MELiSSA follows a rigorous, step-by-step validation approach guided by the ALiSSE criteria (Mass, Energy, Efficiency, Safety, Crew Time).
Today, key recycling functions are demonstrated both on Earth (e.g. the MELiSSA Pilot Plant) and in space, supporting future deep-space missions while generating knowledge and technologies relevant to sustainable water, food, and resource systems on Earth.

Name: Daka Space Technologies LTD
Description: Daka Space Technologies (DST) is the first and only Malawian space technology startup founded by Chisomo Daka and it was incorporated on 29th August 2022 in Blantyre which is the commercial city of Malawi. Our vision is to be the industry leader in space-based technologies that greatly advance digitalization and socio-economic transformation in Africa and beyond. Name: Interstellar Performance Labs
Description:
Interstellar Performance Labs is the first of its kind in human performance training. We are working everyday with the aim of preparing the next generation of analog astronauts whose paths hope to one day reach the stars.

At IP Labs, our purpose is to change the safety culture of space analogs by providing human performance training to enable the crews to feel confident in their abilities and optimize mission success. Our team is designing a training program to address the most common risks in simulated space missions like physical performance, psychological concerns, team dynamics, and human factors, so that injuries are minimized and crews have a cognitive and mental health toolbox to guide them through the challenges that will inevitably occur.

Interstellar Performance Labs is here to prepare you and your crew to launch you on your way to wherever your space journey takes you. Ad astra.
