Name: Orbital Robotics
Description:
Orbital Robotics is pioneering the future of AI driven autonomous space operations on orbit, the Moon & Mars. 

Founded by former Blue Origin leaders in Advanced Development in AI Software and Spacecraft hardware , with deep heritage across reusable spacecraft, experience ranging from over 100 Space Shuttle missions, 6 AI-controlled robotic manipulator missions, and AI to adapt rocket engine control for degrading components, we are engineering the next generation of orbital and extraterrestrial robotic systems.

Our advanced AI enabled spacecraft leverage cutting-edge artificial intelligence, computer vision, and guidance, navigation & control software to perform complex autonomous operations across multiple mission domains. From satellite refueling, surveillance, reconnaissance, and inspection to on-orbit repair, upgrade, space station or solar array assembly and debris removal - Orbital Robotics is performing intelligent servicing capabilities in LEO, GEO and Cislunar Space.

Orbital Robotics is redefining what's possible in space using AI Ensbled Autonomus robotic vehicles and landers.

Name: COSMIC - The Consortium for Space Mobility and ISAM Capabilities
Description:
COSMIC is a pioneering nationwide coalition formulated and funded by NASA’s Space Technology Mission Directorate and managed by The Aerospace Corporation. Our mission is to invigorate the domestic in-space servicing, assembly, and manufacturing (ISAM) capabilities, propelling the space enterprise into a new era of innovation and efficiency.

At COSMIC, we believe that collaboration and synergy are the keys to unlocking the limitless potential of space. By mobilizing, advancing, and leveraging the expertise of federal agencies, industry leaders, and academia, we strive to accelerate the universal adoption of ISAM capabilities. This will enable our nation to develop cutting-edge space architectures and make ISAM an integral and routine part of space missions.

Mandated through the December 2022 National ISAM Implementation Plan and officially launched in the Fall of 2023, our consortium stands at the forefront of the most groundbreaking advancements in space exploration.

Through ISAM community collaboration, COSMIC is set to produce annual products that will steer the future of ISAM as a coordinated and unified effort. Our vision is to redefine space mission lifecycles, ushering in a new paradigm that reduces costs and increases operational efficiency. The enhanced longevity, utility, and resilience of space missions will pave the way for unprecedented achievements.

Why join COSMIC? As a member of our consortium, you and your organization will become part of an influential network of like-minded innovators, researchers, and professionals. You'll have the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge projects, share knowledge, and collaborate on groundbreaking initiatives that shape the future of space mobility.

Connect with us on LinkedIn to stay up-to-date with the latest developments, events, and achievements of COSMIC members. Let's build a future where space exploration knows no bounds!

Name: ICAD - International Centre for Astronautical Development
Description:
The International Centre for Astronautical Development (ICAD) is a non-income-generating, not-for-profit organization that functions through an international volunteer community with a shared vision.

Our mission is to ignite global space sector interest and capability, specifically directing our efforts towards under-represented regions of the world. We are working on various approaches to achieve this goal, including an outreach program, project-based joint ventures within the field of space medicine, and a mentorship program. This threefold approach allows us to create change across all generations for the most sustainable results, all bringing us one step closer to becoming an interplanetary species. 

MENTORSHIP
Our mentorship program reaches postgraduates and established professionals, regardless of age or industry experience. This will guide the development of local space start-ups in regions with emerging or non-existent space sectors, to increase their chances of maturation.

OUTREACH
ICAD’s outreach program aims to inspire and educate the younger generation, from toddlers to young adults, to pursue careers in the space industry for generational continuity. Our vision is to grow this outreach program throughout all under-represented countries in the Space industry.

SPACE MEDICINE
Our Space Medicine division (IRSHAD - Institute of Research into Space Health and Astronautical Development) works on a project-by-project basis. Our goal is to make commercial space flight more accessible internationally; to bring scientific knowledge to the general public; and to build space medicine knowledge in under-represented communities.

If you would like to join our team or learn more about our organisation, please browse our website (www.astronauticalcentre.com) or contact us at info@astronauticalcentre.com

Name: Protostar Labs
Description:
Protostar Labs develops innovative technologies that enable autonomous, efficient, and reliable missions - from orbit to the most demanding environments on Earth.

We design and build fault-tolerant onboard data processing solutions that bring intelligence and autonomy directly to satellites. Our technology helps missions save bandwidth, reduce operational costs, and enhance reliability through efficient in-orbit data handling and edge AI.

Our innovation extends to optical payloads and camera systems, engineered to integrate seamlessly with our onboard processors. This provides complete end-to-end solutions for space imaging, analysis, and autonomous operation.

While space remains our core focus, our technologies also serve terrestrial sectors such as defense, robotics, and industrial automation, where precision and durability are equally critical.

Driven by innovation and guided by engineering excellence, Protostar Labs is shaping the future of autonomous mission technology, in space and beyond.

Name: SEEDS - SpacE Exploration and Development Systems Project
Description:
The international Master in "SpacE Exploration and Development Systems" (SEEDS) started in 2005 following a proposal of Politecnico di Torino addressed to its European partners.   
SEEDS is focused on the human and robotic exploration of space and on the related missions, systems, and technologies. 
The purpose of the programme is to provide young graduates with the core applied physics and engineering skills required to develop space systems and missions from initial concept through design, assembly and verification, to launch and operations.
To reach these objectives, SEEDS relies on the tight collaboration of three European Higher Education Institutions located in three different countries, namely Politecnico di Torino in Italy, Institut Supérieur de l’Aéronautique et l’Espace (ISAE, Toulouse) in France and University of Leicester in the UK. 
All three partners share common features, including strong links with space industry, heritage in space research and exploration and inclusion of high-level expertise in the delivery of teaching, based on both the academia and the industry.

Name: JSRI - Jordan Space Research Initiative
Description: The Jordan Space Research Initiative (JSRI) aims to bridge sustainable development with space R&D, and establish an analog research facility in the Jordanian desert. The JSRI team developed a roadmap for Jordan's participation in the Moon Village as an emerging space country in July 2020. The building blocks for this roadmap were chosen based on the priorities identified in the preliminary roadmap for Jordan’s implementation of the UN Space2030 Agenda, which was created by our founder at the International Space University and presented at the International Astronautical Congress in 2020. Our roadmap focuses on fostering research in Water Resource Management, Renewable Energy, ISRU (in-situ resource utilization) and Robotics, Dust Mitigation, Agriculture, Smart City Infrastructure, Women's Health, and Community Engagement. JSRI was created to carry out this roadmap and engage fellow Jordanians while contributing to lunar exploration. With the objective of eventually establishing an analog facility in Jordan where future space technologies can be tested, our aim is to bring space exploration to Jordan in a way that benefits life on Earth.

Name: SpaceMan Craft
Description:
"Can you imagine a spacecraft with 50+ seats for passengers, who can sit and enjoy the marvel of outer space!!"

Our company operates within the aerospace and defence sector, with a primary focus on space technology and applications. We are dedicated to pushing the boundaries of innovation in the field, aiming to revolutionise the way we explore and utilise outer space. 
Our core mission is to develop cutting-edge electric space propulsion technology that enable us to unlock the full potential of space for both scientific advancements and practical applications. With our game-changing Novel Electric Space thruster, we are poised to change the Space industry. 
At our company, we understand that space technology is not limited to exploration alone. We recognize the significant impact it can have on various industries, including telecommunications, Earth observation, climate monitoring, and navigation systems. By leveraging our expertise, we strive to develop state-of-the-art solutions that will drive progress in these areas and pave the way for a more connected and sustainable future on the Earth, Moon and beyond.
Join us on this exciting journey as we redefine what is possible in the realm of space technology and applications. Together, let us shape a future where space becomes an integral part of our everyday lives, inspiring awe and driving progress for generations to come.

Name: GEOSTRAT
Description:
GEOSTRAT is a leading geospatial innovation firm operating across Africa. We transform complex environmental and socio-economic challenges into actionable opportunities through advanced technologies, local expertise, and a global vision. Our mission is to support sustainable, inclusive, and resilient development by delivering data-driven, context-specific solutions.

We specialize in GeoAI, combining satellite imagery, artificial intelligence, and predictive modeling to generate spatial insights and optimize interventions. Our Climate-Smart Agriculture approach enhances food security and resilience using localized technological tools. We also conduct natural risk assessments with high-precision mapping to prevent disasters and protect communities.

GEOSTRAT’s spatial data analytics turn complex datasets into strategic guidance for governments, NGOs, and businesses. In urban planning, we support the design of smart, inclusive cities adapted to Africa’s rapid urban growth. Our work in biodiversity monitoring leverages species distribution modeling to safeguard fragile ecosystems. Using AI, machine learning, and deep learning, we automate analysis and deliver intelligent, scalable solutions.

GEOSTRAT operates in several key regions of Africa, combining world-class technical know-how with in-depth local knowledge. Our vision is to act locally while creating global impact through geospatial intelligence.

Name: Kalpana Chawla Space Academy
Description:
Kalpana Chawla Space Academy (KCSA) is a first-of-its-kind institution offering space science-centered education to children, aimed at creating next-generation space scientists for India's space sector. 

Narayan Bhargava Foundation, in association with Vidya Niketan Educational Trust, founded Kalpana Chawla Space Academy to actualize its Nation Building mission. KCSA is committed to inspire and nurture young minds to blossom into future space scientists, researchers, astronauts and multidisciplinary practitioners.
 
The academy is dedicated to fostering innovation, knowledge, and excellence in space science and technology application by igniting children’s interest in space exploration and cultivating the scientific temper necessary for serving India’s space industry.  

Aligning with the New Education Policy of India, the curriculum is based on the “Learning by Doing” philosophy and the holistic approach of activity-based experiential learning. Under the guidance of distinguished professionals from education, science and the Indian Space and Research Organization (ISRO), the goal is to create a long-term career path for students through strategic partnerships.

Name: Space Northwest
Description:
Washington’s only space-dedicated association—driving impact through connection, storytelling, and bold industry initiatives.

The Pacific Northwest helped launch the Space Age | Space Northwest is making sure we lead the next one.

The future of humanity points to space—opening new frontiers, safeguarding our planet, and uniting people across the globe. From the Apollo-era Lunar Rovers built in Washington to the rise of Blue Origin and SpaceX, the Pacific Northwest has long been at the forefront of space innovation.

Space Northwest aims to keep it that way.

We’re harnessing the region’s 65+ year legacy in aerospace to grow a modern space economy—fueled by innovation, entrepreneurship, and opportunity. Through strategic partnerships, we’re connecting communities, creating pathways into high-demand careers, and amplifying the Pacific Northwest’s leadership on the global stage.

We’re advancing key objectives including workforce development, entrepreneurship, marketing, academic partnerships, and more—all aimed at strengthening the entire space ecosystem.

Washington’s space sector is globally recognized—home to giants, startups, universities, and innovators across defense, commercial, and research. But to seize the opportunity ahead, we need to grow the workforce and broaden participation in space careers—especially among women and underrepresented communities.

The next wave of space exploration is here.
Space Northwest is making sure the Pacific Northwest leads it.

Therefore, it functions as central hub for current trends and developments of the European space-economy and conducts regular socio-economic impact studies of ESA programmes.

Name: Sirius Space Association
Description:
Sirius Space Association est une organisation à but non lucratif, dédiée à la promotion et au développement des sciences et technologies spatiales au Bénin. La mission principale de l’association, est d’inspirer les jeunes à s’orienter vers des carrières dans le domaine spatial, en éveillant leur intérêt pour les STIM à travers l’astronomie et ses multiples applications.
L’association œuvre également à inciter les décideurs publics et les partenaires nationaux à investir dans le spatial afin de développer les applications des technologies spatiales pour faire du Bénin une nation spatiale. 
Créée en 2021, Sirius Space Association est dirigée par une équipe passionnée de jeunes étudiants, ingénieurs et scientifiques engagés. Ses actions s’articulent autour de trois axes majeurs :

- La vulgarisation scientifique (observation du ciel, conférences et ateliers), pour démystifier et rendre l’espace accessible à tous ;
- La formation, afin de développer et de transmettre des compétences techniques pour renforcer les capacités locales ;
- La recherche et développement, pour concevoir des technologies et des solutions spatiales avec et pour tous. 

Sirius Space Association contribue à inspirer la prochaine génération de scientifiques et d'ingénieurs pour le développement technologique du pays.

Name: CAMPUS DE L'ESPACE

Le Campus propose un écosystème propice à l’innovation dont l’objectif est d’accélérer la collaboration et la mise en réseau des entreprises de haute technologie, des centres de recherche et le monde de la formation. À cet effet, plusieurs équipements initiés et dirigés par la SPL, dont un incubateur, une pépinière, des laboratoires d’essais et de prototypages, sont disponibles sur place.

Le Campus est également un lieu de vie et d’habitat (plusieurs centaines de logements), d’équipements sportifs (gymnase, centre équestre, terrains de sports, …) et de services de qualité (amphithéâtre, salles de formation, restaurant, crèche, conciergerie …)

D’autre part, un partenariat avec EDF inaugure un site énergétique innovant et bas carbone, un véritable démonstrateur en matière de sobriété énergétique, de production d’électricité décarbonée et de nouvelles technologies.

Name: Space Debris DAO
Description:
Space belongs to everyone, and at Space Debris DAO, we're committed to ensuring that it remains accessible for future generations.

To preserve space and keep it accessible for future missions, we are developing a decentral organization (DAO) that incentivizes sustainable practices. Our goal is to keep space accessible, usable, and sustained for everyone.

Our Mission
The Space Debris DAO is a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) dedicated to promoting sustainable space standards and addressing the growing issue of orbital debris. We envision a future where space is a clean and safe environment for all, and we believe that blockchain technology can play a key role in bringing stakeholder together globally.

Our Approach
The Space Debris DAO connects stakeholders from across the space industry, including satellite operators, launch providers, researchers, and policymakers. We leverage blockchain technology to create a transparent and decentralized governance structure that empowers our members to collaborate on innovative solutions to space debris mitigation.

Join our virtual community events

Igniting interplanetary evolution through deep-tech innovation.

Name: ESA Phi-Lab Spain
Description:
ESA Phi-Lab Spain is the competence centre, located in Barcelona, to promote innovation for the commercialization of space technologies in the field of climate resilience. 

It's an initiative of the European Space Agency - ESA, coordinated by the Institut d'Estudis Espacials de Catalunya - IEEC, as leader of a consortium formed by the i2CAT Research Centre, the Institut Cartogràfic i Geològic de Catalunya, the Fundación General CSIC, the Fundación KIMbcn, Arribes Enlightenment, the Universitat de València, the Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (UPC), the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, INNOVA IRV, the Planta pilot de l’ESA MELiSSA de la Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona, and the Institut de Ciències Fotòniques.

 Contact us: esaphilabnet-spain@ieec.cat

Name: Astrodynamics Research Group of Penn State
Description: The Astrodynamics Research Group of Penn State (ARGoPS) is a research lab associated with Penn State’s aerospace engineering department. Established in 2017, ARGoPS was formed to strengthen and exhibit the collaborative astrodynamics research environment that Penn State has to offer. Research areas include all of astrodynamics, such as orbital and attitude motion of Earth satellites, interplanetary mission design, spacecraft situational awareness, orbit determination, and trajectory optimization.

Name: Sonda Aerospace
Description:
Somos uma JointVenture formada por uma aliança Aeroespacial pelas empresas:

Legado Usinagem,  Orbital Engenharia e Plastflow

Soluções Completas:
De foguetes suborbitais a operações de lançamento, oferecemos soluções sob medida para missões científicas e tecnológicas.

Quem somos?
Inspirados pelos conhecimentos adquiridos e pelas tecnologias inovadoras desenvolvidas para o Programa Espacial Brasileiro, com histórico de voo (TRL 9), a Sonda Aerospace iniciou-se através de um grupo corporativo para fornecer foguetes suborbitais com capacidade de transportar cargas úteis compostas por experimentos científicos e tecnológicos e operações de lançamentos.

Name: Celestial Minds Space Lab
Description: Celestial Minds Space Lab is revolutionizing global space communications by deploying an intelligent satellite constellation that serves as the always-on data backbone for satellite networks. With advanced AI-powered routing, dynamic bandwidth allocation, and inter-satellite crosslinks, Celestial enables persistent, real-time data relay from any satellite to ground — anywhere on Earth, 24x7. Whether for Earth observation, IoT, direct-to-device, or defense applications, Celestial ensures zero data latency gaps, network resilience, and mission-critical continuity.

Name: TU Delft Astrodynamics Toolbox (Tudat)
Description: Welcome to the LinkedIn landing page for the TU Delft Astrodynamics Toolkit - or Tudat - project. Tudat is a powerful toolbox for research and education in numerical orbit propagation, state and parameter estimation, and mission design. The project originated at, and is managed by, staff and students at TU Delft, with an ever growing network of users, developers and alumni. The bulk of the functionality is written in C++ (Tudat), but the typical user interface is in Python (Tudatpy), which links to the Tudat core code.

Name: Space It Up!

Name: International Doctoral School
Description:
Remote sensing technologies have become an important source of freely and globally available distributed information. It allows continuous monitoring of various components of the earth, providing the scientific community with key elements that help understand the spatiotemporal dynamics/interactions of various natural processes. Thus, valuable conclusions can be drawn about the major current environmental issues and recommendations can be delivered to support the decision-making process.

The International Doctoral School on Remote Sensing and Spatial Technologies (ETTS) will help young scientists and professionals in the field of natural resource management acquire the necessary knowledge for adequate use of remote sensing data. The ETTS school will be held this year at Mohammed VI Polytechnic University “UM6P” in Ben Guerir (Morocco) from 8 to11 May 2023. This year’s edition is co-organized by the Centre of Remote Sensing Applications “CRSA” and the International Water Research Institute “IWRI” within UM6P University in collaboration with the consortium of the Moroccan universities (UM6P, USMS “Sultan Moulay Slimane”, UCA “Cadi Ayyad, UIZ “Ibn Zohr”, UIT “Inb Toufail” and UM5 “Mohammed V”) and international partners. The school will cover different topics related to the application of remote sensing in the fields of Water resources, Agriculture, and Environmental hazards. Theories and methods about the state-of-the-art of spatial technologies dedicated to estimation, mapping, and monitoring within each of the mentioned fields will be provided.

The overall aim of this training program is to bring PhD students, post-docs, young scientists, and stakeholders, particularly from African Institutions, interested in enhancing their knowledge and technical capabilities in remote sensing application. Towards this aim, the lectures of the ETTS/2023 school will cover theoretical as well as practical lessons.

Name: SATERA
Description:
SPACE-BASED COMPOSITE ADS-B AND MULTILATERATION SYSTEM VALIDATION THROUGH SCALABLE SIMULATIONS (SATERA) will propose and validate an integrity estimator for space-based ADS-B systems based on crosschecking positions reported in ADS-B messages with position estimations provided by space-based MLAT systems.

To reach this goal, SATERA will apply a stepped approach starting by the definition of  high-level operational descriptions, identifying all the involved stakeholders, and detailing the CNS equipage of the fleet and the available air traffic services. 
The research team will develop theoretical models to assess and benchmark the proposed system architecture leading to a full list of functional requirements and interfaces description.

SATERA will develop a system performance prediction tool to compute the theoretical performance of a MLAT system whose receiving stations are onboard of a  constellation of LEO small satellites. This tool will take into consideration all the error sources that can affect the MLAT system performance and will be used to select the most suitable constellation.
For validating the SATERA solution also end-to-end system evaluation  tool that will be developed. Several validation exercises addressing the provision of composite space- based ADS-B+MLAT surveillance in the EURSAM and NAT corridors for realistic traffic will be done.

Funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or SESAR JU. Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: EPROM Electronic Industry Inc.
Description:
The company’s history also reflects its evolution over the years. Initially focused on designing and manufacturing communication and broadcast devices, EPROM entered the market with the EPSAT and Vector brands, leveraging technologies developed through RF (Radio Frequency) research and development studies conducted at the laboratory level. In the 1990s, EPROM expanded its product offerings to include microwave communication systems and later diversified into a wide range of industries, including medical and industrial technologies. This wealth of knowledge and experience has been channeled into the creation of high-quality communication and broadcasting devices under registered brands such as EPSAT, VECTOR, and BIOVECTOR. EPROM’s unique approach and engineering capabilities have attracted the attention of significant institutions, leading to valuable collaborations.

EPROM has consistently monitored technological advancements on a global scale and has remained at the forefront of the RF systems and compact high-voltage power supply systems industry in Turkiye. This commitment to innovation and excellence has allowed EPROM to thrive and make a significant impact in a number of sectors.

Golden Age of Nationalization Award
Türkiye’s Defense Industry Agency has honored EPROM with the Golden Age of Nationalization on 25th July 2023 in acknowledgement of successful collaborative research and development projects with ASELSAN Türkiye’s leading defence contractor.

Name: Spacebourne
Description:
Spacebourne: "Making Earth Just One Chapter In The Story of Humankind."

Spacebourne is an innovative and forward-thinking space technology company set to redefine human existence as we know it. Born out of a profound belief in humanity's future beyond the confines of our home planet, our mission is to create the framework for a human being to be born, live, and grow old entirely off-Earth. We envisage a future where earthbound living is not a necessity but a choice.

Weaving together the intricate threads of bioengineering, aerospace technology, and advanced AI, Spacebourne is forging an unprecedented path toward self-sustaining extraterrestrial communities. Our team of experts is diligently working to solve the multifaceted challenges of long-term space habitation — from health and wellness in zero gravity to reliable resource replenishment.

We are currently developing an MVP for an orbital data center. The center is just the first step towards building the infrastructure for humanity's spacebourne future. 

As we journey into this vast frontier, Spacebourne is not just dreaming of a future in space, but meticulously constructing it. By confronting the urgent problems of today, we're making sure that tomorrow's spacebourne generations have a secure and thriving environment to call home. Spacebourne is more than a space company; we are pioneers and guardians of humanity's celestial future.

Through focused R&D, targeted prototyping, and collaboration with trusted partners, we plan to deliver orbital defence solutions that are both flexible and cost-effective. Our ultimate aim is to empower decision-makers with real-time, autonomous oversight of critical orbits, turning space from a vulnerable frontier into a domain where sovereign control can be swiftly asserted.

The members have authored several scientific publications focusing on different aspects of Remote Sensing and Earth Observation, including utilization and processing of data from heterogenous sensors .

Name: Arctus inc.
Description:
ARCTUS est une entreprise de R&D spécialisée en télédétection optique des milieux aquatiques lacustres et marins. Nous développons et fournissons des solutions de veille environnementale qui s’appuient sur la télédétection, l’observation de la Terre (OT), les systèmes d’information géographique (SIG) et la modélisation numérique spécialement conçues pour les agences gouvernementales, la communauté scientifique et le grand public. Fondée en 2009, l’entreprise est basée à Rimouski, au coeur de la technopole maritime du Québec.

ARCTUS is a private R&D company providing research, development and applications in remote sensing, Earth Observation (EO) and Geographical Information System (GIS) technologies for governmental agencies, scientific communities and the general public. Our company is specialized optical remote sensing of aquatic environments, from lakes to oceans.

For more than a century, anthropic activities have been putting an everly increasing pressure on natural ecosystems. During the last decades, EO technologies have been revealed as a unique tool to detect changes in remote and often inaccessible regions. Remote sensing provides quasi-continuous georeferenced images of the Earth's surface that can be used to monitor environmental changes, helping acquire a better understand of the relationships between the natural ecosystems, global changes and human activities.

Next generation of EO missions will provide an unprecedented amount of data for natural sciences (e.g., climatology, oceanography, hydrology, limnology) and their applications. ARCTUS was created to fulfill the increasing need of the use of remote sensing technologies in such applications in aquatic environments. The company is particularly active in the observation of polar regions where environmental changes are ongoing at higher rates than everywhere else on the Planet. Use of space-based technologies is critical when dealing with such extremely remote and harsh ecosystems.

Name: CARIOQA Quantum Pathfinder
Description:
The CARIOQA Quantum Pathfinder Mission project under the European Commission’s Horizon Europe programme, aims to develop a quantum sensor for space application. 
European industry in collaboration with European research institutions will build a quantum interferometer technology deployed to onboard a satellite mission within the decade, for improving our ability to monitor climate change phenomena. Benefitting from the heritage of its research institutions, this project aims to bring Europe into a leading position for sustainable quantum technologies in space.

Up to date there are 2 phases of the project being implemented:
- The CARIOQA-PMP (Cold Atom Rubidium Interferometer in Orbit for Quantum Accelerometry – Pathfinder Mission Preparation) consortium (CNES, DLR, AIRBUS, LUH, DTU, SYRTE, LP2N, ONERA, TUM, POLIMI, FORTH, LCAR, EXAIL, TELETEL, GAC, LEONARDO) will build an engineering model of the quantum accelerometer. 
 
- The CARIOQA-PHA (Cold Atom Rubidium Interferometer in Orbit for Quantum Accelerometry – Phase A) consortium (CNES, DLR, AIRBUS, GMV, FORTH) will analyse the feasibility of the mission.

Name: CRAFT Tech
Description:
CRAFT Tech (Combustion Research and Flow Technology, Inc.), is a small and well-established CFD consulting and software sales company. We have a proven record of solving complex problems for our customers. Our analysis and design recommendations have resulted in reduced cost and downtime, as well as a better understanding of the physics to improve future designs. Our headquarters is located just outside of Philadelphia in Pipersville, PA. We also have a remote office in Huntsville, AL.

CRAFT Tech specializes in the high-fidelity computational simulation of complex fluid dynamic and combustion problems on large-scale, parallel architecture computers. Services we provide to government and industry include the analysis of complex design problems including: combustion and multi-component mixtures, unsteady relative motion, multi-phase (cavitating) flows, acoustics, and turbomachinery. In addition, we provide technical consultation and on-site services. We also license software that specialize in modeling complex physics and thermochemistry. Finally, we perform fundamental research in CFD, grid adaptation, aeroacoustics, turbulence, combustion and multi-phase flow.

Name: Celestia Energy
Description:
We’re transforming energy delivery by developing safe, long-range wireless power transmission systems using lasers. From Earth to orbit and beyond, our mission is to power the world’s most remote and critical infrastructure, enabling a future where energy flows without limits. 

Our first product, Terra, is a ground-based station capable of recharging drones in flight, delivering energy to remote areas, and supporting disaster relief operations. The next step will be to expand our technology into orbit with Caelus, a space-based system designed to beam power to the ground or to other satellites, and later with Luna, a Moon-orbiting satellite providing energy for lunar landers, rovers, and bases. The ultimate vision is to build the first lunar energy infrastructure, ensuring reliable power anywhere, anytime, without the massive weight of cables and batteries.

Our technology stands out thanks to its combination of long range, high-power output and scalability, and multi-layer safety systems. The modular architecture allows for drone-agnostic integration, tailor-made solutions, and even fully autonomous operation, enabling a wide range of applications both on Earth and in space.

We are a startup from the Czech Republic, excited to lead the nation to the forefront of the wireless power industry. We are members of the ESA BIC Czech Republic supported also by the Technological Incubation by CzechInvest.

If you would like to be a part of the team, please reach out to us at matyas@celestiaenergy.com with your CV and a short motivation letter.

Name: Deep Space Food Consortium
Description:
Leading the realization of the future food system to expand humanity’s presence in the solar system  

Food is one of the top technology shortfalls for long-duration space exploration – yet the history of exploration has demonstrated time and again that food is often the critical factor between mission success and failure.

In 2021, NASA released the Deep Space Food Challenge to identify technologies that could potentially contribute to the future food system for space exploration. 

After final prizes were awarded in 2024, it was recognized the Challenge had uncovered a gap in the definition of the food system. The thought and mindset driving the planning for the future food system must expand from a focus on food technologies to a focus on a food ecosystem. Additionally, the need for the development of an overall food systems roadmap that includes everything from production and safety, through processing and storage, is critical to understanding the full span and complexities of the needs.

The responsibility cannot lie with just one country, organization, or industry. 

The Deep Space Food Consortium was established to continue the work towards expanding the definition of the future food system and bringing together the global community to connect opportunities across disciplines.

Description:
Of all the RAF Bomber Command airfields, RAF Scampton is perhaps the best known and most historically important. 

It originated as a First World War training aerodrome and Home Defence Flight station but closed within two years of the end of the First World War. Selected under the RAF Expansion Scheme, it was built up again in the 1930s, and its squadrons were in action from the first day of the Second World War, including in the ‘Dambusters’ Raid on the Ruhr Dams by Scampton’s 617 Squadron for which the base has become famous. 

During the Cold War, Scampton was also one of only two V-Bomber airfields where the Blue Steel ‘standoff‘ missile was deployed from 1962. The mighty Avro Vulcan bomber continued to operate from RAF Scampton until the early 1980s when it was retired from service. 

This history has continued into the 21st century, when Scampton became the home of the Red Arrows, now synonymous with the skies above Lincolnshire. Although the Royal Air Force Aerobatic Team (RAFAT) has now relocated to RAF Waddington, the links to Scampton, both emotionally and operationally, remain strong, with the airspace above Scampton remaining in use for Red Arrows training sorties.

The overall vision for Scampton Holdings Limited (SHL) is to strengthen and develop the foundations within Lincolnshire for existing business areas and maximise future opportunities and economic growth for Lincs. and the wider East Midlands. We aim to be an internationally recognised centre of excellence in Space & Satellite Technology, Aerospace, Defence, Innovative Technologies (Business Incubator), Education and Heritage.

As we approach the 80th Anniversary of Operation Chastise this year on 16th May, we are strongly reminded of the historical significance of RAF Scampton, whilst at the same time looking ahead and creating the blueprint for the next 50 years of the site's mission and societal and economic relevance.

Name: ESA Technology Broker Czechia
Description:
The European Space Agency is Europe's gateway to space. The Czech Republic has been a member of ESA since 2008. With its main purpose to coordinate and support the development of European space activities, ESA also ensures that related investments bring long term benefits to the Europeans. Technology transfer is a way to achieve that, and that is why the Technology Broker team is here. ESA Technology Broker operates in the Czech Republic under Technology Center Prague since 2015 as a part of Business Support Department. 

Technology transfer aims to increase the frequency of knowledge and technology transfers from ESA space programs to non-space sectors. That includes companies or universities working in the development of space technologies to other industrial areas outside the space sector.

The potential of space technologies engages a wide variety of companies which solve diverse challenges. Leveraging existing know-how and technology for space use often lays down a path for use in the non-space sector. Connecting the non-space sector with space technology provides a competitive advantage for diverse entities through access to innovative space technology solutions. 

A broad range of Czech industries benefit from the technology transfer actively monitored by the Technology Broker team by accessing product and process innovations, while enhancing their resistance to future technology challenges.

Name: The Space Consortium
Description:
The Space Consortium is a Massachusetts-based community and coordination initiative open to all and supported by the MA Space Grant.

It aims to bring together space academics, students, professionals and local space enthusiasts. Organizers of Boston Space Week, our common goal is to connect, network and share resources with each other and with the public, notably through public outreach efforts.

What does the Space Consortium do?
1) It organizes & coordinates large-scale public outreach events, such as Boston Space Week (BSW) and the Boston Space Film Festival, including local recruiting & networking events.
During BSW the Consortium promotes fruitful exchanges between an array of top experts, business and community leaders, faculty, researchers, educators, students, alumni, and space enthusiasts which allows the community to engage with critical discussions related to the multidisciplinary issues affecting the aerospace industry today.

All Space Week events are open to all. Through keynotes, a space career fair, and a week-long Space Film Festival, attendees are invited to discover the intricate hot-button topics in the field. 

It launched this year:
2) The Local STAR (Space Transdisciplinary Academic Research) Gazette
3) A new graduate Space Law, Policy and Ethics (SLPE) online course 
4) A new Space Ethics Workshop program

It also catalogs:
1) Local academic (= university-affiliated) space research projects & initiatives
2) Space-related public events
3) Local academic space faculty & researchers. The Consortium is currently building a catalog of local space faculty & researchers (including a summary of their area of expertise and – when provided – their contact information). If you are looking for a specific space research expert in MA to come speak to your community, or are yourself a local space research expert and would like to be listed on our academic space faculty & researchers page, please email us at contact@spaceconsortium.com.

ESA Business Incubation Centres’ goal are to create jobs and develop regional economies via the ESA BICs program in the ESA Member States. They provide incubation services to promising space-related start-ups to help them become successful companies by exploiting space technologies or utilizing space systems in non-space markets (spin-off), or by becoming suppliers to the space sector, e.g. by exploiting non-space technology in the space market (spin-in).

Name: Mission spatiale Euclid
Description:
Quelle est la nature de l'énergie noire ? Cette substance hypothétique constituerait 70% du contenu de notre Univers, et serait responsable de son expansion accélérée. Nous ne connaissons cependant ni sa nature, ni son origine ...
Et puis, quelle est la nature de la matière noire ? Cette forme de matière invisible qui n'émet aucun rayonnement, mais interagit gravitationnellement avec la matière ordinaire, constitue quant à elle 25% du contenu de notre Univers.
Pour répondre à ces questions, le télescope européen Euclid, opéré par l'Agence Spatiale Européenne et le Consortium scientifique Euclid, va retracer l'histoire de notre Univers sur les 10 derniers milliards d'années en créant une gigantesque carte, en 3D, de la toile cosmique.

Name: Inner Space Training
Description:
Mission: 
Most commercial astronauts have only one chance to get it right and achieve that “once in a lifetime experience”. 

That’s why it is critical to train not only the body but also the mind. IST includes psychological acclimation to ensure that suborbital scientists and space tourists achieve a peak experience in space.   IST provides situational awareness training to address the mental and emotional challenges that may be experienced during flight.  

The training is a one day, face-to-face course for up to 6 participants,  held at either a public or private location. During the course, participants will be asked to undergo a number of personal and team exercises to build their understanding of what the suborbital space environment entails and how their personal mission can be achieved. 

Participants will learn the various flight phases,  identify the associated physical and psychological challenges, and also be taught how to resolve these challenges. After the course, participants will be asked to practice until their space flight using Brainwave Entrainment (BWE) technology. The techniques and the positive effects of the BWE program can be re-activated during space flight to give astronauts calm focus during their entire flight, enabling them to have a peak experience and accomplish their personal mission

Name: LIFE Space Mission
Description:
We are LIFE, a Swiss-led and European-driven space mission dedicated to answer one of humanity’s oldest and most profound questions: Are we alone?

Our goal is simple but bold — to build the first mission designed solely to detect signs of life on planets beyond our Solar System. Using mid-infrared interferometry, we study the atmospheres of distant worlds, searching for chemical fingerprints that might reveal biological activity. Building on Switzerland’s pioneering legacy in exoplanet science, LIFE continues to lead the way in exploring new worlds.

But we’re more than a scientific mission. We’re a team of scientists, engineers, and communicators united by curiosity and purpose. For us, space exploration goes beyond science — it’s a shared journey that brings people together and inspires new ways of thinking.
We challenge conventions, embrace complexity, and explore new ways of working together. By reimagining how space missions are built, we want to show that openness, creativity, and collaboration can move science forward — and inspire the next generation to think bigger.

We’re just getting started — so stay tuned

Description:

Our mission is to promote and facilitate the open practice of planetary science and data analysis for professionals and amateurs by organizing events and conducting collaborative projects aimed at creating scientific, technical and educational resources, tools and data accessible to all.

OpenPlanetary started back in 2015 from an initial participants effort to stay connected and share information related to and beyond the ESA’s first Planetary GIS Workshop. It then continued during the 2nd USGS Planetary Data Workshop, and aggregated more people.

In 2018, we established as non-profit organisation in order to provide us with a legal framework to sustainably fund our community framework, projects and activities, and better serve the planetary science community as a whole.

Name: AfriOrbit
Description: AfriOrbit was founded on the vision of revolutionizing Africa's journey into space. Name: Space 2 Consumer (A division of Space Aye)
Description:
Space 2 Consumer (S2C) is a division of Space Aye which is revolutionising the way we live our lives, changing the way we use real-time images of Earth, merged with IoT data, from space! This decade, thousands of satellites with cameras circling our planet will offer an always-on vision of every corner of the planet. S2C focuses on the relationship between humanity and always-on satellite imagery offering exciting societal, climate-focused and commercial opportunities to explore. 

Our strategy, underpinned by patents, focuses on offering unique S2C capabilities that unlock an entirely new technology sector…Human Focussed, Big Earth Data. 

Imagine the data flowing between 8 billion people, 15 billion IoT devices and thousands of cameras focussed on our planet. This is a new, exciting and a completely unique and patented strategy  – a world-first innovation. Space Aye creates new opportunities which facilitate human focussed solutions that save time, money and potentially our planet. 

We help bring real-time space to the human race! 

Curious? Let’s talk…

Name: GECB Student Satellite Program
Description:
Welcome to the Govt Engineering College Barton Hill Student Satellite Program! We are a passionate team of students and faculty members dedicated to exploring space through hands-on satellite development.

At Govt Engineering College Barton Hill, we prioritize experiential learning. Our CubeSat Program offers students the opportunity to design, build, and operate CubeSats, fostering innovation and technical expertise.

Driven by our passion for space, we collaborate across disciplines and work closely with faculty mentors. Together, we ensure the success of our satellite missions.

Our program goes beyond building satellites; it instills critical thinking, teamwork, and leadership skills. Students gain practical experience in project management, systems engineering, and more.

Connect with us to follow our progress and explore opportunities in the Govt Engineering College Barton Hill CubeSat Program.

Name: Imperial Planetary Robotics Lab
Description:
Imperial Planetary Robotics Lab (IPRL) was founded in 2022 to connect students from all different backgrounds in London who are passionate about space robotics.

The lab aims to allow like-minded students to perform research together on breakthrough robotics projects for space exploration. We want to achieve this by bringing them together and connecting them to experienced academic and industry partners.

IPRL's current flagship project is a full-scale Martian rover aimed at competing in initiatives such as the European Rover Challenge and the University Rover Challenge in the upcoming years. A new branch opened in 2024 focuses on Lunar Water extraction capabilities, teaching the members academic skills as important as practical ones in space exploration.

Description:
Welcome to the official  page of The Galactic Society .

Stay updated with the latest news, discoveries, and breakthroughs in the world of space exploration, as we bring you fascinating articles, breathtaking images, and thought-provoking discussions. 

Come join our community of like-minded individuals who share a passion for all things space-related! Follow us to be a part of our journey as we reach for the stars and uncover the mysteries that lie beyond. 

Together, let's explore the wonders of the cosmos and make our mark on the universe!

Name: Earth and Planetary Institute of Canada
Description:
EPIC is Canada's first independent institution dedicated to Earth observation and planetary science. We aim to help steward a sustainable future on Earth, while spearheading Canada's scientific advancements as humans return to the Moon and venture on to Mars.

As an independent institution, EPIC is empowered with the agility to shift and expand scientific priorities as government and industry needs arise. Beyond research, we catalyse actionable outcomes, creating synergies between scientists, policymakers, and communities. Our goal is not only to advance knowledge, but to translate it into tangible benefits for people and planet(s). We envision a future where inspired Canadians passionately chase the stars, all while remaining grounded in their commitment to Earth's well-being.

Name: ORBES
Description:
ORBES is fixing the space infrastructure problem: astronaut work is dangerous, expensive, and limits scale. Robot workers unlock industrial productivity in space, bringing even more value back to Earth

Our space drone ORB increases safety and saves $300M a year of manual work in space. ORB helps mission control automate tasks, starting in and around space stations and expanding to national security satellites.

ORB also enables novel revenue streams during un-crewed periods, such as live media capture for mission storytelling, enabling access for more people to experience space from Earth.

Name: xOrbita Inc
Description:
At xOrbita Inc, we are pioneering accessible solutions for orbital debris detection. Our mission is to democratize space situational awareness by developing low-cost, scalable technologies that enable global participation in monitoring and safeguarding Earth’s orbital environment.

Leveraging innovative approaches, we aim to make space safer and more sustainable for all. Our flagship project involves the development of a quantum dot-based spectrometer system designed to detect and characterize space debris with high precision. By integrating advanced optical sensing with robust data analysis, we provide actionable insights into the ever-growing challenge of orbital debris.

Through collaboration with international partners and a commitment to open science, xOrbita Inc is dedicated to ensuring the long-term sustainability of space activities.

collaborations, we equip the next generation of engineers with practical skills and real-world experience, bridging the gap between academia and industry to drive meaningful change.

Our core focus revolves around democratising aerospace technologies for greater accessibility, flexibility, and reliability. Join us on our journey and be a part of the new aerospace revolution.

Name: Bradford-Renduchintala Centre for Space AI
Description:
At the Bradford-Renduchintala Centre for Space AI we are laser-focused on being at the forefront of a paradigm shift towards a low-cost, smart, sustainable, and ubiquitous exploitation of space technologies to meet some of the big challenges faced by society. Artificial intelligence (AI) enables fast, optimal, and autonomous decision making, and is therefore rightly at the heart of our research and training in the key areas of satellite communications, earth observation, satellite navigation, network security, distributed systems, signal processing, wireless /mobile/optical/digital communications, and reliability.
For us equality, diversity, and inclusion are not just aspirational ideals or legal obligations but part of our DNA. Our ambition is to be the very best, attracting the finest from around the world, nurturing and giving wings to innovative ideas, and producing world-class engineers who are equipped with industry-ready skills to deliver transformative societal impacts. Our team is diverse, multidisciplinary and multisectoral by design, featuring an exciting mix of top industry experts, distinguished professors, and first-rate researchers.
Our MSc in Satellite Systems Engineering combines a rich blend of peer-commended scholarship, inspirational teaching, industrial collaboration, and cutting-edge research to give our students a complete understanding of key concepts and a strong foundation for a competent, innovative, and successful career in engineering. You can find more information about this MSc, including a competitive £10,000 scholarship and a link to apply online here>>
We partner with industry, from local companies in the Yorkshire region of the UK to multinational high-tech corporations and national space agencies, to upskill staff through short courses and develop novel research solutions in RF, AI, telecommunications, signal processing, network security, satellite communications, earth observation, and other areas of our expertise.

Name: BEAM - Beyond Earth Aristotle Missions
Description:
B.E.A.M. is a student space research group founded by students of the Electrical Engineering and Computer Engineering Department with the aim of participating in the REXUS/BEXUS Programme. The group's first project was called SHADE (SDR Helix Antenna Deployment Experiment) and concerned the study of a mechanism for deploying a helix antenna. Since then, the team participates in every cycle of the REXUS/BEXUS competition, presenting new and innovative ideas to be implemented, while also being active in different competitions related to space research such as IGLUNA, CanSat Greece and NASA Space Apps. To date, the team measures six experiments: SHADE, ECOWISE, LIGHT, Star Trackers, DROPSTAR and LISER, each of which has a different object of study.
The REXUS/BEXUS programme is realised under a bilateral Agency Agreement between the German Aerospace Center (DLR) and the Swedish National Space Agency (SNSA). The REXUS/BEXUS programme is further realised with the help of ESA, ZARM, EuroLaunch, MORABA and SSC.
The IGLUNA competition is a student program aimed at raising public awareness of space and is organized by the Swiss Space Agency.
CanSat in Greece is a panhellenic competition regarding space technology. It is organized by SPIN – Space Innovation in cooperation with ESA and the World CanSat & Rocketry Competition (WCRC).
Nasa’s International Space Apps Challenge is an innovation hackathon with a duration of 48 hours, taking place in over 200 cities worldwide and is organized by NASA.

Name: SERENE (Space Environment), University of Birmingham
Description:
The Space Environment and Radio Engineering (SERENE) research group is a multidisciplinary research group focused on understanding and addressing issues related to the upper atmosphere and space environment. The group's overarching purpose is to serve as a comprehensive resource or "one stop shop" for modelling, measuring, and mitigating the effects of the upper atmosphere on engineering and physical systems.

Key areas of SERENE's research include:
•	Mathematical Modelling
•	Fundamental Physics
•	Engineering
•	Policy Advise

Overall, SERENE integrates scientific research with practical applications, aiming to enhance our understanding of space environment phenomena and their implications on various systems, while also advising industry, governments and academia on policy and risk management in relation to extreme space weather events.

Name: Simons Observatory
Description:
The Simons Observatory (SO) is supported by the Simons Foundation, the Heising-Simons Foundation, and member organizations, dedicated to understanding how the universe originated and evolved over the past 13.8 billion years. The SO project began in 2016 and is constructing and deploying four special-purpose telescopes, a high-altitude site, and cryogenic detectors. The SO will collect a comprehensive range of data spanning angular scales from one arcminute to nearly 70% of the sky visible from its site in the Atacama Desert of northern Chile, at an altitude of 5200 meters (17,200 feet) above sea level.

SO is a team of over 40 institutions world-wide with over 300 participating members, representing scientific/academic/educational personnel, engineers, managers, and other talented team members. Upon completion, SO will be the largest, most ambitious observatory of its kind designed to probe the birth and evolution of the entire universe.

The project office located at UC San Diego is responsible for the construction and operation of the SO. The SO is currently completing its civil construction phase and is installing the telescope systems in 2024 with first science in 2025.

Name: NASA Space Apps Challenge Mountain View

The NASA International Space Apps Challenge, the world's largest hackathon, is back! This year, Mountain View is teaming up with NASA Ames Research Center to host an exhilarating event celebrating the theme "The Sun Touches Everything." Mark your calendars for October 5th and 6th, 2024, and join us in exploring the vast influence of the Sun on our lives and beyond.

Name: SpaceOps
Description:
The International Committee on Technical Interchange for Space Mission Operations and Ground Data Systems (also referred to as the SpaceOps Organization) was formed in the realization that given the large number of people involved in space mission operations, an organized community or technical forum is needed. Since mission operations have become an increasingly large segment of space agency budgets, there is great interest in improving the capabilities and cost efficiencies of mission operations. It was in the spirit of providing the broadest possible managerial and technical interchange between space agencies, academia, and industry that SpaceOps was established.


Since its inception in 1990, SpaceOps has held fifteen biennial conferences hosted by various countries around the world. This international forum discusses state-of-the-art operations principles, methods, and tools and provide an opportunity to foster managerial and technical interchange on all aspects of space mission operations, including such areas as robotics, human, earth orbiting, and deep space aspects of space operations.

Description:
MegaSat Technologies Pvt. Limited, an Indian-owned startup, is a prominent contributor to India’s space industry, driven by a commitment to self-reliance and innovation. Recognized by the Department of Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade, it aligns with India’s agenda of Atmanirbhar Bharat (self-reliant India).

At the core of MegaSat’s mission is the development of a robust ecosystem in India to foster indigenous capabilities in geospatial and space-based intelligence, tailored to the specific needs of India’s defense sector. Leveraging state-of-the-art satellite technology, MegaSat aims to transform the landscape of space-based solutions within the country.

The solutions offered by MegaSat Technologies will comprise satellite imagery services, data aggregator services, integrated ground command stations, and advanced AI-based Geospatial intelligence analytical systems. Arpsoft is a dynamic Italian company, led by a team of four aerospace engineers with vast expertise gained from prestigious research institutions such as the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, Sapienza University of Rome, and the Italian Institute of Astrophysics. 

At Arpsoft, our mission is to drive innovation and excellence in the aerospace industry through our cutting-edge engineering consultancy and software development services. With a focus on ground station operations and spectrum management, we empower our clients to reach new heights in satellite communication and space exploration.

Over the past decade, we have cultivated strong partnerships with esteemed space agencies and prominent industry players. Our remarkable track record boasts the successful development of numerous advanced software tools. Notably, our company has consistently secured and excelled in tenders awarded by the European Space Agency, solidifying our position as a trusted and reliable partner.

Our established relationship with the European Space Operation Center (ESOC) has resulted in ongoing support activities for several critical operational tools. These include the European S/W Delta-DOR Correlator, the Radio Frequency Interference Assessment Tool (RFIAT), and the Link Budget Tool. Name: XELTIS
Description:
Xeltis is redefining the way the world accesses resources. As Earth’s minerals become scarcer and supply chains face constant disruptions, the need for a new approach has never been clearer. Xeltis is answering that call by combining advanced technology, space exploration, and clean energy solutions to build a sustainable resource system for the future.

At the heart of Xeltis is a commitment to efficiency and innovation. On Earth, we’re using AI-driven mining to make extraction faster, safer, and more cost-effective. This approach reduces environmental impact while ensuring a stable supply of critical materials for industries worldwide.

But the real breakthrough lies beyond our planet. Xeltis is pioneering space mining, focusing on asteroids rich in rare metals like platinum, nickel, and cobalt—resources essential for modern technology and clean energy production. These space-based reserves are virtually limitless, and our goal is to make them accessible through fully autonomous mining systems set to launch by 2029.

To support this vision, we’re creating Nova7X, the world’s first digital commodities exchange for Earth and space resources. This platform will allow seamless, secure trading while improving efficiency and transparency across global markets.

Clean energy is another key focus. Xeltis plans to deploy space-based solar farms that beam renewable power directly to Earth. This innovation could revolutionize how we power homes, industries, and future space missions.

Our mission is simple: ensure the world never faces resource shortages again. By combining smart technology with space exploration, Xeltis is building a future where energy is abundant, supply chains are stable, and progress is no longer limited by Earth’s boundaries.

Name: Alpha Mission, Cornell Space Systems Design Studio
Description: The Alpha mission is a research project under the larger Space Systems Design Studio at Cornell University. Developed by a team of largely undergraduate students, Alpha is a 1U CubeSat that deploys a free-flying retroreflective light sail equipped with four ChipSats (satellite-on-a-chip technology). Inspired by the Breakthrough Starshot Initiative to Alpha Centauri, the Alpha mission serves as a preliminary low Earth Orbit demonstration of technologies that enable next-generation capabilities for space exploration. In addition to the light sail payload, the CubeSat bus features secondary technology-demonstration goals of increasing the capability, responsiveness, and affordability of traditional nanosatellite system architectures. These include an entirely 3D printed bus, magnetorquer-only spin stabilization, comms through the Iridium Network, and an electrical system comprised of solely inexpensive COTS electronics. Alpha is also among the first exhibitions of holography in space, a medium that shows longer-term promise in several roles for interstellar travel.

Description:

With four core technical teams and a thriving community of over 300 active students, the society is committed to fostering innovation, collaboration, and capacity building in the geospatial domain.

Driven by a vision to contribute both nationally and internationally, IST Geospatial Society actively collaborates with leading organizations such as NCGSA, FARMDAR, NDMA, PMD, and others. Name: EXLUMINA
Description:
Powering the Final Frontier

Exlumina is a space technology company developing Everlight, the world’s first orbital power utility. Our laser-based satellite network, composed of Hyperion transmitters, IRIS relays, and EOS receivers, delivers scalable energy-as-a-service directly to spacecraft.

By moving power generation and storage off individual satellites, Everlight:
• Cuts satellite mass and launch costs by up to 40%
• Unlocks 2–5× more power for advanced payloads
• Provides continuous energy in eclipse, shadowed lunar regions, and deep-space orbits
• Enhances Earth observation performance by removing deployable solar panels and jitter

Our per-kWh billing model ensures recurring revenue while supporting both new and legacy satellites. Fleet-scale power pooling allows mega-constellations to reallocate energy dynamically, improving efficiency across thousands of spacecraft.

Currently at TRL 4 with validated laboratory prototypes, Exlumina is advancing toward a TRL 7 in-orbit demonstration in 2026 via Falcon 9. Commercial rollout will begin at TRL 8, scaling into a resilient global orbital energy grid.

Vision:
We are building the infrastructure for the next chapter of human civilization. With Everlight, energy becomes abundant and scalable, enabling deep-space exploration, autonomous industry, and permanent habitats beyond Earth. Step by step, Exlumina moves humanity closer to becoming a Kardashev Type II civilization.

Name: West Virginia Small Satellite Center
Description:
The West Virginia Small Satellite Center (WVSSC) is dedicated to designing, developing, and operating small satellite (SmallSat) missions. Located in Morgantown, West Virginia, the WVSSC is a collaboration between West Virginia University and TMC Technologies and is part of the state’s growing aerospace industry.
We provide a wide range of services to support SmallSat missions, including research, design and development, testing and integration, and mission operations. The center’s team of experts works with clients to develop customized SmallSat solutions that meet specific mission requirements and offers training programs to help educate and prepare the next generation of space professionals.

Name: Aeronautical Society of India, CFD Divison
Description:
The Aeronautical Society of India was established in 1948. It was inaugurated by Shri Jawaharlal Nehru, the then Prime Minister of India who also became the first Patron-in-Chief of the Society. Subsequently, Dr. S. Radhakrishnan, Smt. Indira Gandhi, Shri Rajiv Gandhi, Shri P. V. Narasimha Rao, Shri H. D. Deve Gowda, Shri I. K. Gujral, and Dr. Manmohan Singh served as Patrons-in-Chief of the Society.

Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam guided the Society as its President from 1992 to 1994.
The Society Headquarters Building is at 13–B, Indraprastha Estate, New Delhi – 110 002

Over the years, the Society has grown into a major professional body in the field of aeronautics, aviation and aerospace. The Society has 18 branches located at important aviation centres across the country, including Agra, Ahmedabad, Bangalore, Chandigarh, Chennai, Goa, Hyderabad, Kanpur, Kanyakumari, Kochi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Nagpur, Nashik, New Delhi, Pune, Sunabeda and Thiruvananthapuram. Name: Austronaut
Description:
Austronaut programs and workshops are designed to teach fundamental knowledge and skills for improving an individual's capability as an astronaut candidate whilst being relevant and practical to everyday life. 

Instructors teach participants about what astronauts do to prepare and perform for spaceflight as well as about the elements that support on-going success, through the lens of becoming an astronaut.  

Austronaut combines Australian and international capabilities in Personal Development, Human Performance, Astronautics and STEM skills, and shapes them into prototype astronaut training programs.

Name: InterLabs Advanced Technologies
Description: We produce high-tech solutions to contribute to the betterment of society's quality of life. Established with the synergy of interdisciplinary researchers, InterLabs offers next-generation solutions for the world. We perform futuristic actions with the trilogy of science, technology and society by combining the insights internalized from R&D researches conducted with realistic approaches. We are a highly motivated team for our sustainable solutions to increase economic and social impact.

Name: Bruin Spacecraft Group at UCLA
Description:
BruinSpace at UCLA fosters a creative and supportive environment for space mission design and development. Our mission is to inspire enthusiasm for the aerospace industry through innovative space and spacecraft engineering projects. Our diverse teams, composed of students from all backgrounds and majors—including artists, physicists, business professionals, scientists, and engineers—are united by a common passion for space exploration.

Located near the heart of the aerospace industry in El Segundo, BruinSpace empowers UCLA students to apply their passion, skills, and innovation to ambitious real-world space challenges, making tangible impacts at UCLA.

At the core of BruinSpace are three hands-on aerospace projects:

• Project Rapid: Our flagship initiative provides a professional setting for students to develop, manufacture, and launch satellites, laying the foundation for future student space experimentation and research.

• Project Overseer: A quarterly project focused on high-altitude balloon payload development, offering an accessible platform for scientific experiments and technology demonstrations, and providing valuable skills for students with limited engineering experience.

• Forge: An education and development program dedicated to upskilling members through introductory workshops and specialized training, equipping them with essential skills to contribute effectively to projects.

Through these immersive projects and learning opportunities, BruinSpace prepares a skilled next generation of space pioneers. We promote space technology through engineering achievements and outreach, continuously advancing space capabilities and inspiring enthusiasm for the aerospace industry.

BruinSpace operates under the Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Department at UCLA's Henry Samueli School of Engineering and Applied Science.

Join us at BruinSpace, where we are not just building the future of space exploration—we are making an impact here and now at UCLA.

Name: SatCamp
Description: SatCamp is annual geospatial event focused on meaningful conversation, connection, and collaboration. Share and scheme while hiking, biking, climbing, running!

A UK-based space technology startup specializing in the development of advanced satellite subsystems for CubeSats and small satellites. By bridging education, research, and real-world space missions, we aim to expand global access to smarter and more resilient satellite technologies, empowering innovators, academic institutions, and emerging space programs to design, test, and operate next-generation spacecraft.

Name: LCIARS
Description:
At LCIARS, we are passionate about leveraging the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision to transform the field of Geospatial and Remote Sensing. Located in São José dos Campos, São Paulo, Brazil, our team is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of technology to provide innovative solutions tailored to your needs. Whether it’s developing full-stack projects or delivering targeted training sessions, we bring a wealth of expertise to every challenge.

Our commitment to excellence spans across a variety of sectors, offering state-of-the-art consultancy services that integrate AI and cutting-edge image processing techniques. From 3D modeling and statistics analysis to cloud computing and visual communication, we thrive on tackling complex problems and delivering clear, impactful results.

Join us on our journey to revolutionize the way we understand and interact with our world. Follow LCIARS and be a part of our mission to drive progress and innovation in the realm of Geoscience, Remote Sensing, and beyond. Together, we can harness the power of technology to create a smarter, more connected future

Our solutions can easily be integrated into Environmental and Social Impact Assessments (ESIA), Environmental and Social Governance (ESG) reports, risk and actuarial analyses, environmental audits, and cases involving environmental compliance

Description: Our vision is to become the reference technology for the quantification of the past, present and future of natural resources by using the most modern technologies available so far in fields such as earth observation, astrophysics, theoretical and computational chemistry, fluid dynamics, and new models in health.

Name: Aerospace Medicine Commercial Spaceflight Committee
Description:
Welcome to the Aerospace Medicine Commercial Spaceflight Ad-Hoc Committee, a sub-committee of the Aerospace Medical Association. Our organization dedicated to advancing the field of aerospace medicine through focused research on commercial spaceflight.

Our mission is to enhance the safety and well-being of commercial spaceflight participants by conducting rigorous research and developing best medical practices. We bring together experts from various disciplines to foster interdisciplinary communication and provide comprehensive viewpoints on the role of aerospace medicine in commercial spaceflight.

Key aspects of our work include:

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Engaging with commercial space travel experts to ensure diverse perspectives and robust discussions on spaceflight medicine.

Scoping Review: Conducting thorough scoping reviews to identify areas needing further research and support ongoing studies in commercial spaceflight. These reviews are critical in guiding our research efforts and developing evidence-based medical practices.

Assessing Evidence for Guideline Development: Identifying and evaluating existing medical guidelines for commercial spaceflight participants, and offering recommendations for their design and implementation to ensure they meet the highest standards of safety and effectiveness.

Through our dedicated efforts, we aim to position the Aerospace Commercial Spaceflight Ad-Hoc Committee as an integrator in the field, contributing to the ongoing development and improvement of medical practices for the burgeoning commercial spaceflight industry. 

Join us as we explore the frontiers of space medicine and work towards a safer future for all space travelers. For membership https://forms.gle/FJdyL74VvNBwZKjK9

Name: Health from Space
Description:
Following the success of the first edition of Health From Space in February 2023, the best world experts, from both the life sciences, health and space domains will gather on March 4 and 5, 2024 in the Palais des Festivals in Cannes. 

The life sciences, health and related advanced biomaterial domains are on the verge of a disruptive evolution, actually a revolution, thanks to the possibility to establish and operate facilities in space. However, most of the actors in these domains are unaware of these coming dramatic changes.
