Name: A-SpaX
Description:
We are A-SpaX, a Dutch aerospace company located in Delft. A-SpaX stands for 'Affordable Space Access (B.V.)'. Our goal is to enable the opportunities space has to offer for as many people as possible. This involves providing infrastructure for manufacturing and research in space and safely bringing back our reentry capsule with your payload to Earth. Our unique reusable space facility allows us to take advantage of microgravity, ultra-high vacuum, temperature differences, minimal vibrational disturbances, and radiation in space for innovative breakthroughs. A-SpaX facilitates other companies in putting their production machines or experiments in orbit around the Earth, offering an end-to-end service from launch to delivering results and data back on Earth with our reusable capsule acting as a factory facility in orbit. Despite being made in space, our innovations, especially in pharmaceutical and medical research, have benefits for Earth as well.

Feel free to reach out to us, to find out how the space environment can help your company. You can send us an email (info@A-SpaX.com) or find more contact information on our website www.A-SpaX.com

Name: SHIELD NASA DRIVE Science Center
Description:
Overview:
The SHIELD NASA DRIVE Center is a national and international collaboration involving researchers from over half a dozen institutions, and led by Merav Opher from the Astronomy Department at Boston University. The vision of the Solar wind with Hydrogen Ion charge Exchange and Large-Scale Dynamics (SHIELD) DRIVE Science Center (DSC) is to understand the nature and structure of the heliosphere.

Research Thrusts:
What is the global structure of the heliosphere? 
How do pickup ions evolve from cradle to grave, and how do they affect heliospheric processes?
How does the heliosphere interact with and influence the interstellar medium? 
How are cosmic rays filtered by and transported through the heliosphere?

Benefits and potential impacts of successful research:
The heliosphere is an immense shield that protects the solar system from harsh galactic radiation. This radiation affects both life on Earth and human space exploration. The heliosphere is a window into processes occurring in all other astrospheres. Understanding these processes enables predictions about the astrospheric conditions necessary to create habitable planets.

Name: Uzinova Space Systems
Description:
Uzinova, 2019'da Teknofest heyecanıyla bir araya gelmiş mühendislik öğrencilerinin kurduğu bir topluluktur. Sakarya Uygulamalı Bilimler Üniversitesi bünyesinde 2020'de resmi bir kimlik kazanarak, özellikle uzay ve havacılık olmak üzere birçok alanda proje geliştirmeye başladık. 
Ulusal ve uluslararasi bircok yarismaya katilim gosterip cesitli basarilar elde etmistir.
Amacımız, küresel uzay yarışında Türkiye'yi ön sıralara taşıyacak inovasyonlar yapmak ve roket teknolojileri üzerinde yoğunlaşarak ülkemizin potansiyelini artırmaktır. Uzinova olarak, gelecekte de uzay ve inovasyon odaklı çalışmalarımıza devam ederek, Türkiye'yi küresel uzay keşiflerinde bir lider haline getirme vizyonumuzu pekiştireceğiz. Bu yolculuğa siz de katılın, Türkiye'nin uzaydaki izini birlikte çizelim.
Location: Kemalpaşa, Güldeste Sk. No:3, Serdivan, Sakarya

Name: PUTRA SPACE CLUB (PSC) UPM
Description:
Aero-Rocketry UPM is a leading student-driven aerospace organization committed to advancing rocketry, satellite technology, and aerospace engineering through technical excellence and industry collaboration. Our mission is to equip the next generation of aerospace professionals with the skills, knowledge, and experience necessary to drive innovation in the field.

We specialize in high-powered rocketry, satellite systems, and the integration of machine learning in aerospace applications. Our members engage in rigorous technical projects, including propulsion system development, aerodynamics optimization, autonomous flight control, communication module development and space systems engineering. By participating in international engineering competitions, research initiatives, and industrial partnerships, we bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world aerospace challenges.

Beyond technical development, Aero-Rocketry UPM is dedicated to education and outreach. Through mentorship programs, technical workshops, and engagement with secondary school students, we inspire future aerospace engineers while fostering a collaborative and inclusive community.

Driven by innovation and a passion for exploration, Aero-Rocketry UPM is shaping the future of student-based aerospace leadership platform. Join us as we push the boundaries of engineering and technology.

Name: AeroFly LLC
Description: AeroFly LLC is a South Dakota-based aerospace company at the forefront of advancing space technology for a sustained lunar presence and beyond. Rooted in American ingenuity, we are revolutionizing UAS solutions for both precision agriculture and defense applications, while energizing commercial and federal operations with cutting-edge aerospace innovations. By uniting next-generation electric air mobility with state-of-the-art space systems, our mission is to deliver sustainable, technology-driven breakthroughs that strengthen U.S. leadership in space exploration and redefine industry standards here on Earth. Every AeroFly innovation is designed as a stepping stone toward a future where bold ideas become transformative realities.

Description: Extellis is a satellite imaging company dedicated to delivering reliable Earth observations at an industrial scale. Our patented antennas, invented at Duke with DARPA-sponsored research, enable satellites that can take thousands of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images per day. Blending wide-area scanning with high-volume captures brings advanced spaceborne monitoring capabilities with sensible economics to drive widespread adoption.

Name: i-SAIRAS 2024
Description:
i-SAIRAS is an exciting biennial event that brings together the international space community to explore the latest advances in artificial intelligence, robotics, and automation.

The Symposia are led by the Canadian Space Agency (CSA-ASC), the German Aerospace Center (DLR), the European Space Agency (ESA), the Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA), and the National Aerospace Administration (NASA).  The Symposia provide an ideal platform for agencies, researchers, and industry professionals engaged in the fields of AI, robotics, and space exploration, seeking to share knowledge, foster collaborations, and shape the future of space technology. 

The theme for i-SAIRAS 2024 is New Frontiers: Harnessing AI, Automation and Robotics for Space Exploration and Earth's Challenges.  The Symposium will be hosted by Australia’s national science agency, CSIRO, and will include a special focus on exploring the interconnection between advancements on Earth and their applications in space exploration.

Name: DIONYSOS SATELLITE OBSERVATORY
Description:
Dionysos Satellite Observatory (DSO) actively contributes to the teaching and research programs at the NTUA's School of Rural Surveying Engineering, specializing in geodesy and satellite geodesy. We foster collaborations with Greek and international research centers, academic institutions, and scientific bodies. DSO specializes in GNSS and DORIS research, with a focus on precise orbit determination. Operating a GNSS permanent station in Greece, we monitor crustal deformation and engage in satellite altimetry research. Additionally, our contributions extend to maritime geodesy and navigation through GNSS applications. 

DSO  has also established an automated processing system for daily analysis of all operational continuous GNSS stations in Greece. This ongoing analysis has provided valuable insights into Greece's intricate tectonic settings. Furthermore, the results of our analyses are actively contributing to EUREF's Densification Project, contributing to the development of a high-resolution European velocity field. #GNSSAnalysis #TectonicsResearch #GeodeticContributions

DSO also focuses on advancing precise orbit determination tools. This development caters to GSS and DORIS research, enhancing our capabilities in accurately determining satellite orbits. Additionally, our tools contribute to space awareness research, particularly aligning with programs such as the EU Space Surveillance and Tracking, promoting enhanced monitoring and understanding of objects in space. This innovation reflects DSO's commitment to pushing the boundaries of satellite technology and contributing to broader initiatives in space geodesy research. #OrbitDetermination #SpaceAwareness #ResearchInnovation

Name: Avaruustilannekeskus
Description:
Ilmatieteen laitoksen yhteyteen perustettu Avaruustilannekeskus (AVATIKE) vahvistaa Suomen avaruusturvallisuutta.

Suomalaisen yhteiskunnan toiminta on yhä riippuvaisempi satelliittijärjestelmistä, jotka tukevat muun muassa viestintää, navigointia, sääpalveluita ja kriittistä infrastruktuuria. Näiden järjestelmien häiriöt voivat aiheuttaa merkittäviä vaikutuksia kansalliselle turvallisuudelle ja palveluiden jatkuvuudelle. Tämän vuoksi Ilmatieteen laitoksen yhteyteen on perustettu siviilipuolen Avaruustilannekeskus (AVATIKE), jonka tehtävänä on varmistaa ajantasainen ja luotettava avaruustilannekuva sekä sen jakaminen kansallisille toimijoille.

AVATIKE:n siviilijohtokeskus vastaa:
- Kansallisen avaruustilannekuvan tuottamisesta, ylläpidosta ja hallinnoinnista yhteistyössä sotilasjohtokeskuksen kanssa.
- Kansallisten havainnointijärjestelmien käytön johtamisesta.
- Viranomaisten, kriittisen infrastruktuurin toimijoiden ja tarvittaessa väestön tiedottamisesta avaruudesta tai avaruustoiminnasta johtuvista häiriö- ja vaaratilanteista.
- Toimimisesta kansallisena EU SST -yhteyspisteenä eurooppalaisessa ja kansainvälisessä tiedonvaihdossa siviilitoimintojen osalta.
- Palvelutaso-, palvelutuote- ja tiedonsiirtoratkaisujen toteuttamisesta siten, että palveluiden hyödyntäminen ei edellytä käyttäjiltä lisäinvestointeja.

Keskus vastaa jatkossa myös kriittisten satelliittijärjestelmien toiminnan seurannasta ja antaa tarvittaessa varoituksia palvelutason poikkeamista kansallisille toimijoille.

AVATIKE:n siviilikeskusta operoivat Ilmatieteen laitos ja Maanmittauslaitos. Yhteistyön tavoitteena on varmistaa, että Suomi kykenee ennakoimaan ja hallitsemaan avaruudesta johtuvia riskejä sekä tukemaan yhteiskunnan toimintavarmuutta.

Name: Texas Space Summit
Description:
The Texas Space Summit is where space stops being abstract and starts becoming real business. 

Land in San Antonio, at the heart of American industry, and sit shoulder to shoulder with the people who fund, build, regulate, test, and deploy the next generation of space infrastructure. From ground-based solutions and low-Earth orbit to cislunar and lunar operations, data, defense, and energy, this is where ideas become contracts and pilots become programs. 

Across three days, you'll move seamlessly between main-stage conversations, live system demos, investor sessions, and hosted-buyer meetings designed to help you: 

- Showcase technology in front of decision-makers
- Meet partners who can help you deliver
- Turn introductions into real opportunities

In space, the Lone Star outshines the rest.

Name: Ariel Space Mission
Description: During its 4-year mission, Ariel will study what exoplanets are made of, how they formed and how they evolve, by surveying a diverse sample of about 1000 extrasolar planets, simultaneously in visible and infrared wavelengths. It is the first mission dedicated to measuring the chemical composition and thermal structures of hundreds of transiting exoplanets, enabling planetary science far beyond the boundaries of the Solar System.

Name: King's College London Space Society
Description: The King's College London Space Society, founded in 2017, is a multi-award-winning student-run society at King's College London. The King's College London Space Society aims to provide its members with opportunities to engage in stellar space-related activities, organised with our partners in industry and societies. We also work to bridge the gap between the space industry and the students aspiring to become a part of it, whether you study something space-related or not. Name: Earth Observation Lab Aotearoa
Description:
Earth Observation Laboratory Aotearoa seeks to establish a Laboratory for Earth Observation (EO), supported by Te Pūnaha Ātea | Space Institute and Ngā Ara Whetū | Centre for Climate, Biodiversity & Society. Our mission is to be Aotearoa’s nexus for collaborative earth observation and data-driven decisions.

We are committed to inclusivity and welcome all who want to join this community—breaking down barriers within and beyond the field.

Name: KCL Space
Description:
KCL Space is a student run body that aims to provide its members with  opportunities to engage in space related activities organised in the college and international events alike! 

We run many hands-on activities such as Astronomy, building and launching rockets in competitions, high altitude ballooning designs and plenty of social events. We aim to get everyone free membership to the UK Students for Exploration and Development of Space and discounted entrance to our events, including the National Student Space Conference.

We keep members updated on networking opportunities with industry, sponsors, and space organisations, and hence provide access to a national network of students and space enthusiasts, as well as speakers from academia, and all parts of the space industry.

We aim to provide opportunities for interested students to engage with the Space industry, and keep members updated on activities taking place around the UK. Our rocketry division engages in constructing model rockets which are launched during summer in a National rocketry competition.

We have previously been awarded the UKSEDS Most Improved Branch of the Year; KCLSU Activity Group of the Year 2021/22, and we have hosted the National Student Space Conference 2022 at our university, as well as Google DEVFEST UK & Ireland.

Name: D-Orbit USA
Description: Our team members have played key roles on the Starlink, OneWeb, and Kuiper satellite constellations.  We are a joint venture with D-Orbit Italy which has raised over $150 million in 2024 alone, has over 300 employees, and has flown the ION spacecraft successfully in space 14 out of 14 attempts.

Description: Space projects using Web3 technologies are creating highly disruptive values. Often underrated by the traditional space industry, quadratic funding helps to offer these projects with the necessary kick start to prove their utility to push humanity to the next frontier as public goods.

Name: ASSC
Description:
ASSC - Astrommar Space Systems is an Egyptian joint-stock company specializing in providing innovative and integrated solutions in the space sector. Its expertise includes the design and development of space systems, as well as space monitoring and surveillance services, such as tracking space debris, space weather, celestial objects, and other space assets. With extensive experience in advanced technology applications, the company delivers cutting-edge technological solutions to help organizations and institutions effectively monitor and analyze the space environment, with a strong focus on enhancing space research sustainability and strengthening space security.

Astrommar is supported by the Academy of Scientific Research and Technology (ASRT) of Egypt, positioning it as a key partner alongside the Egyptian Space Agency (EgSA). This backing enhances the company’s ability to develop advanced space solutions and supports its vision of achieving technological progress. Additionally, Astrommar is one of the companies incubated by the Egyptian Space Agency’s business incubator, providing it with an ideal environment for growth through access to resources, training, and mentorship. This enables the company to expand its operations and contribute to Egypt Vision 2030.

With a deep understanding of satellite operators' needs, Astrommar specializes in tracking satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), accurately mapping their trajectories, and identifying potential collisions with space debris. By providing precise and reliable data, the company empowers satellite operators to adjust flight paths and prevent costly damages, ultimately enhancing global space safety.

 Why Astrommar?

 Exceptional Coverage – Precise space monitoring and risk assessment.
 Unmatched Speed – Instant response and advanced data analysis.
 Limitless Insights – Accurate information shaping the future of space.

 Innovating for a Safer Space.

Name: Rational Futures
Description: Founded by NASA's Chief Economist Team, we guide capital allocators through complex investment decisions. By combining financial rigor and technical expertise, we help our clients identify and actualize transformative opportunities. Our Partners have led major commercial acquisitions efforts, shaped government procurement policies, and built industry-defining economic frameworks.

Name: New Horizon
Description:
New Horizon Srl is an innovative Italian startup dedicated to environmental sustainability in the space sector, focused on developing advanced solutions for sustainable satellite mission management. 

Currently, we collaborate with research organizations, specialized companies, and European institutions to build a more sustainable future in space. Proudly incubated in the ESA BIC Padua program, we are part of a network driving innovation in the European space industry.

With our patented software, we support satellite operators and space companies in optimizing their constellations through orbital ecodesign and real-time environmental impact assessment. 

We also offer consultancy services for Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and ecodesign, promoting a practical and applicable approach. 

Let's make space greener, one orbit at a time!

Name: Amelia Space Technologies
Description:
Imagine a future where doing good is more profitable. A world where organizations succeed by improving our planet and creating a more optimistic society, on Earth and in Space. Welcome to Amelia.

Access Amelia’s technology to find gaps in the market and pinpoint new opportunities. See how innovative businesses like yours can benefit from positive change: 

10x Faster Detection of Environmental Hazards
To help clean up the drinking water supplied by the UK's largest lake, Amelia’s satellite imaging identified priority areas of harmful algal blooms 10x faster than conventional analysis. 

Improving the ROI for Port Decarbonization
Right now, Amelia is developing a space-based maritime emissions immune system that reduces the costs associated with pollution, while protecting the community from hazards. 

Turning the World’s Trash into Treasure
Amelia’s next big project is using space technology to repurpose (and re-profit) the $2.6 trillion worth of materials in fast moving consumer goods that are thrown away annually. 

A Game-Changing Platform 
Amelia’s Planetary Intelligence Platform is built to give you a competitive edge: 
• Intuitive ask and search engine for analysis in minutes not months 
• Autonomous monitoring and alerts that save time and reduce risk 
• AI systems and proprietary algorithms for deeper sector insights 
• Easier collaboration capabilities for improved project management 
• Goes beyond digital analysis and provides real-world implementation 
All you have to do is Ask Amelia. 

Amelia is derived from the word “Ameliorate,” to improve and make better.

Name: Dot Explorations Pvt. Ltd.
Description:
Dot Explorations is a pioneering force in space robotics, dedicated to pushing the boundaries of exploration and innovation. Guided by our mission to excel in space technology, we are committed to advancing humanity’s reach into the cosmos.

Through Anant Vega, our ISRO-registered Space Tutor subsidiary, we bring the wonders of space to educational institutions by establishing advanced space and astronomy labs. These labs offer hands-on learning, inspiring the next generation of scientists and explorers.

At Dot Explorations, we are driven by curiosity and excellence, creating a future where the stars are within reach.

The phrase "dare the void" refers to the boldness and courage required to venture into the vast and unknown expanse of space, often referred to as "the void." It emphasizes the idea of challenging the emptiness and dangers of the cosmos, symbolizing bravery and innovation in exploring the unknown.

In our context, it signifies Dot Explorations' fearless approach to using robotics for space exploration, navigating the uncertainties of the final frontier with cutting-edge technology.

Name: COSM
Description: Collaboratively powering the progress and sustainability of humanity through space. Empowering cross-industry collaboration.

Name: GLOC2023 - Global Space Conference on Climate Change
Description:
The International Astronautical Federation (IAF) and its member the Norwegian Space Agency (NOSA) are pleased to announce that the 2023 Global Space Conference on Climate Change (GLOC 2023) will be taking place in Oslo, Norway on 23 – 25 May 2023. 

"Fire and Ice - Space for Climate Action" is the title of the first conference.

The programme is designed to bring together the international community, including senior representatives of the major space agencies, industries, governments, policy makers, academia and NGOs, as well as representatives of non-space sectors involved in the climate change debate. 

These leaders in the field will converge in Oslo, Norway to present results, exchange ideas, debate roadmaps, and discuss the future opportunities provided by space activities to contribute to the global climate change mitigation efforts.

GLOC 2023 will address various topics of interest in relation to space and climate change with a specific focus on:

✦ Causes and Effects of Climate Change

✦ Possible Solutions

✦ Space Remote Sensing / Satellite Monitoring / Earth Observation

✦ Maritime Surveillance

✦ Intergovernmental and Interagency Actions

✦ Climate Change and our daily lives

Name: Langkawi International Space Forum 2023 (LiSF23)
Description:
Join us at the Langkawi International Maritime & Aerospace Exhibition (LIMA'23) and the Langkawi International Space Forum from May 24-25 May, 2023, in Langkawi, Malaysia. Driven by the exponential growth in the density of objects in orbit and the increasing risk of satellite collisions, Safe on Orbit emerges with an effective solution: a tool for orbital traffic monitoring based on conjunction analysis and satellite tracking. By providing real-time data and predictive analytics, the product assists satellite operators in performing orbital maneuvers to reduce collision risk, extend satellite life, and optimize operational time. Name: vCity
Description:
Every city is a delicate balance of interwoven networks, and improving them is often disruptive for local residents. So, how do we predict those impacts while transforming our cities into better places to live?

This is possible thanks to Urban Digital Twins: virtual replicas of real cities, that optimise data to map the invisible connections between factors such as air quality, public services, or energy efficiency. They allow us to simulate the ripple effect of urban policies over time, making big changes simple for everyone to understand.

vCity is developed by the Data Analytics and Visualization Group at the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, a multidisciplinary team focused on solving problems with data.

Our goal is to help the space sector innovate for a more sustainable future. To do this, Metasat UK aims to lessen the environmental footprint of their customer's products, processes and services through life cycle assessment (LCA) / ecodesign and develops a variety of space technologies for (and in collaboration with) its clients, designed to aid in boosting sustainable development. Such technologies include (but are not limited to) space debris detection technologies and solar power satellites. The company also creates numerical models, methods, simulations, tools and software using a combination of in-house and external data products which cover both space situational awareness and the Earth system.

The company's innovation and customer-focussed approach has already allowed it to gain considerable interest from a variety of international clients. Connect with Metasat UK on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn to find out more and keep up to date with our progress!

Name: Stratoflight
Description:
Stratoflight is a French company developing a multimodal manned vehicle for stratospheric flights using a zero-pressure balloon. Business activities include space tourism and scientific missions. Stratoflight is the operator of the flights.
Under a balloon using decarbonated hydrogen, a capsule will reach the altitude of 35,000 meters where six passengers (4 passengers and 2 pilots) can stay for up to one hour. The descent will begin by venting some of the lifting gas. Once the altitude of 8,000 m is reached, the capsule is released and a high performance parafoil is deployed. The two pilots will fly the vehicle to a selected landing field and will land at low speed. 
Key developments include the manned vehicle, specific types of parafoils for the descent and pressure suit for passengers. Several innovations will be patented. 
Space tourism is a nascent market targeting individuals looking for unique experiences. 
Beyond the ecological aspect of using decarbonated hydrogen, the main differentiating factor of the project is the extra-vehicular activity (EVA) taking place from a balcony at the rear of the capsule. From there, passengers can admire the extraordinary view and the earth curvature from a horizon from Amsterdam to Barcelona equipped with pressure suits like astronauts.
The first flight is scheduled for 2025.

Name: Alt Propulsion
Description:
The Alternative Propulsion Engineering Conference (APEC) is the world's leading breakthrough conference for Warp Drives, Gravity Control, Energy & UFO Physics featuring hundreds of top physicists, engineers, & innovators.

Founded in late 2020 by Falcon Space and American Antigravity, the conference has quickly established itself as a unique platform for engineers, physicists, and independent innovators to present and debate ideas that fall outside mainstream aerospace research. Its mission is to advance experimental approaches to propulsion, focusing on engineering and practical demonstrations as much as on theoretical models.

What makes APEC distinctive is its diverse mix of participants. The event regularly features PhD researchers and professionals from organizations such as NASA, DARPA, the Air Force Research Laboratory, and MIT, alongside garage experimenters, hobbyists, and entrepreneurs. This cross-pollination of ideas between institutional experts and independent builders creates a fertile ground for innovation, blending scientific rigor with imaginative experimentation.

The topics presented at APEC are as bold as its community. Discussions often center on gravity modification, warp drives, inertial propulsion, electrogravitics, field-effect propulsion systems, and zero-point energy. In recent years, APEC has also incorporated sessions on the physics of Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP), reflecting broader scientific and public interest in anomalous flight technologies. Presentations range from highly technical papers to live demonstrations of experimental hardware, offering a rare mix of visionary science and hands-on engineering.

APEC has been described by the media as the “Woodstock of gravity-modification research” and the “wildest science club on the internet.” Its 2023 return after a brief hiatus reinvigorated the community, with talks covering electromagnetic propulsion, propellantless drives, and speculative warp reactor concepts.

Name: Qalice
Description:
Qalice is a groundbreaking quantum network framework that integrates quantum communication, computation, and energy transmission into a unified, sustainable platform. At its core, Qalice employs quantum mirroring—a dynamic reflective feedback loop that continuously stabilizes qubit coherence and extends superposition states—coupled with photonic integrated circuits (PICs) and quantum electrodynamics (QED) to enable secure, high-speed data exchange.

Beyond data, Qalice revolutionizes energy systems by converting renewable or conventional energy into high-power laser beams using advanced laser pulse interactions (LPIs). These beams are transmitted via free-space links or innovative optical waveguides, where adaptive optics and deformable mirrors ensure precise collimation and minimal losses. Ground-based receivers, such as rectenna arrays and thermophotovoltaic systems, reconvert the laser energy into electricity, facilitating decentralized power distribution and modernizing aging grids.

Central to Qalice is the QE3 (Quantum Efficiency, Environment, and Economy) model, which optimizes energy flows by uniting three pillars: Efficiency (reducing wasted energy), Environment (minimizing emissions with real-time precision), and Economy (enhancing cost-effectiveness and sustainable growth). Pilot studies indicate that QE3 can boost efficiency from 78% to 92%, reduce energy losses from 20% to 7%, lower emissions by approximately 33%, and cut operating expenses by up to 25%.

By merging quantum-enhanced control with innovative energy beaming technologies, Qalice offers both centralized power management and decentralized off-grid solutions. It represents a transformative step toward resilient, efficient, and secure global energy networks that reclaim wasted energy, drive sustainable progress, and foster economic growth.

Name: Integradiv project
Description: Assessing current and future patterns of biodiversity and their underlying drivers is critical to developing relevant conservation programmes and promoting sustainable development and human well-being. While research and action to conserve biodiversity has so far focused on taxonomic diversity alone, consideration of the diversity of organismal traits (functional) and evolutionary lineages (phylogenetic) holds promise for effective conservation and maintenance of resilient ecosystems to global change. Indeed, taxonomic, functional and phylogenetic facets of biodiversity do not respond to drivers, especially anthropogenic perturbations, at the same rate, scale or in the same way. The fragmented nature of our knowledge of biodiversity severely limits our ability to anticipate current and future conservation needs. The main objective of INTEGRADIV is to develop an integrative approach to provide guidance on how taxonomic, functional and phylogenetic facets of biodiversity should be incorporated into realistic conservation plans. INTEGRADIV relies on the assessment of ecosystem integrity (its degree of conservation) and vulnerability to global change, based in particular on an innovative quantification of distributions of trait-based and phylogenetic attributes. The project will benefit from both existing and newly collected occurrence, trait and phylogenetic data to develop an innovative biodiversity assessment framework considering multiple spatial scales (regional and Euro-Mediterranean) and taxonomic groups. Considering the diversity of trees, butterflies and birds in the Euro-Mediterranean biodiversity hotspot, the key questions addressed by INTEGRADIV are 1) Where are the areas of high biodiversity value? 2) What is the ecological integrity of Euro-Mediterranean forests? 3) How have spatial patterns of biodiversity been shaped and how vulnerable are they to climate and land-use changes?

Description:
Team SpiCy was born as a group of students from very different backgrounds with a common purpose: long-term oxygen production for space exploration. 

The team is based in Munich and the members are pursuing Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees in Biology, Aerospace and Electrical Engineering and Communication and Media in the LMU, TUM and HM.

For fulfilling that purpose, our multidisciplinary team has the mission to study the viability of Cyanobacteria in biological Life Support Systems.

To assess the viability of our biological solution, we have to test our hypothesis and we chose the Programme REXUS/BEXUS for it. We have been selected for the BEXUS 15th Cycle. The project is supported by DLR, SNSA and ESA Education Office.

The launch of our experiment to the stratosphere will take place in Kiruna during October 2024. To reach that point, the team has been invited to workshops and training weeks in the main European hubs for space reasearch like DLR HQ in Bonn, ESRANGE in Kiruna and ESTEC in the Netherlands. In all these initiatives, the team has to prove that their experiment and design where properly defined for the mission ahead. 

Throughout the process, we had the chance to meet students and professionals with our same interests in space research!

Name: ASGSR Students - American Society for Gravitational and Space Research
Description:
Welcome to the official student chapter of the American Society for Gravitational and Space Research (ASGSR)! We are a multidisciplinary group of science and engineering students from across the United States and around the world who are interested in microgravity research and space-related sciences.

Our members include undergraduate and graduate students, K–12 participants, and recent graduates. Some of us are actively engaged in spaceflight experiments, while others are just beginning to explore the field. Together, we participate in educational outreach, research communication, and professional development through the framework of our parent organization.

We organize student-led sessions at the annual ASGSR conference, which brings together leading experts in gravitational biology, radiation, aerospace engineering, and space physical sciences. Throughout the year, we also host webinars with researchers, coordinate workshops, and share career and research opportunities with students. We help plan events like student poster sessions, technical symposia, policy trips to Washington, D.C., and more.

ASGSR, founded in 1984, is a nonprofit scientific society partnered with NASA’s Biological and Physical Sciences Division. Its members represent academia, government, and industry, and the society supports research on how living systems and physical processes respond to gravity on Earth and in space.

Research areas covered at the ASGSR annual meeting include:

• Plants
• Microbes
• Vertebrates
• Invertebrates
• Radiation research
• Combustion science
• Fluid physics
• Soft matter
• Fundamental physics
• Materials
• Biophysics
• Biofluids
• Granular materials
• Enabling technologies
• Cross-disciplinary life and physical sciences
• Education and outreach

To get involved, follow us @ASGSRstudents on LinkedIn, Instagram, X, Facebook, and YouTube. Learn more and subscribe to our newsletter at students.asgsr.org.

The Centre fosters hands-on learning and industry collaboration with organizations such as ISRO, IN-SPACe, and private aerospace startups.

Our flagship projects include:

CETSAT-P1: A magnetometer-temperature sensing payload which was designed and tested successfully to be launched aboard ExseedSpace’s platform on PSLV Stage 4 in December 2019.

CETSAT-P2 (ongoing): A gamma-ray photon detection payload in Low Earth Orbit, featuring onboard FPGA-based data handling and LYSO crystal viability testing.

SSTDC bridges CET’s academic legacy with the cutting edge of space exploration—empowering the next generation of engineers and scientists.

Description:
Israel needs new and bold space entrepreneurs and engineers.   

Therefore,
Zion.SPACE  was founded in 2020 with the goal of launching a science self-designed rocket that has the ability to reach space - in order to inspire young Israeli people to pursue education in science, engineering, and math, which will benefit Israel and society
(even if they don't all wind up as rocket scientists).

Armed with determination and the goal of putting a fully design-from-scratch rocket into space,  
The Zion.SPACE Association aims to be Israel’s first civilian organization to successfully launch and recover an 
entirely society-designed and self-built rocket (Zion-1), which will pass the Karman line, the recognized boundary of space at 100 km (331,000 ft). 

Deadline for the first mission: Q2 of 2025,
claiming the record for the highest altitude ever reached by a civilian organization built and designed vehicle, in all of Israel and the Middle East.

Until now, the only space launch from Israeli soil is by the government’s Shavit and takes place, for security, under the cloak of secrecy. Far from the eyes and from the minds of the average Israelis.
Hence, Association’s current space-shot-rocket vehicle is Zion-1 - which is going to inspire and be launched from the Negev Desert, in front of the eyes and minds of Israel’s future generation of engineers and dreamers.
 
In addition, Zion.SPACE aims to provide the Israeli public the opportunity to apply knowledge taught in Associations' lectures and seminars, to a space shot rocket project with both tangible challenges and tangible results.

Across all engineering disciplines, we as an association, have made incredible 
advances in the fields of solid and hybrid propellants, composite structures, on-board flight computers and embedded software, high altitude recovery, thermal protection and avionics systems.

Name: SPACE4OCEAN ALLIANCE
Description:
Space4Ocean Alliance : l’espace au service de l’océan

Initiée par le CNES et soutenue par des partenaires internationaux, la Space4Ocean Alliance est une initiative mondiale qui vise à renforcer l’usage des solutions spatiales pour mieux comprendre, protéger et gérer les océans.

Face à l’urgence climatique, à l’érosion des littoraux, à la pollution marine, à la pêche illégale ou encore à la disparition d’îles entières, les défis sont immenses, en particulier pour les pays en développement et les petits États insulaires en première ligne. L’espace offre des moyens puissants pour y répondre : observation de la Terre par satellite, données en temps réel, cartographie des zones vulnérables, prévision des risques côtiers, et surveillance de la biodiversité.

L’Alliance crée un pont inédit entre le secteur spatial et les acteurs marins, maritimes et côtiers : scientifiques, décideurs, institutions publiques, ONG, et organisations internationales. Elle encourage le partage de données, le dialogue, la formation, et le développement de services opérationnels adaptés aux besoins locaux.

Ouverte, non contraignante, et collaborative, la Space4Ocean Alliance sera officiellement lancée à l’occasion de la Conférence des Nations Unies sur l’Océan (UNOC3) à Nice en juin 2025. Elle vise à fédérer les efforts autour de l’Objectif de Développement Durable 14 (ODD14) sur la vie aquatique, mais aussi à contribuer à d'autres cadres internationaux (climat, biodiversité, sécurité maritime...).

Rejoindre la Space4Ocean Alliance, c’est faire le choix de l’action collective, de la solidarité scientifique, et de l’innovation au service de l’un des biens communs les plus précieux : l’océan.

A lo largo de nuestra trayectoria, hemos obtenido importantes reconocimientos otorgados por la NASA, entre los que se destacan el System Safety Award (2020), el STEM Engagement Award (2022) y el Spirit Award en 2023 y 2024, además de haber alcanzado el segundo lugar general (2nd Place Overall) en 2024. Estos logros nos permitieron recibir el reconocimiento de la Presidencia de la República Dominicana como “Orgullo Nacional” en 2020.

Creemos firmemente en el valor de las alianzas estratégicas. Nuestros patrocinadores se integran a una plataforma de alto impacto educativo, visibilidad internacional y responsabilidad social, asociando su marca con la NASA, la excelencia académica y el desarrollo del talento dominicano.

Name: NanoFF-Nanosatellites in Formation Flight
Description:
The NanoFF mission, conducted at the Chair of Space Technology at TU Berlin, aims to demonstrate both manual and autonomous close-proximity formation flight capabilities using two CubeSats in Low Earth Orbit. Deployed from an orbital transfer vehicle into a carefully designed helix orbit in January 2024, the CubeSat formation ensures passive collision safety and stability. The mission's objectives include the technology demonstration of deployable solar panels, star-trackers, S-band downlink, and a resistojet propulsion system. The primary payload is an Earth Observation camera system with four spectral channels.

Additionally, the satellites are equipped with an ultra-high frequency based inter-satellite link for data exchange during autonomous formation flight operations and precise navigation supported by multi-constellation GNSS receivers and laser retro-reflectors. The NanoFF mission showcases cutting-edge space technology and paves the way for future advancements in satellite formation flight, close proximity operations and Earth observation.

Name: EDHPC 2025
Description:
The European Data Handling &  Data Processing Conference – EDHPC 2025 – will be held from the 13th to the 17th of October 2025 in Elche, Spain. It is organised by the European Space Agency (ESA), with the support of the local tourist office.

The EDHPC will be an international forum covering diverse aspects of the most relevant topics for on-board computers, on-board data handling systems, on-board data processing and microelectronics for space applications including both platform and payload applications (RF and optical).

The main objectives are:
> To bring together and foster cooperation between different experts in the areas of space data handling, data processing, and microelectronics components.
> To discuss architectures / reference designs / microelectronics component developments / performance of current and future data handling – data processing hardware for space.
> To establish closer links between industry and international / national space agencies, to enable further developments in these fields

We highly encourage professionals from industry, academia, research centres, and space agencies to contribute to and attend this conference. Participants will have further opportunities for networking during the industrial exhibition, which will take place throughout the week.

We have selected Elche for this EDHPC edition. Elche is a municipality of the province of Alicante in the Valencian Community in Spain. The rich history of the town reaches back to the ancient Greek, Carthaginians and Romans, who realised one of the very early environmental engineering projects with the Palmeral of Elche. It is the largest palm grove in Europe, which survived the centuries, and is now an Unesco World Heritage Site.

Name: MAREEKH DYNAMICS
Description:
Mareekh Dynamics is a space technology start-up developing habitat systems for low-pressure planetary (Mars and the Moon), and high-altitude (Earth) environments. Our goal is to create low cost, extremely efficient human habitation structures based on our patented Craterhab design that made it to the list of top ten finalists in the International Mars City State Design Competition 2020. For planetary environments such as Mars, the Craterhab Technology incorporates human habitation modules, radiation shield, and a novel power generation technology from in-situ resource utilization of Martian sub-surface ice. Scaled down version of Craterhab Technology can be applied in high-altitude environments on Earth to help prevent and mitigate acute and chronic altitude sickness that affects millions of residents of those environments such as Andes, Himalayas, and Tibet. This also includes hundreds of thousands of workers working in very high-altitude mining sites around the globe particularly in Andes, and the trekkers and mountaineers.
Establishment of Craterhab pressurized habitat systems at the mining sites in excess of 4500m above sea level will also help us realize our visioned Halfway-to-Mars Initiative (H2M) for training the astronauts for Mars (Marstronauts). And not forgetting the promotion of tourism at high altitude regions. The opportunities that can be made possible through the Craterhab Technology are limitless. 
We completion of the patenting process in 2022 and 2023 and presenting it at the International Mars Convention, we have now launched our partnerships and collaborations, and fundraising campaign through the Founders Institute.

Name: PEOPLE-ECCO
Description:
PEOPLE-ECCO is a project funded by the European Space Agency (ESA) under the Earth Observation Science for Society (EO4Society) programme. The two overarching goals of the PEOPLE-ECCO project are:


Goal 1: To engage CSOs/NGOs active in ecosystems conservation by involving them in the co-design of Earth Observation-supported methodologies applicable in their operational practices and though EO capacity building activities, and


Goal 2: To develop, test, demonstrate and validate EO-integrated methods, based on the identified user requirements and co-design. Building on existing EO initiatives and projects, the EO solutions developed in this project will answer two parallel conservation objectives: monitoring conditions and management effectiveness of existing protected areas (Objective A), and site suitability identification of high-priority areas to be protected (Objective B).

𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘 𝗘𝗔𝗚𝗟𝗘 - 𝗙𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗘𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗵 𝗧𝗼 𝗦𝗽𝗮𝗰𝗲 
Designing, building, and launching sounding rockets for scientific missions.

Name: Moroccan Association for Space Studies
Description:
The Moroccan Association for Space Studies (MASS) is dedicated to democratizing access to space exploration and astronomy for children and young people across Morocco. Our mission is to inspire the next generation of scientists, engineers, and dreamers by fostering curiosity through educational programs, interactive workshops, and innovative initiatives.

As pioneers in the field, we aim to position Morocco as a key player in space education and innovation. From organizing space-themed workshops and competitions to exploring analog missions, we strive to make space accessible, exciting, and inclusive.

Name: Black Sand Solutions
Description:
Black Sand Solutions is a service-disabled veteran-owned small business delivering technology solutions for space, aerospace, and maritime domains. Our team leverages AI/ML pipelines, custom software development, and scientific research to tackle the most complex challenges faced by government and commercial partners.

Our Mission
To enhance mission readiness and operational efficiency by providing tailored, innovative, and reliable solutions that bridge technology and mission critical applications.

Name: Applied Plasma Technologies
Description:
Applied Plasma Technologies (APT) is a leading research and development company specializing in plasma-assisted technologies. Founded in 2003 and headquartered in Marshall, VA, APT leverages over 40 years of expertise to provide comprehensive services from idea to prototyping, production, and worldwide maintenance.

Our team has over two centuries of combined experience in developing and manufacturing innovative plasma systems for gas turbines, boilers, nano-material synthesis, and more. We pioneered plasma ignition systems, enhancing reliability and safety for various applications, including aircraft engines.

Our latest breakthrough, the Transient Arc Plasma Torch, offers unparalleled electrode lifespan and operating range. Additionally, our next-generation ICP/RF plasma torches cater to high-pressure operations with power up to 200 kW, ideal for waste gasification, material synthesis, and surface modifications.
In 2022, we expanded our facilities in Marshall, VA, to include state-of-the-art production and testing buildings, supporting our commitment to advancing plasma technology.

Join us in empowering future innovations with plasma and nano technology!

Name: Pluton UPV
Description: Grupo de Generación Espontánea de la Universidad Politécnica de Valencia cuyo objetivo es el diseño ensamblaje, validación y testeo de un nanosatélite o CubeSat.

Name: ASOCIACIÓN ASTRONÁUTICA COLOMBIANA
Description:
La Asociación Astronáutica Colombiana - ASTCOL es una entidad sin ánimo de lucro de carácter académico, científico y social; integrada por Colombianos dentro y fuera del territorio nacional; la cual, promueve, fomenta y fortalece espacios para la formación, uso, investigación, desarrollo y divulgación de las ciencias aeroespaciales en sus diferentes áreas en Colombia; enfocando esfuerzos en la búsqueda y generación de nuevos talentos, motivando a su vez la consolidación de una cultura científica en los diversos sectores de la sociedad.

The Colombian Astronautical Association - ASTCOL is a non-profit entity of an academic, scientific, and social nature; made up of Colombians inside and outside the national territory; which promotes, encourages, and strengthens spaces for the training, use, research,  development, and disclosure of aerospace sciences in its different areas in Colombia; focusing efforts on the search and generation of new talents, in turn motivating the consolidation of a scientific culture in the various sectors of society.

Name: SpaceLab LTD
Description: SpaceLab addresses one of the most pressing challenges in remote operations: reliable data transmission where traditional networks fail. Through its satellite-connected IoT terminals, SpaceLab enables real-time monitoring and early warning alerts in forests, road networks, disaster zones, and off-grid industrial sites. The system ensures that critical data reaches decision-makers without interruption—even in the most infrastructure-limited environments.

Name: South West Aerospace Network
Description: SWAN is a network for enthusiasts and professionals alike who share a passion and curiosity for the space and aerospace sectors. With a vibrant and ever expanding presence, several hubs, and a lot of activity in the aerospace community across the South West and the West of the UK, we hope to organise monthly networking events in the cities across the region. These events provide an opportunity for both professional and personal networking to connect and to get to know your local and regional aerospace community.

Name: SPACE Centre of Excellence
Description:
In astrophysics and cosmology (A&C), HPC (high performance computing)-based numerical simulations are invaluable instruments to support scientific discovery. 
Given the complexities of the problems, they represent essential tools for modelling, interpreting, and understanding the physical processes behind the observed sky. 
Advances in computational power promise a wealth of groundbreaking, new scientific discoveries by making ever greater numerical simulations feasible provided that equally advanced tools are created to exploit these computational resources. 
For a field like astrophysics, which is generally unsuited to laboratory experiments, the significance of this development cannot be overestimated.
Future exascale computing systems are expected to have extremely complex, heterogeneous architectures. The currently used numerical simulation codes are not suitable for use on these systems since they were not purposedly designed for them and therefore cannot effectively take advantage of the superior processing capabilities promised. 
SPACE’s main goal, therefore, is to enable current astrophysical and cosmological codes to be used on the pre-exascale HPC architectures funded by the EuroHPC JU and made available at the end of 2022, as well as on future architectures by re-designing or adapting the existing computational tools for this next-generation hardware.
It will bring together scientists, community code developers, HPC experts, hardware manufacturers, and software developers in co-design activities to re-engineer eight of the most widely used European A&C HPC codes into new products that can efficiently exploit future computing architectures. 

Acknowledgement
Funded by the European Union. This work has received funding from the European High Performance Computing Joint Undertaking (JU) and Belgium, Czech Republic, France, Germany, Greece, Italy, Norway, and Spain under grant agreement No 101093441.

Name: Scarlet Space Technologies
Description:
Our Vision: We are taking a leading role in Türkiye’s space technologies ecosystem by developing analog space research stations and astronaut training programs. Our goal is to create solutions that contribute to scientific research, technological innovation, and humanity’s future in space — positioning our country as a strategic hub in this field.

Our Mission: To become a globally recognized leader in space research and technologies by developing innovative and sustainable solutions. We aim to make Türkiye competitive power in analog space missions and advanced space technologies, shaping humanity’s journey beyond Earth.

Name: CSO(Cognitive Space Optics)
Description:
CSO is a next-generation aerospace company specializing in the development of high-resolution electro-optical payloads for small satellites operating in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and Very-Low Earth Orbit (VLEO).

We design and deliver compact, lightweight, and high-performance camera systems that enable governments, commercial operators, and research organizations to capture precise and reliable Earth observation data. Our engineering team focuses on optimizing optical performance within strict SWaP constraints, allowing mission designers to achieve superior imaging capability without compromising platform agility or launch efficiency.

Our portfolio ranges from small-form-factor optical payloads for constellation missions to larger, high-resolution systems designed for advanced intelligence, mapping, and environmental monitoring applications. Each system is built with robust thermal stability, precision alignment, and cutting-edge image processing architecture that supports today’s rapidly evolving space missions.

CSO is also advancing onboard AI and edge-processing technologies, enabling real-time enhancement, event detection, data compression, and mission-adaptive imaging. These capabilities significantly reduce downlink requirements and accelerate the delivery of actionable insights to end users.

Our commitment to engineering excellence spans the full development cycle—from early concept design and optical simulation to environmental testing, calibration, and delivery. We collaborate closely with satellite manufacturers, mission integrators, and global partners to ensure seamless system integration and reliable mission performance.

At CSO, our mission is to enable a smarter and clearer view of Earth through high-value, high-precision space optics. We continue to expand our global footprint through innovation, technical rigor, and partnerships that shape the future of Earth observation.

Name: BigDipper Exploration Technologies
Description:
We are a passionate team, developing Modular & Autonomous Robots for Lunar missions. These robots will enable a thriving lunar economy, by developing infrastructure and utilizing resources on the Lunar surface. Our robotic platform will enable us to offer Product , Payload , Data and Mining services on the Lunar surface.

For humanity to have a long & sustainable future we need to start looking at Space as a natural extension to Earth, starting with the Moon. Resources on the Moon will be a stepping stone towards a sustainable presence in Space. Water-Ice, for example, will enable a thriving Cislunar economy by serving as a source of propellant.

To meet this oncoming requirement, we are building robots that can be utilized for scientific & prospecting missions in the short term (4-5 years)  and industrial tasks in the medium to long term (6-10 years).

Name: INVAR (Ingeniería y Visión Artificial)
Description:
INVAR es una empresa tecnológica que apuesta por el desarrollo de proyectos basados en algoritmos y rutinas de INTELIGENICA ARTIFICIAL (IA) y/o en la analítica de información registrada por SATELITE, mediante el estudio y la combinación de las bandas espectrales y la generación de índices de satélite.

La finalidad es introducir en el mercado nuevas herramientas que permitan optimizar y mejorar los procesos actuales de gestión de nuestros clientes. 

Un ejemplo son los proyectos de GEOINTELIGENCIA que permiten desarrollar Sistemas de Alerta Temprana (SAT) como herramienta de ayuda a los responsables de activos, de infraestructuras,... para anticiparse a situaciones en las que se puedan producir incidentes o situaciones anómalas durante el proceso de toma de decisiones.

INVAR, en el ámbito de la ingeniería civil, también desarrolla proyectos relacionados con la gestión de infraestructuras mediante la realización de proyectos basados en movilidad, tráfico y seguridad viaria.

En definitiva, la actividad de la empresa INVAR se centra, por un lado, en la prestación de servicios de apoyo técnico, consultoría y asesoramiento y, por otro, en el desarrollo de servicios y productos innovadores bajo el marco de la I+D+i. 

La experiencia laboral del equipo humano de INVAR, permite ofrecer la realización de los siguientes servicios:

- ASESORAMIENTO TÉCNICO Y CONSULTORÍA
- INTELIGENCIA/VISION ARTIFICIAL
- ANALISIS DE INFORMACIÓN SATÉLITE (RADAR/OPTICO)
- GESTIÓN DE INFRAESTRUCTURAS
- FORMACIÓN

Si tienes una idea o un proyecto ponte en contacto con nosotros. Te asesoramos y ayudamos.

Name: Asia-Pacific Space Cooperation Organization
Description: Asia-Pacific Space Cooperation Organization is a social organization that organizes educational training programs.

Name: Australasian Space Innovation Institute
Description:
The Australasian Space Innovation Institute (ASII) is an independent, enduring, not-for-profit organisation dedicated to scaling the impact of space innovation nationally and across the Indo-Pacific.

At the ASII, we are building satellites that will enhance digital infrastructure to transform agriculture, mining, defence, climate resilience, and community safety, all with the ultimate goal of serving the public good and advancing the national interest.

The ASII is underpinned by collaboration between research institutions, industry, and government both locally and globally. By promoting regional leadership opportunities, the ASII strengthens Australia’s partnerships with New Zealand and the Indo-Pacific, reducing reliance on foreign services for critical space infrastructure.

Name: Crysalis
Description:
Crysalis is a project under the Horizon Europe research and innovation programme, led by a consortium of 4 companies: Absolut System, The Exploration Company, Centre Spatial de Liège and the Polytechnic University of Catalonia.

The aim of the Crysalis project is to develop a system for the transfer and long-term storage of cryogenic propellant in microgravity and for refuelling in space. 

This project opens up new prospects for exploration, particularly in terms of transport and storage in orbit and long-duration missions.

Name: Wavcom Technology Private Limited
Description:
Wavcom Technology Pvt Ltd – Pioneering Innovation in RF & Antenna Solutions

Wavcom Technology Pvt Ltd is a leading innovator in RF and antenna technology, dedicated to delivering high-quality, research-driven solutions. With a strong focus on precision engineering and affordability, we have introduced multiple first-of-their-kind products in India, including Spiral Antennas, DF Antennas, and Horn Antennas.

Our expertise lies in providing customized, compact, and cost-effective antenna solutions without compromising on performance. We are committed to quality, innovation, and advanced research, ensuring that every product we create meets the highest industry standards.

At Wavcom Technology Private Limited, we don’t just manufacture antennas—we engineer excellence, pushing the boundaries of RF technology to empower industries with cutting-edge solutions.

Name: Indie Space OZ
Description: Indie Space OZ is a platform dedicated to providing comprehensive and insightful coverage of Australian space, culture, and sustainability. Indie Space OZ aims to bridge the information gap between Australia and the global community, offering a unique perspective on the Australian landscape. We are committed to delivering critical discussions, well-researched reporting that informs and engages a diverse international audience.

Name: Space Physics Research Laboratory
Description: The Space Physics Research Laboratory (SPRL) mission connects the excellence of the University of Michigan to the development of innovative engineering solutions to explore extreme environments throughout the Solar System and enable transformative research. Through our partnerships with principle investigators across the University and in industry we imagine, design, test, build, and deploy unique hardware and software technologies. We specialize in advancing technologies, hardware components, and instruments through technology readiness levels (TRL) into spaceflight instruments for both earth and space observing NASA mission. We also work to develop autonomous, highly reliable, rugged sensors that can be deployed in harsh environments on Earth such as in the ocean or antarctic. Throughout all of our work, we provide unique experiential educational experiences and mentoring for undergraduate and graduate students that will set them on a rewarding career path unlike any other.

Name: Space News
Description: We provide global news coverage of the space domain and related activities, every day.

Name: AstroNova
Description:
The AstroNova society provides a roof to all the space science enthusiast who want to know about astronomy and any of it's domain ranging from Astrophotography, Remote Sensing, Astrophotonics to name a few. Our society also enlightens it's members with various exciting ideas and important lessons through lecture series, presentations, workshops etc.

So if you have any curiosity regarding space research within you and you are one of the fellow peers of the University of Calcutta you can join us in the unending exploration and become a proud member of AstroNova.

Do visit us at our official website and other social media links given below.

Name: Landstronaut
Description: Landstronaut competitively provides products and services for all interests of Human Performance Enhancement (HPE) and Survivability from Land to Space. We focus on radiation detection, protection, and reduction in extreme and exploratory environments for commercial and military use.

Name: ODISSEE project
Description: The ODISSEE (Online Data Intensive Solutions for Science in the Exabytes Era) project, funded by the European Union, brings together 14 partners and 2 associate partners to develop innovative technologies and methodologies to process the unprecedented volume of scientific data produced by research infrastructures such as CERN's HL-LHC and SKAO. On-the-fly AI data processing is a major challenge in the physical sciences, and SLICES RI is uniquely equipped to address it. By leveraging the European HPC ecosystem, ODISSEE is ushering in a new era for science, helping to unlock fundamental mysteries such as the nature of dark matter.

Name: İSTE Havacılık Topluluğu
Description:
Misyon:
İskenderun Teknik Üniversitesi Havacılık Topluluğu, üniversite öğrencilerini havacılık ve uzay sektörü konusunda bilgi sahibi yapmayı ve bu sektöre katılımlarını teşvik etmeyi amaçlamaktadır. Araştırmacı, analitik düşünce yeteneklerine sahip, bilgi ve becerilerini hem insanlığın hem de ülkenin yararına kullanabilen ve aynı zamanda yenilikçi mühendis adaylarının gelişimine seminerler, konferanslar, yarışmalar ve teknik geziler aracılığıyla destek olmaktır.

Vizyon:
Havacılık ve Uzay alanında projelerini hayata geçirebilen, çeşitli etkinlikler, eğitimler, projeler ve yarışmalara katılımlarıyla kendini kanıtlamış, hem ulusal hem de uluslararası düzeyde saygın bir topluluk olmayı hedeflemekteyiz.

Name: SFU Rocketry
Description: An engineering design team at Simon Fraser University working to design, build, and launch a suborbital liquid-fuelled rocket. We are the largest and most comprehensive design team at SFU, with over 85 active members, working on 11 ongoing projects that are all complimentary to the mission goal of a suborbital vessel.

Name: SPARC UPRM
Description: SPARC UPRM is a multidisciplinary student association focused on developing opportunities for students seeking to venture into the space industry. SPARC is the home of NASA's RASC-AL UPRM team, which focuses on designing mission architectures and systems for the Moon, Mars, and other destinations. Since 2018, our team has participated in the NASA RASC-AL Competition, achieving multiple awards, including three consecutive first places, a second place overall, and Best in Theme honors. Additionally, we have added the Satellite Development Initiative into our organization, which aims to design and fabricate a CubeSat with our recent project IONIC-S.

Name: CHiwi Foundation
Description:
The ChiWi Foundation is a nonprofit organization dedicated to encouraging research, education, and innovation in advanced mathematics, alternative energy, and space exploration.

Our goal is to support high-impact scientific research and make it possible for scholars and researchers all around the world to continue making significant strides in these fields.

The Foundation carries out its mission by awarding grants to research projects in space exploration, alternative energy, and advanced mathematics.

Both individuals and organizations can submit grant applications, and they can do so from anywhere in the world. We operate both in Switzerland and abroad.

Name: EPIC | ESA Partnership Initiative for Commercialisation
Description:
ESA Partnership Initiative for Commercialisation (EPIC), established in 2022, is an initiative from ESA's Commercialisation Department connecting corporate partners with ESA supported start-ups. 

EPIC reaches into the world’s largest space innovation network: 

 ESA Business Incubation Centres (ESA BICs) - currently 29 ESA BICs with over 1750 start-ups supported

 Plus other ESA Commercialisation Programmes such as ESA InCubed

EPIC aims to empower entrepreneurship and enhance the commercial cases of European start-ups through brokering clear synergetic offers. Current partners include:

Name: ESA BIC Belgium
Description:
ESA BIC Belgium | Empowering Space Innovation 

Welcome to ESA BIC Belgium – the official ESA Business Incubation Centre in Belgium, where visionary entrepreneurs transform pioneering space-based ideas into thriving businesses 

As part of the prestigious ESA BIC network across Europe, we support early-stage startups with:

• Equity-Free Funding: Access up to €60,000 to accelerate your product development 
• Expert Mentorship: Receive tailored technical, legal, and business guidance from industry leaders 
• Innovative Workspace: Thrive in a dynamic co-working environment within a vibrant start-up community 
• Strategic Partnerships: Connect with the combined expertise of Novaspace and BeCentral, plus an extensive network of business connections 

Whether you’re developing cutting-edge satellite navigation, Earth observation, communication technologies, or cross-sector innovations, ESA BIC Belgium is your launchpad to success 

Join us on our journey to drive space innovation and create tomorrow’s industry breakthroughs. Connect with us today! For more information, reach out at incubation@space-business.be

Name: Keelcur Tecnología Ambiental
Description:
Somos una empresa dedicada al desarrollo, producción y comercialización de equipos de monitoreo y medición de
variables ambientales y equipos para laboratorio. Así mismo realizamos nuestro modelo de prevención de fallas en los equipos de diferentes marcas. Nuestra filosofía como equipo es desarrollar tecnología de alta calidad y de origen 100% nacional.

Name: Copernicus Space Corporation
Description: Pioneering a new Copernican Revolution in Space Exploration

Name: UChicago Space Program
Description:
SEDS is an international student organization whose purpose is to promote space exploration and development through educational and engineering projects. SEDS is fostering the development of future leaders and contributors in the expanding space industry. 
The University of Chicago’s SEDS Chapter was founded in 2018 and has since become the premiere RSO for students looking for experience and careers in astrophysics, engineering, and aerospace.
UChicago SEDS is currently undertaking two major projects: NASA’s CubeSat Challenge and High Power Rocketry.  Interested in joining either of these?  Contact us today!

Name: Sky Bound
Description:
The Aerospace Club : "Sky Bound " at Ensate was established in March 2023 with a strong commitment to the advancement of space exploration. The club members will undertake two major projects, namely the Sintering Rover for the Establishment of Bases and Encampments on the Lunar Surface and the mu-Hab Moon Habitat. These projects seek to provide the necessary infrastructure for humans to live and work on the Moon.

Throughout the journey, the Aerospace Club members will have access to a series of webinars from experts in the field, who will provide valuable insights and guidance to each team. With a focus on leveraging cutting-edge technologies and collaborative efforts, the Aerospace Club aims to make significant contributions to the field of space exploration.

Name: Centro Interdisciplinario de Estudios Espaciales
Description: Centro Interdisciplinario de Estudios Espaciales

Name: FUTURESPACE
Description:
FUTURESPACE is a Vienna-based research project exploring how European integration shapes Europe’s future in outer space—and how developments in space, in turn, shape Europe. It is led by Principal Investigator Nina Klimburg-Witjes, who has received a Starting Grant from the European Research Council (ERC)—one of Europe’s most prestigious awards for researchers.

The project brings together a multidisciplinary team of researchers and collaborators, currently including postdoctoral researcher Joseph Popper, PhD candidate Philipp Kürten, and student research assistants Hannah Schmalstich and Pepijn Deroo.

This research is funded by the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union’s (EU) Horizon Europe research and innovation programme (Grant Agreement No. 101076020). However, the views expressed on this LinkedIn profile are those of the authors and do not necessarily reflect the views of the EU or the ERC; neither the EU nor the ERC can be held responsible for them.

Name: NASA Space Apps Challenge Paris
Description: NASA's Space Apps Challenge is an international hackathon focused on space exploration, taking place over 48 hours in cities around the world. The event focuses on collaborative problem solving with the aim of producing relevant open-source solutions to global needs applicable to both life on Earth and life in space. NASA is leading this global collaboration with a number of government collaborators and over 387 teams of local organizers around the world.

Name: Cyprus Space Industry Association (CSIA)
Description:
The Cyprus Space Industry Association (CSIA) was officially established in May 2025 by some of the most prominent Cypriot space companies, including Ecliptic Defence and Space, Geofem, AmaDema, Ai ZeroCaliber, and WaterCloud. Its founding has received written supported by the Republic of Cyprus, the European Space Agency (ESA), the Department of Electronic Communications of the Deputy Ministry of Research, Innovation & Digital Policy, which is responsible for the national space strategy and ESA cooperation and the Cyprus Ministry of Defence. 

Our vision is to ensure that Cyprus can actively participate in shaping global space policies, accessing funding and partnerships and contributing cutting-edge innovations that impact both the economy and the well-being of our citizens. By uniting industry, academia, and international space leaders, we seek to transform Cyprus from a promising participant into a recognized and influential space nation, delivering tangible benefits for education, technology, infrastructure and the growth of a knowledge-based economy.

Name: The Emirates Mission to the Asteroid Belt
Description:
The Emirates Mission to the Asteroid Belt aims to add to our understanding of the foundation of our solar system and the presence and origins of the building blocks of life found in the asteroid belt, as well as lay the ground for possible future resource extraction from asteroids.

The Mission’s spacecraft is named the MBR Explorer, in recognition of the foundational role driving the creation and growth of the UAE Space Program played by Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum.

The development of the Emirates Mission to the Asteroid Belt is supported by Space Academy, a UAE Space Agency led apprenticeship program for the UAE's space sector that expedites the development of engineering, technical and innovation expertise across a number of national institutions.

Name: Nova-six
Description: Nova-six is a multi-media company centered around the space industry. Nova-six’s mission is to ignite Generation Z’s passion for space by reimagining it through the lens of contemporary culture. To this end, Nova-six has developed its brand to be a space-themed streetwear, street art, and entertainment venture. Through this approach, the #SpaceCulture movement, Nova-six hopes to spark a cultural shift, recentering space exploration in the hearts and minds of Generation Z recapturing the spirit of the first space race.

Name: EDGE SpAIce
Description:
Novel edge-ai system for accurate and near real-time plastic detection and monitoring in marine environment.
Edge SpAIce’s approach aims at hatching full potential of edge-AI technology by drastically improving DNN architectures reduction efficacy to prevent accuracy loss and bolster AI technology deployment in space by demonstrating its application in a challenging use-case scenario for marine plastic litter detection.

Name: SEDS Philippines
Description: Student for the Exploration and Development of Space in the Philippines (SEDSPH) is a national non-profit organization whose goal is to promote space exploration and development in the country by providing a community for students with a common interest in space, introducing them to opportunities that will develop their skills, and empowering their impact in the space sector.

Name: Women4Space
Description: Women4Space is a dynamic and inclusive community dedicated to connecting, empowering, and uplifting individuals passionate about space — with a special focus on those traditionally underrepresented in the aerospace sector. We bridge the gap between industry experts and enthusiasts, providing accessible mentorship, education, and networking opportunities. By fostering collaboration and amplifying diverse voices, Women4Space drives innovation and inspires the next generation of space professionals, advocates, and explorers. Our mission is to make space a welcoming place for all, where everyone’s journey beyond Earth is valued and supported.

Name: Colorado Center for Astrodynamics Research
Description: The Colorado Center for Astrodynamics Research (CCAR) is dedicated to the study of astrodynamics and the application of satellites to science, navigation, and remote sensing of the Earth and planets.

Name: WrapDrive
Description:
Welcome to WrapDrive - your cosmic compass! Here, we explore the mysteries of the universe and the cutting-edge of space technology.
From the enigmas of outer space to the latest tech trends, we uncover it all. Join us as we voyage through the cosmos, shedding light on the unknown and pushing the boundaries of what we know. At WrapDrive, the truth is out there, and we're on a mission to find it!

Name: ORISpace
Description: ORISPACE is the Romanian Space Industry Association. It represents the voice of the Romanian space entities.

Name: CHASM
Description:
CHASM is the community on analogue space missions. With the help of the Mars Society UK chapter, it hosts conferences for analogue space mission organisers and participants, has the first Wiki type database of all analogue missions, foster collaborations and leads the PARACHASM parastronaut research consortium. 

It hosts hybrid workshops in different analogue bases around the world, providing participants with the possibility to exchange and improve specific aspects of analogue missions, while visiting different bases and meeting organisers. 

CHASM also provides a wiki for analogue space missions, giving access to organisers as well as the general public to a centralised content for analogue space missions, as well as latest opportunities and media updates.

Name: Kosmodynamics
Description:
Welcome to Kosmodynamics!
Kosmodynamics is a dynamic, student-founded company specializing in providing end-to-end services for solid rocket manufacturing. We cater to the aerospace and defense sectors by offering customized solutions, including the design, simulation, testing, and manufacturing of rocket components tailored to specific requirements. From concept to launch, our expertise ensures precision and reliability at every stage of production. At Kosmodynamics, innovation fuels our mission to drive the future of space exploration.

Name: The Mars Society at Thunderbird School of Global Management, ASU
Description: As an extension of The Mars Society, we're dedicated to educating the public, media, and policymakers on the benefits of exploring Mars and the necessity of a sustainable human presence there. This chapter is also focused on highlighting the growing opportunities in the global space industry, cutting across management and the technical workforce development

My work emphasizes systemic harmony between corporate law, biodiversity, and interstellar ethics. Name: SC Solutions
Description:
SC Solutions are leaders in space innovation. With a rich space heritage, we set out to create the most comprehensive, integrated earth intelligence platform for the commercial market. We developed Precision Analytics from Earth Observation technology as a solution, and developed I-Sat as the main platform.

SC Solutions enables anyone to access the most comprehensive earth observation data sets available from U.S. and partner country satellites and derive valuable insights from cutting-edge analytics.

Dedicating to serving the needs of the commercial market, SC Solutions’ analytics target use cases in agriculture, forestry, mining, environment and sustainability, and asset management - and we are only just beginning to unlock the potential of earth observation data for the commercial market.

Contact our team to learn what next level earth intelligence can do for your business.

Whether you're an aerospace enthusiast, an architecture afficionate or just passionate about space exploration, BIM4Space offers a deep dive into the innovations that will support humanity's next giant leap.
