Name: Ulugh Beg Astronomical Institute of Uzbekistan
Description:
Ulugh Beg Astronomical Institute (UBAI) of the Uzbek Academy of Sciences is the oldest scientific institution not only in Uzbekistan but in the whole Central Asia as well. It was founded as Tashkent Astronomical observatory in 1873 and reorganized to UBAI in 1966. It was known as an important astrometric center of the FSU. The photographic plate collection obtained with the Normal astrograph installed in Tashkent in 1895 and continued up to the mid of the 80s of the last century with Kitab double astrograph made by Zeiss is one of the richest in the world. Since the end of the 19th century UBAI was involved to the International Latitude Service. Kitab station of UBAI was one of five stations of the ILS network. Today Kitab is hosting the ground based beacon of the GPS satellite navigation system.

At present the researches carried out in UBAI are mostly based on the observational data obtained at Maidanak astronomical observatory, located at a distance of 120 km south of the famous historical city of Samarkand. It lies on the spurs of the Pamir and Alai mountain system at 2700 m above the sea level. Maidanak observatory is unique due to its geographical location at the mid distance between the main astronomical facilities on the Canaries and the Hawaii as well as to its high quality atmospheric conditions. It makes Maidanak a very critical site for continuous monitoring of astrophysical objects.

Name: Space-Fit
Description:
Space-Fit team is working on the research and development of a Whole-Body Electrical Muscle Stimulation Space Wear with integrated biosensors. 
We provide a solution to improve the rehabilitation of astronauts returning from space missions. It can also be used during space flights and preparation as a complementary training method of the current forms of exercises. The main innovation of the technology is the integrated dry electrode and biosensor system. With this new technology, the stimulation (impulse transmission) is ensured without the use of water or conductive gel. The Space Wear is equipped with different biosensors, thus providing the opportunity to monitor the user's physical condition and training intensity.

Whole-Body Electrical Muscle Stimulation (WB-EMS), has a decades-long history in the field of sports, fitness, and rehabilitation. The electrical impulses reach the targeted muscle group through the skin via electrodes, thereby enabling the improvement of metabolic processes and increasing the efficiency of training.

Astronauts' skeletal muscles, bones, and cardiovascular system are severely affected by even a few days of space travel. In a microgravity environment, the lack of gravitational load on the body causes significant muscle and bone loss, which cannot be compensated properly with even intensive traditional trainings. Atrophy can also develop under terrestrial conditions as a result of aging, numerous diseases, or a sedentary lifestyle, which entails significant health risks. 
Our market-leading solutions tackle all these problems.

Name: Synchropal

Synchropal guarantees maximum protection, safety and precision against jamming, meaconing and spoofing attacks in timing and synchronization certification systems.

Synchronisation is a major problem, affecting critical infrastructures like broadcast, telecoms, and electrical utilities. The Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) system currently utilised is vulnerable to threats like jamming and spoofing: to address the problems, the start-up proposes an innovative resilient system for timing and frequency synchronisation based on an ultra-stable, multi-source, disciplined and miniaturised optical oscillator, tolerant to spoofing and meaconing attacks. The solution offers the best combination between local performance in the short term and multi-channel sources stability in the long term.

Way of Genius is a research-driven human performance and wellness company exploring how the subconscious mind, physiology, and symbolic cognition shape clarity, adaptability, and creative intelligence.

We design training and research systems that integrate neuroscience principles, biofeedback, and dream-based cognition to help individuals and teams understand their internal operating systems and perform more effectively in complex or changing environments.

Rather than optimizing surface-level productivity, our work focuses on how conscious awareness, subconscious patterning, and bodily signals interact to drive decision-making, emotional regulation, and innovation.

Our research explores symbolic cognition—the language of dreams, imagery, emotion, and metaphor—as a functional intelligence system. By studying how the subconscious communicates through symbols and physiological signals, we translate internal patterns into practical frameworks that support focus, resilience, and creative problem-solving.

Through applied training and data-informed methods, Way of Genius helps people develop coherence across mind and body, supporting both high performance and long-term well-being.

Edin is the conceptual foundation of the Way of Genius ecosystem, where wellness and performance converge through subconscious training, symbolic coding, and biofeedback-informed practices.

As technology, AI, and global systems evolve, human performance depends on adaptability and internal coherence. Way of Genius supports the next evolution of human intelligence by teaching people how to work with their subconscious systems—rather than override them.

MISTRAL-CFD for the continuum to transitional regime.
MISTRAL-DSMC for the transitional to rarefied regime.
Coupling with thermal and material response codes for degradation modelling.

A full re-entry trajectory to analyse? PAMPERO, the 6 degrees of freedom code developed by CNES is the tool to use! PAMPERO implements a full 3D thermal solver and various material degradation and fragmentation models.

No good accuracy CFD simulation without a good mesh! The meshing software GridPro have supported R.Tech hypersonic CFD simulations for years. R.Tech is the reseller of GridPro licences in France and Netherland. Contact us if you want to know more about GridPro capabilities.
---------------
---------------
R.Tech est spécialisée dans les simulations de rentrée atmosphérique. Cells-ci sont effectuées à l’aide de nos codes de calcul en utilisant des techniques matérielles, schémas numériques innovant(e)s, associé(e)s à des liens étroits avec les instituts de recherche:
MISTRAL-CFD: les écoulements entre le régime continu et le régime transitionnel.
MISTRAL-DSMC: les écoulements entre le régime transitionnel et le régime raréfié.
Des couplages avec des codes de réponse matériaux pour la modélisation de la dégradation.
Besoin d’étudier la trajectoire complète d’une rentrée atmosphérique ainsi que la dégradation de l’objet ?
PAMPERO, le code à 6 degrés de liberté développé par le CNES est l’outil à considérer ! PAMPERO utilise un solveur 3D thermique complet, différents modèles matériaux pour la dégradation ainsi que plusieurs méthodes de fragmentation.
Besoin d'un logiciel CFD robuste, flexible et intégrant maillage, GUI et post-traitement ? Contactez nous pour découvrir HELYX, le logiciel CFD open-source pour les entreprises.

To achieve this, the lab consists of and operates with high-end computational systems, GPUs, and workstations, alongside an observatory that is equipped with few of Pakistan's biggest optical and solar telescopes. Name: Lexion
Description:
Lexion is a mechanical and aerospace engineering R&D company based in Greece. By working from first principles, we engineer novel, tailored solutions that are not bound by existing technologies and carry disruptive potential across both emerging and established industries.

Our crown jewel is the APEX (Airbreathing Pulsed EXplosions) engine — a paradigm shift in high-power applications, offering both rapid response and exceptional adaptability across a wide range of operating conditions.

We envision a truly reusable spaceplane capable of reaching hypersonic speeds in airbreathing mode, then transitioning seamlessly into rocket mode to exit the atmosphere. This approach will not only drastically reduce the cost per kilogram to orbit, but also enable a new kind of launch infrastructure — one that more closely resembles an airport, allowing for frequent, flexible, and low-cost operations.

Name: PROJECT TRANSCENDENCE
Description:
About Project Transcendence:

Project Transcendence is an international, science-driven effort to safeguard Earth’s genetic future by designing and deploying the world’s first lunar seed vault. Launched by U.S. Air Force researchers and global partners, the project responds to an urgent reality: nearly 80% of Earth’s biodiversity has already been lost, and terrestrial seed banks remain vulnerable to climate change, conflict, and instability.

By convening experts across government, academia, industry, and allied nations, Project Transcendence aims to create a resilient off-world archive of Earth’s biological heritage—an insurance policy for humanity and the planet.

Name: Project DIAMANT
Description:
DIAMANT is a research-to-business initiative developing ultrathin, flexible metasurface antennas based on thin-membrane leaky-wave antenna technology for defence and space applications. The antennas are designed for platforms where low profile, lightweight structure, conformability, and energy efficiency are critical, including military vehicles, unmanned systems, and satellites. The technology enables advanced RF capabilities without the mass, volume, and cost penalties of conventional antenna solutions.

The project is supported by Aalto University and funded by Business Finland, with the objective of transferring cutting-edge antenna research into deployable, mission-ready defence and space technology. DIAMANT strengthens Finland’s defence and space ecosystem by advancing domestic expertise and supporting long-term technological sovereignty in critical RF systems. Following the project, the formation of a spinout company is expected in Q1/2025.

Name: TeraGlo
Description:
TeraGlo, an innovative Australian-based company, is at the forefront of sensor technology, specialising in real-time material identification across critical sectors such as mining, health and safety, and defence. . The company's groundbreaking portable sensors utilise novel fluorescent technology to deliver immediate and precise results, a necessity in high-stakes environments where time and accuracy are paramount.

In addition to real-time identification, TeraGlo's sensors compile detailed reports that are securely stored in the cloud, promoting efficient data management and accessibility. This cloud-based approach ensures that vital information is readily available to stakeholders, facilitating informed decision-making in dynamic circumstances.

Moreover, the integration of artificial intelligence into TeraGlo's sensors significantly enhances data processing capabilities. This advancement allows for rapid analysis and the swift generation of reports, further supporting operational efficiency. By bridging cutting-edge technology with practical applications, TeraGlo is poised to revolutionise material identification processes, contributing to safer and more effective outcomes in industries where precision is essential. In summary, TeraGlo's innovative solutions position it as a leader in the sensor technology landscape, equipping industries with the tools necessary for effective decision-making.

Primarily focussed on launching CubeSats into lower Earth orbit, students can participate by researching, designing, building, and operating these satellites and ground-station communications. There are also current research and design projects regarding biological payloads that are to be mounted inside these satellites to conduct biological experiments in space that have practical applications back here on Earth.

Students from all disciplines across the University are welcomed and encouraged to bring their unique set of skills to the team; including business management, finance, journalism and communications, artistic creativity, and so much more! Our teams work directly with industry leaders to provide solutions to real-world demands, and learn how to integrate the science of space research into everyday practicality.

Name: NASA Space Apps Challenge (Magdeburg)
Description:
The NASA International Space Apps Challenge (or Space Apps) is an international hackathon for coders, scientists, designers, storytellers, makers, builders, technologists, and others in cities around the world, where teams engage with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration’s (NASA) free and open data to address real-world problems on Earth and in space. The event embraces collaborative problem solving with the goal of producing open-source solutions to challenges we currently face on Earth and in space.

During the Space Apps Challenge in 2021, we saw more than 28,000 community members join as participants while 323 Local Leads, and 50 Ambassadors joined to support those participants. Space Apps is a NASA-led initiative organized in collaboration with Booz Allen Hamilton, Mindgrub and SecondMuse.

This year it will take place virtually and in person over 2 days for cities around the globe between October 1st and 2nd, 2022. Details on this year’s event can be found at http://spaceappschallenge.org

Name: THE KEPLER FILES
Description:
In the vast cosmic expanse, The Kepler Files emerges as an innovative project, beckoning you to embark on a transformative journey into the unknown and untangle the celestial enigma of exoplanet Kepler 438-B. Our narratives transcend storytelling at the intersection of art, science, and limitless imagination; they are portals to an ever-expanding universe of possibilities.
 
Kepler-438b Facts:
Size: Earth-sized exoplanet with a radius of 1.12 times Earth.
Temperature: Maintains an equilibrium temperature of 276 K (3 °C; 37 °F).
Orbit: Completes an orbit around its Red Dwarf star every 35 days
Light Intensity: Receives approximately 40 percent more light than Earth
Discovery: Identified by NASA's Kepler spacecraft
Location: Located 460 light-years away in the Lyra constellation
Future Exploration: Next-generation telescopes like TESS, CHEOPS, and the James Webb Space Telescope offer potential insights into Kepler-438b's mysteries.

Name: SPACE MENs
Description:
A research project in conjunction with the Canadian Reduced Gravity Experiment Design Challenge investigating the fluid dynamics and hygiene considerations of tampon removal in microgravity environments. Conducted by SPACE MENs ("Space Menstruators") research team.

Despite over 75 women astronauts participating in space missions, there remains a significant lack of research regarding menstrual health and hygiene in space. This study aims to investigate the fluid mechanics of blood removal and disposal in microgravity, addressing critical gaps in our understanding of menstrual product performance in space environments.

This research addresses a critical gap in space health research while contributing to Canada's space sector by:

- Advancing gender-inclusive research in space science
- Supporting long-duration space mission planning
- Developing innovative solutions for astronaut health and safety

https://linktr.ee/spacemenstruators

Description: Southern Cross Outreach Observatory Project (SCOOP) is Australia's first mobile astronomical observatory. While originally designed for public outreach astronomy in remote Australian communities, we aim to extrapolate this concept to create a synchronised network of similar observatories which can gather high precision data of space debris and help mitigate the space debris problem too

Name: Orbital Sentry
Description:
Orbital Sentry replaces static satellite snapshots with a live data stream for an always-changing planet. Instead of periodic images, our inaugural system, Fire Sentry, will deliver continuous, real-time wildfire intelligence. Minute-by-minute detection of new ignitions and evolving flame fronts flowing directly into the decision systems insurers, utilities, and governments rely on.

By operating from GEO, Orbital Sentry will transform Earth observation from a reporting tool into operational data infrastructure that matches the pace of planetary change. Orbital Sentry establishes the first commercial foundation layer for real-time environmental awareness at continental scale.

Based in New Zealand, we strive to advance the fields of drone and sensor technology along with in-space research capability while nurturing the next generation of STEM leaders through inspiring educational programs. 

We are dedicated to joining the pursuit of space and carving out a prominent place for ourselves in the high-tech industry, creating a culture of excellence and collaboration in New Zealand, and making a meaningful impact on both our local and global communities.

Name: The New Frontier
Description:
The New Frontier explores how space and advanced technologies are reshaping the global economy. What began as a focus on launches, regulation and satellite markets has evolved into a broader examination of the emerging industries defining the next decade, from AI and digital infrastructure to robotics, biotech, quantum computing and dual-use defence technologies.

At its core, the publication tracks the business of space: how capital flows, sovereign capability, industry strategy and policy reform are accelerating new missions and unlocking new markets. But it also looks beyond orbit, analysing how converging technologies are transforming sectors on Earth and in space, influencing investment decisions, reshaping supply chains and creating new competitive dynamics.

Each edition distils the signals, trends and strategic shifts that matter, whether it’s a breakthrough in autonomy on-orbit, a major capital raise, a regulatory update, or a move by governments and industry to secure leadership in critical technologies. The goal is to provide practical insight for readers who want to stay ahead of the rapid changes across space, defence and the broader future economy.

Name: Space Exe
Description:
Space Exe is the space and astronomy society at the University Exeter. 

We were founded in 2015 to take over the running of the XRT-C, an award-winning currently-dormant student-run radio telescope project in Caradon. As such, we've been dedicated to working on exciting practical projects, such as model rockets with chemical motors (we even came runners-up in the 2020 UKSEDS National Rocketry Championship)!

We are also incredibly active in our outreach, both within the university and the wider community. There are opportunities for members to join outreach events, like our annual attendance at the Sidmouth Science Festival, which are a great way to gain valuable experience in science communication and share your enthusiasm for space with others. The annual Space Exe Conference is our most popular event, featuring presentations of leading research, science communication, and more. It is open to all members to the public, and we hope to see you at the next edition!

In addition, we run a number of casual social events throughout the year, such as pub trips, dinners, as well as trivia and space-themed movie nights, so that members are able relax away from their studies.Finally, we host regular stargazing nights throughout the term. Our society telescope, S.T.E.V.I.E., is an 8-inch Dobsonian Sky-Watcher that provides fantastic views of the night sky to our members during term time.

For up to date information on what we do, follow us on Instagram @spaceexesoc.
For any queries, please email spaceexe@groups.exeterguild.com.

In 2014, this company entered the field of remote sensing by presenting a scientific paper on the modeling of changes in the coastline of Lake Urmia and in 2019 by presenting a paper to Hamburg University on the topic of reducing Tehran's air pollution using satellite images. In April 2018, this company entered the remote sensing executive work of the country by providing detailed reports of flood zones the IRAN. Name: OU-HVI-SPE-Labs
Description:
The HyperVelocity Impact (HVI) and Space & Planetary Environment (SPE) Laboratories has a wide variety of hypervelocity and space environment simulation facilities available for use by internal and external researchers and commercial companies.  
The All-Axis Two stage Light Gas Gun can fire projectiles up to 4.5 mm in diameter up to 6 km/s at a range of impact angles (0-90 degrees).
The Van der Graaff Dust Accelerator (currently being recommissioned) accelerates ~5 µm iron dust particles up to 80 km/s.
"George" The Large Dirty Mars Chamber is a 1.8 x 0.9 m cylindrical chamber capable of pressures down to ~1 mbar and temperatures between -180 and +20 degrees Celsius. It is dedicated to 'dirty' experiments, perhaps simulating meso-scale surface processes on Mars with fluids and regoliths.
"Edmund" The Clean Cylindrical Chamber is a 1.2 x 0.7 m cylindrical chamber capable of pressures down to 10^-5 mbar and temperatures between -180 and +110 degrees Celsius. This is a clean chamber typically used for
testing space hardware and astrobiological experiments.
"Bob" The Small Desktop Chamber is an integrated cryogenic chamber 14 cm diameter and 15 cm tall capable of pressures down to 10^-5 mbar and temperatures between -180 and +20 degrees Celsius. It has lots of viewports suitable for UV studies. 
"Queenie" The Large Clean Bell-Jar Chamber is 0.8 m tall and 0.7 m diameter capable of pressures down to 10^-5 mbar and temperatures between -100 and +200 degrees Celsius. This is a clean chamber with a large headspace typically for high vacuum extreme environment and astrobiological experiments for icy moons and airless bodies like Moon, Mercury and Bennu.
"Baldrick" The Large Clean Circular Chamber is 60 cm diameter and 20 cm tall and is capable of pressures down to 10^-5 mbar and temperatures between -100 and +20 degrees Celsius. It has large spring-loaded lid and multiple large viewports and feedthrough ports. It is designed for Planetary Protection and astrobiology studies.

Name: Green SWaP
Description:
Green SWaP – Green Solar-to-Propellant Water Propulsion 

Green SWaP is a groundbreaking Horizon Europe project revolutionizing space propulsion by harnessing solar energy to convert water into eco-friendly propellants: hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) and hydrogen (H₂). Inspired by photosynthesis, we are pioneering sustainable in-space fuel production to enable low-cost, self-sufficient, and safer space missions. 

Our Mission 

(1) Develop an innovative bimodal propulsion system using green propellants 
(2) Leverage solar energy for in-space production of H₂O₂ and H₂ 
(3) Design novel in-space storage solutions (inflatable hydrogen tanks) 
(4) Advance solar thermal and chemical propulsion technologies 
(5) Eliminate toxic and hazardous propellants for a greener space future 

Follow us for project updates, research breakthroughs, and collaboration opportunities. 

Sign up for our newsletter to stay informed.

Name: Laboratoire de Géomatique, de Recherche Appliquée et de Conseil (LAGRAC)
Description:
Le Laboratoire de Géomatique, de Recherche Appliquée et de Conseil (LAGRAC) est une institution spécialisée dans l’étude des dynamiques spatiales et environnementales en Afrique Centrale. Créé en 1994 et rattaché au Département des Sciences Géographiques, Environnementales et Marines de l’Université Omar Bongo, le LAGRAC s’est imposé comme un pôle d’expertise au niveau national, capable de transformer les données géospatiales en solutions concrètes pour le territoire.
Le LAGRAC repose sur une communauté scientifique dynamique et intergénérationnelle, avec une équipe solide d’une vingtaine d’Enseignants-Chercheurs et Chercheurs. En plus de ce personnel confirmé, le LAGRAC compte dans ses rangs, une dizaine de doctorants et plus de 30 membres juniors de niveau Master qui forment le vivier de l’innovation et de la relève.
Depuis sa création, le LAGRAC s’engage pour la production du savoir pertinent et accessible avec plus de 128 publications à son actif. Ce qui démontre la contribution active du Laboratoire au rayonnement scientifique de l’Université Omar Bongo et de l’école africaine. En plus des publications scientifiques, le LAGRAC a mené à bien l’encadrement de 5 thèses de doctorat soutenues, formant des experts clés pour le pays et la région.
Le LAGRAC étant le lieu où la science spatiale rencontre la décision territoriale, ses activités sont orientées vers la réponse aux défis majeurs de la planète et du continent africain, en mettant la géomatique au cœur de l’action. Pour cela, ses travaux s’articulent autour de trois principaux axes stratégiques de recherche et d’application : Environnement et Géomatique, la gestion des ressources naturelles et biodiversité, ainsi que la Gestion des risques et aménagement du territoire. De par son expertise, le LAGRAC a tissé un réseau solide de 25 collaborateurs nationaux et internationaux, attestant de ses capacités à mobiliser des synergies pour des projets d’envergure et à fournir des services de conseil de haute qualité.

Name: T-FORS
Description:
Travelling Ionospheric Disturbances Forecasting System (T-FORS)

Travelling Ionospheric Disturbances (TIDs) constitute a specific type of space weather disturbance affecting the performance of critical space and ground infrastructure by disrupting operations and communications in multiple sectors. T-FORS aims at providing new models able to interpret a broad range of observations of the solar corona, the interplanetary medium, the magnetosphere, the ionosphere and the atmosphere, and to issue forecasts and warnings for TIDs several hours ahead. Machine Learning techniques are used to train the models based on existing databases developed in the frames of past Horizon 2020 projects, to estimate the occurrence probability of medium scale TIDs and to forecast the occurrence and propagation of large scale TIDs. Prototype services are developed based on specifications from the users' community and following harmonized standards and quality control similar to the best practices of meteorological services. On ground demonstration tests are organised, by aerospace and civil protection agencies, to validate the performance of the T-FORS prototype services. A comprehensive architectural concept is proposed, including the densification of ground instrument networks, and new space missions, and possible future adjustments in order to develop a real-time operational service fully compliant and complementary to the ESA Space Weather services.
https://t-fors.eu/

Name: Scientific Imagination
Description:
While imagination hovers uncomfortably between what is possible and impossible, it is argued that whatever imagination might or might not reveal about the metaphysical structures of the real world, it provides experimental space to question the established structures of a conceptual framework. As Timothy Williamson put it in ‘Imagination as Knowledge’, ‘at least metaphorically, one might regard imagination as a form of attention to possibilities.’

Scientific Imagination is Sabine Winters. In this philosophical quest I attempt to address this very question: How is the function of imagination in science to be understood? For example, it is my goal to outline what imagination does when it is used as a guide to possibility in thought experiments and science fiction and to sketch a picture of the problems that may be encountered. By sharing podcasts and articles and organizing events I hope to share some of my enthusiasm for this particular field of philosophy.

Name: International Symposium on Rarefied Gas Dynamics
Description: The International Symposium on Rarefied Gas Dynamics is a biennial academic conference. The symposia are a forum for the presentation of recent advances in the field of rarefied gas dynamics. Research presented encompasses applications of space, materials, and propulsion, as well as the basic physics of molecular interactions, gas surface interactions, kinetic theory, astronomical observations, gas transport, multi-phase flows, combustion, non-equilibrium hypersonic gas dynamics, and plasma processing. The first symposium was held in 1958 in Nice, France. Since that time, the symposia have been organized in various countries in North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia.

Name: Aspect Aerospace
Description:
At Aspect Aerospace, we are a dedicated team of satellite designers and builders with flight heritage. Rather than just shrinking a CubeSat into a smaller CubeSat, we've decided to rewrite the script. Leveraging the single-board satellite architecture, we're bringing to market a "constellation in-a-box" -- a whole constellation of 20-100 satellites, deployed from a single CubeSat, for less than the cost of a single CubeSat. Each satellite would cost hundreds of dollars, rather than traditional hundreds of thousands or millions of dollars. From a single launch, we can do:

- Distributed optics, with each satellite hosting a camera, allowing us to do extremely fast revisits, image stacking, 3d stereo optics, etc. While each individual camera is worse than a larger, more traditional camera, by leveraging 20-100 of them, it starts to outperform individual earth observation satellites.

- Deployable mesh networks: a large / government-owned and operated satellite could host a cannister of these satellites, ready to go, in the event of adversarial attack or disrupted radio communications -- and rapidly deploy a mesh radio network on-demand.

- Aggregate data collection: dispense a constellation into a space weather storm, patch of high-density neutral particles, etc. to capture data with extreme accuracy.

- Batch testing in space: the bread-and-butter of the company is rapid prototyping and batch testing in space. We can host a component to test, deploy them in-situ across 100 satellites at a lower price point than on-ground testing, and generate 100 hours of space qualification time per real-world hour. You could get 73,000 hours of space qual time in one month, for cheaper than on-ground testing, with a faster turnaround time as these things are mass produced, ready to go.

Name: Obuda University - Space Lab
Description:
Established in 2024, the Space Lab at Óbuda University is dedicated to achieving research excellence across both applied and theoretical domains of space science and technology. By collaborating closely with faculties, doctoral schools, industry partners, technology parks, and other institutional departments, the Lab plays a pivotal role in realizing the university's strategic goals in space research, science policy, and innovation.

Beyond its core research activities, the Space Lab actively coordinates and promotes scientific excellence and student advancement within the university's space-focused programs. It identifies, secures, and manages funding opportunities to support the endeavors of academic researchers, educators, and research centers specializing in space-related studies.

Currently, Óbuda University maintains established space research and development facilities in Budapest and Székesfehérvár, and is continually expanding capabilities through new facilities within its industrial and technology parks.

The Space Lab’s mission is to inspire and train the next generation of space scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and professionals while contributing to the advancement of space exploration and technological innovation. It aims to build a dynamic, interdisciplinary community committed to space sciences, engineering, management, and economics.

Its key activities encompass the implementation of research projects, tender and innovation management, international partnerships, industrial collaborations, and education initiatives. Research areas include space cybersecurity, space robotics, control theory, remote surgery, Earth observation, and space sustainability – analyzing trends, crafting recommendations, and advising stakeholders.
The Space Lab is committed to fostering international presence, interdisciplinarity, and demonstrable scientific, economic, and societal impact in all its activities.

Name: STEP - EU Project
Description:

Focused on achieving European non-dependence, the project encompasses the development of advanced eSWIR detectors, innovative Read-Out Integrated Circuit (ROIC) lines, and comprehensive testing to ensure space environment compatibility.

Through collaboration among leading European research institutes and industries, STEP is set to enhance Earth observation capabilities and reinforce Europe's autonomy in critical space technologies.

Name: Project FENRIR
Description:
FENRIR (Free fall Experiment uNit for Reduced gravity Investigation and Research) is a student-led space project from Luleå University of Technology (LTU) in Kiruna, Sweden. The project aims to create an easy-to-use platform for ejecting scientific payloads from sounding rockets. The Free Fall Unit (FFU) will carry two payloads: an attitude stabilisation system (ATSS) and an experiment in the field of phase change materials (PCM).

FENRIR is a part of the REXUS/BEXUS programme and is set to fly on the RX34 sounding rocket in March 2025. REXUS/BEXUS is a student experiment programme financed by the Swedish National Space Agency and the German Aerospace Centre with support from the European Space Agency - ESA, the Swedish Space Corporation - SSC, ZARM and more.

Name: SkyQuest Innovations
Description:
SkyQuest Innovations is at the forefront of next-generation aerospace research—where quantum mechanics, astrophysics, and space-time dynamics converge to create radically new ways of navigating the cosmos.

We specialize in the R&D of advanced aeronautical systems, with a focus on developing intelligent spacecraft control mechanisms capable of interacting with and traversing the fabric of space-time. Our technologies aim to move beyond conventional propulsion by leveraging:
	•	Quantum entanglement for real-time communication and distributed control.
	•	Microtube implosion systems that generate localized spacetime distortions for propulsion.
	•	Anti-gravity engineering that manipulates quantum gravitational effects to reduce or neutralize mass-based constraints.

By fusing quantum engineering with spacecraft control dynamics, we are working toward a future where vehicles can bend space rather than simply travel through it. Our systems are being designed to autonomously navigate gravitational wells, relativistic environments, and interstellar pathways, unlocking scalable solutions for long-range space missions.

Our mission is to challenge physical limits through scientific precision—transforming today’s science fiction into tomorrow’s aerospace standard. Whether it’s through space-time navigation, quantum control systems, or field-based propulsion, SkyQuest Innovations is building the framework for a quantum-enabled spacefaring future.

We’re not just building vehicles—we’re reshaping humanity’s relationship with space.

SEDS-USA hosts a yearly conference, SpaceVision, for students to attend a space-centric symposium and job fair. As we continue our outreach and fundraising efforts, we need new members to increase our already amazing talent pool.

With our inclusive atmosphere we welcome students from all majors to join our activities. Modular, robotics, space resource mining, systems

We engineer solutions to enable the inevitable expansion of humanity beyond Earth. But Ararat Robotics is more than a company, it's a cause, the driving force at the forefront of human advancement in space.

Name: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society (GRSS) Argentina
Description: Capítulo argentino de la Sociedad de Geociencias y Sensado Remoto (GRSS) de IEEE, comunidad internacional e interdisciplinaria de investigadores y profesionales dentro de las áreas de geociencias, geomática, ingeniería y sensado remoto.

Name: ModelFLOWs
Description: ModelFLOWs is a research group whose main promoter and tutor is Soledad Le Clainche and was formed at the School of Aeronautics Engineering at Universidad Politécnica de Madrid (UPM). This team uses different data-driven methods, i.e. reduced order models (ROMs) or neural networks (NN), to generate, study and predict databases related to complex flows (turbulent, reactive, etc.).

Name: Missouri S&T Satellite Research Team
Description: As a team of driven Missouri S&T students, we are passionate about designing, building, and launching satellites that push the boundaries of our knowledge of spacecraft design  . From researching new missions to testing intricate mechanics, we are constantly working to make our dreams a reality! Follow along as we work on three satellite missions: M^3, MR & MRS, and MC-1. #solvingfortomorrow #minernation #3...2...1...M-SAT

Name: UniTrento DN SST Dottorato Nazionale in Space Science and Technology
Description:
La pagina LinkedIn del dottorato nazionale in Space Science and Technology; una piattaforma per avviare la rappresentanza sociale e professionale dei dottorandi e facilitare l'intercomunicazione e lo scambio di esperienze. 
--
The LinkedIn page of the National PhD in Space Science and Technology; a platform to initiate the social and professional representation of the doctoral students and facilitate intercommunication and exchange of experience.

Name: Radio Science and Planetary Exploration Laboratory
UMD Space Systems Laboratory
Description: The Space Systems Laboratory (SSL) is part of the Aerospace Engineering Department and A. James Clark School of Engineering at the University of Maryland in College Park, Maryland. A leader in the area of astronautics, the SSL is centered around the Neutral Buoyancy Research Facility, a 50-foot diameter, 25-foot deep water tank that is used to simulate the microgravity environment of space. The only such facility in the world housed at a university, the SSL's neutral buoyancy tank is able to be used for undergraduate and graduate research alike. Research at the SSL emphasizes space robotics, human factors, applications of artificial intelligence and the underlying fundamentals of space simulation.

Name: NASA Space Apps Alexandria
Description:
NASA Space Apps Alexandria is part of the largest global hackathon in the world NASA’s Space Apps Challenge. Hosted in Alexandria, Egypt, our local chapter brings together coders, designers, scientists, storytellers, and space enthusiasts to collaborate on real-world challenges using NASA's open data.

We are not only globally accredited by NASA but also proudly recognized locally by the Egyptian Space Agency. Our mission is to inspire innovation, creativity, and problem-solving by empowering youth, students, and professionals to develop solutions that impact Earth and beyond.

Name: Agnia Aerospace Ltd
Description: Agnia Aerospace is a small, research-based initiative focused on studying electric propulsion for satellites and spacecraft. Currently, we are exploring electrospray propulsion systems and are working on developing a pre-prototype, aiming to make small satellites more efficient, reliable, and sustainable. Our mission is to advance the science of electric propulsion and contribute to a cleaner, safer orbital environment.

Name: Terra Luna Resources Inc. (TLR)
Description:
TLR is a Canadian space mining product development company. We are focusing on the development of lunar water purification and survey technology. Our technologies will not only propel off-world exploration but also aim to mitigate the daily challenges our remote and northern communities face.

Focus on lunar mining processing and survey equipment in a simple & low-cost manner. Overcoming the tyranny of the rocket equation for future lunar pioneers.

Info@terraluna.ca

Name: Phoenix Technology Space
Description:
Somos uma SpaceTech brasileira, especializada em engenharia e design aeroespacial, projetos de inovação e marketing espacial com tecnologias disruptivas.
Nossas missões espaciais são reais com foco em promover experimentos no espaço, trazendo novas possibilidades de P&D abrindo oportunidades, democratizando a forma como podemos explorar novas economias, inspirar pessoas e viabilizar projetos de pesquisa de forma acessível através da nossa tecnologia.
Somos movidos por um sonho coletivo de adultos, adolescentes e crianças que era o de alcançar e explorar o espaço, algo que sempre despertou a curiosidade e fascínio na maioria das pessoas.
Esse sonho se tornou um propósito e é um privilégio poder compartilhar nossa paixão e conhecimento com pessoas que também compartilham da nossa paixão e valores. 
Somos privilegiados em poder retribuir de alguma forma para sociedade tudo aquilo que nos motiva.
Atualmente nosso centro de pesquisa tem como objetivo reduzir os custos de transporte espacial, explorar novas formas de eficiência energética, viabilizar tecnologias inovadoras que contribuam para o meio ambiente e análise de dados coletados para fomentar projetos acadêmicos.
Nossos Engenheiros são especialistas no desenvolvimento de veículos suborbitais remotos, cápsulas de carga remotas, espaçonaves não tripuladas e satélites de comunicação. Para suportar todos esses estudos e investimentos, a Phoenix Technology possui todas as certificações e autorizações junto aos órgãos regulamentadores que certificam todos os nossos projetos.
Após anos de pesquisas, em 2020 recebemos através de um fundo nosso primeiro grande investimento para iniciarmos nossos lançamentos, construções de produtos e protótipos aeroespaciais, aprimorando o nosso conhecimento e expandindo nossos horizontes a cada lançamento realizado.
O propósito é justamente demonstrar a capacidade de transformar um sonho em algo tangível, pois a Phoenix planeja alcançar resultados cada vez mais robustos.

Name: Geometric Space Corporation
Description:
Geometric Space is dedicated to multi-planetary colonization by facilitating the expansion of the private space industry through the Geometric Space Rideshare Program and commercializing key space technologies for Moon and Mars settlements.

Geometric Space offers rideshare services to Low Earth and Lunar orbit aboard SpaceX Falcon 9 launches. With support from our partners, we provide end-to-end services including payload development, integration, ground station, mission management and operational support. 

Geometric-1 Rideshare Mission successfully launched to Low Earth Orbit January 2023. DOGE-1 Mission to Lunar Orbit in development and will launch aboard IM-1. Geometric-2 Rideshare Mission to Low Earth Orbit TBD.

Name: Department of Space (DoS)
Description:
The Department of Space (DOS) has the primary objective of promoting development and application of space science and technology to assist in all-round development of the nation. Towards this, DOS has evolved the following programmes :

• Launch Vehicle programme having indigenous capability for launching spacecrafts.

• INSAT Programme for telecommunications, broadcasting, meteorology, development of education etc.

• Remote Sensing Programme for application of satellite imagery for various developmental purposes.

• Research and Development in Space Sciences and Technology for serving the end of applying them for national development.

Name: HiiLSE Drone
Description: As a Malaysia startup HiiLSE Drone rapidly emerging as the pioneer & market leader in the Drone as a Solutions (DaaS) space to increase efficiency & reduce the cost of production in organizations across the focusing South East Asia and overall globally.
Location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Name: Argo
Description:
Argo is a pioneering robotics and space systems non-profit organization developing cutting-edge automation, AI-driven technologies, and planetary exploration systems. Through engineering, research, and hands-on innovation, we provide students and young professionals with the opportunity to design, build, and deploy advanced robotic solutions.

By collaborating with academia, industry, and research institutions, we bridge the gap between education and real-world technological development. With our projects we partake in competitions on the international stage, contribute to Mars analogue missions, and host educational events domestically and internationally.

At Argo, we are shaping the next generation of automation, robotics, and space exploration, ensuring that talent meets opportunity in a rapidly evolving world.

Name: Prism Neuro
Description:
Prism Neuro develops advanced technology to measure and improve human movement control.
Our solutions provide objective sensorimotor insights that support:
Sport: reducing injury risk, informing return-to-play decisions, and identifying emerging talent.
Space: understanding and mitigating motor-control decline during exposure to microgravity.
Health: addressing movement-control deficits associated with ageing, injury, and disease.
Headquartered at the Australian Institute of Sport in Canberra, Prism Neuro works across elite performance, clinical care, and human spaceflight to help people move better, perform better, and stay healthier.

Name: Space Economic Forum LLC
Description:
About the Event: The Space Economic Forum (SEF) is an annual, premier global gathering for leaders in the projected $1.8 trillion Space economy. Inspired by the World Economic Forum, SEF will be held on September 23-25, 2026.

Mission: SEF’s core mission is to elevate and put the spotlight on major space stakeholders through keynote addresses and powerful panel participations to promote brand identity, accelerate innovation, attract investment, and establish a framework for the responsible development of the space economy. SEF will focus on strategic partnerships, market dynamics, investment, and policy, in addition to space and aerospace technology.

Audience: The forum will attract CEOs, government officials, investors, entrepreneurs, scientists, researchers, and thought leaders. Astronauts will also be a key presence.

Schedule: A cocktail reception on September 22nd, followed by three days
      of expo and forum sessions, and a VIP gala and industry award ceremony on the 26th.

Venue Selection: A Request for Information (RFI) will be launched in 
      September 2025 to select a host city and venue for over 2,000 attendees.

Sponsorship: A structured sponsorship program, from "Diamond Partner" 
      (>$2.5 million) to "Silver Partner" ($25,000), will provide strategic stakeholder interest.

Special Features: 
       - Nations with space ambitions can present their unique propositions to a global audience.                             
       - A special "Future Astronauts Space" zone will be featured to engage children and 
         families with spacecraft models, Astronaut Candidates, and actual Astronauts.

Program: The dynamic program includes keynotes, panel discussions, and breakout sessions. The SEF Expo will feature a "Lunar Village" and "Space Fashion Hub."

Logistics: Meticulous planning will cover international travel, hotel rates, and on-site services including security, medical support, and childcare.

Organizer: Titans Space Industries Inc

Name: TotalView
Description:
Based in Athens Greece and established in 2011, totalview is an innovative establishment focusing on very high resolution (VHR) satellite images and software developing. With over 25 years of experience, Totalview has developed a reputation in Greece, Cyprus and internationally for its unique services, delivery of high-quality products and pioneering solutions for each customer individually.
In addition, Totalview is the only company in Greece and one of a few in Europe capable to provide very high-resolution satellite images, as it is a partner with the leaders in this field. Also is one of the first companies in Greece to participate in the construction of multiple ground stations in several locations in Greece.

Name: Makercie RUG
Description:
We are a team of passionate students from the University of Groningen and the first ever Dutch team to participate and win in our debut year the Remote edition of the European Rover Challenge in 2023. The ERC is an annual international Martian robots competition which has been organized since 2014 in Poland, and the biggest robotics and space event in Europe! 

Currently, motivated by our win and with an even bigger motivation, we've embarked in a new adventure, our sights being set on participating at the On-Site formula of the ERC that will take place in Poland in September 2025. 

If you think this is as exciting as we do and want to be part of our team, support us or want to find out more, you can contact us at makercie@rug.nl.

Follow us on this journey.
With us, sky is no longer the limit!

Name: Costrum

Our AI speeds up the long, complex customer requirements analysis and sales process

So they can send offers before competitors, and close deals faster.

astraterra

With a mission to lead transformative change through innovation and science, we connect space to communities to empower lives and thrive to preserve our planet and what’s beyond.

Name: APAIR I Algerian President Award for Innovative Research
Description:
Le Prix du Président de la République pour le Chercheur Innovant (A-PAIR) est une distinction nationale visant à récompenser les chercheurs et étudiants dont les travaux de recherche et d’innovation se distinguent par leur originalité, leur impact scientifique et économique, ainsi que leur potentiel de valorisation industrielle.

Ce prix s'inscrit dans la vision du Président de la République pour faire de l’innovation et de la recherche scientifique des moteurs du développement de l’Algérie.

Chaque année, des chercheurs, enseignants-chercheurs et étudiants sont récompensés pour leurs contributions exceptionnelles dans des domaines stratégiques tels que :
 Sciences et Technologies
 Santé et Biotechnologies
 Environnement et Développement Durable
 Sécurité Alimentaire et Hydrique
 Sciences Humaines et Sociales
 Paix et Sécurité

 Notre mission
Encourager la recherche innovante, stimuler l’entrepreneuriat scientifique et valoriser les travaux ayant un impact direct sur le progrès économique et social du pays.

Name: STAR Lab PoliTO
Description: The Systems and Technologies for Aerospace Research (STAR) laboratory of the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Enginnering at Politecnico di Torino, led by Prof. Sabrina Corpino, is dedicated to the innovation of aerospace systems and technologies. The main mission focuses on improving sustainability and affordability for future space missions and systems, primarily through research in Systems Engineering, miniaturized space technologies, and space systems design. The STAR team collaborates internationally with universities, research centers, space agencies, and major aerospace companies.

Name: inspireFly at Virginia Tech
Description:
Our Mission:
inspireFly is a Virginia Tech undergraduate design team that designs and develops novel CubeSats which present a unique style of access to space for the general public, while demonstrating new technologies for future satellite applications.

Our Vision:
We will present diverse, quickly-accessible, and affordable outer space opportunities to all individuals through CubeSats and other small satellites while expanding capabilities in the space environment through our commitment to inspire, pioneer, and diversify.

Our Values:
Inspiration | Diversity | Effectiveness | Ambition | Sincerity

Name: INTERE Technologies
Description: We at Intere Technologies are working towards developing advanced systems for thermal regulation in demanding use cases

Name: EarthCast Technologies
Description:
EarthCast Technologies, a commercial terrestrial and space weather technology company, offers real-time global Earth system monitoring and prediction - providing mission critical environmental intelligence products, systems, and services to worldwide clients who require high-end, innovative solutions for difficult environmental challenges. 

EarthCast combines global satellite observations with cutting edge prediction technology using highly specialized super computers, to produce high resolution monitoring and prediction data for the entire Earth system.

The end result is that EarthCast can produce extremely high resolution predictions for any place on the globe - in real time. This information, specifically designed for decision making, provides a truly global-to-local capability allowing you to prepare for what is coming, instead of just responding to it.

Environmental Modeling Data Solutions:
• Global & Hyperlocal Predictions
• Lower Atmosphere
• Upper Atmosphere & Space
• Hydrology, Land & Sea
• Defense & Government
• Aviation & Aerospace
• Space Weather
• Ground Transportation
• Emergency Management
• Maritime
• Environmental Change
• Agriculture
• Energy

Name: Egress Space
Description:
Egress Space was founded to address the challenge of frequent and reliable down mass return from space and is developing technology and solutions spanning launch preparation, mission execution, data collection, and the safe and efficient return of payloads.

Egress Space is developing end-to-end solutions for microgravity research and manufacturing which includes parabolic flight for research and testing, precision return of sounding rocket payloads, on-demand microgravity sample return and air launch capability.

Drawing on its substantial UAS experience, Egress Space is also developing capabilities in space-based sustainment and tactical precision logistics as well as ground, air and space electronic warfare solutions.

Finally, with an international fleet of three L-39 Albatros training jet aircraft, Egress Space is looking into the applications of fast jets for aerial surveillance and search and rescue, airborne imaging, chase photography, flight testing and crew and astronaut training.

Name: PVSPACE
Description:
The rapid progress in modern space economy is entrepreneurial and global accessible with increasingly expanding with private players across a variety of sub-sectors and markets via an increasingly growth in funding and investing. A major step forward in the space technologies is to develop affordable power systems applicable in the harsh space environment. Indeed, owing to the source of power, photovoltaics (PVs) always considered as a key component. In agreement with the fast-growing space economy, emerging photovoltaic systems e.g., perovskite solar cells and multi-junction thin film PVs with some interesting characteristics e.g., high efficiency in ultra-low-cost modules, low energy payback time, large scale solution-process manufacturing, intrinsic stability against high energy particles and rays, light-weight and high specific power, have been illustrated a great potential for compete with conventional PV systems in the space applications. 
The scope of the conference is to provide an opportunity for experts in variety PV sectors and technologies to have a fresh update on the advancement, state-of-the-art and future roadmap of new generation PV in space applications. From space applications we consider not only those which are available e.g., satellite powering and traditional space missions but also new space developments such as extraterrestrial bases, deployable flexible PV arrays, inflatable solar cells, solar power stations, etc. The conference would also focus on space relevant tests of new PV technologies as well as the first experimental demonstration on the space environment.

Name: NaviGate
Description: NaviGate is an innovative deep tech startup revolutionizing spacecraft autonomous navigation with its plug-and-play onboard system. Our technology enables autonomous orbit determination and guidance of satellites and constellations using a large variety of measurements, including GNSS data, radiometric observables, and more. NaviGate's onboard system is designed to integrate seamlessly with satellite hardware, allowing for autonomous satellite navigation without ground inputs.

Name: Space Times
Description: Our thesis is to provide an evolving roadmap of the state of the space industry from a technology and business perspective. The space industry is as vast and evolving as the environment it occupies, and we are here to help you navigate it! Every week we talk about advances in space technology and business, and we want to share our opinions and insights with you. Paul Mayer and Harrison Lambert met a decade ago working as aerospace engineers at Maxar. Since then, Harrison has spent that time working on space systems for a variety of applications, and Paul has become a venture capitalist helping grow nascent space companies. Now we want to share the next decades with you, discussing all the exciting times in space!

Name: Club de Investigación Universitario de Desarrollo en Sistemas Espaciales
Description:
Somos el equipo oficial de proyectos aeroespaciales de la Universidad Autónoma de Baja California, con presencia en Ensenada, Tijuana y Mexicali. 

Hemos participado en ENMICE (2021), Spaceport America Cup (2022-2024), y próximamente en University Rover Challenge 2025.

Le apostamos al desarrollo de proyectos diversos y multidisciplinarios. Somos referentes en México y América Latina en proyectos de cohetería y estamos comprometidos con la formación de profesionistas de excelencia.

Name: Western Aerospace Launch Initiative (WALI)
Description:
The Western Aerospace Launch Initiative (WALI) is a student organization at Western Michigan University (WMU).  Established in 2014 by a small group of undergraduate students interested in learning about spacecraft engineering, WALI has experienced immense growth in its lifetime.  In 2016, WALI was accepted into the University Nanosatellite Program’s (UNP) 9th iteration and participated in the program’s “Phase A” for the following two years, developing the Plasma Spectroscopy (P-Spec) mission.  This experience provided the organization with the tools necessary to establish itself at WMU and learn the fundamentals of CubeSat engineering.



In the fall of 2018, WALI was accepted into the UNP’s 10th iteration with a redefined mission named Optical Plasma Spectroscopy CubeSat (OPS-Cube).  WALI initiated a key partnership with Busek Co. and expanded its scope to explore both electric propulsion (EP) system diagnostics and CubeSat orbital maneuvers.  Through this iteration of the UNP, the organization further matured and developed into a more experienced and trailblazing team at WMU.



Again, in fall of 2021, UNP’s 11th iteration accepted WALI’s current mission called Performance of Electrospray Propulsion on Ground and in Space (PEP-GS). Electric propulsion diagnostics continue to be a main component of WALI’s mission, now with a focus on testing facility effects.



The organization is led by Principal Investigator Dr. Kristina Lemmer and assisted by Co-Principal Investigator Dr. Pablo Gomez. WALI has maintained itself on a voluntary participation basis and is currently experiencing unprecedented growth and retention rates.

Name: Impossible Research
Description:
Why "Impossible Research"? The innovation process is chaotic (in a mathematical sense) meaning although specific innovation paths (like industry emergence) are unpredictable, the underlying patterns are not. This means questions about industry emergence, although ultimately unanswerable, can be studied and better understood, like trying to reach an unreachable star. 

"To dream the impossible dream, 
To fight the unbeatable foe, 
To bear with unbearable sorrow and 
to run where the brave dare not go.
To right the unrightable wrong and 
to love pure and chaste from afar.
To try when your arms are too weary, 
To reach the unreachable star."

And this is the "quest" of Impossible Research, "to follow that star, no matter how hopeless, no matter how far," to find unresolved answers to unanswerable questions.

Name: Bio-Robotic Ecosystems Lab
Description:
The Bio-Robotic Ecosystems Lab aims at developing bio-artificial systems emerging from the deep integration of technology elements into life form communities and populations, sharing their abilities collectively in  organism-robot augmented (eco)systems.

Artificial agents interacting with animals and other organisms are used to monitor, manage, repair, or enhance functionality of such biological entities. Conversely, animal intelligence can inform technological systems, enhancing their performance in real-world challenging scenarios. We focus on many biological models ranging from bacteria to vertebrates, with a particular interest in arthropods.
Major goals of such systems are biodiversity  preservation, sustainable environmental  management, life support in extreme and extraterrestrial conditions (e.g. uncontrolled anthropized areas, fast global change, space environments, etc.).

Research is performed by an interdisciplinary workgroup with expertise in biorobotics, automation, mechanical engineering, biology, ethology, ecology, zoology, biotechnology, physics, mechatronics, computer science. The applications are many, ranging from fundamental biological investigation to bioinspired engineering and biohybrid emerging technologies.

Name: Liberty Rocketry
Description: Liberty Rocketry's mission is to train world-class engineers that are also rich with the love of Christ. The members of our team are willing to take valuable hours out of their week, despite their not-so-easy engineering courses, to innovate, design, and build high-power rockets for the purpose of accomplishing that mission. We compete in the world's largest Intercollegiate Rocket Engineering Competition (IREC) held by ESRA. In 2024, Liberty Rocketry placed 9th out of 122 teams from around the globe, cementing our position among the best rocketry teams in the world. At Liberty Rocketry, we combine technical excellence with unwavering faith to inspire innovation, teamwork, and a passion for engineering.

Name: TELNET Space
Description: TELNET Space is an engineering company that provides space systems services and solutions.

Name: SR Space
Description:
SR Space is a private Russian space company created in July 2020 by entrepreneur Oleg Mansurov.

Our company works in four directions:

- At SR Rockets, we create a suborbital and orbital launch rockets family of ultralight and light classes (Nebo suborbital rocket, Cosmos two-stage ultralight rocket, and Stalker two-stage light rocket).

- At SR Satellites, we develop small spacecraft and space systems. The team is working on the creation of four main satellite groupings: SR OKO — optical and infrared sensing complex, SR SAR — radar sensing complex, SR CMS — climate monitoring system, and SR NET — narrow-band and broadband satellite communications complex.

- At SR Data, we are engaged in the storage, processing, and analysis of remote sensing images of the Earth using artificial intelligence. SR Data has recently become a resident of the Innovative scientific and technological center Sirius.

- At SR Drones, we conduct research and development of unmanned aerial vehicles, specialized software and means for UAVs' interaction, detection and suppression.

In December 2020 we conducted the first firing tests of the engine; in April 2021 — launched a suborbital rocket prototype and completed the first flight tests of on-the-board systems; in November 2021 — presented a demonstration model of our climate satellite at the COP26 in Glasgow; in December 2021 — successfully launched the Nebo 25; in April 2022 — presented the orbital rocket Stalker, the launch of which is expected in 2024-2025.

Name: Interchange Laboratories, Inc.
Description:
The purpose of Interchange Laboratories is to unleash the power of the Mind.

Interchange Laboratories is a corporation of like-minded individuals whose skill sets have joined to develop advanced mind-interface technologies that expand our mental influence and abilities far beyond any current technology.

Name: uOttawa Space Law Society
Description: The University of Ottawa Space Law Society (founded in 2023 within the Faculty of Law) unites and serves students, academia, industry, government, and communities who share a mutual interest in the governance of outer space activities. The Society has three main aims: contribute to the field of space law and policy, both domestically and internationally; create and promote practical opportunities for students with an interest in space law; serve and engage with community, industry, and government. The Space Law Society is committed to the highest standard of ethics and excellence, motivated by humankind’s pursuit of the stars.

Name: Millennium Nucleus on Young Exoplanets and their Moons
Description:
The Millennium Nucleus on Young Exoplanets and their Moons (YEMS) is an astronomy-informatics multidisciplinary effort funded by Iniciativa Científica Milenio and hosted by four universities in Chile. YEMS scientific objectives involve detecting and characterizing young exoplanets and exomoons to answer the long-standing question of their formation.

The mission of the Millennium Nucleus on Young Exoplanets and their Moons (YEMS) is to promote research through interdisciplinary connections between astrophysics and informatics engineering. The Center will create bridges to explore the multidisciplinary nature of these disciplines.

Name: Madhava Space Research Organization (MSRO)
Description:
Madhava Space Research Organisation (MSRO) is an innovative space technology research and development company dedicated to pushing the boundaries of space exploration and aerospace technologies. Founded by Ajay Kumar Saini, MSRO envisions a future where groundbreaking technologies and visionary ideas redefine humanity’s understanding of space and our place in the universe.

Our Vision
At MSRO, we believe that space exploration is not just about reaching new frontiers but about creating transformative technologies that change the way we live and work. Our goal is to lead the development of advanced aerospace systems and propulsion technologies, inspired by both scientific discovery and imaginative concepts, to contribute significantly to global space endeavors.

Description:

Name: RocketRia

Name: S[&]T Italy
Description:
At S[&]T, we are committed to creating a safer life on earth by transforming space data into valuable and actionable insights. We manage data throughout the entire process, from instrument to application. We apply our expertise within our software frameworks, information products and consultancy.

Our society relies on space and scientific instruments for crucial services we all use in our daily lives like climate change monitoring, navigation, defence and national security. These instruments generate a vast amount of complex data, and our mission is to unlock its value.

With over two decades of expertise in instrument calibration, data quality monitoring tools, real-time data processing frameworks and the development of application-specific algorithms, we empower organisations, governments and industries to strengthen their information position and enhance decision-making. We specialize in the following domains: Space & Science, Defence & Security, Environment & Sustainability, and High-Tech Consulting.

Name: Ozark Solutions
Description:
At Ozark Solutions, we specialize in providing expert consulting services for space defense and security. Experienced consultant brings together a wealth of knowledge and expertise in areas such as satellite acquisitions, space-based surveillance, space domain awareness, and space control.

We work closely with government agencies, defense contractors, and other organizations to develop and implement effective strategies for defending against threats to our national security in space. From designing advanced satellite systems to analyzing and mitigating potential risks, we provide customized solutions that meet the unique needs of each customer.

At Ozark Solutions, we pride ourselves on staying at the forefront of space defense technology and innovation. We are constantly learning and adapting to new developments in the field, and ensuring that we are always providing the most up-to-date and effective solutions.

If you're looking for a trusted partner to help you navigate the complex world of space defense and security, look no further then Ozark Solutions. Contact us today to learn more about how we can help protect your assets and secure our future in space.
