It's a membership community catered to both GCC residents and global space industry professionals engaged in space-enabled projects in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain.

Name: Yugen Space
Description:
At Yugen Space, we’re making Earth Observation radically simple.

Through our AI analysis platform, we turn complex satellite imagery into clear, actionable insights - starting with soil and land.

Our vision is to empower every individual on the planet with space-derived intelligence, making space data truly accessible.

Democratizing space begins with understanding Earth. 


www.yugenspace.com

Name: Team Udupi DTU
Description:
Team UDUPI DTU is the Space and Research Team, part of Cosmology Club, DTU. Founded in 2022 with the aim towards innovating the SpaceTech and specializing in satellites. The team builds CAN Shaped Satellites (CANSATs) and CubeSats to take part in various competitions.

We at Team Udupi have developed University's first Cansat, ULKA, in 2024 which was showcased during India's First Cansat Competition held in Ahmedabad.

Name: ISSTT 2026 - International Symposium on Space Terahertz Technology
Description:
The International Symposium on Space Terahertz Technology (ISSTT) 2026 will be held in Santiago, Chile, from March 23 to 26, 2026. ISSTT is a prestigious global conference that brings together leading researchers, engineers, and industry professionals working in the fields of terahertz (THz) technology, radio astronomy, and space science.

Organized as a non-profit academic initiative, ISSTT 2026 aims to foster collaboration and share the latest advancements in THz devices, instrumentation, and applications for space and ground-based systems. The symposium includes technical sessions, invited talks, poster presentations, and networking activities for professionals, students, and industry partners.

ISSTT is recognized as a key event for promoting innovation in the development of high-frequency technologies and their implementation in astronomical observations, planetary missions, Earth science, and beyond.

Name: TÜBİTAK TUG
Description:
TÜBİTAK TUG

TÜBİTAK ULUSAL GÖZLEMEVİ

1991 yılında yeniden belirlenen TÜBİTAK Yönetimi DPT'ye sunulmak üzere TAD Başkanlığı'ndan 5 yıllık bir Ulusal Gözlemevi kuruluş projesi istedi. Hazırlanan proje 20 Temmuz 1991'de TÜBİTAK'a sunuldu. DPT'ye iletilen proje 1992-1996 yılları arasını kapsayan yaklaşık 7 Milyar TL bütçeli bir Ulusal Gözlemevi kuruluş projesi olarak başlamış oldu.

7-11 Eylül 1992 tarihlerinde İnönü Üniversitesi'nde yapılan 8. Ulusal Astronomi Toplantısı sırasında, bu projenin yürütücülüğüne Prof. Dr. Zeki Aslan'ın önerilmesine karar verildi. Zeki Aslan'ın hazırlayıp sunduğu, 1992 yılı için 541 milyon TL bütçeli, TBAG-DPT destekli bir yıllık proje, Ulusal Gözlemevi Kurulması adı altında 1 Ekim 1992'de yürürlüğe girdi.

Gözlemevi kuruluş çalışmaları belirli bir aşamaya geldiğinde TÜBİTAK Ulusal Gözlemevi (TUG) Kuruluş ve İşletme Yönetmeliği olarak 17.07.1995 tarihli ve 22346 sayılı Resmi Gazete'de yayınlandı. Ardından 6 Ekim 1995 tarihinde de ilk müdürü olarak Prof. Dr. Zeki Aslan atandı.

2500 m yükseklikte modern bir araştırma merkezi kurmak hiç de kolay olmadı. Saklıkent'ten gözlemevine yol açılması, elektrik hattının döşenmesi, teleskop ve hizmet binalarının yapılması, gözlemevinde çalışacak teknik, idari ve araştırmacı personelinin oluşturulması gibi önemli aşamalar birer birer aşılarak 5 Eylül 1997 tarihinde dönemin Cumhurbaşkanı ve Başbakanı tarafından TUG'un resmi açılışı yapıldı.

TUG'un ilk teleskopu olan 40 cm çaplı T40 teleskopunda ilk ışık Ocak 1997'de, 150 cm çaplı RTT150 teleskopunda ise ilk ışık Eylül 2001'de alınarak gözlemevinde bilimsel gözlemler başlamış oldu.

Name: ITACUBE
Description:
O objetivo da ITACUBE é proporcionar, aos alunos de graduação do ITA, o contato com as atividades de desenvolvimento de cubesats contendo experimentos científicos a serem embarcados em balões estratosféricos. 
A proposta da iniciativa é totalmente educacional, com projetos simples com ciclo de vida aproximado de 1 ano, de modo a proporcionar um maior aprofundamento na execução do projeto. 
A iniciativa também visa proporcionar um ambiente onde os alunos possam trocar experiências entre si e com engenheiros mais experientes engajados em projetos no ITA. Para isso, a iniciativa já conta com o suporte técnico de professores especialistas na área. Este intercâmbio de conhecimentos tem como objetivos secundários possibilitar aos alunos identificar qual área da engenharia eles se identificam mais para escolherem qual engenharia cursar, propor temas de iniciação científica, trabalhos de graduação e teses de mestrado para o programa de mestrado na graduação.
Location: São José dos Campos, São Paulo

Name: PV-STAR
Description:
PV-STAR has received €2 million from the Occitanie to implement its action plan. The project is led by CNRS. 
The aim of PV-STAR is to adress technology gaps identified in yhe photovoltaic industry and propose concepts that are better suited to non-standard conditions uses (high solar concentrations, high temperatures, degraded or extreme climates, space environment). New physical concepts, adaptations of existing principles, or the use of materials not yet employed in the field, may offer various solutions to improve the performance of existing systems.

Four research topics have been defined for the PV-STAR project:
- Physics of photovoltaic energy conversion under non-standard conditions, exploring the limits.
- Innovative materials.
- The contribution of digital technology to the analysis and collection of data under non-standard conditions.
- New concepts for hybridising solar energy conversion methods.

Name: Bxrderless
Description:
Bxrderless is a for-profit social impact enterprise. Our ‘org’ operates as a living organism that is rooted and connected in the greater ecosystem of climate policy, trade and social justice.

Partner with us to change systems that degrade humanity and the environment and to create humane systems of living, from circular economy employment models to regenerative environmental conservation.

Name: NottSpace
Description: NottSpace group, is an interdisciplinary group within the University of Nottingham that focuses its activities on space systems and space missions. NottSpace includes researchers and students from Engineering, Pharmacy and Computer Science.

Name: Physics and Astronomy Club (PAC)  IIT Delhi
Artemis | Space Master Society
Description: Artemis is the study association for and by MSc spaceflight students of the faculty of Aerospace Engineering of Delft University of Technology. We have just started and are now building the website for communication with our students, communication with our partners, hosting events, and so much more.

Name: Steel Royal Space
Description: We are a pioneering student organization of the Peruvian University of Applied Sciences (UPC) dedicated to promoting aerospace innovation in Peru. Our multidisciplinary team develops cutting-edge technological projects, combining academic excellence with practical vision to expand the frontiers of space exploration from Peruvian territory.

Name: OculloSpace
Description: OculloSpace is a space education and innovation company dedicated to empowering the next generation of space technologists in ASEAN while delivering cost-effective satellite and orbital solutions for real-world applications. Through project-based learning, our flagship collaboration with NASA HUNCH, and partnerships with agencies such as MYSA and OSTIn, we integrate space education into national systems to cultivate future explorers. Our initiatives include CubeSat programs, sounding rockets, IoT connectivity, and the flagship “ElephantJuice” mission, which inspires public engagement by creating human legacies in orbit. With a dual focus on talent development and innovation, OculloSpace strengthens ASEAN’s role in the global space ecosystem.

Name: Aerione Bharat
Description:
Tomorrow Innovations Today

Aerione Bharat is a pioneer in space education and experiential learning, committed to inspiring the innovators of tomorrow.

Our mission is to make the universe not just observable—but something to explore, engineer, and experience.
By merging cutting-edge technology with hands-on programs, we ignite curiosity and cultivate the next generation of space scientists, engineers, and problem solvers.

Our Flagship Programs

Astronomy Expo
A first-of-its-kind Hollywood-style space journey featured in PVR, INOX, and other multiplexes—immersing students in the cosmos through cinematic visuals, interactive storytelling, and real-time simulations.

Edu Rocketry Laboratory (Edu-BOSE) – Bangalore, Bidadi
India’s premier hands-on rocketry initiative where students work with next-gen propulsion, space probes, rover landing tech, and long-duration cube satellites, gaining real-world aerospace experience.

GenNxt Smart Geometry
An innovative STEM platform allowing students to visualize and interact with complex spatial and mathematical concepts, bridging the gap between theory and real-world space science.

Edu-Bose Space Engineering Research Center – Bidadi
We’re laying the foundation for the future of Indian aerospace research.
Starting with a 5,000 sq. ft. facility, our vision is to expand into a 20-acre innovation campus dedicated to research, development, and incubation.
Empowering Future Innovators

We actively support students and researchers with:
Research funding for PhD and PG scholars
Support for aerospace researchers
Innovation grants for young engineers and technologists
Platform access for student-led projects and space experiments
Join us in shaping the next generation of space pioneers.

Together, let’s engineer a future that’s not just among the stars—but beyond them.

Name: Space Humber
Description:
Space Humber is the UK’s first privately funded space cluster, headquartered at in Hull and serving East and North Yorkshire.

We exist to connect, support, and accelerate the people and companies shaping the future of the space economy — from early-stage startups and established businesses to educators, engineers, investors, and space-curious individuals.

Our cluster is free to join and open to anyone, with both physical and digital membership options. We’re building a community that includes:

- A dedicated Space Careers Service to connect talent to opportunity
- The Delta V - Accelerated Incubator Programme, run by deep tech company Pan Galactic
- STEM outreach, hackathons, and engineering competitions
- An online platform for remote members to stay connected
- A long-term vision to develop the Space Humber Campus, featuring space manufacturing and testing facilities

We also work closely with regional and national partners to ensure our work strengthens and complements the wider UK space ecosystem.  
* Partnerships to be announced shortly

We embrace all areas of space and engineering, while placing strategic focus on transformative technologies like AI, digital infrastructure, blockchain, and cybersecurity — the backbone of tomorrow’s space economy.

Name: EDGJ
Description:
At independent consultancy EDGJ — Engineering Data-Driven Growth & Joint-intelligence, advances capability uplift for organisations operating in the New Space economy. Our work is focused on building system-level reasoning capacity for AI-enabled satellite systems, 5G/6G NTN architectures, and digital-twin-based mission evaluation.

Modern space systems are no longer static assembly problems. They behave as adaptive, data-conditioned, decision environments. Hardware is now governed by intelligence. The strategic advantage will belong to those who can engineer architecture choices under uncertainty, negotiate constraints in real-time, and convert complexity into executable clarity.

EDGJ supports this transition through a continuous loop:

• Architecture Consulting
• Capability Training
• Digital Twin Tooling

This is how organisations move from subsystem optimisation to architectural intelligence.

We collaborate with industry, national innovation ecosystems, and higher education institutions to accelerate this transformation — not by adding content, but by upgrading cognition. Our mission is to enable teams to think at the system level, make faster high-confidence decisions, and architect capability that scales.

EDGJ defines the discipline of engineering data-driven growth and joint-intelligence between humans, systems, and AI.

EDGJ — Where systems' architecture, data-driven training, and digital twin intelligence converge.

Description: Developing tailor-made gravity applications for low earth orbit

Description: We help newspace companies succeed with tailored solutions from engineers who know the game.

Name: iAstronomer Club
Description: An online learning platform for all ages developed by a team of educationists, science communicators, and astronomers. A unit of SPACE Group, established in 2001. The trademark of SPACE is innovation, passion, and total commitment to spreading knowledge of astronomy and space sciences to the public. Every project in SPACE is an opportunity for us to communicate our belief in developing scientific temperament to people, especially children, to create an intelligent and analytic global community. SPACE has successfully implemented astronomy and space science curriculum in the Indian education system. In this context, we have developed a variety of astronomy and space science educational concepts through astronomy projects, sessions, tutorials, and modules based on school/college curriculum for education, catering to the needs of schools & students in India.

Name: Precision Navigation Systems
Description:
Precision Navigation Systems OÜ is developing software and hardware solutions for a variety of non-standard applied and infrastructural tasks using GNSS technology and geotechnical sensors.

Our flagman project — Stargate RTK — is a virtual roaming operator that merges a multitude of GNSS correction services to provide pinpoint accuracy with ease and reliability.



Stargate RTK is a cloud-based service that offers seamless access to continuously operating reference stations for highly accurate GPS/GNSS positioning. This service is essential for the success of businesses that rely on GPS RTK equipped vehicles, such as those in construction, road building, mining, marine, drone, and agricultural industries.

Name: Reichman Space Club
Description:
Israel's first student-led entrepreneurial space club, operating under Reichman's Entrepreneurship Club and partnered with Space Campus IL 
We're answering a simple question: How do students from non-technical backgrounds enter the space economy projected to reach $1.8 trillion by 2035?

Our mission is to bridge the gap between talented students and an industry most don't know they can access. We're building Israel's next generation of space industry leaders across business, law, finance, communications, psychology, and beyond.
The space industry needs what Reichman students already have - strategic thinking, cross-disciplinary vision, global perspective, and entrepreneurial drive.

Through partnerships with Space Campus IL and industry leaders, we connect Reichman students from every field to the careers, networks, and insights that will set them apart in this next frontier.

The space race is back. This time, it's commercial. And all you need is to take the first step to be part of it.

Name: RTM European Rover Challenge
Description:
The RTM Team is made up of 6 engineering students who are participating for the first time in the international European Rover Challenge. 

We are studying at ITII Picardie - Institute of Industrial Engineering Techniques located in Beauvais (60).

When we joined the training in September 2022, we were entrusted to develop a rover, able to be totally autonomous, able to recognize its environment, objects and interact with them, but also able to drill a surface, extract samples and analyze them. 
This project will allow us to compete in the EUROPEAN ROVER CHALLENGE being an international competition in partnership with the ESA taking place in Poland.

Our goals are :
- To compete in the European Rover Challenge in 2024.
- To be ranked at least in the top 50 teams in the world (for the selection of the file and the competition) in order to catch up with last year's team from our school (40th) 
- To surpass ourselves, to develop new skills, to learn new fields and sectors of work, to show our potential to our possible future employers, and to represent France as well as our school on the international scene! 

We sincerely hope that you will be interested in our project, which for us is the biggest project of a (young) life!

Name: Artix Technologies
Description: We are an AI SaaS company specializing in transforming geospatial data into actionable insights. By leveraging advanced machine learning algorithms, we process complex geospatial datasets to provide businesses with real-time, data-driven solutions.

Name: Admatis
Description:
Admatis Ltd. coordinates, manages space industry related projects from the mechanical-thermal design and analysis to the manufacturing and
test phase according to ECSS.

Name: African Regional Centre for Space Science and Technology Education-English
Description:
The mission of the organization is to cultivate a high quality of capacity and a critical mass of indigenous Space Scientists and educators across the English speaking African countries. 
We develop skills and knowledge through education in the thematic area of Space Science and Technology (SST), which include Remote Sensing and Geographic Information System, Satelite Meteorology and Global Climate, Satelite Communications, Space and Atmospheric Sciences, Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) and Space Law.

Name: LINSET | Whitelab
Description:
LINSET srl è un avanzato centro di ricerca e testing, che svolge attività di progettazione, sperimentazione e prototipazione per il settore navale, eolico ed industriale in genere.

Fondato nel 2001 come centro R&D della società Azimut-Benetti SpA (leader mondiale nella costruzione di Mega Yachts in composito ed in metallo), LINSET srl ha sviluppato una consolidata esperienza nello sviluppo e nella caratterizzazione meccanica e chimico-fisica dei compositi in fibra di vetro e carbonio.
Dal 2010, LINSET srl è una compagine indipendente che continua a sviluppare il proprio know-how tecnico, ingegneristico e di R&D, rivolgendo la propria attività di consulenza nel campo dei materiali compositi non solo per i costruttori ma anche per i produttori di materie prime.

LINSET srl ha sede a Fano (PU) in uno stabile industriale che si sviluppa su una superficie di 1200 mq, di cui 700 dedicati ai laboratori Meccanico, Termico e Chimico-Fisico, attrezzati con tutta la strumentazione necessaria per una completa caratterizzazione, meccanica e termica, di un materiale composito (in fibra di vetro, carbonio, basalto, fibre naturali, ecc.), di leghe in alluminio e metalliche in genere, nonché di bonders, adesivi, stucchi e vernici.

LINSET srl è un laboratorio di ingegneria certificato dal RINA per l’esecuzione di numerosi test meccanici e chimico-fisici, ed offre quindi la possibilità di ottenere certificazioni ed omologhe presso tale ente senza la necessità di sorveglianza (e relativi costi) da parte di un loro ispettore.

LINSET srl è in grado di eseguire circa 300 test secondo le normative attualmente vigenti a livello nazionale ed internazionale.
LINSET srl rappresenta inoltre, grazie alla consolidata partnership con ENAVE, un ente di riferimento per il rilascio della marcatura CE per il settore imbarcazioni da diporto e componentistica navale.

Name: ASTRA Team Polito
Description: ASTRA is a team of students from the Politecnico di Torino dedicated to creating multiple components and modules necessary for the development of a self-sufficient and operational lunar outpost. The objective of designing a lunar base entails the construction of a comprehensive system of interdependent components that guarantee the ability to sustain life on extraterrestrial planets, including the design of habitats, research into resource and energy reusability and recycling, as well as the use of robotic agents to perform tasks in concert with human counterparts.

Name: UBC Mars Colony
Description: UBC Engineering students looking into developing suitable living conditions for research and habitation on Mars.

Name: Karachi Astronomers Society
Description:
Karachi Astronomers’ Society (KAS) is a dedicated group of astronomers of Karachi and beyong. KAS is the short name given to this society by the founders. Now KAS has its active members beyond Karachi as well, Lahore, Islamabad, Quetta, Sargoda, Hyderabad, other Pakistani cities as well as in other countries such as UAE, USA, Australia, Italy, Germany etc.

KAS is the first Astronomy club and largest Astronomy club of Pakistan in terms of members, affiliations and events, having arranged many types of events promoting Astronomy as hobby and science in Karachi and Pakistan. Since 2008, KAS has been promoting the use of Binoculars and telescopes through various events, workshops and Rutjugas and made enthusiasts aware of usage, availability, and technicalities.

Name: Eu-BEST
Description:
Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of Eu-BEST (European Bench for Engine and Stage Testing)! 
We are a collaborative initiative funded by the European Commission and comprised of seven European space industry leaders and NewSpace companies dedicated to revolutionizing space infrastructure testing.

With the rise in private small launchers in Europe, ground segment capital and operational costs are becoming increasingly high, as 99.99% of a launcher’s lifespan is spent on the ground. To address this, Eu-BEST is offering standardized ground segment services to launchers, significantly reducing these costs.

As part of the Eu-BEST project, we are developing and operating an interoperable, modular, and mobile test bench for rocket engines and stages. Our test bench serves multiple launchers, locations, and development phases, ensuring a uniform user experience. 

By leveraging the latest technologies and industry best practices, we aim to set new standards for space testing, contributing to the advancement of Europe’s space transportation and ground technologies. 

Join us on our journey as we pave the way for a more accessible and cost-effective approach to space testing. Explore our page to learn more about our innovative solution, latest developments, and industry insights. Together, let's unlock the potential of space testing with Eu-BEST!

This project is funded by the European Commission under its Horizon Europe program and is currently under development.

Name: Cornell SmallSat Mission Design School
Description: Unique summer workshop to inspire innovative missions in high-priority space science made possible with small spacecraft.  A collaboration between Cornell University faculty from Aerospace Engineering and Astronomy, SMDS seeks to provide students with first-hand experience in space mission design. Participants shall develop a mission concept with immediate relevance to NASA’s Decadal Survey science priorities, directed at an upcoming announcement of opportunity for a funded flight program. Students will benefit from the combined expertise of both Cornell faculty and external scientists and engineers working in industry.

Name: AS23.Space
Description: AS23.Space Technologies, our mission is to responsibly pioneer space infrastructure that unlocks the immense potential of satellite data to benefit life on Earth and fuel space exploration. With our revolutionary Nebula Data architecture, we are trailblazing the space-based data solutions that will drive innovation and discovery for decades to come.

Name: OklahomaQuantum
Description:
Inertial sensor  provides position when GPS is unavailable. Therefore, it is used in many industries, such as autonomous vehicles, submarines, aircraft and spacecraft. However, inaccuracies or small errors of inertial sensor accumulate and increase over time, so often aircraft and submarines have to self-disclose to readjust their current position. It is often a lethal threat to missions as it reveals its location in unwanted areas.

  Quantum inertial sensors can provide near-absolute accuracy of acceleration because atoms are extremely sensitive to their environment. However, quantum technology has many shortcomings to be put into practical uses. OklahomaQauntum is developing quantum inertial sensor that is highly integrated, compact for navigation.

Name: RunSpace Innovation Challenge
Description:
To nurture Taiwan's space innovation talent and technological capabilities, we proudly present the RunSpace Innovation Challenge. 

In alignment with the dynamic growth of the space industry, this competition encompasses a wide range of thematic areas. We invite business professionals, researchers and students to engage in collaborative exchanges that foster innovative ideas and unveil cross-disciplinary creativity.

Now in its fourth year, the competition promises to generate more solutions and applications in the field of space innovation.

Name: SpaceProbe Pvt Ltd
Description:
SpaceProbe Pvt Ltd is a pioneering company founded with the mission to bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and real-world applications. We are dedicated to developing innovative tools and services that empower communities, industries, and institutions.

Our expertise spans space weather alerts and forecasting, geospatial intelligence through GIS and remote sensing, and advanced technological solutions, all designed to promote sustainable growth and informed decision-making.

Beyond technology, we are equally committed to nurturing a scientific temperament in society. Through science outreach, exhibitions, corporate events, workshops, training schools, and citizen science programs, we strive to make science more accessible and engaging for all.

By uniting scientific expertise with technological innovation, SpaceProbe seeks to build a strong ecosystem where knowledge, exploration, and application move together, helping communities not just understand space but truly benefit from it.

Name: The Overview
Description:
A new age has begun, where the average human can help expand humanity’s endeavors in the sky. Whether it’s sustainable aircraft, satellite swarms, or hypersonic systems — aerospace is enjoying a new resurgence. 

We are graduate students from Stanford University’s Aerospace department, and together we bring experience from every corner of aerospace academia and industry. Our goal is to demystify aerospace and bring to you the latest news and opinions related to aerospace research, space business, and getting your career started. 

This is The Overview.

Join our newsletter and follow our socials to help us make an impact on the new space age.

Name: MAHAKASH AEROSPACE
Description: "MAHAKASH AEROSPACE PVT LTD - Redefining the Boundaries of Space Tourism Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of MAHAKASH AEROSPACE PVT LTD! We are on a mission to make the extraordinary accessible. As a leader in the space tourism industry, we offer unparalleled opportunities for individuals to embark on celestial adventures.Our dedicated team of innovators is pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the cosmos, and we're committed to providing safe, thrilling, and awe-inspiring experiences beyond Earth's atmosphere.Join us on this journey to the stars, where dreams meet reality and the universe becomes your playground. Follow us for the latest updates on our exciting projects, partnerships, and the future of space tourism.  #SpaceTourism #Exploration #Innovation"

Name: RADC
Description:
RGUKT AERODYNAMIC CLUB (RADC)
The idea of this club was started when we wanted to educate ourselves and others through the real-time implementation of what we had read in the books.

Our Vision:-
To make people realize that aerodynamics and rocket sciences are not that much complicated. In fact, they are filled with lots of fun.

Our Mission:-
Our mission is to do research on advanced techniques of rocket sciences and to build advanced aerodynamic models.

We are starting a dynamic website named https://radc.in/ ,there you can access contents related to our club.

Name: Coterrial
Description: At Coterrial Space we are focusing on the future of intergalactic travel through cooperation, collaboration, and teamwork across the world. Working together we create kit rockets to serve the purpose of many while promoting technological advancement through healthy competition, shared lessons learned, and open communication. Future developments will be made in ecofriendly rocket fuel, space debris collection, and missions outside of our planet.

Name: Metacept Inc
Description:
Metacept creates and spins out category-defining startups based on metamaterial technology. 

Our recent spin-offs include companies specialising in through-wall imagers (Resolv), space lasers (Astrobeam), Earth observation sensors (Kapta), and the world's fastest, most energy-efficient AI hardware (Neurophos).

The Duke University research group led by Metacept’s CEO David Smith has been at the forefront of most major metamaterial breakthroughs,  and spun out several unicorns that our team has developed products for.  In addition to our spin-offs, we develop products for NASA and DARPA, further diversifying our portfolio for potential new ventures.

Leveraging our extensive R&D and startup experience, strong ties with manufacturers and government agencies, proprietary simulation software, and exclusive access to the world's largest metamaterial patent portfolio, Metacept excels at rapidly building and de-risking high-caliber startups.

Name: Astronautics Club, IIIT Hyderabad
Description:
Astronautics Club at IIIT Hyderabad is the Space Science and SpaceTech club at IIIT Hyderabad. A vibrant student-run community united by a shared passion for exploring the universe. From stargazing sessions to star parties and hands-on workshops, we bring space science to life for enthusiasts of all levels.

We have participated in numerous National and International competitions, gaining experience and recognition along the way. Join us as we journey into the fascinating world of Astronomy and SpaceTech!

Name: Critical IQ
Description: Critical IQ is a Research and Consulting Company established in Colorado Springs, CO, and has a presence in several USA vital locations. We partner with our customers to conduct research studies and architect solutions on the most bleeding-edge spheres of Space, Cyberspace, and Global Security Policy. Our domain expertise aids our clientele to develop Scientific and Technical Solution Strategies that are practical and meet real-world industrial demands. We have managed to secure partnerships and fellowships with numerous research institutions, and subject matter experts and nurture a deep talent pool in multiple occupational domains. Let's Connect!

Name: SigmaLabs
Description:
SigmaLabs Sp. z o. o. is a deep-tech venture specializing in the research, development, and commercialization of cutting-edge radiation-resilient and radiation-aware electronic systems, radiation detectors, radiation testing and characterization, and predictive maintenance systems for satellite applications.

Additionally, our expertise includes radiation environment modeling and simulation, reliability analysis for electronic systems, test-beam campaign planning and execution, and in-orbit radiation data analysis.

Our team combines deep expertise in high-speed, mixed-signal electronics design with advanced capabilities in radiation-tolerant and radiation-aware system design for extreme environments such as space and high-energy physics.

The company collaborates with international organizations, including the European Space Agency (ESA) and the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN), leveraging extensive experience in radiation-tolerant electronics design and testing. SigmaLabs' team members have a history of contributing to both legacy and new space projects, with activities related to payload design, commissioning, and integration.

SigmaLabs is actively involved in projects for both ESA and private clients, as well as internal development. We are currently engaged in the following ESA projects: RadMon-on-ISS and ESA-PAD, where we are delivering flight hardware to be deployed onboard the International Space Station.

SigmaLabs also collaborates with private clients, supporting them at every stage, from radiation environment analysis to in-orbit data assessment, ensuring the highest level of expertise and reliability in radiation-tolerant electronic systems.

We are committed to continuous product innovation and are actively developing our own products and services. SigmaLabs combines world-class expertise with a client-centric approach to ensure the success and longevity of systems operating in the harshest radiation environments.

Name: StarsFlyer
Description:
“We choose to go to the Moon in this decade and do the other things, not because they are easy, but because they are hard" President John F. Kennedy


Going to space unfolds many risks and dangers that could happen to the human body, and also shows the uniqueness of the human body to be able to adapt to this extreme environment, the space environment. 

That's why in StarsFlyer, we optimise the health of humans in space to be able to not only survive, but to thrive. With a robust team of experience over 60 years in the space medicine industry. In order to put humanity on other planets and make it an interplanetary species.

We provide medical training for pilots and crew members to optimise their health in space, and facilitate the access to do research in extreme environments and analogue missions to develop the exploration and utilization of the space environment for advancing humankind. Ranging from space medicine to deep space technology consulting, research, and development of scientific instruments for people in space.

Name: MilliScan
Description:
Convenient millimeter-wave imaging system for people and mail security inspection. 

MilliScan is a Research to Business (R2B) project funded by Business Finland and Aalto University. We are on our way to revolutionizing the inspection of food, packaging, mail, and people screening by developing a new millimeter-wave imaging solution. In contrast to some of the existing technologies, our solution is compact, lightweight, and maintenance-free. All this without emitting harmful radiation or invading people's privacy.

Name: Data Trends Specialist Research
Description: Market research firms offer services across diverse industries, such as healthcare, technology, retail, finance, and consumer goods. They assist clients in understanding customer needs, optimizing marketing strategies, evaluating product performance, and predicting market shifts. Additionally, these companies often specialize in specific research areas, such as brand health tracking, product development, market entry strategy, or customer satisfaction.

Name: Orion Industries
Description: Orion Industries is an advanced research and product development company based on Earth with a focus on providing quantum based technological advancements to enable the human race to be the most efficient and cutting-edge version of itself. Our fields of professional interest include; quantum computing, artificial intelligence, quantum propulsion, efficient energy creation and storage, data transfer, CVD diamond semiconductor manufacturing, and more.

Name: EARS Project
Description:
In the EARS project, we design a reusable small satellite and develop the related critical technologies.
The reusable spacecraft is intended to de-orbit in a controlled manoeuvre to deliver its products and results back to the Earth after several months in orbit, thereby enabling innovative research that would benefit from the space environment, especially from microgravity conditions.

The long-term vision of the EARS project is a family of small satellite platforms that can be launched and reused frequently at a very competitive price without polluting the environment and wasting resources, both in space and on Earth.

Name: Solunex Spaceworks
Description:
Solunex Spaceworks is shaping the strategic future of space infrastructure through resilient, intelligent energy systems for modular spacecraft.

We are developing a new generation of space power solutions focused on autonomy, adaptability, and scalability, enabling smarter mission operations, cooperative platforms, and energy-aware spacecraft constellations.

Our approach combines forward-looking systems thinking with deep technical insight to address growing challenges in space logistics, power coordination, and modular design.

As part of the UK Space Agency Explore Accelerator, we’re working to validate our strategic direction and build meaningful partnerships toward sustainable, next-gen orbital systems.

Name: Starlight Innovations LLC
Description:
In space programs, success demands precision, foresight, and the ability to see challenges from every angle. Starlight Innovations delivers expert consulting and engineering to the space sector, combining trusted program leadership, risk-informed planning, and technical excellence.

With decades of experience in innovating science and technology across government and industry, we have a proven record of solving complex technical and programmatic challenges. Our multidimensional perspectives, shaped by varied disciplines and experiences, help us uncover solutions others overlook.

From launch vehicles and spacecraft to ground systems and mission operations, we guide high-stakes projects through every phase. Rigorous analysis and innovative thinking turn complexity into opportunity, advancing capabilities that make a lasting impact both in orbit and here on Earth.

Name: Center for Astronomy, Space Science and Astrophysics
Description: The Center for Astronomy, Space Science and Astrophysics (CASSA) at Independent University, Bangladesh (IUB) is the country’s first dedicated academic and research center for exploring the universe. CASSA brings together faculty, students, and collaborators from around the world to advance knowledge in astronomy, astrophysics, and space science. Our mission is to build a vibrant community of professors, scientists and students who will shape Bangladesh’s contribution to the global space era.

Name: LumeCraft Creative
Description:
"LumeCraft Creative is a leading business research and consulting firm. Our mission is to empower clients by identifying emerging opportunities and driving revenue growth across industries. We specialize in providing comprehensive market insights, advanced technology solutions, and actionable strategies that support operational and strategic decision-making.

With a focus on the full value chain, our expert team delivers high-quality syndicate research reports, proprietary databases, and consulting services to help clients navigate today's competitive and dynamic business environment. At LumeCraft Creative, we use advanced analysis to uncover profitable opportunities, enabling businesses to make smart decisions and achieve sustainable success."

Name: Laboratoire de Génie Mécanique
Description: Le laboratoire de Génie Mécanique « LGM » est un laboratoire de recherche universitaire établi depuis 1999. Le laboratoire est dirigé actuellement par Professeur Salah Mezlini. Le laboratoire soutient la formation doctorale en Génie Mécanique de l’ENIM et réunit les enseignants-chercheurs, les personnels techniques et les doctorants de l’Ecole Nationale d’Ingénieurs de Monastir (ENIM) qui réalisent des recherches dans les domaines de la mécanique, de la conception, de la fabrication, de la robotique, de la vibration et de la mécanique des matériaux. Le laboratoire occupe des locaux situés au département de Génie Mécanique de l’ENIM.

The firm engages with investment funds and portfolio companies across the lifecycle of ownership, with emphasis on early-stage through mid-cap assets where technology maturity, execution risk and capital discipline are decisive.

𝗦𝗰𝗼𝗽𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁:
Activities center on investment feasibility, governance readiness and long-horizon risk assessment across space-commerce and infrastructure projects. This includes evaluation of technology at prototype, beta and early production stages and formation of independent positions on market potential, scalability and capital efficiency.

Board participation spans capital formation events, portfolio transitions and exit considerations, ensuring alignment between technical reality, strategic intent and investor responsibility.

𝗧𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗻𝗼𝗹𝗼𝗴𝘆 𝗱𝗼𝗺𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀:
Oversight experience covers:
  • Test and launch simulation
  • Space vehicle components and payload systems
  • Avionics, guidance, navigation and control
  • Propulsion systems and thrust architectures
  • Launch vehicle and mission integration
  • Terrestrial and ground-support equipment

𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗲𝘅𝘁:
Arthenium X provides governance continuity & capital-allocation judgment. The platform neither replaces transaction teams nor operating judgment, but ensures that capital deployment reflects technical truth, execution limits and risk asymmetry.

DCore exists to connect established companies and organizations looking to take their first steps into space with the community, products, and services that will get them there. Our dynamic solutions are a force multiplier at every point on that journey.

Space Underground: A professional association for networking and learning through online events hosted on our private Discord server.

Engineering Services: Custom electro-mechanical system design for teams needing additional and immediate expertise.

Training Packages: Focused on improving systems engineering fundamentals to enhance product lifecycle efficiency.

Marketplace: Coming Soon!

Name: Bartell Industries LLC
Description:
Bartell Industries builds practical, high-impact AI systems for real businesses.

We don’t do fluff. We work directly with teams to understand how they operate — then design AI that fits their world. From real estate to restaurants, investment firms to logistics companies, we help businesses simplify the complex and automate the repetitive.

Whether you’re looking for a smart assistant, intelligent dashboards, or full AI-powered workflows — we’ll build it to match how you actually work.

No gimmicks. Just useful AI.

Name: StarNex Aerotech
Description:
StarNex is an aerospace startup focused on dramatically transforming access to orbit through next-generation reusable launch technology.

Our core mission is to provide the commercial space industry with highly reliable, sustainable, and cost-effective payload delivery services.

The foundation of our technology is the proprietary electric-pump-fed propulsion system. By replacing complex, high-maintenance gas generators with efficient, electrically-driven pump units, we significantly:

Enhance engine reliability and simplify the overall rocket architecture.

Maximize the reusability of our launch vehicles, leading to substantial reductions in operational costs.

Promote sustainable practices in space travel.

Led by Founder Bhavesh Suryavanshi and Co-Founders Shreya Sharma(Technical Simulation Expert) and  and Owais Baba( Space engineer) along with Vibhanshu Hire(Thermal Engineer), StarNex is dedicated to achieving rapid development and establishing a new standard for operational efficiency in space launch. We are building the infrastructure for the future of the commercial space economy.

Follow us to track our development milestones, technological breakthroughs, and team growth.

Name: Newspace North
Description:
Space.

It all begins with an idea. 

Maybe you want to launch a space-related business. Maybe you want to launch a satellite. Maybe you have a creative project to share with the world. Maybe you need to bounce ideas. Maybe you need access to labs, clean-room or maybe you need access to the resources established space companies can provide. 

Whatever it is, we’re here to help you make all the difference.

There are thousands of space companies out there, but our closeness to unique space infrastructure increase your chance of success. And that is what’s going to separate you from the rest

Name: Lunar Habitation Technologies
Description:
Stellar migration and the human experience.  

LunarHab was founded to create safe, permanent habitats on the lunar surface to facilitate low gravity scientific research, pharmacological development, space tourism, and gateway operations to explore the solar system beyond the Earth-Moon system. Our proprietary patent pending bonding technology exclusively uses lunar materials in situ to 3-D print human habitats on the moon. 

Our core value is that in the universe, Earth is exceptionally rare, and perhaps the only place life exists.  It is humanity's great responsibility to preserve and protect life, ensuring life expands throughout the universe.

Name: COCO Project
Description:
COCO (satellite COntrol based on COmpliant thrust vectoring mechanism) is the first project selected for the Orbital Robotics Lab as part of the ESA Academy Experiments programme 2024.

The aim of the experiment is to study an Attitude and Orbit Control System (AOCS) based on compliant mechanisms on a 2D microgravity platform to evaluate its feasibility for future space missions.

The project is carried out with the support of the Education Office of the European Space Agency, under the educational Experiments Programme.

Name: SEDS Juniors

Aerospace Engineering Center is an independent engineering hub focused on solving complex aerospace and industry challenges.
We combine R&D, advanced engineering and practical technical training to support companies working with real world technologies from aerospace systems and UAV engineering to propulsion and production processes.
We bring together engineers, researchers and domain experts to help organisations design, analyse, prototype and validate solutions that require precision, responsibility and deep technical expertise.
Our services include:
• Engineering R&D and technology development
• UAV systems, propulsion and structural engineering
• Technical expertise and on-demand problem solving

AEC was founded as a spin-off of Rzeszów University of Technology and operates as a specialised engineering centre serving aerospace, industry and advanced technology sectors.

Name: 3D2SPACE
Description:
We are expanding human presence in space through the use of next-gen technologies. We are creating customized solutions according to mission requirements.  

We are focusing on minimizing the impact on the space environment to support the long-term sustainability of space operations. We are developing innovative solutions to the challenges of the space industry.

3D2SPACE is dedicated to opening new horizons for human progress in space and creating a new era of industrial possibilities in infinite space. Our expansion into the space industry allows us not only to explore the unknown, but also to contribute to the development of technologies that will have a positive impact on planet Earth."

Name: HI-Spectral
Description: HI-Spectral, LLC specializes in the design and manufacturing of novel Snapshot Hyperspectral Imaging Systems. HI-Spectral's technology was originally designed at the University of Hawaii for the DKIST telescope, the largest and most advanced Solar Telescope in the world. Our technology has the capability to efficiently capture detailed chemical composition information (Spectrum) for each pixel in an image, in one exposure (snapshot). Our avant-garde design resulted in a very compact and robust customizable instrument able to generate data with high spectral resolution at high speed. Its uniqueness will unlock new applications and uses for hyperspectral imaging. The reliable wealth of information generated by our systems will help government agencies and private companies make educated and impactful strategic decisions.

3 - Geopolitical - A renaissance in the race to space and sovereign protectionist agendas in high-tech engineering, data centres, and onshore industrialization are driving a deluge of momentum in frontier tech.

Don't just watch the future unfold - build It. Lead It. Follow and subscribe.

Name: AERO PULSE SYSTEM FOR SPACE TECHNOLOGY - L.L.C - S.P.C
Description:
Our space technology startup specializes in providing thrusters and avionics.
Founded in August 2023, we are systematically advancing through key phases of development.

1. Design Phase: Focused on subsystem propulsion/thrusters and low-level avionics (Current state) 
2. Mockups & Prototyping: Building initial physical models to validate design concepts and manufacturing feasibility.
3. Testing & Verification: Conducting rigorous performance assessments, including functional, environmental, and stress tests.
4. Final Prototype & Ground Testing: A full-scale prototype will be tested in a tethered Earth-based trial, reaching up to 10 meters in altitude.

These phases serve as a proof of concept, demonstrating the startup’s potential to contribute to the UAE’s space sector. We are also actively seeking investors to accelerate development and expand our technological capabilities.

Key Contributions:
1. Led the design phase of propulsion subsystems and avionics, shaping the startup’s core technical foundation in Abu Dhabi, UAE.
2. Integrated cutting-edge space technologies, leveraging industry expertise for innovation and efficiency.
3. Implemented strategic planning and decision-making processes to drive the startup’s success.

El primer gran encuentro nacional de la industria espacial, tecnológica y científica.

La economía espacial ya supera los USD 560.000 millones y crece a un ritmo sin precedentes, proyectando alcanzar los 1.8 trillones de dólares para 2035. Hoy, sectores clave como telecomunicaciones, observación de la Tierra, IA, electrónica avanzada, infraestructura digital, defensa, minería, logística y agricultura dependen cada vez más de soluciones basadas en tecnología espacial. Name: KeepTrack
Description:
KeepTrack.space is an open-source platform providing real-time satellite tracking, astrodynamics tools, and orbital data for researchers, engineers, and space enthusiasts. Used by universities, hobbyists, and military units worldwide, KeepTrack offers accessible, transparent, and reliable space situational awareness without proprietary barriers.

We aim to bridge the gap between raw satellite data and actionable insights through intuitive visualization, API access, and educational resources. Whether you're monitoring space traffic, analyzing orbital maneuvers, or learning about orbital mechanics, KeepTrack.space delivers the tools you need—free and open to all.

Name: INNOVATIONHUB-BLDEACET
Description:
BLDE Association’s College of Engineering and Technology (BLDEACET), Vijayapura was established in the year 1980 and named after the founder of BLDE Association Dr. P.G. Halakatti. The motive behind establishing BLDEACET was to provide quality engineering education to the students of North Karnataka in particular and neighbouring states at large at affordable fees. College started functioning with three basic branches viz., Civil, Mechanical and Electrical with an intake of 40 each by getting approval from Karnataka University Dharwad.

At present BLDEACET is offering eight undergraduate programs, eight post graduate programs (including MBA and MCA) and PhD & MSc. Engg.(by research) through recognized research centres. The competent, qualified and experienced teaching staffs deliver the contents of the curriculum by using modern teaching methods & tools, excellent laboratory facilities.

As a seal of quality, four undergraduate programs (Civil, Mechanical, Computer Science and Electrical) of the college are accredited for a period of four years (2005, 2015, 2018, and 2022) by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) New Delhi. The college also got accreditation status from National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) for a period of five years from 2018.

College offers excellent teaching-learning atmosphere to the students. Library and information center provides best of the best learning resources. Training and placement cell take students through career guidance programs, placement related training programs and finally place them in best organizations.

Students are provided guidance, help through identified student mentors. Each faculty will take care of a group of fifteen students. Faculty member (mentor) will help students in overcoming their personal problems, academic problems and any other issues.

The academic & administrative infrastructure and other facilities such as hostels, canteens, sports facilities, gymnasium, student activity center are h

Name: empiriQa
Description:
At empiriQa, our mission is to neutralize the single greatest future risk to global data security: the advent of the Quantum Computer. As current encryption methods face obsolescence, we provide the only countermeasure offering complete, physics-based security: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD).

Who We Are: We are a deep-tech spin-off established to commercialize 20 years of specialized research in the QKD area from the esteemed SPALab at Nicolaus Copernicus University in Toruń. Supported by the European Space Agency Business Incubation Centre (ESA BIC Poland), we bridge the gap between world-class science and mission-critical commercial applications.

Our Three Pillars:
Technology Provider: We design and deliver the "heart" of quantum networks, including high-efficiency entangled-photon sources and Optical Ground Stations (OGS) with the specialized capability for receiving quantum keys.
Independent V&V Centre: We serve as a European hub for the Verification and Validation of quantum hardware, ensuring that solutions are not just innovative, but objectively secure and certified.
Quantum Resilience System Definition: We help governments, defense sectors, and critical infrastructure operators define and deploy quantum-safe architectures.

The empiriQa Difference: We operate where marketing hype ends and "Technical Truth" begins. Our focus is on eliminating implementation flaws - the "backdoors" - that compromise quantum security in the real world. By combining two decades of academic excellence with industrial expertise, we are building a trusted foundation for Europe’s quantum future.

Name: Women in Space Pro
Description: While there isn't a specific organization named "Women in Space Pro," the term encapsulates the global movement and professional landscape dedicated to advancing women in the aerospace and space exploration sectors. This professional sphere is supported by numerous influential organizations and initiatives aimed at fostering growth, networking, and recognition for women in the field. Foremost among these is Women in Aerospace (WIA), a prominent organization founded in 1985 that is dedicated to expanding women's opportunities for leadership and increasing their visibility within the industry through networking events, professional development programs, and policy advocacy. Globally, networks like the International Network of Women in Aerospace (INWIA) connect professionals across different countries, promoting collaboration and mentorship on an international scale.

Name: Integrated Sensing Solutions (ISS)

With a foundation rooted in advanced materials science and artificial intelligence, we specialize in creating intelligent sensing systems that power applications across industries, including aerospace, automotive, healthcare, and energy.

Our expertise lies in leveraging state-of-the-art materials, such as carbon nanotube-based sensors and integrated smart platforms, to deliver unparalleled accuracy, reliability, and efficiency.
