We are committed to repurposing recycled materials and utilizing renewable resources, ensuring that our advancements in space are in harmony with the preservation of our planet.

Name: Gaucho Rocket Project
Description: Gaucho Rocket Project aims to provide students the tools to design, build, and launch rockets, fostering hands-on learning experiences. Pushing the boundaries of space exploration, we hope to inspire the next generation to further the endeavor into the cosmos.

Name: Open Source Cubesat Workshop
Description:
Join us again for a two-day workshop to see how the open source approach can be applied to CubeSat missions with a focus on innovative and state-of-the-art concepts!

Open source software and hardware is empowering and democratizing all areas of life, so why not apply it to space exploration? The Open Source Cubesat Workshop was created exactly for that: to promote the open source philosophy for CubeSat missions and beyond. The sixth edition of the workshop takes place in Athens, Greece, hosted by Libre Space Foundation.

Name: Space to Grow
Description: Space to Grow is a podcast taking you inside the world of European space entrepreneurship. Through conversations with founders, we uncover the stories, challenges, and insights from those building the future of space technology. From satellite innovations to rocket development, each episode brings you perspectives from the frontlines of Europe's growing space industry.

Name: Gaia Vari
Description: Gaia Vari is a citizen science project funded by the European Space Agency (ESA). Its main objective is to encourage space enthusiasts from around the world to get involved in classifying data collected by the Gaia mission. Users can participate in research on Gaia variable stars, contributing to new scientific discoveries. Join the community and become a space explorer!

Name: BLESS BEXUS
Description:
BLESS (BEXUS HaLow Evaluation in Stratospheric Systems) is an aerospace engineering project selected for the REXUS/BEXUS Programme (Cycle 17).

Our sole mission is to take the cutting-edge Sub-1 GHz IoT standard, Wi-Fi HaLow (802.11ah), and expose it to the harsh reality of the Stratosphere to validate its capabilities in near-space conditions.

Name: Projet Séléné, concours MOVE ON THE MOON
Description: Page du projet du groupe ESTACA Séléné du concours MOVE ON THE MOON du CNES. Retrouvez ici toutes nos publications, vidéos et teasers concernant le concours !

Name: WarpDrive RWA
Description: A constantly growing consumption of energy resources requires  more  active reinvention of existing data related to the electromagnetic field as the most fundamental, convenient and widely applied principle in all fields of electrical engineering. The analysis of existing theoretical and practical data of the field theory description mainly the electromagnetic field was carried out. Artificial calibrations and mistakes were found. The condition where the canceled second part of the electromagnetic field allocates fully was stated. Experiments demonstrated that this component does not interact with iron filings, but interacts between one another specifically with a conductor by which an electric current and gravity flow. The main purpose is to test theoretical data in practice. To achieve the stated above purpose, a laboratory machine was engineered and assembled, thereby allowing testing physically all theoretically grounded and modeled data.

Name: CASR - Center of Aviation and Space Research
Description:
CASR, established in 2016 at the Czech Technical University in Prague, is the research centre for aerospace technologies, especially for testing aircraft engines. CASR offers access to four ground test facilities, including the necessary expertise and know-how to industrial companies. 
As a university training center, CASR offers Masters and PhD level training in the field of aircraft engines engineering. Students enrolled in the programmes have a unique opportunity to work closely with professionals in the aerospace industry and to participate in engine testing and related research and development activities. 
CASR research expertise and other services include:
•	Noise pollution reduction 
•	Emission reduction 
•	EMA – Matlab, Comsol 
•	CFD – Ansys, Comsol 
CASR offers tailored training the following areas: 
•	Model simulations design and set up 
•	Experimental data analysis 
•	FEM – Ansys, Abaques, Comsol 
•	Thermodynamics – NPSS, GasTurb, GAMMA, Matlab 
•	Dynamic engine models configuration 
•	Analysis of temperature deformations and rotor systems 
•	Design of control algorithms

CASR is also a training center for Masters level and PhD level training in the field of aircraft engines engineering.

Name: ELEVATE Space Conference
Description:
3-day event at the heart of the Principality of Monaco focusing on the environmental and social impact of human space exploration.

This groundbreaking event will bring together the brightest minds and innovative leaders in the space industry for three days of inspiring talks, captivating panel discussions, and incredible networking opportunities.

From commercial space exploration to space tourism, from asteroid mining to deep space colonization, Elevate Space Conference will cover it all. Get ready to hear from renowned experts and visionaries who are shaping the future of space exploration and making bold strides towards a new era of space discovery.

Name: Breakthrough Energy Project
Description:
In 2004 the Ansari X-Prize offered a $10 million prize to anyone who could achieve the impossible: launch a spacecraft into orbit, then do it again 2 weeks later. By winning the prize, the winning team created overnight an entirely new industry of low cost, private space flight.

The Breakthrough Energy Project aims to create a similar revolution for the energy sector. We will raise funding for a prize that incentivizes energy inventors to open-source their IP and share it with the world, drastically enabling our ability to fight climate change and lower atmospheric CO2. 

We are specifically looking for unconventional / exotic, new energy sources that 1) require no fuel and 2) create no pollution - producing abundant clean energy without limits. We know the technology exists, we just need to fund it.
If our goal interests you, please contact us.

Name: PLANETERA
Description: Planetera pioneers the development of cutting-edge space environment simulators, revolutionizing research and exploration capabilities for planetary science and space exploration. Our team of experts merges interdisciplinary expertise in geoscience, physics, engineering, and business development to create immersive and realistic simulation platforms. With a focus on innovation, versatility, and scalability, we strive to address the evolving needs of space exploration, enabling breakthrough discoveries and advancements in our understanding of the cosmos. Join us on our journey to unlock the mysteries of the universe and shape the future of space exploration.

Name: Kepler Aerospace GSaaS
Description:
Kepler Aerospace delivers Ground Station as a Service (GSaaS) through a global network of 35+ ground stations across six continents. We provide secure, automated, multi-band support for TT&C and high-throughput payload downlinks for missions in LEO, MEO, and GEO.

Our Kepler Unified Command Center (KUCC) architecture brings virtualized modems, IP-based routing, and automated pass scheduling together into a single, reliable ground network. Operators get seamless access without deploying hardware at any site.

Kepler supports satellites across V/UHF, S-, X- and Ka-band, offering high contact availability, fast data access, and mission-grade security with encrypted VPN tunnels and RBAC controls.

With experience supporting ISRO and multiple commercial missions, we offer LEOP support, RF testing, anomaly contacts, and 24/7 monitoring as part of an end-to-end ground segment.

Name: Serenity Space Technologies
Description:
Serenity Space Technologies provides advanced engineering solutions across industries, with specialized expertise in space systems and propulsion. We offer end-to-end services in systems engineering, mechanical and electrical design, integration and testing, software systems, nuclear power, and advanced R&D. While we specialize in propulsion and space systems, our team’s experience spans multiple industries, allowing us to bring innovative, reliable, and adaptable solutions to complex engineering challenges across sectors.
From mission-critical systems to breakthrough technology development, we bring precision, adaptability, and innovative thinking to every project.

Name: OrbitArch
Description: OrbitArch is revolutionizing space technology by integrating AI-driven autonomy with LiDAR systems to enhance satellite navigation and debris avoidance. Our innovative solutions enable real-time mapping, debris detection, and autonomous maneuvering, ensuring safer and more efficient satellite operations. Beyond space, our technology also has applications in disaster risk management and Earth observation. As a subsidiary of Skorbitron Private Limited, we are shaping the future of autonomous space systems.

Name: Farabi GIS and Remote Sensing Center
NL Space Summer School
Description:
During the week you will meet and learn about the various space programmes, developments and (research) activities at the Leiden Delft and Erasmus universities and go on a tour of space agencies, institutes and industry in South Holland, including (amongst others): ESA ESTEC, SRON, TNO, AirbusDS, SBIC, Galileo Reference Centre and of course the three universities themselves. Starting at the NL Space Campus in Noordwijk at ESA-ESTEC, the technical heart of ESA, you will tour the cities of Leiden and Delft before returning to Noordwijk. During the week, you will meet many experts, and learn about the basics of space technology and recent developments. This way you can work on your team’s challenge fully prepared. 

The week also includes multiple fun and non-space events. You will get to know the other participants as well as learn about each other’s strengths, knowledge and approaches to problem-solving. This is important because you will need to combine your strengths to work in interdisciplinary teams to solve actual space sector challenges that require insight and knowledge from different backgrounds. You will therefore grow many (soft)skills such as collaboration, communication and creative thinking. 

To stimulate working in a team and building a network, we have included teambuilding exercises, industry workshops and collaborative activities. These are combined with project team building, networking drinks, a project presentation and end the week with a networking barbecue.

Name: INAF - Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania
Description:
L'Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania è una delle strutture dell'Istituto Nazionale di Astrofisica.

Ricerca:
Le attività di ricerca all'Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania contemplano: fisica stellare; fisica solare; studio dei sistemi esoplanetari; astrochimica con misure in laboratorio; sviluppo di rivelatori per l'astrofisica; sviluppo di strumentazione di piano focale per telescopi a terra e dallo spazio; calcolo numerico e sviluppo di infrastrutture per il calcolo; cosmologia; radioastronomia; fisica delle alte energie. 

Divulgazione:
L'Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania promuove diverse attività di divulgazione scientifica in ambito astronomico e scientifico. Queste attività includono seminari, visite guidate al telescopio con osservazioni solari e stellari, ed eventi tematici (concorsi, mostre, conferenze pubbliche).

Didattica:
I ricercatori dell'Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania forniscono supporto alle attività didattiche dell'Università di Catania, per tesi di laurea, per il dottorato di ricerca, e per le attività connesse al Progetto Lauree Scientifiche. 
Sono inoltre impegnati nella formazione post-universitaria tramite l'erogazione di borse di studio.
L'Osservatorio Astrofisico di Catania è sede interregionale delle Olimpiadi Italiane di Astronomia e cura l'iniziativa per la Sicilia.

Name: Nano Buddy
Description:
At NanoBuddy, we are dedicated to advancing the field of nanotechnology to protect people from the harmful effects of everyday radiation. As part of Growww Global, our mission is to develop and provide high-quality products that are scientifically validated and certified by prestigious European institutions. Our innovative solutions are designed to enhance the quality of life by offering reliable protection against electromagnetic radiation from common electronic devices.

Our Product Lines Include:

1. Akai: The Akai product line employs cutting-edge nanotechnology to protect against harmful 4G and 5G radiation as well as radiation from devices like PCs, laptops, mobile phones, and Wi-Fi routers. Akai products are designed to be carried with you or placed near the source of radiation, providing protection within a specific radius. These versatile devices ensure users stay safe without compromising their lifestyle, offering maximum protection with minimal disruption.

2. NanoBuddy: Our flagship brand for the European market, NanoBuddy, integrates nanotechnology with innovative design for personalized protection. NanoBuddy products are practical, stylish stickers that are easy to apply directly to devices emitting radiation. They provide continuous protection from radiation wherever you go, ensuring robust defense against electromagnetic exposure in a user-friendly format.

3. Inti: Targeting the South American market, Inti focuses on global protection from radiation. Inti products, like NanoBuddy, are stickers that adhere directly to radiation-emitting devices, such as mobile phones and laptops. These products are rigorously tested and certified, offering peace of mind to users. Our technology not only shields from radiation but also promotes a healthier lifestyle through innovative solutions that are at the forefront of scientific advancement.

Our Goals for 2024:

- Market Testing and Expansion: We are actively testi

Name: Angstrom Designs
Description:
Angstrom Designs is a diverse group of engineers who focus on systems design for automated instrumentation, control, test and measurement. 

Angstrom Designs is currently focusing on the next generation of solar simulation technology for NASA and the aerospace industry, the programmable light emitting diode solar simulator, pLEDSS.  pLEDSS technology enables rapid calibration and automated acquisition of solar cell and string performance with more capability and flexibility than the current state of practice.

Angstrom Designs also conducts high-altitude balloon tests of solar cells and other space hardware in the near-space environment.

Name: RTU High Power Rocketry Team
Description: Established in 2020, we primarily develop rockets and their supporting technologies. Every major subsystem is developed in-house by students of various backgrounds. Thus far we have had two launches - VIP-1 in 2021 and Swift in 2022.

Name: HubBucket Inc
Description: HubBucket Inc is a Research and Development - R&D company,. We focus on Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics. Our R&D includes but is not limited to Space Sciences, Aerospace Engineering, Astronomy, Astrophysics, Computational Astrophysics, Plasma Astrophysics, Space Physics, Stellar Astronomy, Galatic Astronomy, Extragalactic Astronomy, Cosmology, Physical Cosmology, Chemical Cosmology, Planetary Science, Planetary Oceanography, Exoplanetology, Astrochemistry, Astrobiology, Space Biology, Space Chemistry, Astrobotany, Archaeoastronomy, Space Archaeology, Forensic Astronomy, Theoretical Astronomy, Photometry, Spectroscopy, Observational Astronomy (Ground-based and Space-based Observatories), Radio Astronomy, Submillmetre Astronomy, Infrared Astronomy, Optical Astronomy, High-Energy Astronomy, Cosmic Ray Astronomy, X-ray Astronomy, Gamma-ray Astronomy, Neutrino (Neutrinos) Astronomy, Gravitational Wave (Gravitons) Astronomy, Telecommunications, Satellite Communications, Artificial Intelligence - AI, Machine Learning - ML, Deep Learning - DL, Artificial Neural Networks - ANNs, Large Language Models - LLMs, Retrieval Augmented Generation - RAG, Computer Vision, Machine Vision, Robotics, Robot Operating System - ROS, Data Engineering, Data Science, Data Platforms, Intelligent Data Platforms, Medicine in Space, Space Technology, Quantum Computing, High Energy Physics - HEP / Particle Physics, and a lot more.

Name: Aardvark Sensing BV
Description:
Aardvark Sensing provides an autonomous robotic ecosystem designed for large-scale and long-running soil surveys. Our durable robots operate continuously for extended periods without human intervention, conducting systematic scans of agricultural land with navigational accuracy at the centimeter level and a continuous connection to the cloud.

Carrying various sensing payloads on board, including spectrometers, cosmic ray neutron detectors, acoustic sonar, ground-penetrating radar and more, each robot gathers essential data on soil characteristics. These measurements include surface roughness, soil compaction, soil moisture, mineral content, nutrient levels, sub-surface soil structure, and more.

Our primary goals are to improve soil classification models derived from Sentinel data and enhance the resolution of soil moisture assessments from SMOS satellites. By providing ground truth data to improve satellite data application, we leverage the vast availability of satellite data.

Name: Astroclubsohag
Description: (نشاط طلابي بجامعه سوهاج "نادى الفضاء والفلك Astronomy club sohag" ،يهدف إلى تنميه معرفة الطلاب بالفضاء والفلك واستكشاف ودراسة الظواهر عمليا )

Name: Luna Space
Description: Our vision is to grant cost-efficient access to the Moon for all mankind and build the future of the Lunar economy together.

Name: STAR-D
Description:
STAR-D is a student-led experiment aiming to study phagocytosis in real microgravity as part of the ESA–DLR REXUS/BEXUS programme. Using Dictyostelium discoideum as a robust analogue for human neutrophils, the project investigates how microgravity affects particle ingestion and phagosome maturation through a compact microfluidic and optical imaging setup.

Pre-selected for REXUS, STAR-D will be presented at the Selection Workshop in the Netherlands an important step toward launching the experiment on a sounding rocket.

Name: Starfront Observatories
Description: Welcome to Starfront Observatories, the largest remote telescope observatory in the world that's making space exploration more accessible for everyone! With over 400 telescope systems (and counting!) operational every night from our Bortle 1 location in Rockwood, Texas, Starfront is unlocking the possibilities of bringing together the largest remote imaging community in the world.

Name: Ram Accelerator Research Group
Description:
To enable human exploration and survivability in space, we must develop the means to deliver payloads to orbit at a greater capacity than rockets are capable of. The technology that can do this is the Ram Accelerator.

Consider that the mass of a rocket payload is less than 8%, whereas with a ram accelerator the mass of the payload could be 19-50% according to some estimates.
The purpose of the Ram Accelerator Research Program is to solve the remaining engineering problems needed to bring the ram accelerator to the forefront of payload delivery to orbit.

At present the United States has ram accelerator research facilities at the University of Washington in Seattle and Southeastern Louisiana University. However, additional institutions are needed to solve all the engineering problems and infrastructure issues associated with ram accelerators. Therefore, the Ram Accelerator Research Program has established three areas to focus efforts to expand ram accelerator research in the United States.

Name: SAR.Sense Geointelligence Lab
Description: At SAR.Sense, we harness cutting-edge Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology, seamlessly integrating it with diverse geospatial data to deliver precise, timely, and actionable insights. Our comprehensive, research-driven consultancy and specialized training programs are meticulously designed to empower clients across vital sectors, enabling them to make informed decisions and build resilience in the face of complex environmental and societal challenges.

Name: Beyond Exploration
Description:
Welcome to Beyond Exploration: Embark on a Journey of Exploration
Beyond Exploration is powered by a team of passionate explorers who are ardent believers in the power of knowledge
At Beyond Exploration, we believe that the thirst for knowledge has the power to propel us beyond the boundaries of what we know. Our blog is dedicated to technology, science, Space, and Personal Growth insights, and it’s designed to be a virtual expedition into the realms of innovation, exploration, and understanding.

Name: Team Ogrodoot - RUET Rover Team
Description:
Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of Team Ogrodoot, the space technology and research and development organization. We are a dedicated group of robotics enthusiasts from Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology (RUET), Bangladesh.

About Us:
Team Ogrodoot specializes in the field of space technology, research, and development. Our mission is to explore the realms of robotics in space exploration and contribute to the advancement of the field. We strive to create innovative solutions that push the boundaries of technology and inspire the next generation of engineers and scientists.

Achievements:
Over the years, Team Ogrodoot has proudly represented Bangladesh in prestigious international competitions, including:

-Indian Rover Challenge (IRC) 2018: Secured 6th place among 24 teams.
-European Rover Challenge (ERC) 2018: Achieved 25th place globally.
-European Rover Challenge (ERC) 2019: Attained 27th place globally.

Our Goals:

-Pushing the limits of space robotics through cutting-edge research and development.
-Inspiring and nurturing a passion for robotics and space exploration among students.
-Building a strong network of collaborators, sponsors, and industry professionals in the field.
-Representing Bangladesh in prestigious international competitions and showcasing our capabilities on a global stage.

Connect with Us:
Stay updated with our latest projects, achievements, and news by following our LinkedIn page. Join our growing network of robotics enthusiasts, professionals, and supporters. For inquiries or collaboration opportunities, feel free to reach out to us through LinkedIn or visit our socials:

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/team.ogrodoot
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/team_ogrodoot

Join us on this exciting journey as we explore the wonders of space through robotics!

Name: Vestion
Description:
Vestion is poised to revolutionize space access with the development, deployment, and operation of the world's first operational orbital tether, enabled by breakthrough advancements in carbon nanotube (CNT) composite materials. Current space access is characterized by prohibitive costs, limited payload capacities, and environmental concerns associated with traditional rocket launches. Our orbital tether offers a transformative solution, promising orders of magnitude reduction in launch costs, increased payload throughput, and a gentle, sustainable pathway to orbit.

Leveraging proprietary CNT manufacturing and integration techniques, Vestion will construct a terrestrial (or high-altitude platform) to Geosynchronous Earth Orbit (GEO) tether, serving as the foundational infrastructure for a new era of space exploration, industrialization, and tourism. The initial target market includes satellite operators, space agencies, and emerging in-space manufacturing companies, with future expansion into space tourism and resource extraction.

Name: Mothership Engineering
Description: Exploring the fabric of reality

Name: LabMGF - Laboratory of Geodetic and Photogrammetric Measurements
Description:
The Laboratory of Geodetic and Photogrammetric Measurements (LabMGF) is a research laboratory set up at the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering (DICA) at Politecnico di Milano.

Our research focuses on developing geodetic and photogrammetric techniques for civil and environmental applications.

We investigate state-of-the-art techniques for three-dimensional modelling and mapping, 3D/4D environmental and infrastructural monitoring, as well as for managing and sharing geospatial data with GIS platforms.

We employ topographic instrumentation, such as high-accurate multi-stations and geodetic GNSS receivers, combined with terrestrial laser scanners, UAVs and cameras and SAR for 3D measurements and modelling. At the same time, we investigate the usability of low-cost devices for large-scale applications.

Our research spans from glacier monitoring to infrastructure modelling, and bridge crack detection, from cadastral mapping to the use of Deep Learning algorithms and modern computer vision tools for advanced photographic reconstruction.

Name: NeuronSat
Description: Renseignement Militaire pour les Opérations Terrestres Complexes en zones de conflits

Name: n-LOTUSat
Description: n-LOTUSat CubeSat Project is a project initiated by undergraduate students of Istanbul Technical University to understand cubesat technology and work on the necessary satellite subsystems to produce a 1U satellite with a flight model ready in 2023.

Name: Darkmatter
Description:
At Darkmatter, we embody the fervor of innovation blended with the pride of Indian craftsmanship. Our endeavor is not merely about creating products; it's about crafting legacies in the drone and industrial control systems domain. Our meticulously engineered flight controllers, BRAHMA F4, BRAHMA F7, and BRAHMA PX, along with DM-45 DSHOT ESCs and compact 4-in-1 ESC, are milestones of our ingenuity in drone technology. The VAYU drone and DRISTI, our analog FPV goggles, are epitomes of affordable yet advanced aerial solutions, while VAANI, our Radio Controller, ensures seamless command at your fingertips.

As we navigate the certification journey with Betaflight for our BRAHMA F4 and F7 Flight Controllers, we're not just aligning with global standards, but elevating the Made in India narrative on a global stage.

We're also immersing in extensive research towards developing PLC control boards, marking our foray into diverse industrial applications.

We are Darkmatter, a beacon of Indian technological prowess, tirelessly working to etch India’s name onto the global drone and industrial technology map. Our narrative is steeped in the ethos of self-sustainability and a robust Make in India initiative. Each stride we take is a step towards a future where Indian technology resonates with quality, innovation, and global leadership.

Join us as we fuel the Disruptive Momentum, propelling towards a horizon brimming with technological breakthroughs.

Name: Terra Motion Canada
Description: Terra Motion Canada is a leading provider of land subsidence measurements. Our disruptive technology is based upon the big data analysis of satellite radar data.

Name: Helix Space
Description:
Helix Space has two divisions:
(1) Services: We help companies in the realms of Defence, Security and Resilience obtain public & private funding, develop innovative products & services that will boost their sales, and deliver the EDF commitments.
(2) R&D: We want to use existing and future research infrastructure on Earth’s orbit to develop and commercialize biomolecules, biomaterials, and nutritional supplements for preventive and therapeutic management of human and veterinary diseases on Earth.

Since 2022, Helix Space is a Private Research Institution accredited by the Ministry of Economy of Luxembourg.

Name: AERO-ROCKETRY UPM
Description:
Aero-Rocketry UPM is a leading student-driven aerospace organization committed to advancing rocketry, satellite technology, and aerospace engineering through technical excellence and industry collaboration. Our mission is to equip the next generation of aerospace professionals with the skills, knowledge, and experience necessary to drive innovation in the field.

We specialize in high-powered rocketry, satellite systems, and the integration of machine learning in aerospace applications. Our members engage in rigorous technical projects, including propulsion system development, aerodynamics optimization, autonomous flight control, communication module development and space systems engineering. By participating in international engineering competitions, research initiatives, and industrial partnerships, we bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world aerospace challenges.

Beyond technical development, Aero-Rocketry UPM is dedicated to education and outreach. Through mentorship programs, technical workshops, and engagement with secondary school students, we inspire future aerospace engineers while fostering a collaborative and inclusive community.

Driven by innovation and a passion for exploration, Aero-Rocketry UPM is shaping the future of student-based aerospace leadership platform. Join us as we push the boundaries of engineering and technology.

Name: Aerosat launch Program
Description: Aerosat Launch Program is Satellite Launch Project designed by a group of Undergraduate students. Our Mission is to send a satellite to high altitude in atmosphere and study the atmospheric data. We have developed a satellite less with sensors and modules. This Project includes acquisition of atmospheric data, study the stratosphere, capture images.

Name: HYTERA-X (M) SDN BHD
Description:
HYTERA X SDN BHD is a cutting-edge technology company specializing in UAV-based intelligent systems and applications. We offer a platform-as-a-service that helps businesses, governments, and educational institutions navigate the challenges and expenses of drone operations. 

As a leading startup in Asia, we are successfully delivered various UAV projects in Malaysia, offering procurement support, training, and advisory services on drone-related systems, including counter-drone software platforms. Their expertise spans inspection, surveillance, and other operational requirements, making them a valuable partner in the drone industry.

Name: Akdeniz UAV Team
Description:
Arge İHA Takımı 2015 senesinde sn. Dr. Öğr. Üyesi Yalçın Albayrak’ ın danışmanlık yaptığı Arge Topluluğu adı altında kurulmuştur.

Vizyonumuz, bilimsel ve teknolojik alandaki liderliğimiz ile  insansız hava araçları alanında öncü olmayı hedefleyen bir takım oluşturmak ve bu teknolojileri kullanarak topluma fayda sağlamaktır.

 Misyonumuz ise sürekli olarak bilgi ve deneyimi artırmak, üyeler arasında işbirliğini teşvik etmek, takımımızı ulusal ve uluslararası platformlarda başarıyla temsil ederek teknolojik yeniliklere katkıda bulunmak ve insansız hava araçları teknolojilerini toplumun ihtiyaçlarına uygun şekilde geliştirmek ve sunmaktır.

 Bu vizyon ve misyon doğrultusunda, bilim, teknoloji, ekip çalışması ve etik değerlere bağlı kalarak;  üniversitemizdeki İHA laboratuvarını geliştirmek, ülkemizdeki ve yurtdışındaki İHA yarışlarında üniversitemizi temsil etmek ve ardımızdan gelen parlak gençlere sağlam bir altyapı oluşturmaktır.

Name: ASTRA Lab
Description: Besides following us here, you can also follow our Instagram, where our students post about their work and day-to-day experience: https://www.instagram.com/astralabucf/

Name: METASAT Project
Description: Launched in January 2023, the European Commission-funded project METASAT will provide a holistic and modular model-based framework to design and test software modules that target open architecture hardware, high-performance computing platforms for the space and aviation domain.

Name: DARK Cosmology Research Unit
Description: DARK is a leading research unit at the Niels Bohr Institute, University of Copenhagen. Our group of 30+ researchers, including 9 PhD students, collaborates on high-level astrophysics, emphasizing gender diversity in science. We are affiliated with top observational facilities, like ESO, NASA, and the Vera C. Rubin Observatory, and engage in multiple large-scale collaborations, including projects like YSE and LSST. Our focus extends beyond exploration, emphasizing training, teaching, and public outreach about the mysteries of the dark Universe. Join us in exploring the cosmos!

Name: APEx
Description:
APEx provides easy access to ESA's Earth observation application outcomes for the EO community. With a range of services and tools APEx simplifies the transition of algorithms into operational services and encourage the incorporation of current cloud-based EO services and technologies. 

Discover the full ESA APEx offer at https://apex.esa.int/ and follow us to stay informed!

Name: Geoportal.tech
Description:
Are you ready to take your geospatial career to the next level? Look no further than the Geoportal!

Our platform offers a newsletter with the latest news, global event calendar and job opportunities all in one place.

Name: Zero-G AstroLab
Description:
Our mission is to safeguard our planet from potential asteroid threats. We are committed to utilising advanced technology and scientific research to prevent any potential hazards that may arise from asteroids.

Our goal is to ensure the safety and well-being of our planet and its inhabitants.

Name: NASA Space Apps - Beirut
Description: The NASA International Space Apps Challenge (Space Apps) is an international hackathon for coders, scientists, designers, storytellers, makers, builders, technologists, and others in cities around the world.

Name: UHU Technologies
Description:
WHAT WE DO:
We protect GPS using spatial processing techniques that can't be spoofed.

WHY WE DO IT:
We are passionate about solving emerging threats to critical infrastructure and the national defense.

ABOUT:
UHU Technologies is a privately-owned engineering research and development company that designs products to detect and mitigate GPS spoofing and jamming.

We have deep roots in signal intelligence and radio location with decades of experience producing high performance hardware. We are applying our expertise to the protection of GPS receivers for critical applications using techniques borrowed from the Signals Intelligence (SIGINT) world. Our patented techniques assure the integrity of GPS through spatial processing; for each signal we receive, we compute the angle of arrival and compare it to the known satellite location for that signal. Angle of arrival is the only signal feature that cannot be spoofed. Through additional signal processing, we spatially null (subtract) any spoofers or jammers and mitigate the threat.

Name: Astralbeam Organization
Description: Astral Beam's mission is to empower women and inspire the next generation of dreamers through space science education in underdeveloped countries. Together, we can nurture young minds and unlock the potential for a brighter future.

Name: XRTech Group
Description:
When seconds matter, we deliver eyes in the sky.

XRTech gives frontline teams the speed and clarity to stop small problems from turning into national crises.

From wildfires and border breaches to citywide blackouts, we help governments and enterprises act in real time—when every second counts.

In 2024:

→ 160,000+ drone operations
→ 20,000+ rapid-response deployments
→ 65+ battery-swapping docks installed
→ 50TB+ of live aerial data collected

Proudly building from Dubai.
Powering real-time intelligence across the globe.

Three Technologies. One Real-Time Response Platform.

Satellite Intelligence at Scale

→ 60+ satellites + 30 AI models = instant aerial awareness
→ Detects crop failure, pollution, and infrastructure risks

Through Spacecademy Society’s dynamic outreach, we have conducted astronomy awareness sessions in nearly 30 schools across Karachi, searching for the next generation of space enthusiasts. Our impactful sessions have reached top international schools, including Beaconhouse and The Merit School, where we had an amazing time engaging with students, fueling their curiosity, and expanding their knowledge of the universe! 

But our mission doesn't stop there! We bring the cosmos to life through solar observations and night sky explorations in collaboration with schools, giving students hands-on experience with telescopes. These thrilling activities have sparked a passion for astrophotography, pushing students to explore the endless frontiers of space! 

Beyond schools, Spacecademy Society has taken center stage at major science festivals!  We proudly conducted space science shows at events organized by the Thar Educational Alliance, with support from UNICEF. Our journey has taken us to Sukkur, Sujawal, and Karachi, where we performed live space science demonstrations, captivating audiences and creating a wave of excitement for space science and cosmology!

Our mission is clear—to inspire, educate, and shape the future of space exploration in Pakistan!

Description:
Our vision is to have answers to the questions of tomorrow. This includes to be on top of current technological development especially in optical physics. Strong investment in research and out-of-the-box-thinking are key factors in the constant evolution of MUnique Technology to find the best solution for customers´needs.

Our DORT (Dynamic Optical Ranging & Timing) system is an optical pulsed device which offers a new high precise distance measurement. It has performances some orders of magnitudes better compared to other state of the art technologies like LIDAR. This system is originally developed for space application but can be used on ground as well. 
Thanks to the compact and robust design, DORT can be implemented in very small satellites, so called CubeSats (New Space).

In the terrestrial domain, DORT is also used for a wide range of applications, e.g. structural health monitoring of infrastructure structures, large volume metrology, vibration & shock measurements, and strain measurements.

Name: Logispace
Description:
Logispace is a UK-based space venture developing a re-entry capable space capsule that delivers Microgravity-as-a-Service. Our mission is to democratize access to space-based manufacturing and research by providing an affordable, reusable and rapid-turnaround platform.

Each Logispace capsule offers a pressurised, thermally controlled environment for cutting-edge applications in pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, biotechnology and equipment validation. Missions range from a few hours to months in orbit, with fast sample recovery that enables high-throughput R&D cycles at a fraction of the cost of ISS access.

Beyond science, Logispace is pioneering strategic and defense applications. Our capsule constellation can be loaded with critical resources and deorbited on-demand, enabling rapid delivery to ships, offshore installations and active naval theaters - reducing delays and opening new commercial, security and operational opportunities.

Backed by the UK Space Agency Accelerator and supported by partners across academia and industry, Logispace is building the future of affordable, reusable and responsive space logistics.

Name: SPACE Lab
Description:
The Space exPloration And spaceCraft Engineering Laboratory (SPACE Lab), led by Professor Paula do Vale Pereira, is part of the Aerospace, Physics, and Space Sciences Department at the Florida Institute of Technology.

Our research focuses on hardware development for space exploration, mainly through the use of small satellites, CubeSats, and cryobots (probes for the exploration of Ocean Worlds).

The research laboratory space is equipped with a large optical table (4ft x 8ft), workbenches, and testing equipment, such as power supplies, oscilloscopes, and function generators. We are currently working on installing a cleanroom and a TVAC chamber in our lab too.

Through the university, we have access to multiple workstations for professional engineering design software such as AGI STK, PTC Creo, ANSYS Thermal Desktop, and MATLAB. We also have access to multiple prototyping facilities equipped with FDM, SLA, and SLS 3D printers, laser cutters, waterjet cutters, milling machines of 3 and 4 axes, and manual and CNC lathes.

Besides following us here, you can also follow our Instagram, where our students post about their work and day-to-day experience: https://www.instagram.com/spacelabfit/

Name: EXOM
Description:
Exom offers advanced AI infrastructure tailored specifically for space missions and space tourism. It provides psychological assessment tools that monitor and evaluate the mental state and well-being of astronauts and crew members throughout their mission, utilizing cutting-edge AI algorithms and machine learning techniques.

The AI infrastructure continuously monitors and analyzes psychological data, enabling the early detection of potential mental health issues. This allows for timely interventions and support, mitigating risks associated with mental health and ensuring optimal crew functioning.

Exom's solution goes beyond assessment and intervention. It includes cognitive support systems that assist astronauts and crew members in making informed judgments during critical situations. By leveraging AI algorithms to analyze complex data sets, these decision-making tools provide real-time insights and enhance the overall decision-making capabilities of the crew.

Description:

The project goal is the development, integration and validation of lunar water extraction and purification technologies for in-situ propellant and consumables production for future space exploration.  LUWEX will push the research and development of In-Situ Resource Utilization advancing the state-of-the- art.

Description:
Space LiinTech | Pioneering Space Medicine in Microgravity
We are revolutionizing space medicine and biomanufacturing through cutting-edge microgravity and space-based research. Our mission is to unlock new frontiers in drug discovery, regenerative medicine, and biomedical innovation by leveraging the unique environment of space.

 What We Do
We develop microgravity research and production platforms that accelerate breakthroughs in biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, and life sciences. Our expertise spans:
•Microgravity Drug Development: Enhancing drug efficacy and stability through crystallization in space.
•Space-based Biomanufacturing: Producing advanced biomaterials and therapeutics under optimal conditions.
•Regenerative Medicine & Stem Cell Research: Investigating cellular behavior and organoid growth in microgravity.

 Our Technology
•Microgravity Research Platforms – Conducting advanced biological and pharmaceutical experiments aboard satellites and space stations.
•Drop Tower Testing Facilities – Developing prototype experiments for space-based applications using vertical shaft drop towers.
•Autonomous Biomanufacturing Systems – Innovating scalable production of protein therapeutics, regenerative tissues, and cell therapies.

 Why Space?
The microgravity environment offers unique advantages in biomedical research, allowing for:
•More stable and higher-quality protein crystallization for drug development.
•Stem cell and tissue engineering advancements for regenerative medicine.
•Accelerated scientific discoveries that impact both space and terrestrial medicine.

 Looking Ahead
As commercial spaceflight, low Earth orbit (LEO) research, and private space stations expand, Space LiinTech is pioneering the future of space medicine. Our goal is to develop cutting-edge solutions that benefit both astronauts and patients on Earth.

 Contact us for research collaborations and partnership opportunities

Name: IGNIS - Infrared Geological Nano Imaging System
Description:
IGNIS (Infrared Geological Nano Imaging System) is an ongoing project led by a group of students at the University of Naples “Federico II”. The main goal is to develop a 3U CubeSat by adapting a Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) thermal camera to the space environment, combining an educational and an Earth Observation program. 
The mission’s purpose is to acquire thermal infrared remote sensing data for mapping the surface temperature of an area of interest. The target of this work is the Campi Flegrei area, a vast volcanic territory located northwest of the city of Naples. By adapting the COTS thermal camera and exploiting the multiple advantages of a miniaturized satellite, IGNIS may be the perfect combination of education and innovation. It allows the students to put into practice their theoretical knowledge by designing innovative and low-cost space technologies. The success of the mission should prove the reliability and feasibility of COTS applications in this field, to ensure future developments. For this reason, IGNIS aims to be the pioneer of a series of dedicated, cost-effective missions using COTS instrumentation to face local issues, such as the risk-prone Campi Flegrei area, with high quality and frequency of collected data.

Description:
Uttarakhand Space Application Centre (USAC) is the nodal agency in Uttarakhand state for space-technology related activities and has the mandate to employ space-technology for the benefit of the state and its people. It was constituted as an autonomous organization in 2005, under the Department of Science & Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The Government of Uttarakhand is acutely concerned about the states vulnerability to the consequences of the fragility of Himalayas and the inexorable intensification of climate change. It is very keen not to let these constraints come in the way of ensuring that the last person of the state gets access to a decent quality of life, without compromising on its responsibility as a trustee of our environment for future generations. To overcome the daunting challenges that Uttarakhand faces due to its formidable terrain and the fragility of Himalayas, enhanced use of technology, especially space technology, is of dire necessity. Hence, the prime objective of USAC is to explore and deploy geospatial technology for the development of the state. Consequently, USAC boasts of a close relationship with the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) that has benefited the state immensely and constantly strives to further develop and intensify this productive association.

USAC works in crucial areas, such as Natural Resource Management, Water Resource Management, Glacier Studies, Environmental Monitoring, Land use & Urban planning, Disaster Mitigation, Web-based School Information System, Health Information System etc. Notable among the programmes that it has been implementing on an ongoing basis are State Natural Resource Management System (SNRMS), Forecasting Agriculture using Space, Agrometeorlogy & Land based observations (FASAL) programme, Coordinated programme on Horticulture Assessment and MAnagement using geoiNfromatics (CHAMAN), Development of National Forest Fire Danger Rating, among others.

Name: StarDrive inc
Description:
StarDrive Inc. is pioneering the future of space travel with the StarDrive Mass Relay System, a groundbreaking approach to interplanetary transportation. Our innovative propulsion technology leverages superconducting magnetic rings, strategically positioned in orbit, to enable spacecraft acceleration through Lorentz force interactions. This system eliminates the need for onboard propellant, allowing spacecraft to reach unprecedented speeds efficiently and sustainably.
The StarDrive Mass Relay offers transformative capabilities, cutting transit times to Mars by tenfold—making journeys that currently take months achievable in just 30-60 days. By accelerating spacecraft without traditional fuel, StarDrive dramatically reduces mission costs and expands payload capacities, creating new possibilities for deep-space exploration, defense logistics, and commercial ventures such as space tourism and asteroid mining.
Our team brings extensive expertise in advanced propulsion systems, material science, and high-temperature superconductivity. As we advance from successful simulations and scaled prototype testing, we are committed to refining the system's reliability and scalability. Our network-based infrastructure design allows StarDrive to adapt to various mission profiles, from rapid satellite deployment to cislunar operations, offering unmatched versatility in space transportation.
With an estimated addressable market of $420 billion by 2030, StarDrive is positioned to be a leader in next-generation space infrastructure. Our work aligns with the strategic goals of space exploration, enhancing humanity’s reach beyond Earth and supporting a sustainable, thriving future in space.

- Competitions and Challenges: Participate in fun competitions, astrophotography challenges, and quiz nights designed to test and enhance your astronomical knowledge.

- Field Trips and Excursions: Embark on field trips to observatories, planetariums, and other astronomical landmarks, providing firsthand exposure to professional astronomical facilities.

Join us in exploring the mysteries of the cosmos, fostering a deeper understanding of the universe, and forming lasting friendships among fellow astronomy enthusiasts. Whether your interest lies in the science, beauty, or sheer wonder of space, the Astronomy Club invites you to be a part of our cosmic journey!

Name: UASPACE - Student Organization
Description:
UASPACE strives to design, test and deploy space technologies through a culture of collaborative development, fostering engineering excellence and business leadership in students at The University of Alabama and the surrounding community through STEM-related outreach.

UASPACE's BAMA-1 CubeSat was selected for the NASA CubeSat Launch Initiative in 2020 and was aboard the Astra LV-0008 vehicle for the failed attempt on February 10, 2022. BAMA-2 will be a re-build and re-flight effort of BAMA-1. Due to the unfortunate mission failure during launch, NASA has given us a second opportunity to build and launch our BAMA-2 CubeSAT through the NASA CubeSat Launch Initiative. We aim to have a fully built and functional satellite by May 2023.

BAMA-2 is a technology demonstration mission that will conduct a flight demonstration of a drag sail module by rapidly deorbiting the satellite. Spacecraft equipped with drag sail technology will be able to reliably and rapidly deorbit, thus reducing space debris and the risk to operational satellites, space stations, and crewed vehicles.

Jorge Toledo-Chacón (Jatch), CEO founder.

Terraqia builds public-access space infrastructure.

We develop autonomous satellites, modular robotics, and AI-coordinated systems to make space usable for people—not just governments and billionaires.

Our approach is simple and compliance-first:

We don’t sell tokens.

We don’t promise profits.

We don’t issue equity.

Instead, we offer Mission Access Pass (MAP) to real technologies—starting with clean orbital data and autonomous debris mitigation through our Spacenet satellite network.

This is space infrastructure built the right way:

#SpaceIsForUs

We're not speculating on the future.

Description: The power of data in imaging is transformative and has become a must-have advantage to government and enterprise alike. The goal of hyperspectral imaging is to obtain the spectrum for each pixel in the image of a scene, with the purpose of finding objects, identifying materials, or detecting processes. Hyperspectral imaging is a quantum leap in imaging data analysis beyond the limitations of panchromatic and multispectral imaging alternatives. A space-based hyperspectral imaging system has the additional advantages of unfettered global coverage with repeat view and change detection capabilities.

Name: Akilaris
Description:
Akilaris is a Space Intelligence Platform that provides autonomous AI-powered space traffic management.

The company is addressing the critical challenge of space safety, with over 5 million objects orbiting Earth at 17,500 mph, posing collision risks that could trigger cascading failures (Kessler Syndrome).
Key Points:
•	Mission: Transform reactive space surveillance into proactive, autonomous traffic management
•	Solution: AI agents that predict and prevent collisions before they occur, operating like "air traffic control for space" but with superhuman capabilities
•	Technology: Real-time tracking, predictive analytics, and automated collision avoidance with sub-second threat assessment
•	Partnership: Selected ESA BIC Northern Germany 
•	Market: Targeting the €50B+ space economy, serving satellite operators from startups to mega-constellations
•	Impact: Aims to achieve zero collisions for managed assets, 50% reduction in fuel costs for avoidance maneuvers, and 99.999% uptime for critical space services
•	Location: Bremen

The platform democratizes access to space safety infrastructure, enabling the sustainable growth of the space economy while preventing catastrophic orbital debris cascades.

Name: LUNAR EXCHANGE
Description:
As humanity ventures beyond Earth, establishing a sustainable lunar economy is critical for long-term space exploration and off-world colonization. The Moon is more than just a celestial body—it is a gateway to interplanetary expansion and a treasure trove of resources. Water ice, rare metals, helium-3, and structural materials like aluminum and titanium will serve as the foundation for fuel production, space manufacturing, and deep-space missions.

However, accessing these resources presents significant technological and economic challenges. Traditional funding models for space exploration rely on government budgets and institutional investment, limiting private sector participation. To overcome these barriers, Lunar Exchange.Space introduces a decentralized trading system that allows anyone to invest in and trade real-world lunar resources using blockchain technology and asset tokenization.

What is Lunar Exchange.Space?

Lunar Exchange.Space is a next-generation marketplace for lunar resource trading and investment. By integrating blockchain-based asset tokenization, smart contracts, and decentralized finance (DeFi), the platform enables users to buy, sell, and trade lunar-derived commodities, finance space mining missions, and participate in the emerging lunar economy.

Key Features of Lunar Exchange.Space:
 Lunar Commodity Market: Trade tokenized versions of oxygen, water ice, helium-3, titanium, and more.
 Smart Contracts: Enable secure, automated, and tamper-proof transactions for resource trading and mission financing.
 Mission Tokenization: Invest in real-world lunar mining expeditions, structured as Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs).
 Futures and Options Trading: Hedge against market fluctuations by locking in future commodity prices.
 Decentralized and Transparent: Built on Ethereum and blockchain-based protocols, ensuring trustless, secure operations.

Name: Northeastern SEDS
Description:
The Northeastern University Chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space, SEDS, is a student group on campus which offers students the opportunity to indulge in their passion for space by conducting capstone level research and design before their senior year. We are made up of over 200 students in the colleges of Engineering, Science, Computer Science, Business, Social Studies, Art and Design, and Humanities. 

The Northeastern chapter of SEDS was founded in Fall of 2018. We are the only group on campus that works in both a professional and social environment focusing on space exploration and technologies. 

This year, we have 6 active project teams. NU Rover, SUITS (Spacesuit User Interface Technologies for Students), NEST (Networked Exploration Swarm Toolkit), and Lunabotics are each preparing for and competing in their respective annual NASA competitions. Name: Microsatellites and Space Microsystems Laboratory
Description:
Research activities of the Microsatellites and Space Microsystems Laboratory include:

- MicroPropulsion Systems
Started with the experience gathered through the development of the cold-gas μ-propulsion system of ALMASat-1 microsatellite, the research activity is now focused towards the development of warm gas high test peroxide monopropellant thrusters. 

- Autonomous GNSS Navigation Systems
The focus is on the study and development of accurate and cost effective solutions for autonomous real-time orbit determination on board of micro- and nano-satellite platforms.

- Spacecraft Operations
Oriented towards the application of artificial intelligence to enhance operations, research topics are: space traffic management, satellite monitoring and control, and autonomous operations. Thanks to ESEO project, our laboratory has its own ground segment facility where to gain hands on experience and experiment new techniques in spacecraft operations.

- Attitude Determination and Control Systems
Pointing accuracy and stability is critical in S/C operation. Algorithms for attitude control and determination of nanosatellites rely on limited hardware and have to meet specific requirements. As part of our laboratory work, advanced control topics are employed to guarantee a higher level of performance in nanosatellites operation.

Description:
At Amino Five, we’ve solved one of the most persistent challenges in functional formulation: how to stabilise amino acids and other actives in liquid without encapsulation, chemicals, bitterness, or cloudiness.

Our patented stabilisation technology transforms unstable amino acids and powdered bioactives into shelf-stable, water-clear solutions – ready for immediate commercial use in beverages, pharmaceuticals, veterinary products, and agriculture.

Unlike traditional methods that rely on encapsulation, emulsifiers, or synthetic additives, our approach maintains purity, taste neutrality, and visual clarity – all while extending shelf life significantly.

The result? A new standard for liquid-ready actives:
	•	No bitterness
	•	No slime
	•	No artificial carriers
	•	Just crystal-clear, high-performance amino acids in solution

With applications across sports nutrition, health, and high-value crop science, Amino Five is enabling the next generation of clean-label, functional products – and partnering with leading brands to bring them to market.

If you’re formulating with amino acids or functional powders and want to move into clean, stable liquid formats – we’re ready to help.

Name: Simon Fraser University (SFU) Aerospace Physiology Laboratory (APL)
Description:
The Aerospace Physiology Laboratory conducts research related to human physiological responses and adaptations to Terrestrial and Space environments. The Director, Dr. Blaber, has a special interest in modeling cerebral blood flow regulation under various environmental conditions. The lab conducts research related to understanding human physiological responses to gravitational stress before, during, and after exposure to weightlessness, hypoxia and exercise. Located in the Department of Biomedical Physiology and Kinesiology at Simon Fraser University the laboratory has cardiovascular, respiratory, blood flow, tilt table, lower body negative pressure and exercise testing capabilities. The lab frequently utilizes European Space Agency facilities for Bed Rest, Parabolic Flight, and Short-arm Human Centrifuge studies.

Research in the lab is focused on an integrated approach to the problem of syncope and falls, as well as Epigenetics and Gut-Brain Axis' influences in isolation and ultimately in spaceflight. This has included the investigation of several homeostatic mechanisms related to the maintenance of sufficient delivery of oxygen to the brain during orthostatic stress, and will lead to epigenetic, gut-brain axis research to determine influences during analog studies among others.

HRPG has a history and culture of giving students a unique hands-on experience, as well as completing ambitious projects at a breakneck pace. Our members have leveraged their experiences to land jobs at companies such as SpaceX, Northrop Grumman, Ball Aerospace, and Relativity Space. Through HRPG, Harvard undergraduates learn about leadership, ownership, iterative design, and large-scale project execution. With our “Phase 0” initial rocket engine, we proved that we can safely and efficiently build and test fully-functional rocket engines. With our upcoming “Phase 1” rocket engine, we plan to elevate our capabilities into a thrust class that will allow us to compete on a national level.

Name: HARTS
Description:
We are HARTS (Hybrid Atmospheric Re-entry Thermal Shield), a team from the Faculty of Aeronautics at the Technical University of Košice, proudly representing Slovakia as the first Slovak team to participate in the REXUS 35/36 program.

We aim to develop a hybrid heatshield that combines the passive protection of ablative materials with active cooling. The hybrid heat shield makes use of the structural strength and repeatable usability of composite heat shields and compensates for their high conductivity and lack of ablation with helium cooling used to pressurize fuel tanks.  This technology is designed to improve spacecraft reusability and reduce the cost of space access while providing a safe and reliable protection from the elements.

We plan to launch the heat shield on an ejectable experimental capsule on a REXUS sounding rocket to an altitude of ~85 km in March 2026 which will provide us with important insights into the function in real-world atmospheric descent conditions, allowing us to improve hybrid shield technology.

Name: PANORAMA Project
Description:
PANORAMA will significantly enhance the quality and accuracy of EO products for supporting data-driven decision making in 3 priority areas of GEO (climate action, disaster risk reduction, sustainable development), and 7 policy areas of the European Green Deal (climate, energy, environment and oceans, agriculture, transport, research and innovation, the new European Bauhaus). The next generation of Copernicus Sentinels and EUMETSAT MTG and EPS-SG satellite constellation provides a wide range of observations for advanced monitoring of the atmosphere.

PANORAMA aims to multiply the value of this information by realising multi-sensor synergies to achieve advanced products, along with improved applications on atmospheric composition and NWP (e.g., motion-estimation of aerosols & clouds, fast radiative transfer calculations, top-down emission estimations through ML/AI, data assimilation, and new physical parameterisations).

The products and applications will be tested and fine-tuned in 6 pilot demonstrators to assess the impact of the new EO data on the representation and prediction of extreme weather, floods, energy, and air pollution. In its final phase, PANORAMA will materialise 3 pre-operational integrated applications over the Copernicus DIAS, to demonstrate the use of the project’s results in seamless climate-weather and environmental European services, but also to make available the tools to other users and stakeholders (e.g., integrated applications on fire-smoke and volcanic ash dispersion for CAMS and EMS, and solar energy nowcasting for the NextSense EuroGEO service).

Contributions to the C3S/CMEMS Copernicus services and the Destination Earth are also foreseen. The dissemination, communication, and exploitation plan will accelerate the adoption of the project’s results by the wider community and the commercial sector to ensure impact maximisation.

Name: Astraeus Systems
Description:
Astraeus provides specialized services for the telecommunications and space industry sectors, focusing on engineering design, data systems, mission operations, and training. In engineering design, we develop critical subsystems such as imaging systems, sensor arrays, and payload modules for space applications.

We also build advanced data processing and analysis systems that integrate inputs from imaging detectors, spectrometers, RF sensors, and navigation instruments. By applying AI/ML algorithms for image classification, spectrogram analysis, and anomaly detection, we transform raw mission data into structured knowledge, enabling real-time decision-making and maximizing scientific return.

Through scientific support, training, education, and outreach, we empower researchers, engineers, and students with skills in data analysis and data interpretation, instrument operation and mission operational planning.

Our mission is to establish a global hub for up-to-date space news, scientific discoveries, and aeronautics research.

Looking forward, we envision expanding our business into direct involvement in space exploration, pioneering scientific discoveries, and advancing aeronautics research to new heights.

Name: Lagrangian
Description:
Lagrangian is an interdisciplinary research and advocacy organization dedicated to advancing the future of space exploration. Through rigorous analysis, thought leadership, and strategic partnerships, we explore the intersection of space technology, policy, economics, and culture—shaping sustainable, equitable, and innovative space industries for humanity’s benefit.

Our Work includes:

Research - Cutting-edge studies on space policy, ethics, infrastructure, and societal impacts.
Advocacy - Influencing decision-makers to foster responsible space development.
Consulting - Advising governments, NGOs, and private sector leaders on space-related challenges.
Publishing - Disseminating insights via reports, white papers, and our flagship weekly publication, Lagrangian.

Why It Matters:
Space is no longer the domain of a few nations—it’s a global ecosystem with profound implications for security, sustainability, and human progress. We bridge the gap between innovation and governance, ensuring space exploration benefits all.

Join the Conversation:
Follow us for research updates, event announcements, and collaborations. Together, we can build a thoughtful future for space.

 Learn more: https://www.lagrangian.space/

Quick Facts on the SpaceTech Masters in Space Systems and Business Engineering of TU-Graz.
    Duration: 4 semesters (22 months)
    Final degree: MSc - Master of Science (Continuing Education)
    ECTS credits: 120
    Language of instruction: English

Description: The Cricket Rearing, Collection, and Transformation System (CRCTS) is an innovative food production device in development by a group of current and former McGill University Engineering students in the context of the Canadian Space Agency's Deep Space Food Challenge. Our device allows for live crickets to be reared, harvested, and converted to a protein-rich powder for human consumption in tasty recipes. Name: MVIPER - Magnetohydrodynamic Vortex-Inducing Photobioreactor Experiment
Description:
The Magnetohydrodynamic Vortex-Inducing Photobioreactor Experiment (MVIPER) investigates the angular speed, flow characteristics, and bubble-moving ability of a magnetohydrodynamically induced vortex in an algae-suitable nutrient solution within a circular small-scale photobioreactor geometry. 

The experiment is intended for the ESA Academy Experiments Programme 2024/25 and operates under the support and supervision of the Professorship of Human Spaceflight Technology of the Technical University of Munich.

J&P holds SBA-certified SDB [Native American owned], Women’s Business Enterprise [WBE], and Texas Historically Underutilized Business [HUB] certifications.

Name: Pacific Aerospace Launch & Spaceport
Description:
Pacific Aerospace Launch & Spaceport (PALS) is leading efforts to establish Washington State’s first horizontal take-off and landing (HTOL) launch capability expanding access to orbit and advancing the Pacific Northwest’s growing space economy. PALS is a long-term, strategic initiative to strengthen Washington's strong position as a key hub for commercial, civil, and defense space operations. 

While the initial phase focuses on HTOL operations, consideration is also being given to a future phase featuring vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) capability to broaden mission flexibility and support a wider range of orbital missions. 

By developing local launch infrastructure, PALS strengthens supply chains, reduces logistics costs, and creates high-value jobs — driving growth and ensuring Washington plays a defining role in the next era of space access and innovation.

Name: Orbtrix
Description:
Orbtrix Space builds satellites and mission systems designed to operate intelligently in orbit. We develop spacecraft where autonomy is not an add on but a core design principle, integrating onboard intelligence, mission operations, and system level decision making from the ground up.

Our mission is to build satellites that can plan, adapt, and respond to mission conditions with minimal dependence on continuous ground control. By combining spacecraft engineering with intelligent onboard and ground systems, we aim to reduce operational complexity while increasing mission efficiency and reliability.

Our vision is a future where space missions are scalable, resilient, and operational by default. As missions grow more complex and constellations become larger, Orbtrix is focused on enabling space systems that can sustain operations independently and make informed decisions in real time.

We are building the foundation for smarter space missions where satellites are not just controlled from Earth, but are capable of operating on their own.

Name: SCHUMANN - Horizon EU
Description:
The SCHUMANN Project (co-funded by the European Union) researches new scalable satellite platform concepts and building blocks to increase satellite modularisation.

Satellite life extension, refueling, in-space assembly and reconfiguration, and in-space recycling are exciting prospects in the field of NewSpace applications. In this context, SCHUMANN strives to enhance the pillars of the future space ecosystem by showcasing the potential for developing functional satellite modules that seamlessly integrate into a specific ecosystem.

Space Applications Services is coordinating the project, along with other partners consisting of ReOrbit, ArianeGroup, GMV in Defense and Security and FZI Forschungszentrum Informatik.

Name: AVINYA
Description:
Team Avinya is a group of enthusiastic students interested various domains from the School of Electronics and Communication Engineering, REVA University, Bengaluru, India. 

Our Mission: To build and launch a can-sized Satellite under 1kg, upto 1km altitude! The CanSat will be transmitting sensor data live throughout its flight, just like real satellites. The IN-SPACe CanSat Competition is our chance to get hands-on with the technology that powers space exploration. By building and launching our own mini-satellite, we'll gain a deep understanding of how real satellites work, from launch systems to data transmission. Name: Titan Research and Test Center
Description:
Titan Research & Test Center is an industrial-scale test site in Eastern France, built to take high-constraint technologies from prototype to proven hardware.

Developed on a former military base within XCR Airport, Titan is designed for programs that require isolation, safety perimeters and serious infrastructure. The site enables partners to run demanding test campaigns in controlled conditions: propulsion and fluid systems, cryogenic and high-pressure operations, structures and mechanical validation, energy and battery experiments, and other “can’t do this in a standard lab” activities.

Titan is a secure, ICPE-authorized environment with an operational framework tailored for hazardous and sensitive testing, including the ability to manage co-activity: multiple test campaigns, one site, one safety culture.

For Latitude, Titan is where Zephyr Launcher’s subsystems are matured, qualified and industrialized. For industrial partners, it’s an opportunity to access rare testing capacity in Europe, reduce qualification cycles, and validate hardware faster, without compromises.

Looking for a site to push your technology to proof? Let’s discuss your next campaign at Titan.

DEPI Space is a research and innovation hub focused on product development in the space sector, creating mission management software, advanced systems, and scalable solutions through a strategy centered on innovation, cross-disciplinary collaboration, and practical application.


The idea of DEPI Space Hub was born in 2021 out of a shared vision: to bring Armenia back into the conversation of space exploration, to connect local talent with international research, and to ensure that Armenian innovation contributes to global advancements in space science and technology. Through international partnerships, research initiatives, and educational programs, our goal is to inspire and educate the next generation of Armenian space scientists, engineers and individuals who are passionate about space. By this we are not just building a hub;we are creating a platform for long-term collaboration and impact.

Our founding members have strong academic and research backgrounds in space exploration and are dedicated to elevating Armenia’s presence in international space initiatives. DEPI Space serves as Armenia’s first official representative and national point of contact for the Space Generation Advisory Council (SGAC), bridging Armenia with global space organizations, the United Nations Programme on Space Applications, and leading research institutions. Our members played a key role in organizing Armenia’s participation in the Austrian Space Forum’s Mars analog mission (AMADEE-24). Through these collaborations, we are not only positioning Armenia within the global space community but also creating opportunities for the next generation of space researchers and innovators.

STARAX Group is a growing space architecture and technology design group that ambition to advance extraterrestrial habitations for human beings beyond Earth. 

Human beings have inhabited Earth for over millions of years, but such is merely a tiny fragment of the entire Milky Way Galaxy. Despite the radical progression of modern technologies, the lack of development in the fields of extraterrestrial habitation persists. 

The goal of STARAX Group is to create, design, and advance the potential of architecture beyond this world by utilizing aerospace research and cutting edge technology, advanced efficient construction techniques, combined with architecture and artificial intelligence to develop "living in space" as a norm. 

Our expertise involves designing and developing habitations including modular living spaces, commercial, and infrastructural components.

Moreover, STARAX Group fosters collaboration within the space architecture community, partnering with academia, industry, and government agencies from all over the world to catalyze a renaissance in space dwelling. As humanity stands in the cusp of a new era in space exploration, STARAX Group leads the charge, transforming the cosmos into a realm of boundless opportunity and discovery. Join us in redefining architecture among the stars.

GeoNature Analytics es una iniciativa especializada en el análisis de datos geoespaciales, capacitación técnica e investigación aplicada para la gestión ambiental y territorial. Nace con el propósito de conectar la tecnología espacial con las necesidades actuales del planeta, a través del uso de sistemas de información geográfica (SIG), sensores remotos, estadística ambiental y ciencia climática.

Nos enfocamos en formar capacidades técnicas en estudiantes, profesionales, consultores y tomadores de decisiones, mediante cursos, talleres y asesorías prácticas orientadas a la realidad ambiental del Ecuador y América Latina. Apostamos por una educación accesible, actualizada y aplicada, que combine teoría, casos reales y herramientas tecnológicas como QGIS, ArcGIS, Google Earth Engine, ModelBuilder, R, Python y otras plataformas de análisis ambiental.

Además de la formación, ofrecemos servicios técnicos como generación de mapas temáticos, desarrollo de indicadores ambientales, análisis satelital multitemporal, visualización de datos en dashboards geográficos y apoyo en proyectos de monitoreo ambiental, ordenamiento territorial y cambio climático.

Nuestros temas principales incluyen:

SIG para gestión ambiental, territorio y recursos naturales

Climatología y análisis de datos meteorológicos

Detección y análisis de contaminantes atmosféricos

Modelamiento espacial y análisis multivariado

Monitoreo ambiental con imágenes satelitales

Desarrollo de productos cartográficos e índices espectrales

Capacitación en técnicas de análisis espacial, visualización y automatización

En GeoNature Analytics creemos que el conocimiento geoespacial es una herramienta clave para entender, proteger y transformar el entorno natural. Nuestro objetivo es empoderar a las personas con herramientas técnicas que les permitan tomar decisiones más informadas y sostenibles frente a los desafíos ambientales actuales.

Name: O ANALYTICS INCORPORATED
Description:
COMPANY FOCUS: O Analytics is a premier space object characterization company applying signal processing, physics-based techniques, and machine learning (ML) to detect, classify and characterize satellites and orbital debris. Our experienced team of computer science, physics, machine learning, astrophysics and data science experts with 40+ combined years in digital signal processing and information science is committed to ensuring safety and protection of assets orbiting the Earth.

O ANALYTICS SOLUTION: Our innovative Deep Learning Track Analytics (DELTA) quickly answers important questions about active and inoperable satellites – such as What is it? What is it doing? and What is it likely to do next? Our core solution combines convolution, machine learning and physics-based technology to detect and characterize space objects – fast and agnostically. Our solution involves tuning to the sensor environment versus time consuming and arduous AI training, processes with low resource consumption, and is adaptable to new and emerging sensing technologies. O Analytics is also developing a comprehensive space object custody management system (SOCMS) that addresses the critical question - What are objects likely to do next?

CUSTOMERS/USE: Military, civil, and intelligence agencies; and commercial space asset owners. Especially useful for ISR, space domain awareness, satellite fleet custody management, space traffic management, rendezvous proximity operations and collision avoidance.

TECHNOLOGY STATUS: Our patent-pending DELTA algorithmic software combines Newtonian physics principles, correlation inference, and real-time multi-hypothesis Markov tracking techniques to detect, conclusively classify and characterize pattern of life behavior of space objects. DELTA is designed to trigger downstream Deep Neural Network (DNN) AI/ML, sensor tasking and other processes that support autonomous or person-in-the-loop operations with an underlying goal of providing awareness.
