Name: Protosolar Labs
Description:
At Protosolar Labs, we are engineering sustainable supply chains and logistics architectures for the space resource value chain. Our mission is to enable a vibrant and sustainable space economy for everyone.

Our blockchain-based supply chain management and intelligence platform will enable unprecedented collaboration between companies in space and unlock new business opportunities across the space resources value chain. We are taking the best supply chain technologies and practices from Earth and redesigning them from the ground up for the space environment.

In combination with our supply chain platform, we are developing the first mission design and analysis software for the commercial space age. Together, these tools will allow companies and space agencies to design robust supply chains and conduct sustainable long-term operations.

Our team has a unique blend of experience in astrodynamics, space mission design, and blockchain technology. We are committed to using this expertise to build a vibrant, equitable, and sustainable future for humanity beyond Earth.

Name: SKYARTH Embedded Systems
Description:
Using the experience gained from various projects carried out in commercial, industrial, and aerospace sectors within different organizations, we set out with the goal of developing high-value-added embedded electronic systems in a more dynamic and sustainable way.

Our name, chosen as an abbreviation of the phrase SKY ART HOUSE, signifies our aim to be a boutique design house with its sights set on the skies. We wanted our name to emphasize our belief that electronic design is a future-shaping art form and that the future lies in the skies. In our logo, the triangle representing the letter “A” contains a small door right at its center. That door represents our gateway to the future; unfortunately, it’s very small and hard to open. We have shouldered the task of opening it and are striving with all our might.

Our expertise lies in electronic hardware design, specifically for embedded systems, and transforming these designs into products.

We aim to provide solutions to your tasks where outsourcing design, prototyping, and testing activities for commercial, industrial, and aerospace electronics offers cost and time advantages. We can support your sub-systems where you seek a contractor or assistance.

On your challenging journey towards a product, we promise the comfort of a companion who knows the shortcuts and speaks the same language as you.

Name: Projet 100km
Description:
Bienvenue sur la page du Projet 100km !

Nous sommes une association de passionnés déterminés à rendre l'exploration spatiale plus accessible et durable .

Avec une approche collaborative novatrice  et des technologies disruptives , notre mission est simple (enfin, presque !) :

 à coûts réduits,

Ici, l'innovation spatiale prend forme grâce à un collectif passionné , diversifié et inclusif. Nous croyons en l'intelligence collective pour repousser les limites de l'innovation, en plaçant l'écologie et l'égalité des chances au cœur de nos actions.

Que vous soyez astrophysicien , bricoleur de génie , ingénieur retraité en quête de nouveaux défis , ou étudiant curieux de l'avenir de l'espace , votre place est parmi nous. Chaque talent a son rôle : qu'il s'agisse de résoudre des défis techniques, d'apporter des idées nouvelles ou de contribuer à une vision audacieuse.

Alors, prêt à rejoindre une aventure qui vous propulse… littéralement vers de nouvelles perspectives ?

Name: Mars Desert Research Station Crew 299
Description: Our mission is designed to pioneer new techniques for exploring Mars and analyzing extraterrestrial materials through a simulated Martian environment. We aim to conduct geological research, map a detailed 3D mock-Martian terrain, analyze the nutritional data of plants grown in mock-Martian soil, and predict space weather events via the Musk Observatory.

Name: SIMAQ Ltd
Description:
SIMAQ is leading the development of Saudi Arabia’s first Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) infrastructure, establishing the Kingdom as a global leader in quantum-secure communications.
We are building advanced quantum optical ground stations and QKD satellites, integrating space-based transmission with terrestrial fiber networks to deliver future-proof cybersecurity for governments, businesses, and critical sectors.
Through strategic partnerships with global QKD leaders, SIMAQ is positioning Saudi Arabia at the forefront of quantum innovation, ensuring its role in shaping the future of secure communication.
SIMAQ isn’t just building infrastructure: we’re securing the future.

Description:
We are a physical and online astronomy store with a full range of telescopes, binoculars, accessories & services.
Find the latest in telescope technology and the perfect telescope for your needs.
Whether you are a beginner, an experienced astronomer or astrophotographer, we have the right telescope for you to enjoy the dark sky.
Our showroom is Larnaca, near Mackenzie beach.

Name: Astronomy and Space-Water-Environment Nexus E-center (SWEN) Initiative
Description:
Space-Water-Environment Nexus non-profitable E-Centre for Research and Education (SWEN): A non-profitable center to educate all people whether specialized or enthusiastic audience. This e-center is based on the idea that space, water, and the environment are interrelated. Water mining on planets is the main aim of many space agencies. Also, it is believed that water came to Earth partially from comets and it was recently discovered that some kinds of asteroids also contain water.
Global warming, aerosols; for example, micro-plastics in oceans and the atmosphere, and the change in the water reservoirs on earth due to gravitational effects and other causes are closely monitored via space satellite and remote sensing missions. Also, there is an environmental impact assessment done for missions that are launched into space.
The of SWEN e-center is to be dedicated to space, space technology, water, and environmental improvements that foster a sustainable future to improve the social, economic, and environmental well-being of mainly all communities around Africa and also the whole world. We fulfill our mission through a commitment to reach out to people especially the new evolving generations to educate them about space technologies, water and environmental research, and capacity building.
Fostering strong partnerships with  academic and research institutions, and the civil community will enhance our goals which are in great part dedicated to research that will in turn lead to community service. We encompass people from all disciplines related to space, water, and environmental sciences, engineering, and technology and even non-specialized and enthusiastic people. As we believe science and engineering are for all, the center welcomes anyone from any background, and this is to encourage interdisciplinary work. The center will provide online webinars, seminars, and educational materials.
The center is associated with Pan Africa Citizen Science e-lab.
Science is for all!

Name: Flux Space
Description:
The Flux Space team includes PhD physicists, aerospace & electrical engineers, and experimental specialists all working towards a common goal: developing the science & technology required for advanced space propulsion. For the time being, our company is operating in stealth mode, but as research progresses we will unveil our core projects.

Our focus areas include propellantless propulsion devices utilizing a variety of reactionless drive principles. Some of these are based on known physics, and for others our research team is pushing the boundaries of physics to develop new principles.

We’re working on gravitational-control technologies that allow mass to move free of the influence of both gravitational and inertial forces. Our research in this area is based on decades of experimental evidence combined with new avenues of research and development.

We’re also developing technologies for high-speed interstellar propulsion, based on a variety of popular concepts related to bending space-time. This was initially popularized by Miguel Alcubierre, and over time his groundbreaking speculative physics has been extended by others to overcome the physical limitations of his initial proposal.

Name: ULVI NASA Human Exploration Rover Challenge 2023 Team
The Landolt Mission
Description: The $19.5 million recently approved Landolt NASA Space Mission that will put an artificial “star” in orbit around the Earth. This artificial star will allow scientists to calibrate telescopes and more accurately measure the brightness of stars ranging from those nearby to the distant explosions of supernova in far-off galaxies By establishing absolute flux calibration, the mission will begin to address several open challenges in astrophysics including the speed and acceleration of the universe expansion.

Name: National Space Society of North Texas
Description:
The National Space Society of North Texas (NSS-NT) is an award-winning chapter of the National Space Society, dedicated to promoting space exploration and development in the North Texas region. 

 We host business meetings with presentations on the second Sunday of every month.
 We also gather for social happy hours on the last Friday of every month.

Follow us on X (@NSSofNT) for updates! It's the best way to discover space events in the DFW area, meet astronauts, space executives, and professionals. Become a member today!

Name: Okuyama Lab Nihon University CST
Description: In our laboratory, we are conducting research activities in fields such as small spacecraft system technology, special material technology, and robot technology to realize earth observation, space exploration, and space utilization. We are also making efforts to provide these research results in the industry.

Name: Micro Vehicle Technology LLC
Description:
Micro Vehicle Technology LLC develops patented electric Micro Vehicles spanning watersports, rescue platforms, and personal mobility systems.

The company’s current focus is Powered Water Skis — a patented, fully buoyant, zero-tow electric watercraft that lets riders step on and go. No boats. No towlines. No extended instruction. Just immediate balance, power, and real carving performance underfoot.

Powered Water Skis are advancing through the Founder-Series, engaging early-adopter watersports enthusiasts alongside select watersports resorts. The program focuses on ride feel, safety, operational simplicity, and scalable deployment—supporting both individual ownership and fleet-based resort use across a wide range of skill levels.

Beyond watersports, Micro Vehicle Technology’s patented bipedal, turn-carving architecture underpins an expanding family of Micro Vehicles engineered for:

• Urban mobility and personal transport
• Water rescue and rapid-response platforms
• Walking rehabilitation and balance-assistive mobility
• Electric power sports for land and water
• Ground troop mobility and tactical logistics
• Lunar surface mobility and future xEVA applications

All Micro Vehicle systems are designed and developed in Cocoa Beach, Florida. The company’s IP portfolio—including multiple granted U.S. utility and design patents—forms a defensible foundation for strategic co-development and patent licensing with manufacturers across consumer, industrial, defense, and aerospace markets.

Registered on SAM.gov
CAGE Code: 9DAQ8

 www.MicroVehicleTechnology.com

 Cocoa Beach, Florida
 Keith.Rudofsky@MicroVehicleTechnology.com

Name: Space Club Unilorin
Description:
Welcome to Space Club Unilorin. 

We are a vibrant community of space enthusiasts and aspiring professionals  in the University of Ilorin dedicated to fostering curiosity, innovation, and collaboration in aerospace and space sciences. 

Our mission is twofold:  

- Within the university: To inspire, educate, and connect students and young professionals interested in space and/or space careers. Through guest lectures, workshops, space policy debate sessions, stargazing sessions, satellite design projects, rocket-building projects, networking events and more, we empower members to explore opportunities in the space industry.  

- Beyond campus: To bring space education to the grassroots level by engaging elementary and secondary school students. Our members volunteer to deliver interactive outreach programs, demos, and talks that spark early interest in STEM and space.

Whether you're a undergraduate dreaming of Mars missions, a graduate researching satellites, or an alumnus in the field, Space Club Unilorin is your launchpad for growth, mentorship, and real-world impact.

Join us in shaping the future of space exploration — one inspired mind at a time. Follow our page for updates on events, member spotlights, industry insights, and outreach stories. Connect with us to get involved!

#SpaceForAll #Aerospace #STEMEducation #SpaceExploration

Name: Apogee Missions Advisory Ltd
Description:

Name: AIMM Center- Advanced Inspection Methods and Materials
Description:
The AIMM Center (Advanced Inspection Methods and Materials) is a next-generation non-destructive evaluation (NDE) and R&D hub located in Ogden, Utah, created through the strategic partnership of CICNDT and Omni NDE. Our mission is to provide cutting-edge inspection services, engineering support, and training for advanced materials and critical infrastructure across aerospace, defense, wind energy, and other high-performance industries.

We specialize in solving the most complex inspection challenges—where conventional methods fall short—by integrating advanced robotics, automation, and multi-modal NDT technologies under one roof. Our tools include robotic computed laminography (CL), CT imaging, laser ultrasonics, shearography, infrared thermography, bond testing, eddy current, and phased array ultrasonics. These capabilities allow us to provide high-resolution defect detection and full data traceability on composite structures, bonded joints, and metal components.

AIMM operates on a flexible “Scan-as-a-Service” model. We empower companies to access powerful inspection systems and expert analysis without the need to invest in capital equipment or staffing. Whether you’re a prime contractor, OEM, MRO, or R&D team, AIMM enables faster turnaround, reduced rework, and actionable insights that help you stay ahead of schedule and in compliance with demanding specifications.

We also serve as a demonstration and training center for emerging technologies, offering hands-on workshops, method feasibility studies, and certification-ready education for NDT professionals. With direct links to Utah’s aerospace ecosystem, major defense contractors, and top-tier industry innovators, AIMM is a catalyst for regional growth and national innovation.

Name: RAIN Lab
Description:
Our group’s research focuses on analyzing and synthesizing networked distributed autonomous systems operating in complex environments. We examine fundamental and theoretical questions at the intersection of control theory, optimization, networks, guidance, navigation, and control issues for space, air, and ground robotic systems.

As networks become ubiquitous in many areas of engineering, it has become of paramount importance to examine how the network and its structure affect the dynamic properties of the overall system, such as its stability, performance, and robustness, both in the random and deterministic settings. We also consider some of the more fundamental system-theoretic questions such as controllability and observability of networked systems. Our research team examines a wide range of questions from a theoretical point of view as well as their ramifications for systems such as multiple spacecraft formation flying, networked robotics, UAV formations, heterogenous dynamic networks, and more recently, in the setting of social and molecular networks.

Another facet of our research deals with the operation of autonomous vehicular systems in complex environments, such as guidance laws for spacecraft attitude control in nonconvex constraints, trajectory planning for solar-powered unmanned aerial vehicles, and formation flight. Large-scale optimization for the operation of dynamic systems provides yet another facet of our research, with particular applications to energy management and green technologies.

Name: Ventspils International Radio Astronomy Centre (VIRAC)
Description:
Engineering Research Institute Ventspils International Radio Astronomy Centre (ERI VIRAC) of Ventspils University of Applied Sciences (VUAS) is a science education center specializing in the implementation of high-quality future research services in the field of space technology and signal processing. The strategic goal of ERI VIRAC is to become a global provider of research services in the field of astronomy and space technology, to create an internationally competitive, balanced and sustainable team of researchers as well as an internationally competitive and demanding research infrastructure for science infrastructure.
The Irbenes radio telescope complex is equipped with two completely rotatable, parabolic antennas “RT-32” and “RT-16” as well as LOFAR-LATVIA station that contains 96 low band (LBA) (10 - 90 MHz, total area 3200 sq.m) and 96 high band (HBA) (110 - 240 MHz, total area 2400 sq.m) antennas.
The intensive reconstruction and instrumental refurbishment carried out in 2014-2019 made it possible to use radio telescopes for the international scale fundamental and applied research in the field of radio astronomy. The most important aspect of this work is participation in the VLBI (Very Long Baseline Interferometry) international experiments.
VIRAC is a member of pan-European networks such as CERN (Member State since 2021), International Low Frequency Array (LOFAR) Telescope (ILT) since 2019, European VLBI (EVN) and JIV-ERIC networks since 2016.
VIRAC also encapsulates strong scientific groups in satellite communication, space technologies, remote sensing and high-performance computing. VIRAC's main ambition is to become a part of the European Space Industry with Ground Segment services based on adaptation and sharing of infrastructure model providing maximum benefit on European and Global scale for both Space Missions and Fundamental Research in Radio Astronomy. VIRAC is carrying out this task by close cooperation with European Space Agency (ESA) and others.

Name: Theia Labs
Description:
Theia Labs develops wireless power transfer (WPT) systems for space applications.

Founded in 2024, Theia Labs was created to solve one of space exploration’s main challenges: reliable power when sunlight is unavailable. The company originated from a project during the SpaceTech Master’s program at TU Graz, where the team designed a wireless power system to help lunar rovers survive the 14-day lunar night.

What started as a lunar power concept has evolved into a broader mission to build scalable WPT solutions for both space and Earth. Theia Labs is now developing its first prototype to demonstrate wireless energy transfer for satellites and future planetary missions.

We focus on three key applications:
• Space-to-space wireless power: Laser-based power beaming for CubeSats and SmallSats in Low Earth Orbit
• Precision agriculture: Laser systems for selective, herbicide-free weed control
• Lunar missions: Power delivery from orbiting satellites to rovers and landers for operations during the lunar night

Name: QUBE TESTING
Description: ALQUBE is redefining how emerging space actors access thermal-vacuum testing. We deliver a cost-efficient, high-performance solution that enables universities, research labs, and startups to validate CubeSats and space hardware locally—eliminating the financial and logistical burden of relying on distant, oversubscribed facilities. Our chamber architecture is fully aligned with ECSS and NASA testing standards, while our proprietary control software ensures precise, reliable temperature cycling for a wide range of mission profiles. Built for scalability and customization, ALQUBE supports both standard CubeSat formats and specialized component-level testing. By decentralizing environmental qualification, we make advanced space testing accessible to regions and organizations previously excluded from this critical step of mission development.

Name: gEICko Project
Description: Funded by the European Innovation Council, the gEICko project is developing a capture kit for docking uncooperative spacecraft such as defunct satellites or rocket stages. It uses gecko-inspired micro-patterned dry adhesives (MDA) that mimic a gecko’s foot, enabling strong yet reversible attachment to surfaces like solar panels, even if not designed for docking. A two-phase system combines flexible MDA contact with a robust anchor for safe handling, supporting operations such as controlled re-entry or recycling. For tumbling targets, gEICko employs a tether with whip-like deployment to enable capture. The project also explores harvesting energy directly from the solar panels of captured satellites. Together, these innovations open new possibilities for satellite servicing, debris removal and versatile payload attachment in space.

Name: Hexos Space Heritage
Description:
Hexos Space Heritage

At Hexos Space Heritage, we are dedicated to exploring, preserving, and advocating for the cultural and historical significance of human activity beyond Earth. Our mission is to bridge the gap between terrestrial and extraterrestrial heritage, ensuring that the legacy of space exploration is understood, respected, and protected for future generations.

We provide training and education on the importance of space heritage, equipping individuals, organizations, and policymakers with the knowledge they need to make informed decisions about historical preservation in space. From the earliest satellites in Earth's orbit to human footprints on the Moon and the growing presence of technology on Mars, we examine the past, present, and future of space heritage.

Our work encompasses:

Heritage Assessments & Advisory Services – Whether on Earth or beyond, we conduct assessments of heritage sites and significant artefacts, from early rocket launch sites to abandoned lunar modules.

Space-Based Heritage Projects – We study key objects and locations, such as the Apollo landing sites, the International Space Station, and the first satellites of their kind still in orbit.

Space Law & Policy – We explore the evolving legal landscape, addressing questions of ownership, responsibility, and the right to preserve cultural and historical sites in space.

Future Heritage Management – As humanity expands further into space, we advocate for frameworks that ensure responsible stewardship of significant sites and artefacts on the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

Space heritage is a critical yet often overlooked aspect of exploration. As we move toward a future where off-world travel and habitation become commonplace, it is essential to consider how we protect the historical markers of this journey. If you need expertise in space heritage—whether for terrestrial assessments, research collaborations, or policy discussions—please contact us.

Our deep-space constellation will conduct frequent asteroid flybys and gravity assists, enabling a regular cadence of one asteroid encounter per month. This allows for rapid-response missions to newly identified threats or high-interest bodies, often within a month of detection.

Like its namesake in the game of Go, Fuseki represents a bold opening strategy: placing spacecraft across the solar system as the foundation for a future of exploration, resilience, and commercial activity in deep space.

Description:

Desde el 26 de Abril de 2007 debido a la actividad desarrollada por el Grupo GASuR, el Consejo Superior de la Universidad Tecnológica Nacional otorgó el reconocimiento como “Centro de Investigaciones de Atmósfera Superior y Radiopropagación” (CIASUR).
El CIASUR realizó gestiones para la creación de una nueva estación ionosférica en colaboración con el Rectorado de la UTN y la Embajada de Italia, se realizaron las acciones necesarias para que el Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología e Innovación Productiva de nuestro país, junto con el INGV, co-financiaran la construcción de un nuevo ionosonda, que se instaló en la Facultad Regional Bahía Blanca (FRBB) de la UTN en Noviembre de 2016. De esa manera se fortaleció la infraestructura de la Argentina para estudios de la atmósfera.

Name: Loren Aerospace Ltd
Description:
Design & Test Rocket Engines Interactively

Meco Rocket Simulator introduces rocket engine concepts in a fun way to inspire budding rocket engineers/scientists to support our multi-planetary future. For realism, Meco crunches the numbers using principles from well-cited research and published practitioners.


The Rocket Engine Simulator for Interactive Design

Say goodbye to pages of endless numbers and equations because you’ll see the outcome of your design choices in seconds. It’s a fun way to learn – perfect for next-gen engineers and scientists to have fun while learning to be trailblazers in our multi-planetary future.


On The Shoulders of Giants

Play campaigns with missions based on real-life events. For example, build and simulate rocket engines that show:
- How the USA developed the Redstone-Mercury rocket engine, which in essence, sent the first American to Space.
- Progression of SpaceX’s improvements to Merlin Engines, in particular, from Falcon 1 to Falcon 9 full-thrust.
- Development of engines for the Saturn V moon rocket that were exceptionally reliable despite the technology available at the time.
- How the Europa I/II Rocket engines were developed, which enabled Europe’s first orbit-capable Launch Vehicle.


Detailed Fluid and Combustion Simulation

Meco’s detailed simulation allows you to observe the following:
- Gas and liquids flow in lines and ducts due to pressure and friction.
- Ignition, throttle and shutdown sequences following value valve timings.
- Thrust chamber and nozzle temperatures following regenerative, film and ablative cooling.
- Evaluate the pros and cons resulting from gas generator, expander, and other engine cycles.
- Nozzle gas exit velocity and flow separation change when the shape of the nozzle is changed.
- Lastly, get the expected engine thrust, efficiency, and ISP readings to evaluate your design.

Other Features
- Comprehensive design options
- True-to-life rocket engine simulation
- Analyze
- Examples included

Name: Centre for National Security and Defense Research - Bulgarian Academy of Science

As a result of the agreement, for the first time in 10 years, scientists and specialists from BAS are involved in various projects and scientific events (seminars, symposia) in the field of research and technology for defence needs.

In the period 2000-2002, more than 30 projects were developed in various fields (informatics, sensors and systems, energy sources, etc.). The first joint international projects (under a contract between the Ministries of Defence of the Kingdom of the Netherlands and the Republic of Bulgaria) have also been carried out, with institutes from BAS and the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Research (TNO-Defence) as the main contractors.

As a result of the Framework Contract and the implemented projects, the Centre for National Security and Defence Studies (CNSDS) was established as an independent academic specialised unit by a decision of the General Assembly of BAS on 24 June 2002. The aim is to preserve and develop the Bulgarian scientific and technical potential to master new and create its own cutting-edge technologies in the field of national security.

The IDID conference is designed as an international platform where both emerging and experienced researchers from academia and industry can converge to exchange their latest findings in the ever-evolving realm of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) in the domain of International Development. Theconference is part of the agenda of working group 13.8 committed to advancing HCI research, practices, and education on a worldwide scale, championing interaction design that caters to the needs of the developing world, and advocating for cross-cultural HCI practices, particularly within burgeoning economies. Tuklik is the first cloud computing platform purpose-built for space. We turn orbit into a programmable environment by providing a containerized, hardware-agnostic operating system that runs across satellites—from legacy sensors to next-generation constellations.

Our platform aggregates idle satellite compute into a distributed orbital supercomputer, allowing developers to deploy, update, and process data in orbit as easily as on Earth. This reduces latency, lowers downlink costs, and enables real-time insights for missions ranging from climate monitoring to advanced aerospace applications.

Built with zero-trust security and fault-tolerance by design, Tuklik ensures that mission-critical data remains secure against both cyber threats and the challenges of space.

We are redefining what software in space can do—empowering agencies, commercial operators, and developers to treat orbit as a dynamic, scalable, and intelligent computing layer.

From low-Earth orbit to deep-space trajectories, Orbix Global’s mission is simple yet transformative: to make space operations transparent, trustworthy, and insurable.

Vision, Mission, and Purpose

Vision

To build a universally recognized standard for orbital sustainability and governance—empowering every organization operating in space to measure, disclose, and improve its performance for a safer, more sustainable orbital environment.

Mission

To provide verified orbital intelligence, sustainability ratings, and assurance services that enable responsible decision-making, risk mitigation, and long-term asset protection across the global space economy.

Purpose

Orbix Global exists to align technical integrity, financial accountability, and environmental responsibility in orbit.
We translate complex orbital data into actionable intelligence—allowing stakeholders to understand not just how satellites operate, but how responsibly they behave.

⸻

Our Framework: The Orbital Sustainability and Governance (OSG) Model

At the core of Orbix Global lies the Orbital Sustainability and Governance (OSG) framework—an independent, multi-dimensional model that evaluates satellites and orbital missions across environmental, operational, and governance criteria.

Name: Andraste Group
Description:
Andraste Group Ltd. (Andraste) is a start-up space tech company specialising in Space Sustainability. The company is aiming to provide a novel solution to the growing problems with debris in space especially in LEO (Low Earth Orbit). 

The company is UK based and will operate where possible in the UK. Andraste's main commercial operation is orbital servicing providing a platform to extend the life of satellites beyond their mission end date.

It will also provide active debris removal solutions in two tiers. A service targeting smaller debris (<1m) in orbit which could pose risks to current and future space operations. 

A secondary asset recovery service will actively target defunct satellites, capturing them and safely transporting them to a hub unit. This unit will deorbit with a view to safely retain the history associated with the asset, and allow it to be displayed or IP (Intellectual Property) protected whilst maintaining the primary goal of Andraste, making LEO safe.

As a group company, Andraste is looking to expand its operations and become a key player in the Space Economy further cementing the UKs place in the Space-Tech market.

SolarSURFER (Solar Sail Utilizing Radiative Forces Examining Radio-waves) is a joint Johns Hopkins University, Olin College of Engineering, and University of Maryland College Park Cube Satellite (CubeSat) that aims to explore technological advances in the realm of Solar Sailing. The mission is set to be built in a 6U form factor (30cm x 20cm x 10cm) with a 15m x 15m solar sail (compressed volume: 4U (20cm x 20cm x 10cm)) and the other flight dynamics and communications systems filling the other 2U. 

SolarSURFER will explore many new technologies to fulfill its mission requirements, including experimentation with sail composition, the utilization of the sail as a High Gain Antenna, and the integration of the science instruments with the shape memory allow powered booms of the sail, among other developments.

Name: Siranga
Description:
About Siranga

Siranga is a deep-tech company from Warsaw, Poland, developing breakthrough imaging technologies that harness the power of quantum tunneling. NB. So far six Physics Nobel Prizes were awarded for discoveries related to quantum tunneling, including 2025 Nobel Prize for macroscopic quantum tunneling effect. Founded in 2020, Siranga's mission is to transform the way we observe Earth and space by creating tera-pixel sensors that go beyond the physical limits of today’s optical technologies. We combine quantum physics, nanotechnology, and space engineering to deliver imaging systems that are far more sensitive than any existing solution.

Our Core Technology: QTIP

At the heart of our innovation lies the Quantum-Tunneling Image Photodetector (QTIP) - a patented* imaging sensor capable of detecting light with unprecedented precision.
Unlike traditional CMOS or CCD sensors, QTIP uses quantum tunneling effects to capture more information, even in extreme low-light or cloud obscured conditions.
* Siranga's patent portfolio contains a family of ten patents granted in seven countries: USA, Japa, China, Singapore, Germany, France and Great Britain.

Applications
Our technology addresses some of the most critical challenges of the 21st century, from food security to climate change and space safety.

Precision Agriculture → Enhanced crop monitoring, yield prediction, and irrigation optimization through accurate multispectral imaging.
Climate and Environmental Monitoring → Reliable atmospheric and surface data for better models and sustainability insights.
Space Situational Awareness (SSA) → High-resolution tracking of small orbital debris to improve satellite safety.
Global Security and Disaster Response → Real-time imaging through clouds, smoke, and dust - delivering clear vision when it matters most.

Our Vision
We aim to set a new standard for quantum-enhanced imaging, enabling clearer data, smarter satellites, and more sustainable use of our planet’s resources.

Name: Stellarbug
Description:

Bug= A harmful micro-organism, typically a bacterium; An error in a computer program or system; Annoyance. 
"Ever had a failed Volcanic lava model at a science fair as a kid? or a code, circuit model, not finding the end point while titrating a solution, a rejected patent, disregarded research paper or an essay? ever had a hard time accepting that things didn't work but not receiving a closure or an explanation? or an alternative, correction , review or at least advice? In STEM, such situations often leave a curious mind feeling hopeless and stagnant, especially if habituated to Instant Gratification. 
I had multiple failed projects, experiments and some tests, numericals and work rejection which often made me feel isolated and guilty, as I believed there was no "growth" happening and no takeaways. I needed Consistency. Acceptance. Patience. 
Stellarbug is not just an organization of a person who wants self-motivation. It aims to be an entity, humane and scientific, which will demonstrate this trail 
Mistakes~Experience~Wisdom~Growth~Success
Initiative by a struggling student and supported by Students, Mentors, Experts, Guests and Critics, Stellarbug aims to provide a platform with non-hierarchical discussions, Practice sessions, credited corrections & solutions to a struggling project/ research , suggestions, reviews & necessary resources  to creative & original endeavors in the realm of Space and STEM, encouraging collaboration and being inclusive +Ongoing original projects & research at Stellarbug ."
-simz

Name: Lei A While Research Center
Description:
Lei A While Research Center is an independent scientific institute dedicated to advancing resonance-anchored physics, sealed hardware engineering, and coherence-based technologies grounded in the Quantum Time-Energy Theory (QTET) developed by founder Destiny Machwaya.

The Center’s mission is to translate the QTET First Principle — “In any closed or semi-closed system, resonance acts as an anchor for coherence to counter entropy” — into practical, ethical, and testable applications across physics, engineering, medicine, and secure systems design. With a focus on non-weaponization, transparency, and rigorous falsifiability, the Research Center operates at the intersection of theoretical discovery and hardware validation.

Through a portfolio of utility patent–pending technologies — including sealed systems, coherence-lock architectures, radiation-hardened enclosures for space and defense environments, and next-generation resonance-based medical and cryogenic prototypes — the Center is building a new class of scientific tools designed for high-integrity research, space deployment, and real-world problem-solving.

The Research Center maintains a strict ethical framework emphasizing civilian benefit, scientific reproducibility, and IP-protected collaboration with universities, medical institutions, federal agencies, and philanthropic organizations. Its work spans cosmology, quantum engineering, regenerative biology, and secure laboratory systems, unified by the goal of establishing resonance as a foundational organizing principle across scales.

Vision:
To build a globally recognized scientific institution where coherence science, ethical engineering, and open-access knowledge coexist — enabling safer technologies, deeper theoretical understanding, and practical tools for the next century of discovery.

By pairing hands-on technical execution with thoughtful IP strategy, Partàger helps innovators move ideas quickly from concept to commercial reality.

From inception, Partàger has emphasized engineering talent as the true engine of progress. Aerospace development often occurs in silos, with propulsion, materials, avionics, and satellite teams tackling the same problems independently. Partàger was built to remove those barriers by bringing diverse technical disciplines into one collaborative environment where ideas can be built, tested, refined, and validated together rather than isolated on paper.

This vision operates out of Partàger’s Long Beach facility, a modular R&D “sandbox” modeled after integrated development chains used in heavy industry. The site is not an incubator or academic lab—it is a working engineering hub where teams rapidly de-risk technologies through hands-on development, testing, and real-world validation, preparing systems for licensing or deployment.

Located in the heart of Southern California’s aerospace corridor, the Long Beach campus sits among leading space companies, manufacturers, and specialized suppliers. The region’s deep talent base and industry density allow Partàger to collaborate directly with local partners while advancing next-generation aerospace solutions with speed, efficiency, and technical discipline.

Currently, the main research topics include TT&C Physical Layer (Modulations, Signal Processing, Synchronization, Error Correcting Codes, Binary Sequences), 5G NTN Performance Evaluation, OTFS for space links, Jamming Detection and Mitigation, Handover, Routing, Radio Resource Management for LEO/VLEO constellations, Direct-to-Satellite Internet of Things, Massive Multiple Access, Mega-Constellation Services for LEO Satellites, Satellite Constellation Design, and Pilot Channels for LEO positioning. We are keen to establish research collaborations on these topics and participate in joint ESA and European projects.

Name: Fléttan
Description:
Soil erosion destroys fertile land, increases pollution in streams and rivers, destroys ecosystems, and can lead to catastrophic events such as flooding and mud slides. Soil erosion can be prevented, but once it has started progressing it is extremely difficult to reverse.  

Fléttan is developing an algorithm that will measure the risk of soil erosion more accurately than any other existing model. We harvest earth observation data from the Sentinel-2 satellite, calibrate our data with localized historical data, and using machine learning and artificial intelligence we measure soil erosion risk. Our risk values are displayed as a map on a user-friendly graphical interface.  

When our developmental phase has concluded and we have successfully tested our concept within the Icelandic market, we hope to acquire access to more data from satellites and local data vendors throughout the world to create a global product that can be utilized by customers ranging from government institutions, research facilities to insurance companies and environmental NGO’s.

Name: STARC Lab
Description:
STARC Lab (Space Technology and Radio Cosmology Laboratory) is a multidisciplinary research group within the Department of Astronomy, Astrophysics and Space Engineering (DAASE), Indian Institute of Technology Indore, led by Prof. Abhirup Datta.

Our research focuses on the development and application of radio astronomy and space-science technologies to address key scientific questions related to the Universe, heliosphere, and near-Earth space environment. The lab integrates theory, observations, instrumentation, and data analysis, with strong expertise in both ground-based and space-based systems.

 Core Research Areas

Radio Astronomy & Radio Cosmology (including low-frequency and 21-cm science)

Space Weather & Ionospheric Physics

Space Technology & Scientific Instrumentation

Radio Interferometry, Calibration, and Signal Processing

Data Analysis, Astrostatistics, and Machine Learning Applications

 Our Mission

Advance fundamental research in astronomy, astrophysics, and space science

Develop innovative instrumentation and analysis techniques for space and ground missions

Foster interdisciplinary collaboration across science and engineering

Train students and early-career researchers through hands-on research and mentoring

STARC Lab is committed to conducting impact-driven research, contributing to national and international collaborations, and nurturing the next generation of scientists and engineers in space science and technology.

Name: Aclectic Systems Inc.
Description:
Aclectic Systems Inc., specializes in application specific computing, and has in-depth experience with system bring-up, device drivers, heterogeneous parallel computing, coupled multi-physics simulation and 3D graphics.  The company is currently developing a custom hardware / software integrated appliance platform initially targeted at private cloud servers for visual high performance computing workloads.  Aclectic has extensive experience working with key corporations in this space including AMD, Intel, Microsoft, Pixar, ARM, and Xilinx, among others.  Aclectic's initial target market segments include (1) aerospace (CFD, parametric modeling and simulation, space access flight simulators), (2) Entertainment (Virtual Production, feature film visual effects and rendering), and (3) Supercomputing Centers whose workloads are dominated by adaptive multi-physics visual simulation.

Aclectic’s vision is to directly impact the bottom-line cost and quality of digital twins / productions by enabling reuse of procedurally generated assets, computation of behavior through physical simulation, and reduced time to solution through parallel execution. Aclectic's mission is to “Increase engineer / artist productivity by turning overnight simulations to into hourly simulations."

Name: Team MAVERICK
Description:
Planet formation is a mesmerizing, yet challenging, bottom-up process – from cosmic dust to planetary giants. However, this journey is hampered by critical growth barriers that simple collisions alone cannot overcome.

 Our Maverick Approach: We, team MAVERICK from the University of Duisburg-Essen, pose a vital question: What influence do magnetic fields have at this crucial stage? Despite magnetic fields being ubiquitous in the cradles of early planets, their role in forming dust aggregates has been largely overlooked.

This innovative, maverick approach demands to be tested!

 The Mission: Investigate the long-term stability of magnetically connected particle clusters under microgravity conditions.

 The Expected Outcome: We aim to determine the maximum possible size of these aggregates and define critical parameters governing their growth and destruction.

 How We Test It: Our experiment is a candidate for the upcoming REXUS 37/38 Campaign. We plan to utilize the suborbital flight environment to bring this innovative research to reality.

 Join Our Mission! We are actively seeking collaboration, sponsors and scientific exchange to propel MAVERICK toward success.

 Get in touch with us to learn more about our experiment and accompany us on our journey to overcome the barriers in planet formation!

Name: Vitamin Greens Inc.
Description: VitaminGreen’s mission is to support both Mars astronauts and our communities Earth-side by growing fresh produce with personalized nutritional quality to address neurocognitive challenges that arise from space travel and micronutrient deficiencies.  VitaminGreens meets 100% of your recommended daily allowance (RDA) by using a six step grow process to enhance plant vitamin biosynthesis.  We're taking vertical farm produce to the next level!

Name: Quasy Tech
Description:
Near-Earth space has become quite a crowded place since the first spacecraft were launched. Rising complexity demands we change our approach to operations from reactive to predictive.

Surely, Active Debris Removal programs are still our best hope but are years ahead in our future. In the meantime, we still have to handle an unprecedented level of complexity to protect our assets in space.

Quantum computing and AI technologies can help us accurately simulate
what is going to happen way before it happens thereby providing owners and operators with intelligent decisions-aiding tools for optimal spacecraft manoeuvring.

We believe that unleashing the power of current and future data with Quantum computing and AI to shift Space Collision Avoidance maneuvers and operations from reactive to predictive is the way.

This is what Quasy is all about.

Explore our website and web application prototypes.
We look forward to hearing from you, we’d love to get your feedback!

Help us make Quasy a reality, join us for the change!

https://www.quasy.tech

Name: StratoStar
Description:
ABOUT STRATOSTAR

StratoStar is an education company that travels cross country to start project based learning programs in junior high, high school, college, and graduate schools seeking to put a greater emphasis on their STEM Education initiatives.
 
Using weather balloons, easy to use software, and a teaching plan that has been implemented in schools at all levels, as well as, high profile government institutions, StratoStar takes students away from the textbook and into a real life mission.  Your students will plan, build, launch, and analyze the results of a successful balloon launch that will travel to the edge of space complete with video and computer generated flight analysis.

HOW DOES STRATOSTAR SUPPORT STEM THROUGH PROJECT BASED LEARNING?

It's easy to think of StratoStar as a weather balloon company.  However, we are first and foremost an education company looking to provide unique ways to encourage STEM Education initiatives.  We use weather balloons because we have found them to be the best vehicle for incorporating all areas of STEM and increasing education and absorption of subject matter.
 
Educational institutions and government agencies work with StratoStar as a partner to accomplish STEM based learning goals.

Name: BraiNAVLab
Description:
The BraiNAVLab is a laboratory located within the University of Verona, Italy. The mission of the lab is investigating and modeling the brain structure and function, as well as their associations with the underlying genetic determinants relying on statistical/machine and deep learning methods. The main current research activities focus on modeling structural/functional/effective connectivity, brain microstructure, imaging genetics and brain computer interfaces (BCI). 

Regarding ML/DL, the focus is on explainable, methods allowing to open the box and decrypt the outcomes of the algorithms. The lab activities cover a wide spectrum of applications ranging from neurosciences to translational, with particular emphasis of neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer's, multiple sclerosys and stroke. The lab is also actively participating to the activities of international standardization initiatives that is ISO/IEC JTC1 within the JPEG DNA IG and DICOM WG32.

By combining robust GNSS hardware with intuitive control and monitoring software, PIXAM makes advanced positioning and automation accessible to farmers, engineers, and researchers for applications such as agriculture, surveying, and smart infrastructure.

Our vision: to bring the potential of space to every organisation.
By moving boldly and early to embrace the wealth of data, services and other resources available in or from space, public sector bodies and private enterprise organisations in the UK have the opportunity to effect performance-transforming change, and establish long term competitive advantage on the global stage.

We see space data and capabilities as an already rich resource offering possibilities for organisations in every field to improve performance, productivity and efficiency.

With the certainty of exponential expansion of these space-related resources in the years ahead, our aim is both to evangelise for this potent resource, and to be world leaders in assisting in its successful utilisation.

Name: Exploration Spaceport
Description:
One of 14 FAA-licensed spaceports in the nation, Exploration Spaceport is under the direction of the Titusville-Cocoa Airport Authority (TCAA) and is located at Space Coast Regional Airport in Titusville, Florida. Licensed for horizontal launch and landing activities, Exploration Spaceport is conveniently situated approximately six miles from Kennedy Space Center and offers easy access to I-95, U.S. Highway 1, Port Canaveral (and FTZ-136), SR 405 (NASA Causeway), and rail service.

Development is underway for multiple industrial facilities that will be able to accommodate a variety of needs, such as satellite processing areas, high-tech manufacturing, clean rooms, high bays, and more. Corporate hangars are also in the development phase. 

The quality of life on Florida's Space Coast is unparalleled and boasts a temperate year-round climate, a multitude of outdoor and recreational amenities, and plenty of new residential construction. We have become an essential hub for the commercial space industry.

Our website is coming soon and will provide additional information on everything Exploration Spaceport has to offer. So, give us a call at 321.276.8780 and tell us about your endeavor! We would love to meet you!

At a time when space is becoming part of our shared reality, this project brings together diverse voices : artists, scientists, designers, writers, educators, and more, to reflect on how we are shaping this new chapter beyond our planet, and what it reveals about who we are here on Earth.

Name: Mimir Space Technologies
Description: Mimir is building a platform to compute data in space to empower humanity to seek answers to the biggest questions on and beyond earth. Modern satellites produce 100s Tb data each day, and with the falling cost to orbit we are now producing more data than can physically be sent back (the physical bandwidth limit of light). However only a fraction of this data is useful, thus calculating the results in space can reduce the amount of data having to be sent back cutting both cost and time to action for an end-user. Data requires compute which has been true from the early days of the home-computers (Spreadsheets/Documents + Microprocessor = Apple & Microsoft) to more recently AI (Big data + Parallel Compute = OpenAI & Nvidia), and the same will be true for Space. We are on the mission to build the compute platform for the New Space Age, that if successful will power a multi-trillion dollar industry!

Over time, we expanded into developing cutting-edge technologies like CubeSat flight computers, weather satellites, and reusable launch systems. Description:

Guided by the mentorship of highly qualified professionals, our society blends academic knowledge with real-world application, while helping members develop the technical and leadership skills essential to their future careers.

This year marks an exciting chapter for the UNB Rocketry Society as we prepare to compete on multiple national stages:

Zaphod (Restricted Category) — a high-powered rocket engineered to reach 10,000 feet, developed for competition at Launch Canada, where our team will showcase advanced propulsion, recovery, and structural design.

Vortex — an aerial drone being prepared for the Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) Competition, expanding our Society’s expertise in autonomous flight and mission execution.

Rocket Engine Project (Technology Development Challenge) — Details still in the works, stay tuned!

Think of TransLunar as a protostar - a not-yet star that is still forming and evolving. If you are trans and gender diverse in the space ecosystem or ally, please say hello!

While using the phrasing “trans and gender diverse people”, TransLunar acknowledges that this umbrella phrase can never capture the many ways in which trans and gender diverse people self-identify.  

TransLunar also recognises there is no singular trans and gender diverse experience, and that trans and gender diverse people have intersecting identities, making each experience unique.

Name: AIMVIE
Description:
Vietnam’s extensive mangrove forests (138,318 hectares), with a carbon sequestration rate of around ten times that of terrestrial forests, should be helping to battle climate change for the country. In addition, mangrove forests provide additional ecosystem services such as nursery and breeding grounds for a myriad of species, water filtration, sediment trapping, and coastal protection for the eight million Vietnamese citizens living along the coastline. However, 38% of mangroves have been lost in recent decades, and more data is needed to track mangrove changes and implement restoration.

We propose to develop AI models that fuse satellite and drone imagery to accurately map the mangrove extent in Vietnam at a high resolution. We will develop AI models to assess the carbon storage capacity of mangrove forests, which could be used in Vietnam’s Nationally Determined Contribution. The team will also implement a mangrove monitoring system to provide real-time information on the mangrove forests’ health and help plan restoration. The research will help protect and expand the mangrove forests, capture carbon dioxide, and provide the Vietnam coastline and its residents with better protection from landslides and extreme weather.

WTP SERVICES 

1. Private Astronaut Representation
2. PAM Support Services
3. Private Missions
(See website for full details)

WHY DO WE EXIST?

WTP Space is built on the conviction that human spaceflight needs a truly competitive and collaborative ecosystem. Drawing from our pioneering role in the earliest commercial Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs), we bring unmatched operational expertise to drive innovation, lower barriers, and evolve the PAM landscape—delivering smarter, customer-focused missions through WTP Space. 

STRATEGIC OVERVIEW 

WTP Space leads the evolution of commercial human spaceflight through our groundbreaking Independent Mission Operator (IMO) model for Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs) to Commercial LEO Destinations (CLDs). 

We bridge the future: accelerating the integration of emerging CLDs with full-spectrum mission services and operations as lead provider or strategic partner in a thriving, multi-operator ecosystem.

By serving as a specialized, neutral third-party operator for private astronaut missions and payload integration across multiple CLDs, an IMO standardizes mission optimization, logistics, and integration protocols. CLDs stand to benefit significantly from subcontracting such an IMO: it allows each station to focus on core platform development and operations while leveraging shared expertise to maximize utilization, stabilize pricing, enhance customer experiences, and drive higher returns on orbital time—ultimately fostering a more robust and interconnected LEO economy.

Name: Global Space League
Description:
Global Space League (GSL)
Strategic Framework for International Cooperation, Innovation, and Sustainable Space Development

Executive Summary
The Global Space League (GSL) is an international, multidisciplinary coalition that fosters collaboration and structured competition in the advancement of space exploration, technology development, and planetary sustainability. GSL functions as both a consortium and competitive platform, uniting national agencies, private enterprises, academic institutions, and civic innovators under a globally governed league format.

Mission Statement
To accelerate humanity’s advancement into space through structured global cooperation, competitive innovation, and inclusive participation—ensuring responsible, equitable, and scalable solutions for long-term space habitation and utilization.

Vision Statement
To establish the Global Space League as the premier international platform that governs, incentivizes, and supports a unified approach to space exploration, commercialization, and colonization across planetary frontiers.

Strategic Objectives
Standardize global cooperation frameworks for peaceful space exploration and development.

Advance applied research and development through structured challenge programs and missions.

Foster a sustainable space economy by integrating public, private, and academic sectors.

Name: Clean It up (C.I.U.)
Description:
CIU (Clean It Up) is building the orbital risk-intelligence layer and bounty marketplace for low Earth orbit.

CIU is not a spacecraft company. We do not build debris “chasers.”

We operate on top of the existing space traffic management ecosystem, transforming orbital data into neutral, quantitative risk signals that governments, operators, and insurers can act on.

As orbital congestion accelerates, collision risk has reached a point where single failure events can plausibly trigger cascading impacts in days, not months. Governments are already funding debris-removal missions, and operators are spending billions annually on monitoring, avoidance maneuvers, and compliance. What’s missing is a shared, independent way to prioritize which objects matter most and how mitigation should be priced.

CIU fills that gap.

Our platform delivers:
AI-driven classification and clustering of orbital objects
Transparent risk scoring and prioritization
A bounty-style marketplace aligning cleanup incentives across stakeholders

Because CIU is software-first and leverages existing space situational awareness data, it scales economically as congestion grows. Its value increases with regulation, coordination complexity, and orbital density.

Turning Space Risk Into Actionable Intelligence.

Name: VOLANDO project
Description:
VOLANDO (VOlcanic pLume chAracterization using sounNDing balloOns) is a PRIN (Progetti di Rilevante Interesse Nazionale) project started in September 2023 with a duration of 2 years.

In this project, three innovative balloon borne systems, equipped with different instrumentations for ash and gases measurement and sampling, will be developed. A customized “volcanosonde” sensor package will be designed, manufactured and airlifted by sounding balloons in free-flying and tethered configurations or will be dropped by a high-altitude balloon from above the volcanic cloud. It will include a sensor package for ash (concentration and PSD), gases (H2O, CO2, SO2 and HCl) and atmospheric parameters (pressure and temperature) estimations, telemetry and monitoring system. A dedicated mission software will be also developed for the real time analysis of the data collected. All the configurations of the “volcanosondes” are planned to provide a profile of the species with a spatial resolution of at least 50 m. The tethered balloon will also provide a high temporal resolution and relatively long-term coverage, while the sensors dropped by the high-altitude balloon will be able to profile the cloud at different locations. This will allow us to make a precise estimation of the volcanic cloud structure, ash and gases parameters, thus to both improve and validate satellite, ground based and dispersion models products.

After an extensive testing phase in the laboratory and in flight, a number of field campaigns will be organized on several Italian volcanoes having different topographic characteristics such as Vulcano, Stromboli and Etna. The results of the VOLANDO project will provide, for the first time, a comprehensive view of a volcanic cloud inner structure and composition contributing to understand volcanic processes and to mitigate the risks on both local population and airspace.

As a spin-off of the Paul Scherrer Institute, SE2S GmbH excels in radiation detection systems, utilizing advanced technologies and cutting-edge electronics. The company also develops ground support equipment and software to facilitate radiation detection.

SE2S GmbH provides a comprehensive range of radiation qualification and testing services. Their extensive offerings include radiation testing, detector calibration, and the development of systems and electronics for radiation qualification. They possess extensive expertise in radiation hardness assurance, ensuring the durability and resilience of their products.

In addition to their hardware solutions, SE2S GmbH offers radiation analysis software and services. Their proficiency lies in space environment and radiation modeling, enabling them to accurately calculate and simulate radiation environments. They are also highly skilled in assessing the effects of radiation on electronics.

By combining their proficiency in radiation qualification, particle detection, and data analysis, SE2S GmbH delivers innovative solutions that effectively tackle the complex challenges of the high-tech industry.

Name: Space Data Centers
Description:
Overcoming RF bandwidth and latency limitations of existing "every satellite has it's own downlink" scenarios requires radically new solutions. Among others: Aggregating data at the edge, i.e. in space, creating actionable insights at the edge, i.e. yet again in space, high-speed point-to-point networks between aggregation points, i.e. space data centres, distributed workload management, artificial intelligence, hardware- and software acceleration of workloads and networks etc.
In this project we derive, study and benchmark different space data centre architectures for selected use-cases, from user-requirements down to the hardware and software stack and the network architecture. 
We are building a publicly available tool for everyone to play with, select key-technologies, configurations, scale and system architectures to, as objectively as possible, compare different scenarios.
We are excited to have assembled a highly interdisciplinary team from KP Labs and IBM bringing together expertise ranging from AI/ML, hardware, software, data centre design and operation, networking, security, space craft integration, edge computing software & hardware, etc.
On top of that, key stakeholders from the space industry are building our backbone on our advisory board.
If you would like to get involved in any way, don't hesitate to reach out to us by direct contact or leave us a post.

Name: Instytut Nauk Meteorologiczno-Astronomicznych w Piaskach - Stowarzyszenie
Description:
„Związek nieba i Ziemi to równanie, które staramy się rozwiązać.” 

W nieskończonej przestrzeni rozpiętej między gwiazdami a kroplą rosy, między ruchem planet a wiatrem nad równinami Piasków, istnieje głęboka, choć nie zawsze widoczna, jedność.
To właśnie tę jedność – fundamentalne powiązanie między tym, co ponad nami, a tym, co pod stopami – próbujemy zrozumieć, badać i wyrazić językiem nauki.

Niebo nie jest dla nas tylko przedmiotem kontemplacji – jest źródłem danych, polem obserwacji, archiwum przyszłości i przeszłości.
Każde zjawisko – od burzy magnetycznej po opad śniegu – jest dla nas zmienną w równaniu, które łączy astronomię z meteorologią, fizykę z geografią, światło z wodą, czas z przestrzenią.

W duchu naszego patrona, Henryka Arctowskiego – badacza, podróżnika, myśliciela epoki wielkich odkryć – podejmujemy próbę połączenia nauk przyrodniczych i kosmicznych w jedno spójne spojrzenie na świat.
Bo wierzymy, że przyszłość Ziemi nie może być rozumiana bez spojrzenia ku niebu – a przyszłość nieba nie będzie miała znaczenia, jeśli nie zatroszczymy się o Ziemię.

 Nasza praca – to obserwacja, analiza i synteza.
 Naszym narzędziem – wiedza, skromność i pasja odkrywcy.
 Naszym celem – zrozumienie i służba: lokalnej wspólnocie, nauce i przyszłym pokoleniom.

Tak powstaje nowa przestrzeń myślenia – Instytut, w którym spotykają się nie tylko dziedziny wiedzy, ale także pokolenia i światy.

Związek nieba i Ziemi nie jest już tylko poezją – jest zadaniem naukowym. I my to zadanie podejmujemy.

Name: CySpace Connected Capabilities Network

The domain of space is becoming increasingly congested, contested and competed as space actors, state and private enterprise, seek to reap the benefits space systems can provide. As innovation continues to broaden the capabilities of space systems, these are playing an increasing role in facilitating functions of society, economy and national security. Space is identified as part of our Critical National Infrastructure and strengthening the security of our space assets must be a priority for the sector and government to ensure our social and economic prosperity.  

The CySpace Connected Capability Network aims to create a more resilient space domain by bringing together partners from across industry, academia and government within and outside the space sector to map relevant capabilities and expertise that can be leveraged to create new products and services that address our most critical vulnerabilities.

This CySpace CCN is led by Space West and delivered in partnership with Frazer-Nash Consultancy, Space South Central, North West Space Cluster, Space North East, Space Wales, Space East and NI Space as well as our cyber cluster from across the UK. As the first indigenous manufacturer of diamond wafers and quantum-grade diamond materials, we are proud to drive innovation in sectors where performance and durability are non-negotiable—space, defense, quantum computing, energy, and advanced electronics.

Our core expertise lies in producing ultra-pure single-crystal and polycrystalline diamond wafers engineered for extreme environments and cutting-edge performance. These wafers offer unmatched thermal conductivity, exceptional radiation resistance, optical transparency, and quantum-grade stability, enabling breakthroughs far beyond the capabilities of conventional semiconductors like silicon, GaN, and SiC.

We enable disruptive technologies across key sectors:

Quantum computing: Diamond NV centers enable ultra-stable, low-decoherence quantum systems.

High-power electronics: Our diamond wafers manage extreme voltages and thermal loads in aerospace and defense systems.

Photonics & optics: High-purity diamond substrates enhance performance in lasers, sensors, and LiDAR systems.

Thermal management: Diamond’s unmatched conductivity provides ideal heat dissipation for chips and high-frequency devices.
In parallel, we are pioneering the future of long-lasting energy with our nuclear diamond battery technology. These solid-state batteries, powered by radioisotopes and encapsulated in diamond, deliver maintenance-free, ultra-long-life power—ideal for deep space missions, remote sensing, and defense-grade IoT systems where conventional batteries fail.
With a relentless commitment to sustainability, innovation, and precision engineering, we are laying the material foundation for tomorrow’s intelligent, resilient, and energy-efficient systems. Our technologies are shaping the future from quantum labs to the edge of space.

Diamond isn’t just rare — it’s essential. Engineered in carbon, perfected by science.

Name: SOLER Project
Description:
The SOLER Project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon Europe research and innovation programme (Grant agreement ID: 101134999). 

This project will investigate the most energetic phenomena occurring at the Sun and provide new knowledge on their interrelations, variability and energy partitioning. 

Using the newly expanded unprecedented heliospheric spacecraft fleet we investigate energetic solar eruptions starting from three perspectives: fast coronal mass ejections (CMEs), strong X-ray flares, and large solar energetic particle (SEP) events. Key parameters of the various eruption phenomena will be determined and their interrelations examined to make significant leaps in our understanding on how the eruptive phenomena are linked to each other, how they interact with each other, and how they result in acceleration and release of high energy particles from the solar corona into interplanetary (IP) space. 

Because of their direct link to particle energisation, large-amplitude coronal waves and shocks related to these events will be in focus as well. Magnetic connections of sources with each other and with the in-situ observers will be determined. 

In addition to producing significant amounts of new scientific knowledge in the field, SOLER will provide the wider scientific community a wide array of advanced data products, and novel data analysis and visualisation tools that will be openly distributed. This will greatly facilitate future interdisciplinary studies of energetic solar eruptions.

Založena byla 8. prosince 1917 a k roku 2024 má 744 členů organizovaných v místních pobočkách a odborných sekcích, například sekce Proměnné hvězdy a exoplanety nebo Společnost pro meziplanetární hmotu. Mezi naše kolektivní členy patří hvězdárny, vědecké ústavy a další instituce. ČAS je v evropském kontextu unikátní tím, že propojuje profesionály i nadšené amatéry.

Nejvyšším orgánem společnosti je sjezd, který se koná každé 4 roky, mezi sjezdy práci řídí výkonný výbor. Předsedkyní ČAS je Soňa Ehlerová. Naším hlavním informačním kanálem je server Astro.cz, který od roku 1995 poskytuje aktuální informace a popularizační obsah z oblasti astronomie.

We approach this domain with the mindset of engineers and system builders, not speculators. The objective is not speed, but correctness; not publicity, but capability. Meaningful progress in space requires patient design, rigorous validation, and collaboration across disciplines such as robotics, remote sensing, materials science, and planetary geology.

LUNABERS is currently in its early research and systems-development phase, laying the architectural and technical groundwork for future demonstration missions and institutional partnerships.

We are building carefully, with long horizons in mind.

Name: Journal of Eco Astronomy
Description: The exploration of celestial and terrestrial phenomena has long been a cornerstone of scientific inquiry, necessitating a multidisciplinary approach that integrates diverse fields of research. In response to this need, the Journal of Eco Astronomy (JEA) was established in 2017 by Eco Astronomy Inc., providing a comprehensive platform that unites Astronomy, Astrobiology, Planetary Geology, Paleontology, Earth science and Planetary Analogs and Archeoastronomy. As an open-access journal, JEA is committed to the widespread dissemination of original research, emphasizing ethical observational practices and systematic experimentation in science education and evaluation. By prioritizing the publication of cutting-edge research from the Eco Astronomy Institute and its affiliated scientists, JEA serves as a vital conduit for sharing significant scientific discoveries with the global scientific community. The journal’s content spans a broad spectrum, from comprehensive reviews addressing cross-disciplinary topics to focused studies delving into specific scientific phenomena. Through its dedication to fostering collaboration among researchers from diverse scientific domains, JEA not only advances the understanding of astronomical and Earth sciences but also upholds the highest standards of scientific integrity and ethical research methodologies. JEA has officially begun accepting submissions that incorporate AI-assisted writing, with a key requirement: all content must maintain originality and reflect genuine human insight. This progressive approach recognizes the growing role of AI in content creation while ensuring that human wisdom, creativity, and critical thinking remain at the core of every piece. By adhering to rigorous peer review processes, JEA continues to play a pivotal role in the evolution of knowledge in eco-astronomy and its interconnected disciplines, contributing to the broader scientific discourse and innovation.

Name: DISPEC Project
Description:
The DISPEC project offers new high-level data products based on advanced data processing techniques that improve data quality, provides estimates of ionospheric characteristics based on the joint processing of space and ground data, provides results from post-processing of data for improved ionospheric specification, and exploits long-term time series for the study of long-term trends in the ionosphere in connection to atmospheric long-term dynamics and geophysical phenomena. Specifically, DISPEC derived high-level data products aim at:
- Providing fast processing of time series that lead to curated data.
- Deriving new high-level data products based on the exploitation of raw data from multi-locations and multi-instruments, useful for input to ionospheric specification models.
- Deriving new ionospheric indicators based on the post-processing of raw satellite and ground-based instruments’ data useful for a quick mapping of ongoing ionospheric irregularities.
- Providing proxies for geophysical phenomena and long-term trends in the ionosphere.

The main objective of DISPEC project is the exploitation of bottomside and topside ionospheric data, provided by space missions – such as Swarm, DORIS, GRACE, GRACE-FO, Spire, COSMIC-2 – and by ground-based GNSS receivers and ionosonde sounders, to support research activities for improved ionospheric specification, through the derivation of high-level data products. The project outcomes have the potential to complement the ESA Space Science Archives and the Space Weather Network, the data collections provided by the global networks of ionosondes and GNSS ground based receivers, and to enhance the capacity of European Research Infrastructures.

------------

Name: Space Renaissance 4 All
Description:
Space Renaissance 4 All is a worldwide joint initiative to bring science, art and technology to space. Spearheaded by Bernard Foing

“Space Renaissance for All Gallery”  integrated and ready for 2025 launch with Nyx capsule
LUNEX EMMESI (EuroMoonMars Earth Space Innovation) has partnered with SRI Space Renaissance international for the initiative “Space SDG18 : Space4All on Earth and Beyond”. Space SDG18 is endorsed by more than 110 institutions, and covers many countries, disciplines joining space with science, technology, astronautics, humanities, business, society and arts.
LUNEX  EMMESI has developed a “Space Renaissance for All Gallery” SR4AG payload for space. It will be carried on board of The Exploration Company’s Nyx cargo capsule Mission Possible, due to be launched by SpaceX in June 2025 and recovered after Earth re-entry.
The “Space Renaissance for All Gallery” will carry samples from partners that have collaborated with EuroMoonMars, as well as science, cultural and artefacts supporting Space4All SDG18 initiative. The payload uses a spare of a sub-tray from EXPOSE-R astrobiology payload  that was sent to the ISS International Space Station (2009-2011). Each sample fits within a cylinder slot of 10 mm diameter by 8 mm depth. Some samples from partners will consist of a glass disk with thickness 4 mm that will carry a printed logo.
The director and curator of “Space Renaissance for All” project is Prof Bernard Foing (president SRI & CEO LUNEX EMMESI), assisted by Adriano Autino, Sabine Heinz (SRI vice-presidents), Frank Molster, Boudewijn, Kuipers, Frans Folst (LiS),  Mary Kuiper, Marieke Feenstra & Johanna Nyqvist (MoonMars Museum) LUNEX Space4All/EuroMoonMars students (Eanna Doyle, Helena Tataj) and partners. Benefits will go to Space4All SDG18 project.  Organisations that collaborated with LUNEX, Space Renaissance & Space4All SDG18 are  represented with artefacts/logos placed on special 10mm glass disks in lower tray.

Name: PAST (Pundarikaksha Agency of Space Technology)
Description:
Pundarikaksha Agency of Space Technology (PAST)
Founded by Fleming Panchal
Pundarikaksha Agency of Space Technology (PAST) is a visionary space research and development initiative founded by Fleming Panchal. The organization is committed to advancing model rocketry, hybrid propulsion systems, and innovative aerospace technologies through hands-on experimentation and engineering excellence.
The Meaning Behind Our Name
The name "Pundarikaksha" is derived from Sanskrit, meaning "Lotus-Eyed," a sacred title of Lord Krishna. It represents clarity of vision, divine focus, and higher purpose. Inspired by this philosophy, PAST aims to combine cultural depth with scientific innovation to reach new heights in space exploration.
What We Do
PAST focuses on practical, ground-up aerospace development. Our work includes:
•	Designing and building hybrid-fuel model rockets
•	Precision fabrication of custom rocket parts using lathe machines and CNC tools
•	Developing avionics and telemetry systems for real-time data tracking
•	Creating reliable recovery systems for safe return
•	Conducting mission simulations and ground testing
•	Supporting STEM learning and engaging students through real-world space projects
•	Exploring mission concepts capable of reaching the edge of space
Our Vision
Our goal is to establish a student-led space agency rooted in Indian values and dedicated to scientific advancement. We believe meaningful innovation starts with imagination, hard work, and purpose.
About the Founder
Fleming Panchal is a passionate engineer and the founder of PAST. He leads the organization with a hands-on approach, bringing together mechanical skill, curiosity, and a deep commitment to making space technology more accessible and inspiring for the next generation.
Based in India
Led by vision. Built by effort. Inspired by culture.

Our mission is simple yet far-reaching: to ensure that high-quality space environmental data reaches the people who need it most — to protect, navigate, and shape the future, both in and beyond space.
