Name: SpaceAI
Description:
Space is becoming critical infrastructure.

Satellites, launch systems, space stations, and defense constellations now underpin communications, navigation, earth observation, climate monitoring, and national security. Yet many space systems are still operated with fragmented software, delayed telemetry, and manual decision loops.

This isn’t a launch problem.
It’s a control and coordination problem.

Mission planning, satellite operations, ground systems, collision avoidance, payload scheduling, and space domain awareness are optimized in isolation. Decisions are made with incomplete data in environments where seconds matter.

Space systems should behave like coordinated infrastructure.

SpaceAI builds AI assets that help space companies and agencies operate space systems more effectively.
We develop intelligence layers—models, agents, and decision-support engines—that integrate with existing flight software, ground control, and mission systems to improve planning, safety, and operational efficiency.

Our AI assets continuously model orbital dynamics, system health, mission constraints, and environmental risk—predicting failures, identifying conflicts, and recommending optimal actions in real time, with humans supervising instead of reacting.

A small improvement in mission efficiency, satellite utilization, or anomaly detection can unlock hundreds of millions in value—without new launches or hardware.

AI assets don’t replace mission control.
They upgrade space control systems.

The future of space isn’t more launches.
It’s better coordination across space infrastructure.

That’s SpaceAI.

Description:
The Australian Rocket Systems Training Network (ARSTN) is a coordinating body of government, educational and industry organisations which aims to establish a world class training and education ecosystem in Australia in support of all aspects of rocketry and propulsion for the defence and space sectors. We aim for a sector-wide approach covering vocational, professional and research education as well as targeted training to upskill workers and provide experiential training and internships for students in tertiary institutions. ARSTN’s relationship with internationally recognised researchers and educators, access to world class laboratory and computational facilities, and proximity to the talent pool coming out of Australia’s numerous award-winning University rocketry teams are keys to our future success.

ARSTN was conceived in 2023 through a series of workshops to discuss education and training in propulsion and launch technologies to meet the Australian Government’s manufacturing ambitions related to the Guided Weapons and Explosive Ordnance (GWEO) Enterprise as well as ongoing commercial and government interests in access to space. These workshops were hosted by the University of Sydney and involved representation from the University of Queensland, Monash University, and RMIT, as well as representatives from Airforce, Navy, VET colleges, the Australian Space Agency, Defence Science and Technology Group (DSTG), Engineers Australia, Austrade, NSW Premier’s Department, State and Commonwealth Government departments, and more than 30 private and public companies.

Our mission is to navigate infinities, resolve long-standing paradoxes, and develop new mathematical and computational frameworks that push the boundaries of human understanding.

At YFRL, we focus on:
- Mathematical Innovation – Advancing Yearning-Based Infinity Mathematics to redefine recursion, computability, and set theory.
- AI & Decision Intelligence – Creating models for non-halting recursive AI architectures and self-expanding knowledge systems.
- Philosophy & Physics – Investigating the nature of self, paradox resolution, and universal structures.
- Collaborative Research – Partnering with leading universities, research institutions, and industry pioneers to drive the next era of discovery.

By reimagining computation, knowledge, and infinity itself, YFRL is laying the foundation for a new intellectual renaissance—one that transcends disciplines and reshapes the way we perceive and interact with reality.

Name: OUROBOROS Project
Description:
OUROBOROS is a student-led electric propulsion research and development team at the University of Alabama in Huntsville. Our team is dedicated to advancing Hall-effect thruster technology, with the goal of completing a five-year development cycle to complete a Hall thruster suitable for a 12U cubesat.

We are working to mature a Hall thruster design that incorporates advanced features including magnetic shielding, and a low-cost glow discharge hollow cathode. Optimized for argon propellant, the final thruster will be integrable with CubeSat-class nanosatellites, providing a low-cost alternative to operators such as small businesses and universities who have historically had few options for on-orbit propulsion.

In pursuit of these goals, the OUROBOROS project provides students with hands-on, cutting-edge propulsion experience, contributing to the broader research community, preparing members for entry into the industry, and demonstrating the feasibility of student-developed space propulsion technologies. Emphasis is placed on interdisciplinary collaboration and the recruitment of undergraduate and graduate students to further the team’s goals.

Name: Ingenion LLC
Description:
By working closely with our clients, we are able to construct custom-made electronic test benches that verify the operation of their designs against their design requirements. This process involves the following services.

Design Requirements
Analyzing and reviewing design requirements and/or specifications for completeness, testability, and traceability.
 
Test Bench Architecture
We will architect a test bench for simulation and lab testing that is capable of verifying your requirements using automated, self-checking directed and random-constrained tests. 
 
IP Core Development
We develop the IP cores and software needed to implement the test bench.  Each IP core is fully documented and revision controlled.
 
Test Bench Integration and Testing
We purchase, integrate, and test elements of the test bench.  Elements may include COTS equipment, bench test equipment, measurement tool, or programmable equipment like our Total Verification System.  We integrate these elements together into a unified environment that is controlled by the test software.  A certification procedure is developed that validates the proper operation of the test bench.
 
Test Bench Documentation
We provide thorough documentation that fully describes the capability, use, and operation of the test bench. 
 
Design Verification Plan and Procedure
Using design requirements and specifications, we compile a verification plan that describes exactly how the functionality of the client's design will be tested by the test bench.  Care is taken to clearly show the traceability of each requirement and design feature to a test. 
 
Coverage Analysis
Using coverage tools and methods, we perform coverage analysis to demonstrate the completeness of the test bench delivered.  Holes in testing uncovered during this step are addressed to achieve an acceptable level of coverage.

Name: Origin Space
Description:
Origin Space is a student-led science and technology team under AAFI – UNAM, focused on space exploration research and development with academic, scientific, and strongly social impact commitments decentralizing scientific knowledge with public outreach and science communication.

We integrate engineering, AI, robotics, astrobiology, and planetary sciences to design and develop experimental projects inspired by real space missions. Our work bridges astronomy and exploration, transforming scientific knowledge into functional engineering solutions.

Current projects include:
- Planetary rovers for terrestrial and simulated extraterrestrial exploration
- Virtual Martian environment simulators for navigation, risk analysis, and mission planning
- Astrobiology and bio-signature research, including laboratory simulations of extraterrestrial environments
- Autonomous navigation systems using AI
- Concept development of aerial–ground hybrid exploration systems inspired by NASA’s Ingenuity

Beyond technical development, Origin Space is committed to:
- Scientific outreach, public engagement, and science communication
- Decentralization of knowledge through field activities, workshops, and astronomy events
- Interdisciplinary education and student training for industries
- Collaboration with academic institutions, industry, and science communicators

 Collaborations & Sponsorships
Origin Space maintains active collaboration with Blended Learning by MIT which we are part of the Global Talent Community (GTC), a network formed by some of the world’s most prestigious universities and academic institutions.

Our work is supported by strategic partners and sponsors, including:
- Grupo SSC, Mescalea, Ansys
- Steren

These collaborations enable us to strengthen our technical capabilities, research methodologies, and educational outreach.

We remain open to new collaborations, partnerships, and sponsorships to continue expanding our projects and keep improving our path and future.

Name: Exellios
Description:
La actual generación de soluciones IOT son mucho más avanzados que sus predecesoras, a menudo llamadas de primera generación, que se caracterizan por su diseño simple y funcionalidad muy básica. La segunda ola de dispositivos M2M a menudo se diseñan como sistemas colaborativos basados en la comunidad. Por esta razón, el balanceo de carga efectivo del tráfico de estos sistemas M2M es esencial.

Hay otro factor importante a tener en cuenta en los sistemas M2M – IOT ya que van a multiplicarse de forma masiva la gran cantidad de dispositivos que se adhieran a Internet. Exellios dispone tanto dispositivos hardware como virtuales preparados para el paso hacia este desafío.

El concepto M2M (Machine to machine) engloba una serie de tecnologías que se utilizan en el proceso de intercambio de información entre dos máquinas remotas, ya sea mediante redes fijas o móviles, y sin intervención humana.

En este contexto de sobreabundancia de información, EXELLIOS [ I+D ] está desarrollando nuevas plataformas para recopilar y administrar la gran cantidad de información que origina el uso del M2M. Entre sus objetivos está ofrecer nuevas fuentes de información a los usuarios, transformar los flujos de datos disponibles de las ciudades inteligentes en información viable y habilitar el procesamiento masivo de datos de manera flexible y escalable para mejorar el control de los mismos.

Name: Center for Detectors

The Center for Detectors designs, develops, and implements photon devices to enable scientific discoveries. The CfD educates and trains students through research and development in detectors, instrumentation, observational astrophysics, nanostructures, silicon photonics, quantum optics and photonics, and wide bandgap materials.

Established in 2010, it includes seven professors and over two dozen students at all levels of matriculation. Staff and student researchers investigate high impact engineering and development problems through external financial support from federal agencies, private foundations, national laboratories, and industry. The CfD operates nine laboratories in three buildings and has an average of 32 externally funded projects (~$2.5M/year).

Name: Space DDS Ltd.
Description: A startup. To address the need to reduce the number of space debris orbiting the Earth, the innovative low-cost Space Debris Deorbiting System (Space DDS) is under development. It will allow frequent rate intercepting and decelerating of debris to the velocity necessary for subsequent deorbiting. The system is based on an existing level of technology and aims to achieve a low cost for each deorbiting mission by utilizing available commercial launch systems, common environmentally friendly chemicals, and reusable hardware. The targeted size of the space debris can vary from tiny untraceable particles to full-size relict boosters and defunct satellites.

Name: Cosmo Equidad
Description:
At CosmoEquidad, we are redefining the future of space through gender equity, education, and inclusive policy.
We are a specialized consulting firm dedicated to ensuring that the aerospace sector reflects the diversity and potential of all humanity. Our mission is to create an equitable space industry where women and underrepresented groups not only participate in decision-making, but also see their realities and needs reflected in policies, programs, and workplaces.
Our Expertise
Founded by Lourdes García Hernández, political scientist and project manager with over a decade of experience in space policy, international cooperation, and gender equity, Cosmo Equidad builds on work with the Mexican Space Agency (AEM), the United Nations Office for Outer Space Affairs (UNOOSA), Women in Aerospace networks, and international organizations.
What We Do
Analyze space policies and programs to identify and eliminate gender bias.
Support governments, agencies, and companies in designing inclusive governance frameworks.
Review and redesign training and education programs in aerospace and STEM with a gender lens.
Provide strategic consulting for equitable hiring, promotion, and workplace transformation.
Conduct market and workforce studies with a focus on equity and inclusion.
Our Approach
We go beyond traditional diversity initiatives by addressing the full spectrum of inclusion: ergonomic work environments, adapted space suits, hormonal health, caregiving responsibilities, and comprehensive well-being.
Our Vision
We envision an aerospace sector where equity drives innovation. From policy rooms to training simulators, Cosmo Equidad works to ensure that inclusion is not an afterthought but a foundation for progress — on Earth, in orbit, and beyond.

Name: Beyond Vision: Physics meets AI 2023
Description:
Workshop organized in conjuction with ICIAP 2023, 11-15 September 2023, Udine (Italy) The workshop Beyond Vision: Physics meets AI, to be held within the 22nd International Conference on Image Analysis and Processing (ICIAP 2023), is the first ICIAP workshop dedicated to applications of Statistical Learning methods and Artificial Intelligence techniques to Nuclear and other Physics-based Imaging technologies. In the recent years, we have witnessed the incredible rise of Artificial Intelligence in all physical sciences: from Computer Vision techniques driven by Nuclear Imaging for painting restoration, to deep generative models used to look for signs of New Physics with the big experiments at CERN. Those technologies are becoming ubiquitus in research activities, pushing research towards the most advanced tools and techniques developed in Computer Vision. The aim of the workshop is to furnish researchers with different backgrounds a place to share experiences, learn best practices and contribute to the discussion about the current challenges on the application of Statistical Learning and Artificial Intelligence methods to various physics domains.

Link:  https://physicsmeetsai.github.io/beyond-vision/
Submissions (CMT3): https://cmt3.research.microsoft.com/BVPAI2023

Name: Mimir
Description: Mimir is building a platform to compute data in space to empower humanity to seek answers to the biggest questions on and beyond earth. Modern satellites produce 100s Tb data each day, and with the falling cost to orbit we are now producing more data than can physically be sent back (the physical bandwidth limit of light). However only a fraction of this data is useful, thus calculating the results in space can reduce the amount of data having to be sent back cutting both cost and time to action for an end-user. Data requires compute which has been true from the early days of the home-computers (Spreadsheets/Documents + Microprocessor = Apple & Microsoft) to more recently AI (Big data + Parallel Compute = OpenAI & Nvidia), and the same will be true for Space. We are on the mission to build the compute platform for the New Space Age, that if successful will power a multi-trillion dollar industry!

Without the technology innovation of the Space Age…we'll be back in the Stone Age.

Our collective goal as a team is to achieve fully autonomous systems that do not rely on the traditional sensor-decision-act pipeline. We are currently focusing on developing data-driven techniques that can bring us closer to achieving full autonomy in aerospace systems.

Description:
Infineo Space is dedicated to the business and economics of space. We cut through the hype to analyse the market models, investment flows, competitive landscapes, and commercial strategies shaping the global space sector.

From in‑orbit servicing and refuelling to Earth observation, launch economics, lunar infrastructure, and orbital sustainability, we spotlight the ventures, investors, and policies driving commercial growth. Our focus is on actionable intelligence for founders, investors, policymakers, and industry leaders who want to understand not just the technology, but the economics behind it.

Expect deep dives into:

- Competitive analysis of startups and incumbents across orbital and lunar domains

- Economic modelling of emerging markets like ISRU, SBSP, orbital logistics, and lunar development

- Policy impacts on commercialisation, investment, and long-term sustainability

We believe the next decade of space will be defined not only by engineering breakthroughs, but by business models that scale.

Description:
After obtaining a balloon flight ticket from the French space agency CNES students of M2TSI work on a cubesat based balloon, LACRA (Lab for Aerosol Counting Research in the Atmosphere). This cubesat carries Two experiments on board in a stratospheric flight; one is an exact copy of the main instrument of ALMA, the OSAC, and the other one is the METEO France OPC (Optical Particle Counter).
The launch of LACRA successfully took place at the end of March and is a collaboration between Université Paul Sabatier Toulouse III, CNES, UNIVERSEH European University, ISAE Supaero, Luleå University of Technology and METEO France.

Description:
We are a student-led rover team made up of 10 engineers and computer scientists from SwanSEDS at Swansea University seeking to compete in the Olympus Rover Trials (ORT) at Airbus. This is our first year competing in the Advanced ORT stream, designing and manufacturing a functional Mars rover for competition this summer! 

Our mission is to develop an autonomous rover capable of remotely locating and imaging QR codes of varying sizes, a challenge designed to simulate missions on Mars where human intervention and control are minimal.

Swandler I hopes to be a solid foundation that future teams will be able to build upon and improve beyond our currently conceivable ideas. Our goal is to not only do as best we can but also convince other engineering undergraduates to get stuck in and bring their creativity and skills to future missions.

Name: Fibonacci Cellars
Description:
The Future of Agricultural Evolution Starts with Wine.

Founded by Alexandra Jenkins, a former SpaceX operations professional, Fibonacci Cellars is pioneering the intersection of luxury, science, and sustainability through the cosmos.

Headquartered in Cape Canaveral, Florida, Fibonacci Cellars is redefining what it means to create, cultivate, and collect. By sending fine wine into orbit to age in microgravity, we explore how cosmic conditions influence the molecular evolution, taste, and structure of wine — advancing both luxury craftsmanship and agricultural innovation.

Each space-aged bottle tells a dual story: one of ultra-luxury provenance and one of scientific discovery. Through partnerships with Lady Rocket Foundation and Copernic Space, Fibonacci Cellars is democratizing access to space while funding agricultural and sustainability research that benefits life on Earth.

Our work bridges premium winemaking, blockchain authenticity, and space research to create a new era of experiential luxury — one that connects collectors, investors, and scientists in a shared mission to explore and evolve.

Fibonacci Cellars is more than wine. It’s a movement to expand human taste, technology, and understanding beyond gravity itself.

Core Pillars:
 Space-Aged Wine Research & Provenance
 Sustainable Agriculture & Space-Aided Viticulture
 Luxury Collaborations & Ultra-Limited Space Bottles
 Blockchain & Tokenized Ownership with Copernic Space
 Partnerships with Lady Rocket Foundation for Research & Access
 Scientific Innovation for Earth & Beyond

Name: منصة فكر لخدمات البحوث والاستشارات الاكاديمية
Description: منصة فكر لخدمات البحوث والاستشارات الاكاديمية هي منصة متطورة تعمل على توجيه الطلاب على طول مساراتهم الأكاديمية منذ إنشائها في عام 2011. نحن نفخر بأننا نقف كشركة رائدة عالميًا في منصات خدمات البحثية للشركات والطلبة الاكاديميون، ملتزمة بإعادة تشكيل مشهد الدعم الأكاديمي والبحثي. تقدم المنصة محرك البحث المبتكر الخاص بنا:  لتلبية الاحتياجات المتعلقة بالبحث عن افكار ومقترحات بحثية للطلاب الذين يبحثون عن مقترحات بحثية غير مكرره. يدعم هذا المحرك عددًا لا يحصى من المساعي الأكاديمية مثل خدمات الدراسات العليا لرسائل الماجستير والدكتوراه و بالاظافة الى مشاريع التخرج والأوراق البحثية. يغطي محرك البحث الخاص بنا مجموعة متنوعة من المجالات، مع التركيز الشديد على التطورات الأخيرة في علوم البيانات، والذكاء الاصطناعي (بما في ذلك أفكار التعلم العميق والتعلم الآلي)، وعناوين تكنولوجيا المعلومات، ومفاهيم الإدارة، وموضوعات هندسة البرمجيات، ومبادرات الأمن السيبراني، وعلوم البيانات. الابتكارات. نحن ملتزمون بتوفير مستودع شامل للأفكار التي لا تلبي توقعات الطلاب في مختلف التخصصات على مدار السنوات. كما نوفر دورات خاصة للباحثين وشوراحات مناهج البحث العلمي بكافة انواعها. انضم إلينا في استكشاف طليعة الإلهام الأكاديمي والابتكار.

Name: JMJ Propulsion Labs

JMJ Propulsion Labs is an SBA 8(a) certified aerospace and defense technology firm headquartered in Detroit, Michigan. We deliver NDAA-compliant unmanned aerial systems (UAS), AI-driven analytics, cybersecurity, and mission resilience solutions for federal, civilian, and allied partners.

As a small disadvantaged business with DBE and MBE certifications, JMJ ensures compliance with Buy America and NDAA Section 848, making our drone platforms and services fully eligible for DoD, DHS, and federal agencies.

Our core capabilities include:
• UAS Solutions for ISR, logistics, perimeter security, and smart farming.
• Tactical Drone-as-a-Service (DaaS) with modular payloads and C4ISR readiness.
• AI/ML & Edge Computing for adaptive control and real-time analytics.
• Cybersecurity & CMMC 2.0 compliance for secure drone networks.
• Mission Resilience through radar, telecom, and satellite sustainment.

Past performance highlights include supporting smart agriculture NDVI analysis, telecom infrastructure monitoring, and CMMC-aligned UAS command networks.

Led by CEO Oscar Netto, JMJ Propulsion Labs combines aerospace expertise with federal procurement intelligence to deliver innovation, compliance, and operational readiness.

At JMJ Propulsion Labs, we don’t just build systems — we propel missions forward.

Name: ExoLabs
Description:
We operate as a research lab dedicated to investigating the question of habitability beyond Earth. By leveraging publicly available exoplanet datasets—primarily from NASA’s Kepler mission—ExoLabs focuses on identifying the key factors that make planets potentially livable, while creating tools and frameworks to understand and visualize these distant worlds.

Our flagship research effort, the Eden Program, catalogs confirmed exoplanets and evaluates their potential to support life through a comparative analysis framework. This initiative lays the groundwork for our long-term goal: to transition from analyzing known worlds to independently validating and detecting new ones through future programs like Lumen.

ExoLabs was founded following award-winning research recognized by NASA, rooted in a vision to reimagine how we study planetary habitability. Alongside our scientific work, we’re building educational tools, outreach materials, and visual media designed to inspire curiosity and communicate complex ideas in an engaging, human way.

We believe meaningful science shouldn’t be confined to institutional walls. ExoLabs is built on the principle that discovery belongs to everyone—and that bold, creative thinking has a place at the frontiers of space science.

Whether you’re a researcher, educator, collaborator, or explorer at heart, we invite you to join us in asking one of the most profound questions humanity has ever faced:
What makes a world livable—and where might we find the next one?

Name: Gravity Assist
Description: The history of the beginning of interplanetary space travel, and the exploration of the solar system only occurred one time and will never happen again. Therefore, it is important in the history of science to preserve an accurate documented account of this history, and the key breakthrough invention that made it possible. It was achieved by the invention of gravity propelled interplanetary space travel popularly known as "gravity-assist trajectories." The invention broke all the high-energy barriers of interplanetary space travel based on the classical theory of space travel using reaction propulsion, and, as a result, made it not only possible to explore the entire solar system, but it also made it easy with small launch vehicles propelled by conventional chemical propulsion. It resulted in all of NASA's high-energy deep-space gravity propelled multi planetary missions used to explore the entire solar system!!

Name: Cosmic Tortuga
Description:
Space is humanity’s next frontier — but right now it’s a junkyard moving at 17,000 mph. One dead satellite, one lost bolt, one forgotten rocket stage can end a billion-dollar mission. We fix that. With humans in the loop and tortoises in our soul.

What we do:
1. Big Debris Relocation - Our crewed spacecraft rendezvous with large objects (dead sats, rocket bodies, mystery chunks). Pilots and robotic arms gently grab, boost to a safe graveyard orbit, and log every gram for future recycling.
2. Precision Orbit Sweeping - Swarms of orbital drones guided by human operators on-site sweep constellations clean of smaller debris. Think street-sweeper meets starfighter squadron — leaving pristine lanes for your satellites.
3. Heritage Recovery Missions - We catch historic spacecraft (think early comms sats, Apollo-era relics, even Cold-War spy birds) and bring them home intact. Delivered to museums, universities, or private collectors who want a real piece of space history on their mantle.

Every mission is human-led from a manned spacecraft. Real pilots, real engineers, real eyes on real screens — backed by autonomous drones that never sleep. Nothing is left behind. Everything we move gets cataloged, recycled, or returned.

Why humans + drones? Because space doesn’t forgive automation-only mistakes. A tortoise doesn’t rush; it verifies. We’re the same: slow enough to be safe, stubborn enough to get it perfect.

The payoff:
- Launch providers get guaranteed clean orbits
- Satellite operators get lower insurance rates
- Museums get authentic artifacts
- Collectors get bragging rights
- Humanity gets a reusable junkyard instead of a growing hazard

We’re not just cleaning space. We’re turning yesterday’s trash into tomorrow’s treasure — one careful maneuver at a time.

Name: SHROOM BEXUS
Description:
Shroom BEXUS is a student-led stratospheric flight experiment developed within the REXUS/BEXUS Programme (17th cycle), organized by DLR and SNSA in cooperation with ESA. The project investigates the radiation-attenuation potential and post-irradiation biological response of selected radiotrophic fungi under near-space conditions.

The experiment exposes multiple fungal samples and ground-reference controls to the stratospheric radiation environment during a high-altitude balloon flight. Individual radiation sensors positioned beneath each sample measure transmitted radiation, enabling quantitative comparison between fungal shielding performance and control media. Environmental parameters including temperature, pressure, and humidity are actively regulated within an airtight payload to ensure controlled and repeatable experimental conditions.

The payload architecture consists of an aluminum structural frame housing isolated sample compartments, integrated radiation sensors, an onboard computer, and a thermal control system. The design prioritizes modularity, sensor fidelity, and compliance with REXUS/BEXUS flight and safety requirements.

Post-flight laboratory analysis focuses on growth kinetics, colony morphology, and final biomass to assess the long-term biological effects of irradiation and to correlate biological response with in-flight radiation exposure. The results aim to evaluate the feasibility of radiotrophic fungi as self-growing, biologically based radiation shielding concepts for future space exploration and habitation scenarios.

The balloon launch campaign is planned for October 2026.

Name: KILL SAT
Description:
KILL SAT is a next-generation orbital sanitation and space sustainability company focused on active debris removal and decommissioning of obsolete or non-functional satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), Medium Earth Orbit (MEO), and Geostationary Orbit (GEO). In a time where humanity’s ambitions in space are colliding with decades of neglected infrastructure and rising risks of Kessler Syndrome, KILL SAT operates at the intersection of precision engineering, orbital mechanics, and planetary stewardship.

Our mission is simple: remove what no longer serves, before it becomes a threat.

KILL SAT deploys specialized kill vehicles, tracking systems, and autonomous orbital drones designed to identify, capture, and deorbit defunct satellites and dangerous fragments. We focus on:
	•	Actively tracking unstable or defunct satellites using advanced radar cross-section and optical telemetry data.
	•	Neutralizing collision threats using non-explosive tethered capture tech, net-based recovery, and controlled atmospheric deorbiting.
	•	Partnering with governments, defense agencies, and commercial satellite operators to maintain clean and secure orbital corridors.
	•	Preventing future debris events by offering predictive analytics, end-of-life satellite deorbit consulting, and post-mission disposal services.

Whether it’s remnants from Cold War space programs, rogue anti-satellite (ASAT) test fallout, or outdated commercial payloads, KILL SAT exists to restore Earth’s orbital commons — one object at a time.

Name: Orbital Data Ethics Council - ODEC
Description:
The Orbital Data Ethics Council (ODEC) is an independent, international, and non-profit body dedicated to establishing ethical, transparent, and sustainable governance for data generated and processed in outer space. As humanity’s digital infrastructure expands into orbit through satellite constellations, orbital cloud systems, and AI-driven analytics, ODEC works to ensure that this new data ecosystem operates in accordance with universal principles of fairness, accountability, and planetary stewardship.

By developing ethical standards, certification frameworks, and multi-stakeholder dialogue platforms, ODEC bridges the gap between existing space law and terrestrial data governance. It serves as a neutral forum for governments, industry, academia, and civil society to collaborate on shared rules and practices for responsible orbital data management — safeguarding trust in the information systems that will shape the future of the orbital economy.

Name: SARA Team
Description:
The SARA team is composed of aerospace engineering students from Politecnico di Torino that performed a feasibility study (phases 0 and A) of a possible space mission servicing the Arctic. The mission's statement is reported below:

"The SARA mission aims to support and promote economic growth and further development of local communities in the Arctic region. This objective will be met by providing crucial information that allows for: safer, hence more frequent navigation, civil and environmental protection purposes, and scientific research, addressing the shortcomings of the current Earth observation missions servicing the region. The mission will provide high-resolution radar images of the Arctic on a sub-daily basis and in all-weather conditions, increasing awareness of sea ice concentration and drift in a rapidly changing environment. Further processing will provide wind data for the area, which permits the determination of the most suitable locations for wind turbine installation. Optical imaging will assist and integrate radar data during the Arctic summer. The mission will also allow for a better understanding of ice behaviour in the region, unlocking new possibilities in nautical route planning and scientific research."

Name: Iris Data Labs
Description:
The Intelligence Platform for Autonomous Space Exploration

SpaceEdge by IrisDataLabs is the Intelligence Platform for Autonomous Space Missions.
We convert raw, heterogeneous space datasets from NASA, ESA, and commercial sources into production-grade, machine-usable intelligence for landers, rovers, orbiters, and robotic outposts. Through deep mapping, harmonization, validation, and dual-layer scoring, SpaceEdge creates a trusted intelligence foundation that autonomous systems can rely on.

SpaceEdge is not a workflow — it is the operating layer that standardizes, validates, and distributes the intelligence required for mission autonomy.

What We Build
	•	Semantic Intelligence Graphs
A unified graph that connects datasets, instruments, missions, terrain features, and environmental context.
	•	Dual-Layer Scoring Engine
Metadata trust + data quality scoring for certification-grade validation of mission-critical datasets.
	•	Auto-Harmonized Schema Layer
Automatic alignment of projections, units, timestamps, and ontology mappings to eliminate integration friction.
	•	Mission-Ready Intelligence APIs
Domain modules optimized for autonomy, onboard decision-making, and constrained environments.

From lunar terrain intelligence to orbital autonomy models, SpaceEdge provides the foundational intelligence layer that next-generation space missions will depend on.

Live Now

Lunar Landing Site API — analyzing 1.18M candidate locations across the lunar south pole, built entirely on SpaceEdge’s trusted mapping and scoring infrastructure.

Our satellite network is engineered to provide global coverage every 24 hours, capturing rich spectral data across hundreds of bands. This enables the detection, monitoring, and prediction of complex environmental and industrial phenomena in real time. From tracking crop health and soil composition in agriculture, to identifying mineral compositions in mining, monitoring deforestation and water quality in environmental protection, and optimizing infrastructure and consumption patterns in the energy sector—Ural Space empowers decision-makers with actionable intelligence derived from space.

But we’re not just imaging the Earth—we’re decoding it. Our advanced analytical tools leverage AI and machine learning to transform raw hyperspectral data into meaningful insights, offering clients across industries a strategic advantage in sustainability, risk management, and operational efficiency.

In addition to our commercial satellite program, Ural Space is actively engaged in cutting-edge research across a range of new space technologies. This includes innovation in remote sensing methodologies, onboard AI systems, advanced data compression algorithms, and next-gen satellite propulsion systems. Through these efforts, we aim to not only enhance the capabilities of our own platform but also contribute to the broader evolution of space-based data science.

At Ural Space, we are bridging Earth and orbit to create a smarter, more responsive world—powered by precision from space.

Name: Clubul de Fizica
Description: As a community of passionate learners, we have continuously worked on many projects throughout the past two years. By far our most notable achievement was forming AuDacia, a team of now 12th-graders who set to conquer the solar system last year when they won the first prize at the international competition NSS Gerard K O'Neill Space Settlement Contest. AuDacia developed a highly original concept of a space settlement and received an invitation from the organizers of the International Space Development Conference to give an oral presentation about our work in front of NASA engineers and other experts in the field of space. A few months later, we came up with a concept for a small cubical satellite. This project included gathering important atmospheric data, while also detecting the land form below the satellite in order to compute the best possible landing spot. Later competing in the European Competition Qube2Space, we reached the finals out of 40 teams for a contest for funds and were invited to the University of Science and Technology Politechnica Bucharest, during the biggest European Robotics Festival (RoboFest 2023), to build our concept. At the end of the competition, we won 4th place out of 16 finalist teams, gaining a spot on a waiting list to be launched into space by university rockets.

The project is designed as a reference for strategy, investment, product, and engineering teams who want to base their work on structured, transparent lasercom data.

Lasercom Intelligence currently covers:

- Optical Communication Terminals (OCTs): vendor and terminal landscape
- OCT Tenders: public opportunities involving space laser communications
- Missions & Constellations: who flies optical links, where and when
- OGS Networks: optical ground stations and network operators
- Standards Tracking: SDA, ESTOL, CCSDS, ECSS and related frameworks

We also publish occasional briefings and short summary notes highlighting notable market and technology developments.

Lasercom Intelligence does not use confidential, proprietary, or non-public information.

Name: DRAKON
Description:
DRAKON is a human-centered orbital intelligence platform built to help satellite operators make fast, confident decisions in an increasingly congested space environment. As Low Earth Orbit fills with satellites and debris, traditional SSA tools overwhelm users with data but fail to communicate risk clearly when seconds matter.
DRAKON changes this by transforming complex orbital behavior into intuitive, visual insight - revealing constellation structures, congestion hotspots, and close-approach risks at a glance. It combines high-fidelity 3D visualization with stress-optimized workflows and built-in maneuver guidance, ensuring operators can understand threats and act immediately without switching between scattered tools. Designed to scale from tens to tens of thousands of objects, DRAKON enables safer missions, fewer false alerts, and stronger operational confidence

Name: LifeFinder SGLT Foundation
Description:
Solar Gravitational Lens Telescope (SGLT) missions involve sending fleets of small-telescope & instrument-laden spacecraft deep into interstellar space in the opposite direction, relative to the sun, to where previously determined most-promising earth-like, potentially intelligent-life inhabited, exoplanets reside.

Once the SGLT spacecraft reach the minimum 550+ Astronautical Unit (AU) SGL-focal region out in interstellar space, they look back at the Sun in a direct line-of-sight focal line to the earth-like exoplanet (with the Sun in-between) and – exploiting the remarkable optical properties of an SGL astronomical telescope – extract spectroscopy data on and a detailed direct megapixel high-resolution image of that exoplanet from the distorted exoplanet image & spectroscopy data contained in the Einstein ring, formed through gravitational lensing, that is visible around the Sun.

And while an SGL telescope is theoretically feasible, the scope & precision-standards of the engineering, material science, propulsion, navigation, data transmission, & other challenges of an SGLT mission are unprecedented & will require substantial financial & human expertise resources in order to design spacecraft to operate at such vast distances from Earth & with the needed precision for SGLT data collection & for the transmission of the Einstein ring-displayed data on the exoplanet back to earth for analysis.

With an SGLT mission being the only technologically viable means to directly observe, through high-resolution megapixel imaging, & assess the potential habitability of and unambiguously detect & study intelligent life-forms on an earth-like exoplanet – there's been growing public interest & enthusiasm about the possibilities & promise of a successful SGLT mission, not only with respect to finally having an answer to that age-old question of “Are we alone in the Universe?”, but also through ascribing new meaning & perspective for the human species on our place in the universe.

Whether neutralizing attacks on satellites, safeguarding ground-based infrastructure, or conducting cyber-operations to protect space assets, Space Special Forces embody the precision and agility required to counter emerging threats.

Beyond defense, these elite teams play a proactive role in identifying vulnerabilities, innovating solutions, and ensuring mission success in high-stakes environments. Name: 3D and Modelling Team - PBA IMF DLR
Description:
The 3D and Modeling Team at IMF has a long heritage in generating Digital Surface Models (DSMs) from optical stereo data, beginning with the first digital stereo camera mission (MOMS) in the early 1990s. Since then, the team has continuously advanced stereo image matching, enabling city-scale 3D modeling from space using very high-resolution imagery such as WorldView, IKONOS, GeoEye, Pléiades, Cartosat, and ZY3. Their work goes beyond DSM creation toward high-level semantic modeling, focusing on building footprint extraction, 3D change detection, classification, and full 3D reconstruction of buildings and trees. These developments support applications in urban growth analysis, infrastructure monitoring, disaster mapping, and environmental studies.

Current research focuses on four main areas:
- DSM/DTM Generation: Advanced algorithms for DSM/DTM production from spaceborne, airborne, and UAV imagery. A modified Semi-Global Matching (SGM) algorithm integrates a hierarchical search strategy and hybrid cost functions (Census, Mutual Information, CNN-based) for faster and denser results. The software, licensed to GAF AG, is operationally used for commercial DSM/DTM generation.
- 3D Change Detection: Developing automatic change detection methods for urban and forest environments by fusing DSMs and optical data. Region- and fusion-based approaches utilize multi-temporal stereo imagery to detect surface and structural changes.
- Building and Infrastructure Reconstruction: Integrating DSMs with multispectral and panchromatic data to overcome resolution limits and achieve detailed 3D models. The team combines traditional methods (segmentation, line detection) with deep learning (fully convolutional networks).
- 3D Forest Monitoring: Quantitative analysis of forest structure using multi-sensor datasets (WorldView, Cartosat, PRISM, RapidEye, LiDAR), supporting large-scale forest change analysis and close-range 3D reconstruction of trees.

Name: Amalgamated Data Union Local 42, LLC
Description:
Amalgamated Data Union Local 42 is a consulting firm founded and run by Dr. Demitri Muna, an astronomer, professional software developer, and interface designer. It specializes in enabling scientists to significantly reduce the time in going from data to science. This is accomplished by designing data interfaces that are domain-specific, i.e., speaking the same language as the users of the data instead of forcing them to learn the tedious intricacies of data storage and access.

A particular emphasis is placed on the user interface (UI) and user experience (UX), which may mean:

• a Python interface that encapsulates not only data access but documention, journal references, related/derived data, units, and more
• a data design language that makes traversing multiple datasets, sources, and formats transparent
• clean API that are human readable and intuitive to use
• integrating all of the above into graphical user interfaces that pull data from multiple, disparate sources

The underlying design principle is the aggressive removal of work from the user to handle deterministic implementaion details required to access, use, parse, or visualize data. It is in this way our interfaces allow the user to focus on understanding what the data are saying – the science, reducing the time to discovery.

Muna also has extensive teaching experience having created SciCoder (http://scicoder.org), an intensive five day data science workshop aimed at early career researchers. Over 14 workshops have been run at institutions such as NYU, Yale, the American Museum of Natural History, and as far away as Perth, Australia.

The company is built on top of 20+ years of professional research, software development, project management, interface design, and teaching experience. Muna has worked in multiple domains including astrophysics (Jet Propulsion Laboratory), medical patient data (Dascena, Inc.), genomics (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory), and commercial desktop and mobile applications.

O produto central é uma solução completa de monitoramento via satélite, desenvolvida especificamente para atender às necessidades dos agricultores e investidores agrícolas que enfrentam o desafio de gerir a agricultura tropical de média a grande extensão.
Em um mundo onde as mudanças ambientais são constantes, os agricultores se deparam com novos desafios diariamente. Essas mudanças não afetam apenas a viabilidade do cultivo, mas também têm um impacto direto na produtividade e nos rendimentos das lavouras. Nossa missão na Data Situ é fornecer as ferramentas e os insights necessários para que os agricultores possam enfrentar esses desafios de forma eficaz e sustentável.
Nossa solução de monitoramento permite que os agricultores tenham uma visão detalhada e em tempo real de suas lavouras e pastagens. Utilizando aprendizado de máquina e estatística avançada, a tecnologia interpreta dados complexos e os transforma em insights práticos. Isso possibilita aos agricultores fazerem ajustes em tempo hábil, otimizarem a utilização de recursos, insumos e, melhorando assim, o rendimento de suas lavouras.

Name: Association d'Astronomie elBattani -AAB
Description:
L'association d'astronomie Elbattani s'engage pleinement dans la promotion de l'astronomie auprès du grand public. Son objectif principal est de susciter l'intérêt et de partager la fascination pour l'univers au travers d'une variété d'activités enrichissantes.
Pour atteindre cet objectif, Elbattani propose des cours interactifs, accessibles à tous les niveaux, afin de familiariser les passionnés d'astronomie et les novices avec les concepts fondamentaux de cette science passionnante.
En plus des cours, l'association organise des ateliers pratiques où les membres peuvent se plonger dans des expérimentations concrètes. Ces ateliers permettent aux participants de manipuler des télescopes, de découvrir les différentes constellations, d'apprendre à reconnaître les planètes et d'explorer les mystères de l'espace en observant des objets célestes fascinants. Ils offrent ainsi une occasion unique d'expérimenter par eux-mêmes et de développer leurs compétences en observation astronomique.
Elbattani organise également des sorties pédagogiques pour offrir aux membres l'opportunité de s'immerger dans des lieux propices à l'observation du ciel étoilé. Ces sorties sont des moments privilégiés où les participants peuvent échanger avec des amateures dastronomie, participer à des conférences spécialisées et vivre des expériences uniques sous le ciel nocturne. Elles permettent de créer des liens forts entre les membres et de renforcer leur passion commune pour l'astronomie.

En somme, l'association Elbattani met tout en œuvre pour éduquer, inspirer et divertir le grand public en proposant des cours, des ateliers et des sorties pédagogiques. Elle aspire à créer une communauté engagée et curieuse, où la fascination pour l'astronomie se transmet de génération en génération.

Name: Brendani Space
Description:
Brendani Space is a student-led aerospace team designing an advanced spacecraft for an interstellar comet interceptor mission. Brendani's Interstellar Comet Interceptor (ICI) will be capable of intercepting and studying a comet entering the solar system from interstellar space — one of the rarest and most scientifically valuable opportunities in deep space exploration.

We're not stopping there! After rendezvous with the interstellar object, the ICI will continue it's journey toward the heliopause boundary to collect data on the edge of our solar system. This makes the Brendani ICI a dual purpose mission - conducting novel research, as well as building on the legacy of missions like Voyager and New Horizons.

Our Mission
To engineer a high-performance, reliable spacecraft capable of intercepting and collecting scientific data from an interstellar comet, and then continuing on to explore the outer limits of our solar system.

We're responsible for the entire design, including propulsion, guidance, navigation and control (GNC), communications, power, structures, thermal management, and scientific payload integration. 

We're not just designing a spacecraft, we're opening a window to the unknown.

Name: SFA San Diego
Description:
Space Force Association – San Diego (SFA San Diego) serves as a vital bridge between the space and maritime communities in one of the nation’s most strategically important defense hubs. 

Situated at the intersection of space operations, naval warfare, and joint force integration, our chapter strengthens collaboration across the United States Space Force and the United States Navy, industry, and academia.


San Diego plays a unique role in the national security space enterprise, particularly through its deep operational ties to naval space systems and satellite communications. A prime example is the region’s longstanding connection to the Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) program, an essential capability enabling secure, beyond-line-of-sight communications for joint and coalition forces worldwide. MUOS exemplifies how space and maritime domains are operationally intertwined, with San Diego at the center of that integration.


SFA San Diego is committed to supporting Guardians and Sailors alike by fostering professional development, advancing public understanding of spacepower, and advocating for policies that strengthen assured access to space and joint warfighting superiority. Through events, partnerships, and community engagement, we ensure San Diego remains a cornerstone of America’s space and maritime advantage.

The mission is driven by the desire to investigate one of humanity's greatest scientific questions - are we alone in the universe?
This mission is informed by scientific experts in hot springs, early Earth, and Mars geology and designed by engineers with world-leading capabilities for sample return. LifeSpringsMars targets hot spring opaline silica deposits discovered by the Spirit Rover in the Columbia Hills that may represent humanity’s best chance to find signs of past life on Mars.

This project is an example of Telepresence: The projection of human minds to space, of being here (Earth) and there (the Moon).  As George Friedman,  Founder of Geopolitical Futures, best-selling author, and geopolitical forecaster says:  "Telepresence will be an indispensable technology on the moon, bringing the human mind to the moon, while allowing the human body to remain on the earth." 
Limitless Telepresence, working in collaboration with the University of California, San Diego's Innovation Center and San Diego State University’s VizCenter  -- among other universities, brands, and industry -- is excited to announce the first in an ambitious series of lunar-focused competitions, contests, and events to expand the concept of telepresence for the good of humankind.  Robert Harkins, SDSU's VizCenter Director, refers to the center 
as "a gateway to the Moon ... and a collaborative space where scientists, students, and community members come together to turn imagination into reality."

Description:
Astrolith is a pioneering Web3 company utilizing digital art (NFTs) to advance the conversation around space resource utilization (SRU) and champion diversity within the space and tech sectors.

Our Mission and Vision
Our mission is to powerfully connect humanity's future in space with solutions for Earth's most urgent problems, ranging from racial inequity to natural resource depletion. We showcase how off-world innovation can directly benefit life here. Our vision is to inspire a new generation of galactic pioneers and ambassadors from a planet worth saving.

Driving Diversity in STEAM
We are dedicated to increasing gender and racial diversity across STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Math) and Web3, sectors where women of color are significantly underrepresented. Through our art, we specifically celebrate and elevate the contributions of black women in the space industry.

Our Foundation
Astrolith was founded by Lisa Haley and Taryn Haley Van Deusen, a mother-daughter team with specialized backgrounds in the field of space resource utilization. As the first NFT company to feature three generations of women on its team—including Taryn's daughter as the design muse—Astrolith is built on a foundation of generational expertise and shared commitment to a better future.

As nations like the US, China, India, and Japan deploy valuable assets into space, we aim to be the first company on Earth extending their operational life — while applying the same technology to critical assets here, in industries that face harsh environments, inaccessibility, and costly downtime.

Powered by ADAM (Autonomous Detection and Maintenance) and driven by our proprietary, patent pending Coatelligence, BAWA-786 can recognize surface conditions and act with precision, redefining maintenance in extreme conditions.

Our breakthrough technology is designed to serve:
 Oil & Gas

 Outer Space (Mars and the Moon)

Our competitive advantage lies in our unique image database and AI-driven Coatelligence, enabling BAWA-786 to make autonomous, highly accurate maintenance decisions — both on Earth and beyond.

¿Quiénes somos?

Asociación Interamericana en Materia Espacial -AIAME-, es un Organismo
Interamericano sin fines de lucro, que integra a instituciones americanas públicas,
privadas y del tercer sector, que están relacionadas o tienen interés con la materia
espacial, ya sea como generadoras o como usuarias de información y productos que
contengan datos satelitales que puedan ser útiles tanto en el sector productivo,
somo social o el ambiental.

La AIAME es el fruto del trabajo conjunto de las instituciones que desde 2014,
vienen desarrollo de productos y sistemas regionales de información satelital,
contando al momento de su fundación, con la Plataforma multiproducto SIRIS.

La plataforma SIRIS, utiliza información captada por satélites y la transforma en
aplicativos para el monitoreo en temáticas tales como agricultura, forestación,
hídrica, salud y gestión de emergencias de incendios, sequías, inundaciones,
desforestación, y que consideramos solo una muestra del amplísimo universo que
los datos satelitales ofrecen para mejorar la toma de decisiones.

Name: Longstride Dynamics Company
Description: Longstride Dynamics builds the autonomy stack that connects humans, robots, and remote sensing into one operational fabric. We engineer adaptive EVA exoskeletons paired with immersive VR training to make astronauts stronger, safer, and more efficient in partial‑gravity environments, informed by model‑predictive control and intent‑aware sensing. Our field systems extend from human mobility to autonomous construction: coordinated swarms that excavate, transport, and assemble using in‑situ resources with safety‑constrained learning and fault‑tolerant decentralization. The same stack enables force‑adaptive manipulation with sub‑millimeter precision and energy‑aware autonomy that scales from a single robot to large, heterogeneous teams. Headquartered in Malvern, Pennsylvania, our team spans aerospace robotics, AI‑driven perception, embedded systems, and mechanical design—focused on mission impact over hype. We build for the realities of space—dust, radiation, latency, limited power—designing systems that degrade gracefully and recover quickly. From Earth infrastructure to lunar base construction, we turn research‑grade algorithms into field‑ready systems that let people and robots work together—safely, efficiently, and sustainably—on Earth and beyond.

We aim to identify natural resources such as water, the key energy source on the Moon. In this way, Lunar Exploration can provide an instrumental tool for space development.

Lunar Exploration’s platform combines a wide range of mission datasets, including imagery, altimetry, spectroscopy, radar, neutron and gamma signatures, thermal models, and topographical data—into a harmonised 3D lunar environment.

Description: The Vermont Space Grant Consortium (VTSGC) is part of a national network of colleges and universities connecting NASA with the educational and research institutions of the United States. For college students, VTSGC funds NASA internships at NASA Centers or virtually, graduate fellowships to support students doing NASA-relevant research, and research experiences for undergraduates pursuing degrees in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM). Name: Progressor technology
Description:
In the startup Progressor & Co, we are engaged in researching the processes of oxygen combustion in energy systems. The goal of the research is to provide laboratory confirmation of a new perspective on the combustion process, shifting the paradigm from “fuel burns” to “oxygen burns.”
This means the possibility of converting any energy system that uses fossil fuels to perform useful work or produce energy to a new mode of operation without consuming organic fuel and with zero emissions in the combustion products, and without significant changes to its design.
Application of the technology: It immediately has high compatibility with the existing transport and energy infrastructure, as there is no need to produce new engines and energy systems that will operate on new principles, but to use existing capacities and simply reconfigure them to a new mode of operation without consuming organic fuel. This will allow the technology to be quickly introduced to the European and global markets.

Name: StellarPath
Description:
The AI Intelligence Layer for Space Operations

The space economy is expanding rapidly, but its intelligence layer remains fragmented. Space operations today are reactive, siloed, and reliant on outdated decision-making systems. StellarPath is changing that.


What We Do

StellarPath is building the AI-powered decision and operations layer for space industrialization, integrating orbital intelligence, planetary resource optimization, autonomous logistics, and space servicing into a unified framework. Our platform enables space operators to make real-time, data-driven decisions, optimizing safety, efficiency, and sustainability across the entire space economy.


Core Capabilities

- AI-Powered Orbital Intelligence – Predictive SSA, space traffic management, and multi-sensor data fusion for enhanced situational awareness.
- Autonomous Space Logistics – AI-driven refueling, servicing, debris removal, and infrastructure coordination.
- Planetary Intelligence for ISRU & Exploration – AI-enhanced geophysics, resource mapping, and autonomous extraction for sustainable space development.
- AI for Mission Planning & Operations – Real-time adaptive AI for trajectory planning, orbital risk mitigation, and autonomous decision support.
- Federated AI for Secure & Scalable Intelligence – Privacy-preserving AI models that enable collaborative intelligence across agencies, operators, and defense sectors.


Why StellarPath?

- Unified AI Decision-Support – We transform raw data into actionable intelligence, bridging orbital logistics, planetary resources, and space infrastructure.
- Scalable from LEO to Deep Space – Our AI-driven framework seamlessly integrates with commercial, defense, and scientific missions.
- Mission-Critical Autonomy – We enable predictive, AI-enhanced mission planning to reduce operational risk and optimize long-term space sustainability.

Join StellarPath. Build the Future of Space Intelligence.

Name: Hickson Extraplanetary Systems Institute (Off-World Society & Policy Design)
Description:
Hickson Extraplanetary Systems Institute exists to make sure humanity’s next chapter beyond Earth feels deeply human, not experimental or elite. Rather than treating space as a blank slate for the same mistakes made on Earth, HESI designs social, cultural, and policy operating systems that keep real people, real cultures, and everyday life at the center of off‑world planning.

HESI listens first on Earth—across neighborhoods, nations, and cultures—to understand what people actually need to feel safe, respected, and at home. Those insights are translated into space‑ready norms, protections, and daily living patterns that can guide orbital habitats, lunar stations, and early Mars societies. The work spans questions of governance, conflict, care, culture, food, and family life, focusing on how to build communities that feel familiar and fair even in unfamiliar environments.

Instead of leaving critical decisions to small groups living in bubbles—often far removed from everyday realities—HESI acts as a bridge between planetary citizens and future space operators. Through community education, dialog, and participatory design, people are invited into the conversation about where our species is headed and how they want that future to feel in their bodies, families, and cultures. Those perspectives feed directly into policy frameworks and design principles crafted to protect dignity and wellbeing at a biochemical, emotional, and social level.

As part of Hickson Endeavours, HESI connects with brain health, housing, and capital ventures to ensure that what is learned on Earth about thriving systems can be carried forward into orbit and beyond. The long view is simple: when humanity steps onto new worlds, it should bring better habits, wiser structures, and kinder defaults than the ones that caused harm here. HESI’s role is to help make that upgrade quiet, steady, and shared by as many cultures as possible.

Description:
PAI promuove la divulgazione dei principi, degli ideali, dei valori e dei saperi che, integrati, favoriscono l’attuazione della Cittadinanza planetaria, ovvero evidenziando come ciò che unisce l’umanità è più grande di ciò che la divide. Si attua così la via della cooperazione, della solidarietà civile, culturale e sociale senza confini, la sostenibilità, l’ecologia integrale e quindi quella che viene definita “Logica planetaria”, ossia l’aspirazione dell’arrivare a formare ogni persona sul nostro pianeta come un Cittadino della Terra, consapevole di sé e delle conseguenze delle proprie azioni, presenti e future.

Si pongono le basi per un’organizzazione capace di incrementare un “Dialogo planetario”, fondato sul quel luogo comune che è la ricerca del miglioramento ad oltranza, del bene comune e del giusto: un orizzonte condiviso da tutti gli interlocutori, al di là di ogni differenza e di qualunque diversità di idee. 

Quindi un dialogo che costituisce un tessuto relazionale di interazioni, fondato sulla capacità integrativa ed inclusiva del suo essere sempre riscoperto e promosso perché assuma occasioni di iniziative benefiche planetarie, in ogni ambito e contesto (ad es. sociale, economico, ecologico, ecc…). 

Si viene a creare un orizzonte condiviso da tutti, reso florido dalla diversità di idee che porta a concretizzarsi l’idea di promuovere una cultura planetaria, ampia e complessa, finalizzata alla creazione di un’unica comunità terrestre, in grado di lavorare cooperando attraverso tutti i cittadini che si riconoscono abitanti consapevoli della comune “Patria Terra”, nostra “Casa comune” e della Comunità planetaria tutta.

Ciò in quanto è necessario attuare un radicale ripensamento del modo di intendere le relazioni tra il tutto e le parti, fra un orizzonte unitario e comune di una Comunità planetaria e l’eterogeneità, le diversità che vanno certo mantenute per il miglior funzionamento della nostra civiltà, la quale abbisogna però di una convergenza dinamica,

Name: Terrax Geospatial
Description:
TerraX Geospatial – Inteligencia Geoespacial Aplicada

TerraX es una software especializado en analítica geoespacial avanzada para la exploración y gestión de recursos naturales. Combinamos imágenes satelitales, datos climáticos y de suelo, inteligencia artificial y modelos físicos para ofrecer soluciones predictivas y visuales que potencian la toma de decisiones en sectores estratégicos como:

 Petróleo y Gas
Monitoreo de infraestructura crítica (oleoductos y gasoductos), detección temprana de fugas y predicción de performance de pozos no convencionales.

 Minería
Detección de litio y uranio mediante índices espectrales personalizados, mapas de potencial geológico y análisis ambiental.

 Agricultura
Predicción de rendimientos, clasificación de cultivos, seguimiento de salud vegetal y análisis multitemporal de NDVI, GCI, NDMI y otros índices.

Nuestra plataforma combina procesamiento local y en la nube, y se despliega como una solución web interactiva basada en tecnologías modernas.

 Diferenciales de TerraX

Modelos híbridos: integración de modelos fisiológicos + machine learning.

Interfaz web intuitiva para clientes no técnicos.

Personalización total según el área de estudio o necesidad.

Infraestructura robusta para ejecutar análisis complejos en tiempo real.

This amalgamation of GPS prowess and networking expertise has set Dozera X on an upward trajectory toward becoming an unrivaled global player.

Gursharn Arya's unwavering commitment to innovation has led Dozera X to push the boundaries of satellite technology. By launching an impressive fleet of cutting-edge satellites into orbit, the company has not only transformed the way we connect with one another but has also revolutionized how we perceive the world itself. Moreover, Dozera X's relentless pursuit of excellence extends beyond technological marvels. The company is deeply invested in sustainable practices, recognizing the need to minimize its ecological.

Name: Kardashev Alliance
Description: The Kardashev Alliance is a visionary consortium dedicated to propelling humanity forward on its cosmic journey. Rooted in the principles of collaborative innovation and profound exploration, we seek to answer the beckoning call of the stars. Our principal venture, STARSHIP, encapsulates a bold mission: to pioneer the realms of faster-than-light (FTL) travel through a harmonized blend of generative artificial intelligence, advanced simulations, and reinforcement learning. Through this ambitious platform, we aim to optimize spacecraft designs, converting the imaginative lore of science fiction into a tangible reality for humankind. The Alliance believes in an inclusive and global endeavor, uniting thinkers, scientists, technologists, and dreamers from every corner of the globe. Our initiatives stretch beyond the technical realm, embracing diverse contributions from project management, marketing, social engagement, and much more. The Kardashev Alliance invites not only the intellectually curious but every individual who envisions a grander narrative for humanity within the vast cosmic theater. Together, we step towards transcending our current Kardashev Scale status, ushering in a new era of interstellar enlightenment and potential habitat expansion. As we lay down this path of audacious exploration and collective endeavor, we strive to ensure the enduring legacy and advancement of human civilization amidst the boundless cosmic frontiers. Join us in this historic venture, as we craft the narrative of human destiny, unifying our endeavors towards achieving the extraordinary and unveiling the cosmic unknown.

Name: Surrey AI Imaging Limited
Description:
We build AI imaging software that turns satellite data into useful products.

We have strong experience in computer vision and deep learning for Earth Observation (EO) data. Whatever product is needed from imaging data (enhancement, correction, detection, 3D, or analytics, etc.), we can quickly design and deploy best-fit, customised AI pipelines.

We also develop custom AI model optimisations for low-power (~3.5–7.5W) on-board platforms, including smallsats, drones, aerial and handheld devices, reducing computation and data costs while delivering insights much faster.

We have delivered and are delivering AI imaging solutions across multiple ESA-, UKSA-, FFG-, and NASA/JPL-funded projects, working with academic and industrial partners in the EO and planetary sectors.

UFDC will launch space police where their duty is to make sure anything coming into the Earth from other planets are unharmed and is legal. UFDC will contribute to building a space hub where star fleets and star shuttles can dock for refuel and move further for their missions, discovery, and expeditions.

UFDC will play an integral role in protecting earth from other space particles. Precautions will be made so nothing from Earth will be harmed.

UFDC will be contributing to making a college program for space for a university degree. With contribution coming from other space companies such as the USS, or the US Enterprise, UFDC will be rolling out different space-related majors to ready for the space planetary immigration.

■ Atmospheric Fuel Engineering
■ Space Medicine
■ Space Agriculture & Soil Science (Astrobiology / Astronomical Biology)
■ Terraform Engineering

The first rocket prototype that UFDC will be building is called Starlight.

UFDC will invest heavily on orbital satellite chains, StarSat, to connect Mars with Earth and build a galactic infrastructure for better connection between both planets.

Name: Space Exchange LTD.
Description:
Space Exchange is a neutral, evidence-led convener and think tank dedicated to orchestrating global collaboration in the space ecosystem to bridge gaps across industry, governance, and innovation. We aim to solve the problems of fragmentation, funding gaps, and policy inconsistency in the global space industry by turning fragmented interests into executable collaboration.

Our work is structured around four core pillars of impact:

Policy and Impact : We operate as a research hub that informs evidence-based decision-making and supports effective space governance. We produce white papers and reports on key industry issues (like sustainability and geopolitics) and convene policy dialogues to strengthen cooperation between governments, academia, and industry.

Accelerator : We empower the next generation of space entrepreneurs by providing access to mentorship, training, and investment opportunities. Our programs nurture innovative startups and scale-ups, driving commercial growth and strengthening the competitiveness of the space economy.

Ecosystem Engagements : We serve as a global platform for dialogue through annual and regional conferences, expos, and roundtables. We bring together government leaders, commercial enterprises, defence stakeholders, researchers, and investors to exchange knowledge, form partnerships, and catalyse innovation.

Name: Now Take Unity
Description:
NTU es un programa aeroespacial chileno dedicado al desarrollo integrado de infraestructura de lanzamiento en la macrozona norte y un vehículo de pequeña carga basado en propulsión híbrida. Nuestro trabajo se fundamenta en la tesis 23°S, que identifica condiciones únicas en la Región de Antofagasta para habilitar capacidades de lanzamiento desde el hemisferio sur hacia órbitas LEO de alto valor estratégico.

Nuestro roadmap tecnológico 2026–2032 comprende: diseño y pruebas de un sistema de propulsión híbrida, desarrollo de GNC y aviónica, arquitectura de telemetría y comunicaciones, definición de corredores operacionales y despliegue progresivo de infraestructura terrestre destinada a operaciones de lanzamiento reguladas.

NTU busca contribuir a la construcción de capacidades espaciales en Chile y Latinoamérica, articulando tecnología, industria e infraestructura para habilitar una nueva etapa de desarrollo estratégico en la región. Trabajamos con estándares internacionales, enfoque técnico riguroso y colaboración institucional desde la fase inicial.

Name: ASAPspace
Description:
We do SSTO stands for Single Stage to Orbit, particularly for space tourism and other commercial applications. which refers to a spacecraft that is capable of reaching orbit without discarding any parts or stages along the way. This is in contrast to traditional rockets that typically use multiple stages, with each stage being discarded as it burns out and is no longer needed.

Building a viable SSTO spacecraft is challenging due to the need to carry all the necessary fuel and other supplies for the entire journey. It requires a very high thrust-to-weight ratio, which means the spacecraft must be very light and have powerful engines.

In the past, there have been a few attempts to develop SSTOs, but none have been successful. However, there are ongoing efforts to develop SSTO vehicles.
